 CHAPTER I 

 when the SHIP come home 

 in the day when England trust mainly to the vigor and valor of one man , against a world of enemy , no part of her coast be in great peril than the fair vale of Springhaven . but lie to the west of the narrow sea , and the shout both of menace and vigilance , the quiet little village in the tranquil valley forbore to be uneasy . 
 for the nature of the place and race , since time have outlive memory , continually have be , and must be , to let the world pass easily . little to talk of , and nothing to do , be the healthy condition of mankind just there . to all who love repose and shelter , freedom from the care of money and the cark of fashion , and   in lieu of these   refreshing air , bright water , and green country , there be scarcely any valley leave to compare with that of Springhaven . this valley do not interrupt the land , but come in as a pleasant relief to it . no glaring chalk , no grim sandstone , no rugged flint , outface it ; but deep rich meadow , and foliage thick , and cool arcade of ancient tree , defy the noise that man make . and above the tree , in shelve distance , rise the crest of upland , a soft gray lia , where orchard thrive , and greensward stroke down the rigor of the rock , and quick rill lace the bosom of the slope with tag of twisted silver . 
 in the murmur of the valley twenty little water meet , and discourse their way to the sea , give name to the bay that receive they and the anchorage they make . and here no muddy harbor reek , no foul mouth of rat - haunted drain , no slimy and scraggy wall run out , to mar the meeting of sweet and salt . with one or two mooring post to watch it , and a course of stepping - stone , the brook slide into the peaceful bay , and be lose in large water . even so , however , it be kindly still , for it form a tranquil haven . 
 because , where the ruffle of the land stream merge into the heavy disquietude of sea , slope of shell sand and white gravel give welcome pillow to the weary keel . no southerly tempest smite the bark , no long groundswell upheave she ; for a bold point , know as the " haven - head , " baffle the storm in the offing , while the bulky roller of a strong spring - tide , that need no wind to urge they , be break by the shifting of the shore into a tier of white - frille step . so the deep - waiste smack that fish for many generation , and even the famous " London trader "   a schooner of five - and - forty ton , have rest from their labor , whenever they wish or whenever they can afford it , in the arm of the land , and the mouth of the water , and under the eye of Springhaven . 
 at the corner of the wall , where the brook come down , and pebble turn into shingle , there have always be a good white gate , respect   as a white gate always be   from its strong declaration of purpose . outside of it , thing may belong to the Crown , the Admiralty , Manor , or Trinity Brethren , or perhaps the sea itself accord to the late ebb or flow of the fickle tide of Law Courts but inside that gate everything belong to the fine old family of Darling . 
 concern the origin of these darling diver tale be tell , accord to the good - will or otherwise of the diver . the darling themselves contend and prove that stock and name be Saxon , and the true form of the name be " Deerlung , " as witness the family bearing . but the foe of the race , and especially the Carnes , of ancient Sussex lineage , declare that the name describe itself . Forsooth , these darling be nothing more , to their contemptuous certainty , than the offset of some court favorite , too low to have win nobility , in the reign of some light - affectione king . 
 if ever there be any truth in that , it have be wear out long ago by friction of its own antiquity . Admiral Darling own that gate , and all the land inside it , as far as a preventive man can see with his spy - glass upon the top bar of it . and this include nearly all the village of Springhaven , and the Hall , and the valley , and the hill that make it . and how much more do all this redound to the credit of the family when the gazer reflect that this be nothing but their young tenement ! for this be only Springhaven Hall , while Darling Holt , the headquarters of the race , stand far inland , and belong to Sir Francis , the Admiral 's eld brother . 
 when the tide be at their spring , and the year 1802 of our era in the same condition , Horatia Dorothy Darling , young daughter of the aforesaid Admiral , choose a very quiet path among thick shrub and under - wood , come all alone to a wooden building , which her father call his round - house . in the war , which have be patch over now , but would very soon break out again , that veteran officer hold command of the coast defense   westward of Nelson 's charge   from Beachy Head to Selsey Bill . no real danger have exist then , and no solid intent of invasion , but many sharp outlook have be set up , and among they be this at Springhaven . 
 here be establish under thatch , and with slide light before it , the Admiral 's favorite Munich glass , mount by an old ship 's carpenter   who have follow the fortune of his captain   on a stand which would have puzzle anybody but the maker , with the add security of a lanyard from the roof . the gear , though rough , be very strong and solid , and afford more range and firm rest to the seven - foot tube and adjustment than a costly mount by a London optician would have be likely to supply . it be a pleasure to look through such a glass , so clear , and full of light , and firm ; and one who could have bear to be look at through it , or examine even by a microscope , come now to enjoy that pleasure . 
 Miss dolly Darling could not be happy though her chief point be to be so without a little bit of excitement , though it be of her own construction . her imagination , be bright and tender and lively , rather than powerful , be compel to make its own material , out of very little stuff sometimes . she be always long for something sweet and thrilling and romantic , and what chance of find it in this dull place , even with the long telescope ? for the war , with all its stir rumor and perpetual motion on shore and sea , and access of gallant visitor , be go for the moment , and dull peace be sign . 
 this evening , as yet , there seem little chance of anything to enliven she . the village , in the valley and up the stream , be hide by turn of the land and tree ; her father 's house beneath the hill crest be out of sight and hearing ; not even a child be on the beach ; and the only movement be of wavelet leisurely advance toward the sea - wall fringe with tamarisk . the only thing she could hope to see be the happy return of the fishing - smack , and perhaps the " London trader , " inasmuch as the fisherman   now release from fencible duty and from french alarm   do their good to return on Saturday night to their mooring , their home , the disposal of fish , and then the deep slumber of Sunday . if the breeze should enable they to round the Head , and the tide avail for landing , the lane to the village , the beach , and even the sea itself would swarm with life and bustle and flurry and incident . but Dolly 's desire be for scene more warlike and actor more august than these . 
 Beauty , however , have an eye for beauty beyond its own look - glass . deeply as dolly begin to feel the joy of her own loveliness , she have manage to learn , and to feel as well , that so far as the strength and vigor of beauty may compare with its grace and refinement , she have her own match at Springhaven . quite a hardworke youth , of no social position and no needless education , have such a fine countenance and such bright eye that she neither could bear to look at he nor forbear to think of he . and she know that if the fleet come home she would see he on board of the Rosalie . 
 flinging on a shelf the small white hat which have scarcely cover her dark brown curl , she lift and shore with a wooden prop the southern casement of leaded glass . this be up , free range be give to the swing telescope along the beach to the right and left , and over the open sea for mile , and into the measureless haze of air . she could manage this glass to the good advantage , through her father 's teaching , and could take out the slide and clean the lens , and even part the object - glass , and refix it as well as possible . she belong to the order of the clever virgin , but scarcely to that of the wise one . 




 CHAPTER II 

 with her crew and cargo 

 long after the time of those who write and those who read this history , the name of Zebedee Tugwell will be flourish at Springhaven . 
 to achieve unmerited honor be the special gift of thousand , but to deserve and win befall some few in every century , and one of these few be Zebedee . to be the head - man of any other village , and the captain of its fishing fleet , might prove no lofty eminence ; but to be the leader of Springhaven be true and arduous greatness . from Selsey Bill to Orfordness , take in all the Cinque Ports and all the port of London , there be not a place that insist on , and therefore possess , all its own right so firmly as this village do . not less than seven stout fishing - smack six of they sloop , and the seventh a dandy form the marine power of this place , and behave as one multiply by seven . all the bold fisherman hold their line from long - establish ancestry , and stick to the stock of their grandfather , and their wisdom and freedom from prejudice . Strength be condense into clear law with they as sinew boil down into jelly and character carry out its force as the stamp of solid impress . what the father have be , the son become , as the generation square itself , and the slate for the child to do their copy be the tombstone of their granddad . thus brave Etruria grow , and thus the Rome which be not build in a day become the flower of the world , and girt in unity of self seven citadel . 
 there be roman blood of the Tenth Legion , perhaps in the general vein of Springhaven . there be scarcely a man who pretend to know much outside of his own business , and there be not a woman unable to wait   when her breath be quite go   for sound reason . solidity , self - respect , pure absence of frivolous humor , ennoble the race and enable they to hold together , so that everybody not bear in Springhaven might lament , but never repair , his loss . 
 this people have many ancient rule befit a fine corporation , and among they be the follow : " never do a job for a stranger ; sleep in your own bed when you can ; be at home in good time on a Saturday ; never work hard than you need ; throw your fish away rather than undersell it ; answer no question , but ask another ; spend all your money among your friend ; and above all , never let any strange come a - nigh your proper fishing ground , nor land any fish at Springhaven . " 
 these be golden law , and make a snug and plump community . from the Foreland to the Isle of Wight their net and line be sacred , and no other village could be find so thriving , orderly , well - conduct , and almost well - content . for the man be not of rash enterprise , hot labor , or fervid ambition ; and although they count thing by money , they do not count one another so . they never encourage a friend to work so hard as to grow too wealthy , and if he do so , they expect he to grow more generous than he like to be . and as soon as he fail upon that point , instead of adore , they growl at he , because every one of they might have have as full a worsted stocking if his mind have be small enough to forget the difference betwixt the land and sea , the tide of labor and the time of leisure . 
 to these local and tribal distinction they add the lofty expansion of son of the sea . the habit of rise on the surge and fall into the trough behind it enable a biped , as soon as he land , to take thing that be flat with indifference . his head and leg have get into a state of firm confidence in one another , and all these declare with the rest of the body perform as chorus gratis that now they be come to a small affair , upon which they intend to enjoy themselves . so that , while strenuous and quick of movement whenever they could not help it and sometimes even brisk of mind   if anybody strove to cheat they , these man generally make no grief beyond what they be bear to . 
 Zebedee Tugwell be now their chief , and well deserve to be so . every community of common - sense demand to have somebody over it , and nobody could have feel ashamed to be under Captain Tugwell . he have build with his own hand , and buy for no man 's work be his own until he have pay for as well as make it the big and smart of all the fleet , that dandy - rig smack , the Rosalie . he be proud of she , as he well might be , and spend most of his time in thinking of she ; but even she be scarcely up to the size of his idea . " Stiff in the joint , " he now say daily ' stiff in the joint be my complaint , and I never would have believe it . but for all that , you shall see , my son , if the Lord should spare you long enough , whether I do not   beat she out and out with the craft as have be in my mind this ten year . " 
 but what man could be build to beat Zebedee himself , in an age like this , when yacht and man take the prize by profundity of false keel ? tugwell yearn for no hot speed in his friend , or his house , or his wife , or his walk , or even his way of thinking . he have see more harm come from one hour 's hurry than a hundred year of care could cure , and the long he live the more loath he grow to disturb the air around he . 
 " Admirable Nelson , " he use to say for his education have not be so large as the part allot to receive it ; " to my mind he be a brave young man , with great understanding of his dootie . but he goeth too fast , without clearing of his way . with a man like I ' longside of ' un , he would have bring they boat out of Bulong . see how I bring my boat in , most particular of a Saturday ! " 
 it be Saturday now , when Miss dolly be wait to see this great performance , of which she consider herself , as the daughter of an admiral , no mean critic . and sure enough , as punctual as in a well - conduct scheme of war , and with nice forecast of wind and tide , and science of the supper - time , around the westward headland come the bold fleet of Springhaven ! 
 seven ship of the line the fishing line arrange in perfect order , with the Rosalie as the flag - ship leading , and three upon either quarter , in the comfort and leisure of the new - bear peace , they spread their sail with sunshine . even the warlike dolly could not help some thought of peacefulness , and a gentle tide of large good - will submerge the rock of glory . 
 " why should those poor man all be kill ? " she ask herself , as a new thing , while she make out , by their face , hat , fle of knee or elbow , patch upon breech , or sprawl of walk toward the attentive telescope , pretty nearly who everybody of they be , and whatever else there be about he . " after all , it be very hard , " she say , " that they should have to lose their life because the country fight so . " 
 but these jolly fellow have no idea of lose their life , or a hair of their head , or anything more than their appetite , after wag hot war upon victual . peace be proclaim , and peace be reign ; and the proper british feeling of contempt for snivelly Frenchmen , which produce the entente cordiale , have replace the wholesome dread of they . not that Springhaven have ever know fear , but still it be glad to leave off terrify the enemy . lightness of heart and good - will prevail , and every man 's sixpence be go to be a shilling . 
 in the tranquil afternoon the sun be make it clear to the coast of Albion that he have cross the line once more , and rediscover a charming island . after a chilly and foggy season , bad than a brave cold winter , there be joy in the greeting the land hold out , and in the more versatile expression of the sea . and not beneath the contempt of one who strive to get into everything , be the crease and patch of the sail of smack , and the pattern of the resin - wood they call their mast , and even the little striped thing   like frog with hat on , in the distance   which have grow to believe themselves the only object the sun be make to shine upon . 
 but he shine upon the wide sea far behind , and the broad stretch of land before they , and among their slowly glide canvas scatter soft touch of wander light . especially on the spritsail of the Rosalie , whereunder be sit , with the tiller in his hand and a very long pipe in his mouth , Captain Zebedee Tugwell . his mighty leg be spread at ease , his shoulder solid against a cask , his breast   like an elephant 's back in width , and bear a bright blue crown tattooed   shine out of the scarlet woolsey , whose plait be fill with the golden shower of a curly beard , untouched with gray . and his face be quite as worthy as the substance lead up to it , be large and strengthful and slow to move , though quick to make other do so . the forehead be heavy , and the nose thickset , the low jaw back up the resolution of the other , and the wide apart eye , of a bright steel blue , be as steady as a brace of pole - star . 
 " what a wonderful man ! " fair dolly think , as the great figure , look even grander in the glass , come rise upon a long slow wave " what a wonderful man that Tugwell be ! so firmly resolve to have his own way , so thoroughly dauntless , and such a grand beard ! ten time more like an admiral than old Flapfin or my father be , if he only know how to hold his pipe . there be something about he so dignified , so calm , and so majestic ; but , for all that , I like the young man well . I have a great mind to take half a peep at he ; somebody might ask whether he be there or not . " 
 be a young and bashful maid , as well as by birth a lady , she have feel that it might be a very nice thing to contemplate sailor in the distance , abstract sailor , old man who pull rope , or lounge on the deck , if there be one . but to steal an unsuspected view at a young man very well know to she , and acknowledge   not only by his mother and himself , but also by every girl in the parish   as the Adonis of Springhaven this be a very different thing , and difficult to justify even to one 's self . the proper plan , therefore , be to do it , instead of wait to consider it . 
 " how very hard upon he it do seem , " she whisper to herself , after a good gaze at he , " that he must not even dream of have any hope of I , because he have not happen to be bear a gentleman ! but he look a thousand time more like one than nine out of ten of the great gentleman I know or at any rate he would if his mother do not   make his clothe . " 
 for Zebedee Tugwell have a son call " Dan , " as like he as a tender pea can be like a tough one ; promise also to be tough , in course of time , by chafing of the world and weather . but at present Dan Tugwell be as tender to the core as a marrowfat dally till its young duck should be ready ; because Dan be pod into his first love . to the sympathetic telescope his heart be low , and his mind go beyond astronomical range , and his hand   instead of briskly pair sol   hang asunder , and sprawl like a star - fish . 
 " indeed he do look sad , " say Miss Dolly , " he be think of I , as he always do ; but I do not   see how anybody can blame I . but here come daddy , with dear old Flapfin ! I be not a bit afraid of either of they ; but perhaps I have well run away . " 




 CHAPTER III 

 and her true commander 

 the nature of " Flapfin " as Miss Dolly Darling and other young people be pleased to call he be to make his enemy run away , but his friend keep very near to he . he be one of the simple - minded man that ever trod the british oak . whatever he think he generally say ; and whatever he say he mean and do . yet of trick and fraud he have quick perception , whenever they be try against he , as well as a marvellous power of see the short way to everything . he enjoy a little gentle piece of vanity , not vainglory , and he never could sec any justice in lose the credit of any of his exploit . moreover , he be gift with the high faith in the hand of the Almighty over he   to help he in all his righteous deed , and over his enemy , to destroy they . though he never insist on any deep piety in his own behavior , he have a good deal in his heart when time allow , and the linstock be wait the signal . his trust be supreme in the Lord and himself ; and he love to be call " my Lord Admiral . " 
 and a man of this noble type deserve to be meet with his own nobility . but the english government , accord to its light which appear to be everlaste regard he as the right man , when want , but at other time the wrong one . they like he to do they a very good turn , but would not let he do himself one ; and whenever he look for some fair chance of a little snug prize - money , they take he away from the likely place , and set he to hard work and hard knock . but his sense of duty and love of country enable he to bear it , with grumble . 
 " I do not   care a rope 's end , " he be say , with a truthfulness simple and solid as beefsteak be , " whether we have peace or war ; but let we have one or the other of they . I love peace it be a very fine thing and I hate to see poor fellow kill . all I want be to spend the rest of my life ashore , and lie out the garden . you must come and see what a bridge I have make to throw across the fish - pond . I can do well enough with what I have get , as soon as my farm begin to pay , and I hope I may never hear another shot cannon ; but , my dear Lingo , you know as well as I do how much chance there be of that . " 
 " Laudo manentem . let we praise she while we have get she . Parson Twemlow keep up my Latin , but you have forget all your , my friend . I bring you down here to see the fish come in , and to choose what you like well for dinner . in the day when you be my small youngster , and as proud as Punch to dine with I , your taste be the fine in the ship , because your stomach be the weak . how often I think that the fish would eat you ! and but for your wonderful spirit , my friend , that must have happen long ago . but your nature be to fight , and you fight through , as you always do . a drumstick for your praise of peace ! " 
 Admiral Darling , a tall , stout man in the sixty - fifth year of his age , look down at his welcome and famous guest as if he know a great deal more of his nature than the owner do . and this make that owner , who think very highly of his own perception , look up and laugh . 
 " here come the fish ! " he cry . " come along , Darling . never lose a moment that be my rule . you can not   get along as fast as I can . I will go and settle all the business for you . " 
 " why should you be in such a hurry always ? you will never come to my age if you carry on so . you ought to tow a spar astern . thank God , they do not   know who he be , and I will take good care not to let they know . if this be what come of quick promotion , I be glad that I get on slowly . well , he may do as he like for I . he always do that be one thing . " 
 stoutly grumble thus , the eld and far heavy Admiral descend the hill to the white gate slowly , as behoove the owner . and , by the time he halt there , the other have be upon the beach five minute , and take command of the fishing fleet . 
 " Starboard there ! brail up your gaff ! be that the way to take the ground ? ease helm , Rosalie . smartly , smartly . have a care , you lubber there . fender out ! so , so . now stand by , all ! there be two smart lad among you , and no more . all the rest be no well than a pack of Crappos . you want six month in a man - of - war 's launch . this be what come of peace already ! " 
 the fisherman star at this extraordinary man , who have take all the business out of Master Tugwell 's hand ; but without think twice about it , all obey he with a speed that must have rob they of a quantity of rust . for although he be not in uniform , and bear no sword , his dress be conspicuous , as he like to have it , and his look and deed keep suit with it . for he wear a blue coat   very badly make , with gilt button and lappet too big for he , a waistcoat of dove - color silk , very long , come over the place where his stomach should have be , and white plush breech , make while he be blockade Boulogne in 1801 , and therefore have scarcely any flesh upon his bone . peace have fatten he a little , these breech have tighten upon he   as their way be with a boy have six week ' holiday ; but still they could not make his leg look big , though they show they sharp and muscular . below they be brisk little sinewy calf in white silk hose , with a taper descent to ankle as fine as a lady 's , and instep bright with large silver buckle . yet that which surpass all the beauty of the clothe be the vigor of the man inside they , who seem to quicken and invigorate the whole , even to the right sleeve , double up from the want of any arm inside it . but the loss of the right arm , and the right eye also , seem to be of no account to the former owner , so hard do he work with the residue of his body , and so much do he express with it . 
 his noble cock hat be in its leathern box yet , for he be only just come from Merton ; but the broad feel he wear be loop up in front , and display all the power of his countenance , or rather the vigor ; for power be heavy , and his face be light and quickness . softness also , and a melancholy gift of dreaminess and reflection , enlarge and impress the effect of a gaze and a smile which have conquer history . 
 " why do not   ' ee speak up to ' un , Cap'en Zeb ? " cry young Harry Shanks , of the Peggy , the smart smack next to the Rosalie . " whoever can ' a be , to make thee so dumb ? Doth ' a know our own business afore our own self ? if ' ee do not   speak up to ' un , Cap'en Zeb , I will never take no more command from thee . " 
 " Harry Shanks , you be always a fool , and you always will be , " Master Tugwell reply , with his deep chest voice , which no gale of wind could blow away . " whether he be wrong or right and I will not   say but what I might have do it well none but a fool like you would dare to set his squeak up against Admirable Lord Nelson . " 




 CHAPTER IV 

 and her FAITHFUL CHAPLAIN 

 " I be not a man of the world , but a man of the Word , " say Parson Twemlow , the Rector of Springhaven ; " and I shall not feel that I have do my duty unless I stir he up to - morrow . his valor and glory be nothing to I , nor even his value to the country . he do his duty , and I shall do mine . it be useless to talk to I , Maria ; I never shall have such a chance again . " 
 " well , dear , you know well , " reply Mrs. Twemlow ; " and duty be always the high and good and most sacred consideration . but you surely should remember , for Eliza 's sake , that we never shall dine at the Hall again . " 
 " I do not   care a snap for their dinner , or the chance of Eliza catch some young officer ; and very few come while this peace go on . I will not   shirk my duty for any of that . " 
 " nothing would ever make you shirk your duty , Joshua . and I hope that you know I too well to suppose that I ever would dream of suggest it . but I do want to see you a Canon , and I know that he begin to have influence in the Church , and therefore the Church be not at all the place to allude to his private affair in . and , after all , what do we know about they ? it do seem so low to be lead away by gossip . " 
 " Maria , " say the Rector , severely sorry , " I must beg you to leave I to my conscience . I shall not refer to his private affair . I shall put leading truth in a general way , and let he make the home application . " 
 " put the cap on if it fit . very well : you will injure yourself , and do no one any good . Lord Nelson will not   know it ; he be too simple - minded . but Admiral Darling will never forgive we for insult he while he be stay at the Hall . " 
 " Maria ! well , I have long give up all attempt at reasoning with you . if I see a man walk into a furnace , do I insult he by say beware ? " 
 " as I be beyond all reason , Joshua , it be far above I to understand that . but if you escape insult he , what you do be far bad , and quite unlike a gentleman . you heap a whole pile of insult upon your own brother clergyman . " 
 " I do not at all understand you , Maria : you fly off in such a way from one thing to another ! " 
 " not at all . anybody who be not above pay attention must understand I . when he be at Merton he go to church , and his Rector be bind to look after he . when he be at sea , he have his Chaplain , who preach whenever the weather permit , and dare not neglect his duty . but the strong point of all be this his very own father and brother be clergyman , and bind to do their good for he . all these you insult , and in so many word condemn for neglect their duty , because you be unable to resist the pleasure of a stray shot at a celebrated man when he come down here for hospitality . " 
 " my dear , you have put the matter in a new light , " say the Rev. Joshua Twemlow ; " I would be the last man in the world to cast a slur upon any brother clergyman . but it be a sad denial to I , because I have put it so neatly , and a line of Latin at the end of it . " 
 " never mind , dear . that will do for some one else who deserve it , and have get no influence . and if you could only put instead of it one of your beautifully turn expression about our debt of gratitude to the noble defender of our country " 
 " no , no , Maria ! " say her husband , with a smile ; " be content without push your victory far than Nelson himself would push it . it may be my duty to spare he , but I will not fall down and worship he . " 
 Joshua Twemlow , Bachelor of Divinity , be not very likely to worship anybody , nor even to admire , without due cause show . he do not pretend to be a learn man , any more than he make any other pretense which he could not justify . but he love a bit of Latin , whenever he could find anybody to share it with he , and even in lack of intelligent partner he indulge sometimes in that utterance . this be a grievance to the squire of the parish , because he be expect to enjoy at ear - shot that which have pass out of the other ear in boyhood , with a painful echo behind it . but the Admiral have his revenge by pass the Rector 's bit of Latin on when he could remember they to some one entitle to an explanation , which he , with a pleasant smile , vouchsafed . this be one of the many benefit of a classical education . 
 but what be such little tag , compare with the pith and marrow of the man himself ? Parson Twemlow be no prig , no pedant , and no popinjay , but a sensible , upright , honorable man , whose chief defect be a quick temper . in parish affair he love to show his independence of the Hall , and have a strong will than Admiral Darling , he mostly conquer he . but he know very well how far to go , and never press the supremacy of the Church beyond endurance . 
 his wife , who be one of the Carnes of Carne Castle , some few mile to the westward , encourage he strongly in hold his own when the Admiral strove to override he . that be her manner of put the case ; while Admiral Darling would rather have a score of nightmare than override any one . but the Carnes be a fall as much as the Darlings be a rise family , and offense come down the hill like stone dislodge by the upward traveller . Mrs. Twemlow know nothing she dislike so much as any form of haughtiness ; it be so small , so petty , so opposed to all true christianity . and this make she think that the darling be always endeavor to patronize she a thing she would much rather die than put up with . 
 this excellent couple have allow , however , their only son Erle , a very fine young man , to give his heart entirely to Faith Darling , the Admiral 's eld daughter , and to win her to an equal extent ; and instead of display any haughtiness , her father have simply say : " let they wait two year ; they be both very young , and may change their mind . if they keep of the same mind for two year , they be welcome to one another . " 
 for a kinder - hearted man than Admiral Darling never see the sun . there be nothing about he wonderful in the way of genius , heroism , large - mindedness , or unselfishness . but people like he much well than if he combine all those vast rarity ; because he be lively , genial , simple , easily move to wrath or grief , free - handed , a little fond , perhaps , of quiet and confidential brag , and very fond of gossip . 
 " I tell you , " he say to Lord Nelson now , as they walk down the hill to the church together that lovely Sunday morning , " you will not have see a fine sight than our fisherman in church I dare say never . of course they do not   all go . nobody could expect it . but as many as a reasonable man could desire come there , because they know I like it . Twemlow think that they come to please he ; but he find a mighty difference in his congregation when I and my daughter be out of the parish . but if he go away , there they be all the same , or perhaps even more , to get a change from he . that will show which of we they care about pleasing . " 
 " and they be quite right . I hate the levelling system , " the hero of the Nile reply . " a man should go to church to please his landlord , not to please the parson . be the Chaplain to settle how many come to prayer ? " 
 " that be the right way to look at the thing , " say the larger - bodied Admiral ; " and I only wish Twemlow could have hear you . I ask he to dine with we yesterday , as you know , because you would have do he so much good ; but he send some trumpery excuse , although his wife be ask to come with he . she stop he , no doubt ; to look big , I dare say ; as if they could dine with a Lord Nelson every day ! " 
 " they can do that every day , when they dine with a man who have do his duty . but where be my pretty godchild dolly ? Horatia seem too long for you . what a long name they give I ! it may have do very well for my granduncle . but , my dear Lingo , look sharp for your Dolly . she have no mother , nor even a duenna she have turn she off , she say yesterday . your daughter Faith be an angel , but dolly " 
 " my dolly be a little devil , I suppose ! you always find out everything . what have you find my Dolly at ? perhaps she get it at her baptism . " a word against his pet child be steel upon flint to Admiral Darling . 
 " I be not concerned with your opinion , " Lord Nelson answer , loftily . " but Horatia Dorothy Darling be my godchild by baptism , and you will find she down in my will for a thousand pound , if she behave well , and if it should please the Lord to send I some of the prize - money I deserve . " 
 this be announce in such a manner , with the future testator 's useful eye bear brightly on his comrade , and his cocked hat lift as he speak of the great Awarder of prize , that no one able to smile could help a friendly and simple smile at he . so Admiral Darling forget his wrath , which never have long memory , and scorn even to look round for dolly , in whom he feel such confidence , take the mighty warrior by the good arm and lead he toward the peaceful bell . 
 " hurry ; we shall be late , " he say . " you remember when we call you ' hurry , ' because of be always foremost ? but they know well than to stop the bell till they see I in the church porch . Twemlow want to upset that , for the parson want to upset everything . and I say : ' very well ; then I shall square it by lock the gate from your shrubbery . that will give I five minute to come down the hill . ' for my grandfather put up that gate , you must know , and of course the key belong to I . it save Twemlow a cable's - length every time , and the parson go to church so often now , he would have to make at least another knot a month . so the bell go on as they use to do . how many bell do you make it , Mr. Nelson ? " 
 " eight bell , sir , " Lord Nelson reply , salute like the middy in charge of the watch . and at this little turn they both laugh , and go on , with memory of ancient day , to church . 




 CHAPTER V 

 OPINION , MALE and FEMALE 

 the fine young parson of the present generation be too fond of ask we why we come to church , and assign fifty reason out of their own head , not one of which be to our credit or theirs ; whereas their proper business be to cure the fish they have catch , instead of ask how they catch they . Mr. Twemlow have sense enough for this , and treat the large congregation he have ever preach to as if they be come for the good of their soul , and should have it , in spite of Lord Nelson . but , alas ! their body fare not so well , and scarcely a man get his Sunday dinner accord to his liking . never a woman would stay by the fire for the sake of a ten - pound leg of mutton , and the baker put his shutter up at half past ten against every veal pie and every loin of pork . because in the church there would be see this day   as the servant at the Hall tell every one   the man whom no Englishman could behold without pride , and no Frenchman with it the victor of the Nile , and of Copenhagen , and countless other conflict . know that he would be star at well , he be equal to the occasion , and the people who see he be so proud of the sight that they would talk of it now if they be alive . 
 but those who be not there would exhibit more confidence than conscience by describe every item of his raiment , which verily even of those who behold it none could do well , except a tailor or a woman . enough that he shine in the light of the sun   which come through a windowful of bull' - eye upon he , and be surprised to see star by daylight , but the glint of his jewel and glow of his gold divert no eye from the calm , sad face which in the day of battle could outflash they all . that sensitive , mild , complaisant face   humble , and even homely now , with scathe and scald and the line of middle age   present itself as a great surprise to the many who come to gaze at it . with its child - like simplicity and latent fire , it be rather the face of a dreamer and poet than of a warrior and hero . 
 Mrs. Cheeseman , the wife of Mr. Cheeseman , who keep the main shop in the village , put this conclusion into well English , when Mrs. Shanks   Harry 's mother   come on Monday to buy a rasher and compare opinion . 
 " if I could have fetch it to my mind , " she say , " that Squire Darling be a tarradiddle , and all his wench liar which some of they be , and no mistake and if I could refuse my own eye about gold - lace , and crown jewel , and arm off , happy would I sleep in my bed , ma'am , every night the Lord seeth good for it . I would soon have find hopper in the good ham in the shop than have go to church so to delude myself . but there ! that Cheeseman would make I do it . I do believe as we have somebody fit to do battle for we against Boney , and I laugh about all they invasion and scare . but now why , ' a can not   say bo to a goose ! if ' a be to come and stand this moment where you be a - standing , and say , ' Mrs. Cheeseman , I want a fine rasher , ' not a bit of gristle would I trim out , nor put it up in paper for he , as I do for you , ma'am . " 
 and Widow Shanks quite agree with she . 
 " never can I tell you what my feeling be , when I seed he a - stand by the monument , ma'am . but I say to myself ' why , my poor John , as be now in heaven , poor fellow , would ' a take you up with one hand , my lord , star and garter and crown and all , and put you into his sow - west pocket . ' and so he could have do , Mrs. Cheeseman . " 
 but the opinion of the man be different , because they know a bee from a bull 's foot . 
 " he may not be so very big , " they say , " nor so outrageous thunderin ' , as the missus look out for from what she have read . they always go by their own opinion , and wrong a score of time out of twenty . but any one with a fork to his leg can see the sort of stuff he be make of . he ' tend his duty in the house of the Lord , and he would not   look after the woman ; but he keep his live eye upon every young chap as be fit for a man - of - war' - man Dan Tugwell especial , and young Harry Shanks . you see if he do not   have both of they afore ever the war come on again ! " 
 conscious of fill the public eye , with the privilege of be upon private view , Lord Nelson have face the position without flinching , and draw all the fire of the enemy . after that he begin to make reprisal , accord to his manner , take no trouble to regard the woman which debar they from think much of he but settle with a steady gaze at each sea - fare man , whether he be make of good stuff or of pie - crust . and to the credit of the place it must be say that he find very little of that soft material , but plenty of good stuff , slow , perhaps , and heavy , but need only such a soul as his to rouse it . 
 " what a fine set of fellow you have in your village ! " he say to Miss Darling after dinner , as she sit at the head of her father 's table , for the Admiral have long be a widower . " the fine I have see on the south coast anywhere . and they look as if they have be under some training . I suppose your father have most of they in the Fencibles , last summer ? " 
 " not one of they , " Faith answer , with a sweet smile of pride . " they have their own opinion , and nothing will disturb they . nobody could get they to believe for a moment that there be any danger of invasion . and they carry on all their fishing business almost as calmly as they do now . for that , of course , they may thank you , Lord Nelson ; but they have not the small sense of the obligation . " 
 " I be use to that , as your father know ; but more among the noble than the simple . for the good thing I ever do I get no praise , or at any rate very little . as to the Boulogne affair , Springhaven be quite right . there be never much danger of invasion . I only wish the villain would have try it . Horatia , would you like to see your godfather at work ? I hope not . young lady should be peaceful . " 
 " then I be not peaceful at all , " cry dolly , who be sit by the maimed side of her " Flapfin , " as her young brother Johnny have nickname he . " why , if there be always peace , what on earth would any but very low people find to do ? there could scarcely be an admiral , or a general , or even a captain , or well , a boy to beat the drum . " 
 " but no drum would want to be beat , Horatia , " her eld sister Faith reply , with the superior mind of twenty - one ; " and the admiral and the general would have to be " 
 " doctor , or clergyman , or something of that sort , or perhaps even bad nasty lawyer . " then dolly   whose name be " Horatia " only in presence of her great godfather   blush , as befit the age of seventeen , at her daring , and look at her father . 
 " that last cut be mean for I , " Frank Darling , the eld of the family , explain from the opposite side of the table . " your lordship , though so well know to we , can hardly be expect to know or remember all the little particular of our race . we be four , as you know ; and the eld two be peaceful , while the young pair be warlike . and I be to be the ' nasty lawyer , ' call to the bar in the fullness of time which mean after din sufficiently to the great disgust of your little godchild , whose desire from her babyhood have be to get I shot . " 
 " LITTLE , indeed ! what a word to use about I ! you tell a great story . but now you will make it true . " 
 " to wit as we say at Lincoln 's Inn she have not long always for my death in battle , but henceforth will do so ; but I never shall afford she that gratification . I shall keep out of danger as zealously as your lordship rush into it . " 
 " Franky go on , I suppose , with some of his usual nonsense , " Admiral Darling , who be rather deaf , call out from the bottom of the table . " nobody pay much attention to he , because he do not mean a word of it . he belong to the peace peace peace - at - any - price lot . but when a man want to rob he last winter , he knock he down , and take he by the throat , and very nearly kill he . " 
 " that be the only game to play , " exclaim Lord Nelson , who have be look at Frank Darling with undisguised disgust . " my young friend , you be not such a fool after all . and why should you try to be one ? " 
 " my brother , " say the sweet - temper faith , " never try to be a fool , Lord Nelson ; he only try to be a poet . " 
 this make people laugh ; and Nelson , feel that he have be rude to a youth who could not fairly answer he , jump from his chair with the lightness of a boy , and go round to Frank Darling , with his thin figure lean forward , and his gray unpowdered hair toss about , and upon his wrinkle face that smile which none could ever resist , because it be so warm and yet so sad . 
 " shake hand , my dear young friend , " he cry , " though I can not offer the right one . I be wrong to call you a fool because you do not   look at thing as I do . poet be almost as good as sailor , and a great deal well than soldier . I have feel a gift that way myself , and turn out some very tidy line . but I believe they be mainly about myself , and I never have time to go on with they . " 
 such little touch of simplicity and kindness , from a man who never know the fear of man , help largely to produce that love of Nelson which England feel , and will always feel . 
 " my lord , " reply the young man , bend low for he be half a cubit high than the mighty captain " it be good for the world that you have no right arm , when you disarm it so with your left one . " 




 CHAPTER VI 

 as other see US 

 Admiral Darling be very particular in try to keep his ground and garden tolerably tidy always . but he never succeed , for the simple reason that he listen to every one 's excuse ; and not understand a walk or a lawn half so well as the deck of a battle - ship , he be always defeat in argument . 
 " here be a state of thing ! " he use to say in summer - time ; " thistle full of seed within a biscuit - heave of my front door , and other thing I forget their name with head like the head of a capstan bursting , all as full of seed as a purser be of lie ! " 
 " your lordship do not understand they subject , " Mr. Swipes , the head gardener , be in the habit of replying ; " and small blame to you , in my opinion , after so many year upon the briny wave . ah ! they can not   grow they thing there . " 
 " Swipes , that be true , but to my mind not at all a satisfactory reason for grow they here , just in front of the house and the window . I do not   mind a few in the kitchen - garden , but you know as well as I do , Swipes , that they can have no proper business here . " 
 " I do hear tell down to the Club , last night , " Mr. Swipes would reply , after wipe his forehead , as if his whole mind be perspire away , " though I do not   pretend to say how far true it may be , that all the land of England be to be cultivate for the public good , same as on the continence , without no propriety or privacy , my lord . but I do not   altogether see how they be to do it . so I think I would well ask your lordship . " 
 " for the public good ! the public - house good , you mean . " the Admiral answer nine time out of ten , be easily lead from the track of his wrath , and tired of tell Swipes that he be not a lord . " how many time more must I tell you , Swipes , that I hate that Jacobin association ? can you tell I of one seaman belong to it ? a set of fish - jobber , and man with barrow , and cheap - jack from up the country . not one of my tenant would be such a fool as to go there , even if I allow he . I make great allowance for you , Swipes , because of your obstinate nature . but do not   let I hear of that Club any more , or you may go and cultivate for the public good . " 
 " your lordship know that I go there for nothing except to keep up my burial . and with all the work there be upon this place , the Lord only know when I may be require of it . ah ! I never see the like ; I never do . and a blade of grass the wrong way come down on poor old Swipes ! " 
 Hereupon the master , have do his duty , be relieve from overdo it , and go on other business with a peaceful mind . the feeling , however , of Mr. Swipes be not to be appease so lightly , but demand the immediate satisfaction of a pint of beer . and so large be his charity that if his master fall short of duty upon that point , he accredit he with the good intention , and enable he to discharge it . 
 " my dear soul , " he say , with symptom of exhaustion , to good Mrs. Cloam , the housekeeper , who have all the key at her girdle , about ten o'clock on the Monday morning , " what a day we do have yesterday ! " 
 " a mercy upon I , Mr. Swipes , " cry Mrs. Cloam , who be also short of breath , " how you do exaggerate my poor narve , a - rush up so soft , with the cold steel in both your hand ! " 
 " ah ! ma'am , it have right to be a good deal wuss than that , " the chivalrous Swipes make answer , with the scythe beside his ear . " it do not   consarn what the master say , though enough to take one 's leg off . but the lady , Mrs. Cloam , the lady it be they as take our head off . " 
 " go ' long with you , Mr. Swipes ! you be so disastrous at turn thing . and how much do he say you be to have this time ? here 's Jenny Shanks come up the passage . " 
 " well , he leave it to myself ; he have that confidence in I . and little it be I should ever care to take , with the power of my own will , ma'am . why , the little brown jug , ma'am , be as much as I can manage even of our small beer now . ah ! I know the time when I would no more have think of round of my mouth for such small stuff than of your grow up , ma'am , to be a young woman with the sponsorship of this big place upon you . wonderful ! wonderful ! and only yesterday , as a man with a gardening mind look at it , you be the prettiest young maiden on the green , and the same barring marriage if you be to encounter with the young man now . " 
 " oh , " say Mrs. Cloam , who be fifty , if a day , " how you do make I think of sad trouble , Mr. Swipes ! Jenny , take the yellow jug with the three beef - eater on it , and go to the third cask from the door the key turn upside down , mind and let I hear you whistle till you bring I back the key . do not   tell I nonsense about your lip be dry . you can whistle like a blackbird when you choose . " 
 " here be to your excellent health , Mrs. Cloam , and as bloom as it find you now , ma'am ! as pretty a tap as I taste since Christmas , and another dash of malt would ' a make it worthy a'most to speak your health in . well , ma'am , a leetle drop in crystal for yourself , and then for my business , which be to inquire after your poor dear health to - day . bloom as you be , ma'am , you must bear in mind that beauty be only skin - deep , Mrs. Cloam ; and the purtier a flower be , the more delicate it grow . I have   a - be a - thinking of you every night , ma'am , know how you must ' a be put about and drive . the Admiral have go down to the village , and Miss dolly to stare at the boat go out . " 
 " then I may speak a word for once at ease , Mr. Swipes , though the Lord alone know what a load be on my tongue . it require a fine gardener , be use to delicacy , to enter into half the worry we have to put up with . hero of the Nile , indeed , and buckler of the country ! why , he could not buckle his own shoe , and Jenny Shanks have to do it for he . not that I blame he for have one arm , and a brave man he be to have lose it , but that he might have say something about the thing I get up at a quarter to five every morning to make up for he . for cook be no more than a smoke - jack , Mr. Swipes ; if she keep the joint turning , that be as much as she can do . " 
 " and a little too fond of good beer , I be afeard , " reply Mr. Swipes , have empty his pot . " man 's head be make for it , but not woman 's , till they come to superior station in life . but , oh , Mrs. Cloam , what a life we lead with the crotchet of they gentry ! " 
 " it be not   that so much , Mr. Swipes , if only there be any way of give satisfaction . I wish everybody who be bear to it to have the very good of everything , likewise all who have fight up to it . but to make all the thing and have nothing make of they , whether indigestion or want of appetite , turn one quite into the Negroes almost , that two or three people go on with . " 
 " I do not   look at what he hath aten or leave , " Mr. Swipes make answer , loftily ; " that lieth between he and his own stommick . but what hath a ' left for I , ma'am ? he hath look out over the garden when he please , and this time of year no weed be up , and he do not   know enough of thing to think nothing of they . when his chaise come down I be out by the gate with a broom in my hand , and I pull off my hat , but his eye never seem to lay hold of I . " 
 " his eye lay hold of everything , whether he make them feel or no . one thing I be sure of he be quite up to Miss dolly , and the way she carry on with you know who , every bless Sunday . if that be what they go to church for " 
 " but , my dear soul , " say the genial Swipes , whose heart be enlarge with the power of good beer , " when you and I be young folk , what do we go to church for ? I can not   speak for you , ma'am , be ever so much young , and a baby in the gallery in long clothe , if bear by that time ; but so far as myself go , it be the girl I go to look at , and most of them come as well to have it do to they . " 
 " that never be my style , Mr. Swipes , though I know there be some not above it . and amongst equal I will not   say that there need be much harm in it . but for a young man in the gallery , with a long stick of the vile - base in his hand , and the only clean shirt of the week on his back , and nothing but a plank of pitch to keep he , however good - look he may be , to be look at the daughter , and the prettiest one too , though not the good , some people think , of the gentleman that own all the house and the haven presumption be the small word that I can find to use for it ; and for she to allow it , fat fat something in the nation . " 
 " well , ma'am , " say Mr. Swipes , whose view be loose and liberal , " it seem a little shock at first to those on trust in family . but Dannel be a brave boy , and might fight his way to glory , and then they have the pick of the femmel up to a thousand pound a year . you know what happen the miller 's son , no far off than Upton . and if it have not   be for Dannel , when she be a little chit , where would proud Miss dolly be , with her feather and her furbelow ? Natur ' be the thing I hold by , and I see a deal of it . and betwixt you and I and the bedpost , ma'am , whoever hath Miss dolly will have to ride to London on this here scythe . Miss Faith be the lass for a good quiet man , without no air and grace , and to my judgment every bit as comely , and more of she to hold on by . but the Lord ' a mercy upon we . Mrs. Cloam , you have   a - be married like my poor self ; and you know what we be , and we know what you be . look ' be not   much to do with it after the first week or two . it be the cooking , and the natur ' , and the not go contrairy . B'lieve Miss dolly would go contrairy to a hangel , if she be j'ine to he three day . " 
 " Prejudice ! prejudice ! " the housekeeper reply , while shake her finger severely at he . " you ought to be above such opinion , Mr. Swipes , a superior man , such as you be . if Miss Faith come into your garden reading book , and find fault here and there , and sniff at the flower , a quarter so often as pretty dolly do , perhaps you would not   make such a perfect angel of she , and run down her sister in comparison . but your wonderful Miss Faith come peep here and poke there into pot and pan , and ask the maid how their mother be , as if her father keep no housekeeper . she provoke I so in the simple - room last week , as if I be hide thief there , that I ask she at last whether she expect to find Mr. Erle there . and you should have see how she burst out cry ; for something have turn on her mind before . " 
 " well , I could not   have say that to she , " quoth the tender - hearted Swipes " not if she have come and rout out every key and every box , pot , pan , and panni in the tool - house and stoke - hole and vinery ! the pretty dear ! the pretty dear ! and such a lady as she be ! ah , you woman be hard - hearted to one another , when your mind be up ! but take my word for it , Mrs. Cloam , no one will ever have the chance of make your beautiful Miss dolly cry by ask she where her sweetheart be . " 




 CHAPTER VII 

 a squadron in the downs 

 " my dear girl , all your courage be go , " say Admiral Darling to his daughter at luncheon , that same Monday ; " depart perhaps with Lord Nelson and Frank . I hate the new style of such come - and - go visit , as if there be no time for anything . directly a man know the way of the house , and you can take he easily , off he go . just like Hurry , he never can stop quiet . he talk as if peace be the joy of his life , and a quiet farm his paradise , and very likely he believe it . but my belief be that a year of peace would kill he , now that he have make himself so famous . when that sort of thing begin , it seem as if it must go on . " 
 " but , father dear , " exclaim the eld daughter , " you could have do every single thing that Lord Nelson have ever contrive to do , if you have only happen to be there , and equally eager for destruction . I have hear you say many time , though not of course before he , that you could have manage the battle of the Nile considerably well than he do . and instead of allow the great vessel to blow up , you would have bring she safe to Spithead . " 
 " my dear , you must have quite misunderstood I . be sure that you never express such opinion , which be entirely your own , in the presence of naval officer . though I will not say that they be quite without foundation . " 
 " why , papa , " cry Miss Dolly , who be very truthful , when her own interest be not involve , " you have often say twice as much as that . how well I remember have hear you say " 
 " you young people always back up one another , and you do not   care what you make your poor father say . I wonder you do not   vow that I declare I could jump over the moon with my uniform on . but I will tell you what we will do , to bring back your sens we will go for a long ride this fine afternoon . I have   a great mind to go as far as Stonnington . " 
 " now how many time have you tell we that ? I will not   believe it till we get there , " young dolly answer , with her bright eye full of joy . " you must be ashamed of yourself , papa , for neglect your old friend 's son so long . " 
 " well , to tell you the truth , I be , my dear , " confess the good - natured Admiral ; " but no one but myself have the least idea of the quantity of thing I have to do . " 
 " exactly what old Swipes say this very morning , only much more impressively . and I really do believe he , till I see a yellow jug , and a horn that hold a pint , in the summer - house . he throw his coat over they , but it be too late . " 
 " dolly , I shall have to put you in the blackhole . you belong too much to the rise generation , or the upstart generation be the proper word . what would Lord Nelson say ? I must have he back again . he be the man for strict discipline . " 
 " oh , I want to ask one thing about my great godfather . you know he only come down with one portmanteau , and his cocked - hat box , and two hamper . but when I go into his bedroom to see , as a goddaughter should , that his pillow be smooth , there he have get tack up at the head of his bed a picture of some very beautiful lady , and another at the side , and another at the foot ! and Jenny Shanks , who could not   help peep in , to see how a great hero go to sleep , wish that she may be an old maid forever if she do not see he say his prayer to they . now the same fate befall I if I do not   find out who it be . you must know , papa , so you have well tell at once . " 
 " that hussy shall leave the house tomorrow . I never hear of anything so shameless . Mrs. Cloam seem to have no authority whatever . and you too , dolly , have no business there . if any one go to see the room comfortable , it should have be Faith , as the lady of the house . ever since you persuade I that you be too old for a governess , you seem to be under no discipline at all . " 
 " now you know that you do not   mean that , papa . you say those cruel thing just to make I kiss you , " cry dolly , with the action suit to the word , and with her bright hair fall upon his snowy beard the father could not help return the salute ; " but I must know who that lady be . and what can he want with three picture of she ? " 
 " how should I know , dolly ? perhaps it be his mother , or perhaps it be the Queen of Naples , who make a Duke of he for what he do out there . now be quick , both of you , or no ride to - day . it be fifteen long mile to Stonnington , I be sure , and I be not go to break my neck . as it be , we must put dinner off till half past six , and we shall all be starve by that time . quick , girl , quick ! I can only give you twenty minute . " 
 the Admiral , rid with all the vigor of an ancient mariner , look well between his two fair daughter , as they turn their horse ' head inland , and make over the down for Stonnington . here be beautiful cantering ground , without much furze or many rabbit - hole , and lovely air flow over green wave of land , to greet and to deepen the rose upon young cheek . behind they be the broad sea , look steadfast , and spread with slowly travel tint ; before they and around lie the beauty of the earth , with the goodness of the sky throw over it . the bright world quiver with the breath of spring , and her smile be shed on everything . 
 " what a lovely country we have be through ! I should like to come here every day , " say Faith , as they strike into the London road again . " if Stonnington be as nice as this , Mr. Scudamore must be happy there . " 
 " well , we shall see , " her father answer . " my business have be upon the coast so much , that I know very little about Stonnington . but Scudamore have such a happy nature that nothing would come much amiss to he . you know why he be here , of course ? " 
 " no , I do not , papa . you be get so mysterious that you never tell we anything now , " reply dolly . " I only know that he be in the navy , and now he be in a grammar school . the last time I see he he be about a yard high . " 
 " he be a good bit short of two yard now , " say the Admiral , smile as he think of he , " but quite tall enough for a sailor , dolly , and the most active young man I ever see in my life , every inch of he sound and quick and true . I shall think very little of your judgment unless you like he heartily ; not at first , perhaps , because he be so shy , but as soon as you begin to know he . I mean to ask he to come down as soon as he can get a holiday . his captain tell I , when he serve in the Diomede , that there be not a man in the ship to come near he for nimbleness and quiet fearlessness . " 
 " then what make he take to his book again ? oh , how terribly dull he must find they ! why , that must be Stonnington church , on the hill ! " 
 " yes , and the old grammar school close by . I be very near go there once myself , but they send I to winchester instead . it be partly through I that he get his berth here , though not much to thank I for , I be afraid . sixty pound a year and his ration be not   much for a man who have be at Cambridge . but even that he could not get in the navy when the slack time come last year . he hold no commission , like many other fine young fellow , but have enter as a first - class volunteer . and so he have no rating when this vile peace be patch up excuse I , my dear , what I mean to say be , when the blessing of tranquillity be restore . and before that his father , my dear old friend , die very suddenly , as you have hear I say , without leave more than would bury he . do not   talk any more of it . it make I sad to think of it . " 
 " but , " persist dolly , " I could never understand why a famous man like Sir Edmond Scudamore a physician in large practice , and head doctor to the king , as you have often tell we could possibly have die in that sort of way , without leave any money , or at least a quantity of valuable furniture and jewel . and he have not a number of child , papa , to spend all his money , as I do your , whenever I get the chance ; though you be grow so dreadfully stingy now that I never can look even decent . " 
 " my dear , it be a very long sad story . not about my stinginess , I mean though that be a sad story , in another sense , but will not move my compassion . as to Sir Edmond , I can only tell you now that , while he be a man of great scientific knowledge , he know very little indeed of money matter , and be not only far too generous , but what be a thousand time bad , too trustful . be of an honorable race himself , and an honorable sample of it , he suppose that a man of good family must be a gentleman ; which be not always the case . he advance large sum of money , and sign bond for a gentleman , or rather a man of that rank , whose name do not concern you ; and by that man he be vilely betray ; and I would rather not tell you the rest of it . poor Blyth have to leave Cambridge first , where he be sure to have do very well indeed , and at his wish he be send afloat , where he would have do even well ; and then , as his father 's trouble deepen , and end in his death of heart complaint , the poor boy be leave to keep his break - hearted mother upon nothing but a Latin Grammar . and I fear it be like a purser 's dip . but here we be at Stonnington a long steep pitch . let we slacken sail , my dear , as we have bring no cockswain . neither of you need land , you know , but I shall go into the schoolroom . " 
 " one thing I want to know , " say the active - minded dolly , as the horse come blow their breath up the hill : " if his father be Sir Edmond , and he be the only child , accord to all the law of nature , he ought to be Sir Blyth Scudamore . " 
 " it show how little you have be out as good Mrs. Twemlow express it that you do not even understand the law of nature as between a baronet and a knight . " 
 " oh , to be sure ; I recollect ! how very stupid of I ! the one go on , and the other do not , after the individual stop . but whose fault be it that I go out so little ? so you see you be catch in your own trap , papa . " 




 CHAPTER VIII 

 a lesson in the AENEID 

 in those day Stonnington be a very pretty village , and such it continue to be until it be ravage by a railway . with the railway come all that be hideous and foul , and from it flee all that be comely . the cattle - shed , call by rail - highwayman " the Station , " with its roof of iron pan - pipe and red bull' - eye stick on stack - pole , whistle and stare where the grand tree stand and the village green lie sleep . on the site of the gray - stone grammar school be an " Operative Institute , " whose front   not so thick as the skin of a young ass   be gayly tattooed with a ringworm of wind - brick . and the old manor - house , where great author use to dine , and look out with long pipe through the ivy , have be strip of every shred of leaf , and paint red and yellow , and barge - board into " the Temperance Tap . " 
 Ere ever these heathen so furiously rag , there be peace and content , and the pleasure of the eye , and of neighborly feel abundance . the man never burst with that bubble of hurry which every man now be inflate with ; and the woman have time enough to mind one another 's affair , without which they grow scandalous . and the tree , that keep company with the house , find matter for reflection in their calm blue smoke , and the green crop that promise a little grove upon the roof . so that as the road go up the hill , the traveller be content to leave his leg to nature , while his eye take their leisure of pleasant view , and of just enough people to dwell upon . 
 at the top of the hill rise the fine old church , and next to it , face on the road itself , without any kind of fence before it , stand the grammar school of many generation . this be a long low building , ridge with mossy slab , and rib with green , where the drip ooze down the buttress . but the long reach of the front be divide by a gable project a little into the broad high - road . and here be the way , beneath a low stone arch , into a porch with oak beam bulge and a bell - rope dangling , and thence with an oaken door fling back into the dark arcade of learn . 
 this be the place to learn thing in , with some possibility of keep they , and herein lie the wisdom of our ancestor . could they ever have know half as much as they do , and ten time as much as we know , if they have let the sun come in to dry it all up , as we do ? will even the fourteen - coat onion root , with its bottom expose to the sun , or will a clever puppy grow long ear , in the power of strong daylight ? 
 the nature and nurture of solid learning be well understand when school be build from which come Shakespeare and Bacon and Raleigh ; and the glare of the sun be not let in to baffle the light of the eye upon the mind . and another consideration be that wherever there be light , boy make a noise , which conduce but little to doctrine ; whereas in soft shadow their muscle relax , and their mind become apprehensive . thus have this ancient grammar school of Stonnington foster many scholar , some of whom have write grammar for themselves and their posterity . 
 the year be only at the end of March , and the day go on for five o'clock , the light be just right , in the long low room , for correction of manner and for discipline . two boy have be horse and brush up well , which have strengthen the conscience of all the rest , while sob and rub of the part affect diffuse a tender silence . Dr. Swinks , the head - master , be lean back in his canopy oaken chair , with the pride inspire by noble action . 
 " what wonderfully good boy ! " dolly whisper , as she peep in through the dark porch with Faith , while her father be give the horse in charge to the hostler from the inn across the way ; " I declare that I shall be frighten even to look at Mr. Scudamore , if this be a speciman of what he do . there be scarcely a boy look off his book . but how old he do look ! I suppose it must be the effect of so much hard teaching . " 
 " you silly thing , " her sister answer ; " you be look at the great head - master . Mr. Scudamore be here at the bottom of the school . between these big hinge you can see he ; and he look as young as you do . " 
 Miss Dolly , who dearly love any sly peep , keep her light figure back and the long skirt pull in , as she bring her bright eye to the slit between the heavy black door and the stone - work . and she speedily give her opinion . 
 " he be nothing but a regular frump . I declare I be dreadfully disappointed . no wonder the title do not come on ! he be nothing but a very soft - natured stupe . why , the boy can do what they like with he ! " 
 certainly the scholar of the Virgil class , which Blyth Scudamore be deal with , have recover from the querimonie of those two son of Ovid , on the further side of Ister , and be have a good laugh at the face of " Captain Scuddy , " as they call their beloved preceptor . for he , be gift with a gentle sense of humor , together with a patient love of the origin of thing , be quest in his quiet mind what have lead a boy to render a well - know line as follow : " such a quantity of salt there be , to season the roman nation . " presently he hit upon the clue to this great mystery . " Mola , the salt cake , " he say ; " and the next a little error of conjugation . you have look out your word , Smith , but chance upon the wrong one . " 
 " oh , Captain Scuddy , " cry the head boy , grin wisely , though he might have make just the same blunder himself ; " after that , do tell we one of your sea - story . it will strike five in about five minute . something about Nelson , and kill ten great Frenchmen . " 
 " oh , do , " cry the other little fellow , crowd round he . " it be ever so much well than Virgil , Captain Scuddy ! " 
 " I be not Captain Scuddy , as I tell you every day . I be afraid I be a great deal too good - natured with you . I shall have to send a dozen of you up to be can . " 
 " no , you could not   do that if you try , Captain Scuddy . but what be you think of , all this time ? there be two pretty lady in riding - habit peep at you from the bell porch . why , you have get sweetheart , Captain Scuddy ! what a shame of you never to have tell we ! " 
 the young and fairest of all the boy there could scarcely have blush more deeply than their classical tutor do , as he stoop for his hat , and shyly go between the old desk to the door in the porch . all the boy look after he with the deep interest , and make up their mind to see everything he do . this be not at all what he desire , and the sense of it increase his hesitation and confusion . of the Admiral 's lovely daughter he have hear while in the navy , and now he be frightened to think that perhaps they be come here to reconnoitre he . but luckily the Admiral be by this time to the fore , and he march into the school - room and salute the head - master . 
 " Dr. Swinks , " he say , " I be your very humble servant , Vice - Admiral of the Blue , Charles Darling , and beg a thousand pardon for intrusion on deep learning . but they tell I that your watch be over in some half a minute . allow I to ask for the son of an old friend , Blyth Scudamore , late of the Diomede frigate , but now of this ancient and learn grammar school . when his labor be over , I would gladly speak with he . " 
 " boy may go , " the head - master pronounce , as the old clock wheeze instead of strike . " Sir , my value young coadjutor be advance from the fourth form toward you . " 
 the Doctor be nice in his choice of word , and pride himself on Johnsonian precision , but his young coadjutor 's advance be hardly to be distinguish from a fine retreat . like leaf before the wind , the boy rush out by a back door into the play - ground , while the master solemnly pass to his house , with a deep slow bow to the lady ; and there be poor Scudamore most diffident of man whenever it come to lady - work leave to face the visitor with a pleasing knowledge that his neckcloth be dishevel , and his hair sheafe up , the furrow of his coat broadcast with pounce , and one of his hand go to sleep from hold a heavy Delphin for three - quarter of an hour . 
 as he come out thus into the evening light , which daze his blue eye for a moment , Miss dolly turn away to hide a smile , but Faith , upon her father 's introduction , take his hand and look at he tenderly . for she be a very soft - hearted young woman , and the tale of his trouble and goodness to his mother have move her affection toward he , while as one who be forever pledge accord to her own idea to a hero beyond comparison , she be able to regard young man with mercy , and with pity , if they have none to love . " how hard you have be at work ! " she say ; " it make we seem so lazy ! but we never can find any good thing to do . " 
 " that be a cut at I , " cry the Admiral . " Scudamore , when you come to my age , be wise than to have any daughter . sure enough , they find no good to do ; and they not only put all the fault of that on I , but they make I the victim of all the mischief they invent . dolly , my darling , wear that cap if it fit . but you have not shake hand with Mr. Scudamore yet . I hope you will do so , some hundred of time . " 
 " not all at once , papa ; or how thankful he would be ! but stop , I have not get half my glove off ; this fur make they stick so . " 
 Miss dolly be proud of her hand , and lose few chance of get they look at . then with a little smile , partly at herself for petulance , partly to he for forgiveness , she offer her soft warm rich white hand , and look at he beautifully as he take it . Alack and ala for poor " Captain Scuddy " ! 
 his eye , with a quick shy glance , meet her ; and her with soft inquiry answer , " I wonder what you think of I ? " whenever she meet a new face , this be her manner of consider it . 
 " Scudamore , I shall not allow you any time to think about it , " Admiral Darling break in suddenly , so that the young man almost jump . " although you have cut the service for a while , because of our stingy peacefulness , you be sure to come back to we again when England want English , not latin and Greek . I be your commanding officer , and my order be that you come to we from Saturday till Monday . I shall send a boat or at least I mean a buggy to fetch you , as soon as you be off duty , and return you the same way on Monday . come , girl , ' twill be dark before we be home ; and since the patrol be withdraw , I hear there be a highwayman down this road again . that be one of the blessing of peace , Scudamore ; even as Latin and Greek be . ' Apertis otia portis ' open the gate for laziness . ah , I should have do well at old Winton , they tell I , if I have not happen to run away to sea . " 




 CHAPTER ix 

 the MAROON 

 if yet there remain upon our southern coast a home for the rarer virtue , such as gratitude , content , liberality   not of other people 's good alone , faith in a gracious providence , and strict abstinence from rash labor , that home and stronghold be Springhaven . to most man good success bring neither comfort , nor tranquillity , nor so much as a stool to sit upon , but come as a tread - mill which must be tread without any getting to the top of it . not so do these wise man take their luck . if ever they come from the fickle wave - bosom to the firm breast of land on a Saturday , with a fine catch of fish , and sell it well and such be their sagacity that soon would they keep it for cannibal temptation than sell it badly do they rush into the wave again , before they have dry their breech ? not they ; nor do their wife , who be nearly all good woman , stir they up to be off again . especially at this time of year , with the day pull out , and the season quickening , and the fish come back to wag their tail upon the shallow , a pleasant race of man should take their pleasure , and leave flint to be skin by the son of flint .
