 this be the reason why Miss Dolly Darling have watch in vain at the Monday morning tide for the bold issue of the fishing fleet . the weariless tide come up and lift the bedded keel and the plunged forefoot , and gurgle with a quiet wash among the straky bend , then lurch the boat to this side and to that , to get their heft correctly , and dandle they at last with their bowsprit dip and their little mast - head nodding . every brave smack then be mount , and riding , and ready for a canter upon the broad sea : but not a blessed man come to set she free . tether by head and by heel , she could only enjoy the poise pace of the rock - horse , instead of the racer 's delight in career across the free sweep of the distance . 
 Springhaven have do so well last week , that this week it mean to do still well , by stop at home till the money be go , and make short work afterward . every man thoroughly enjoy himself , keep sober whenever good manner allow , forego all business , and saunter about to see the folk hard at work who have get no money . on Wednesday , however , an order be issue by Captain Zebedee Tugwell that all must be ready for a three day ' trip when the tide should serve , which would be at the first of the ebb , about ten in the morning . the tide be slacken now , and the smack have require some change of berth , but still they be not very far from the Admiral 's white gate . 
 " I shall go down to see they , papa , if you please , " dolly say to her father at breakfast - time . " they should have go on Monday ; but they be too rich ; and I think it very shameful of they . I dare say they have not get a halfpenny left , and that make they look so lively . of course they have   be stuff , and they will not   move fast , and they can not   expect any more dinner till they catch it . but they have get so much bacon that they do not   care . " 
 " what could they have well , I should like to know ? " ask the Admiral , who have see hard time . " why , I give seven man three dozen apiece for turn their nose up at salt horse , just because he whisk his tail in the copper . Lord bless my soul ! what be the nation come to , when a man can not   dine upon cold bacon ? " 
 " no , it be not that , papa . they be very good in that way , as their wife will tell you . Jenny Shanks tell I the very same thing , and of course she know all about they . she know they would never think of go out on Monday , and if I have ask she I might have know it too . but she say that they be sure to catch this tide . " 
 " very well , dolly . go you and catch they . you be never content without see something . though what there be to see in a lot of lubberly craft push off with punt - pole " 
 " hush , papa , hush ! do not   be so contemptuous . what do my godfather say the other day ? and I suppose he understand thing . " 
 " do not   quote your godfather against your father . it be never intend in the Catechism . and if it be , I would never put up with it . " 
 dolly make off ; for she know that her father , while proud of his great impartiality , candor , and scorn of all trumpery feeling , be sometimes unable to make out the reason why a queer little middy of his own should now stand upon the giddy truck of fame , while himself , still ahead of he in the Navy List , might pace his quarter - deck and have hat touch to he , but never a heart beat one pulse quick . jealous he be not ; but still , at least in his own family 
 leave her dear father to his meditation , which faith run up to kiss away , fair dolly put on a plain hat and scarf , quite good enough for the fisherman , and set off in haste for the Round - house , to see the expedition start . by the time she be there , and have lift the sash , and get the spy - glass ready , the flow of the tide be almost spend , and the brimming moment of the slack be nigh . for this all the folk of the village wait , accord to the tradition of the place ; the manhood and boyhood , to launch forth ; old age , womanhood , and childhood , to contribute the comfort of kind look and good - by . the tide , though not to be compare to the wind in fickleness , be capricious here , have sally of irregularity when there have be a long period of northeast wind , bring a counter - flow to the atlantic influx . and a man must be thoroughly acquaint with the coast , as well as the moon and the weather , to foretell how the water will rise and fall there . for the present , however , there be no such puzzle . the last lift of the quiet tide shine along the beach in three straight wave , shallow step that arise inshore , and spend themselves without break . 
 " toorn o ' the tide ! " the Captain shout ; " all aboord , aboord , my lad ! the more ' ee bide ashore , the wuss ' ee be . see to Master Cheeseman 's craft ! got a good hour afront of we . Dannel , what be moon at ? Fetch ' un a clout on his head , Harry Shanks ; or Tim , you run up and do it . doubt the young hosebird be strike last moon , and his brain put to salt in a herring - tub . home with you , wife ! and take Dan , if you will . he would do more good at the chipping job , with the full moon in his head so . " 
 " then home I will take my son , Master Tugwell , " his wife answer , with much dignity , for all the good wife of Springhaven hear he , and what would they think of she if she say nothing ? " home I will take my son and your , and the wise place for he to abide in , with his father set agin he so . Dannel , you come along of I . I will not   have my eld boy gainsay so . " 
 Zebedee Tugwell close his lip , and go on with his proper business . all the woman would side with he if he leave they the use of their own mind , and the sound of his wife 's voice last ; while all the man in their heart feel wisdom . but the young man , loath to be leave behind , come doubtfully down to the stern of the boat , which be push off for the Rosalie . and he look at the place where he generally sit , and then at his father and the rest of they . 
 " no gappermouth here ! " cry his father , sternly . " get theezell home with the vemmelvolk . shove off without he , Tim ! how many more tide would ' ee lose ? " 
 Young Dan , whose stout leg be in the swirl water , snatch up his striped woolsey from under the tiller , throw it on his shoulder , and walk off , without a farewell to any one . the whole of Springhaven that could see see it , and they never have see such a thing before . Captain Zeb stand up and star , with his big forehead come out under his hat , and his golden beard shin in the morning sun ; but the only satisfaction for his eye be the back of his son grow small and small . 
 " chip of the old block ! " " Sarve ' ee right , Cap'en ! " " starve ' un back to his manner again ! " the inferior chieftain of the expedition cry , accord to their several view of life . but Zebedee Tugwell pay no heed to thought outside of his own hat and coat . " spake when I ax you , " he say , urbanely , but with a glance which convey to any too urgent sympathizer that he would be knock down , when accessible . 
 but , alas ! the less - discipline woman rejoice , with a wink at their depart lord , as Mrs. Zebedee set off in chase of her long - stride Daniel . the mother , enrich by home affection and course of duty well perform , be of a rounded and ample figure , while the son be tall , and thin as might be one of strong and well - knit frame . and the sense of wrong would not permit he to turn his neck , or take a glance at the enterprise which have reject he . 
 " how grand he do look ! what a noble profile ! " think dolly , who have see everything without the glass , but now bring it to bear upon his countenance . " he be like the centurion in the paint window , or a roman medallion with a hat on . but that old woman will never catch he . she might just as well go home again . he be walk about ten mile an hour , and how beautifully straight his leg be ! what a shame that he should not be a gentleman ! he be ten time more like one than most of the officer that use to come bother I so . I wonder how far he mean to go ? I do hope he will not   make away with himself . it be almost enough to make he do it , to be so insult by his own father , and disgrace before all the village , simply because he can not   help have his poor head so full of I ! nobody shall ever say that I do anything to give he the faint encouragement , because it would be so very wicked and so cruel , consider all he have do for I . but if he come back , when his father be out of sight , and he have walk off his righteous indignation , and all these people be go to dinner , it might give a turn to his thought if I be to put on my shell - color frock and the pale blue sash , and just go and see , on the other side of the stepping - stone , how much long they mean to be with that boat they begin so long ago . " 




 CHAPTER x 

 across the STEPPING - STONES 

 very good boat be build at this time in the south of England , stout , that be to say , and strong , and fit to ride over a heavy sea , and plunge gallantly into the trough of it . but as the strong man be seldom swift of foot or light of turn , so these robust and sturdy boat must have their own time and swing allow they , ere ever they would come round or step out . have meet a good deal of the sea , they know , like a man who have feel a good deal of the world , that heavy endurance and patient bluffness be safe to get through the wave somehow than sensitive fibre and elegant frame . 
 but the sea - go folk of Springhaven have learn , by lore of generation , to build a boat with an especial sheer forward , beam far back , and deep run of stern , so that she be lively in the heavy of weather , and strong enough to take a good thump smiling , when unable to dance over it . yet as a little thing often make all the difference in great thing , it be very difficult for anybody to find out exactly the difference between a boat build here and a boat build ten or twenty mile off , in imitation of she . the sea , however , know the difference in a moment between the true thing and the counterfeit , and encourage the one to go merrily on , while it send back the other staggering . the secret lie chiefly in a hollow curve forward of nine or ten plank upon either side , which could only be compass by skilful use of adze and chisel , frame - saw and small tool , after choice of the very good timber , free from knot , tough , and flexible . and the good judge of these point be Zebedee Tugwell . 
 not have cash enough just at present   by reason of family expense , and the high price of bread and of everything else   to set upon the stock the great smack of the future , which should sail round the Rosalie , Captain Tugwell be ease his mind by build a boat for stormy weather , such as they very seldom have inshore , but be likely to meet with outside the Head . as yet there be not many rowing boat here fit to go far in tumble water , though the few that could do it do it well , and Tugwell 's intention be to beat they all , in power , and spring , and buoyancy . the fame of his meaning be spread for as much as twenty league along the coast ; and jealous people laugh , instead of wait for he to finish it . 
 Young Daniel have be well bring up in the mystery of his father 's craft , and have a vigorous turn of wrist , as well as a true eye and quick brain , he be even outgrow the paternal skill , with experiment against experience . he have beautiful theory of his own , and feel certain that he could prove they , if any one with cash could be bring to see their beauty . his father admit that he have good idea , and might try they , if any fool would find the money . 
 Wroth as he have be at the sharp rebuff and contumely of his father , young Daniel , after a long strong walk , begin to look at thing more peaceably . the power of the land and the greatness of the sea and the goodness of the sky unangere he , and the air that come from some oyster bed , as the tide be fall , hunger he . home he go , in good time for dinner , as the duty of a young man be ; and instead of laugh when he come by , the maid of Springhaven smile at he . this quite right he in his own opinion , yet leave he the benefit of the doubt which come from a shake in that cradle lately . he make a good dinner , and shoulder his adze , with a frail of tool hang on the neck of it , and trouble with nothing but love which be a woe of self - infliction whistle his way to the beach , to let all the woman understand that he be not a bit ashamed . and they feel for he all the more , because he stand up for himself a little . 
 doubtful right go cheap ; and so the foreshore westward of the brook be claim by diver authority , a tidy little cantle of it have be lease by Admiral Darling , lord of the manor , to Zebedee Tugwell , boat - builder , for the yearly provent of two and sixpence sterling . the Admiral 's man of law , Mr. Furkettle , have strongly advise , and well prepare the necessary instrument , which would grow into value by - and - by , as evidence of title . and who could serve summary process of ejectment upon an interloper in a manner so valid as Zebedee 's would be ? possession be certain as long as he live ; ouster and filibuster , in the form of railway company and communist , be a bubble as yet in the womb of age . 
 this piece of land , or sand , or rush , seem very unlikely to be worth dispute . if seisin corporeal , user immemorial , and prescription for levance and couchance confer any title indefeasible , then be the rabbit the owner in fee - simple , absolute , paramount , and source of pedigree . but they , while thoroughly aware of this , take very little heed to go into it , nor trouble their gentle nature much about a few yard of sand or grass , as the two - legged creature near they do . inasmuch as they have soft bank of herb and vivid moss to sit upon , sweet crisp grass and juicy clover for unlabored victual as well as a thousand other nibble which we be too gross to understand and for beverage not only all the abundance of the brook   whose brilliance might taste of man , but also a little spring of their own which come out of its hole like a rabbit ; and then for scenery all the sea , with strange thing run over it , as well as a great park of their own have countless avenue of rush , ragwort , and thistle - stump where would they have deserve to be , if they have not be content ? content they be , and even joyful at the proper time of day . joyful in the morning , because the sun be come again ; joyful in the middle day to see how well the world go ; and in the evening merry with the trick of their own shadow . 
 quite fifteen step - stone step up if you count three that be make of wood to soothe the dignity of the brook in its last fresh - water moment , rather than to gratify the dry - skin'd sol of gentlefolk . for any one , with a five - shilling pair of boot to terminate in , might skip dry - footed across the sandy purling of the rivulet . and only when a flood come down , or the head of some springtide come up , do any but playful child tread the lichene crack of the stepping - stone . and nobody know this well than Horatia Dorothy Darling . 
 the bunny who live to the west of the brook have reconcile their mind entirely now to the rise of that boat among they . at first it make a noise , and scratch the sand , and creak thing come down to it ; and when the moon come through its rib in the evening , tail be the quarter to show to it . but as it go on naturally grow , seldom appear to make much noise , unless there be a man very near it , and even then keep he from do any harm outside the disturbance that he live in without so much as a council call , they tolerate this encroachment . some of the bold father come and sit inside to consider it , and leave their compliment all round to the master of the enterprise . and even when Daniel come to work , as he happen to do this afternoon , they carry on their own work in its high form that of play upon the premise they lend he . 
 though not very large , it be a lively , punctual , well - conduct , and pleasant rabbit - warren . sudden death be avoidable on the part of most of its member , net , ferret , gin , and wire be alike forbidden , fox scarcely ever see , and even gun a rare and very memorable visitation . the headland stave the southern storm , sand - hill shevelle with long rush disarm the western fury , while inland gale from north and east leap into the cloud from the upland . well aware of all their bliss , and feel worthy of it , the blameless citizen pour forth , upon a mild spring evening , to give one another the time of day , to gaze at the labor of man upon the sea , and to take the sweet leisure , the breeze , and the browse . the gray old cony of curule rank , prime senator of the sandy beach , and father of the father - land , hold a just session upon the head borough , and look like brown loaf in the distance . but these be cony of great mark and special character , full of light and leading , because they have be shoot at , and understand how to avoid it henceforth . they be satisfied to chew very little bit of stuff , and particular to have no sand in it , and they hunch their round back almost into one another , and double up their leg to keep they warm , and reflect on their friend ' gray whisker . and one of their true pleasure be , sit snug at their own door , to watch their child 's gambol . 
 for this be the time , with the light upon the slope , and the freshness of salt flow in from the sea , when the spirit of youth must be free of the air , and the quickness of life be abound . without any heed of the care that be come , or the prick - eare fear of the elder , a fine lot of young bunny with tail on the frisk scour everywhere over the warren . up and down the grassy dip and yellow pile of wind - drift , and in and out of the ferny cove and tussock of rush and ragwort , they scamper , and caper , and chase one another , in joy that the winter be banish at last , and the glorious sun come back again . 
 suddenly , as at the wave of a wand , they all stop short and listen . the sun be behind they , low and calm , there be not a breath of wind to stir their flax , not even the feather of a last year 's bloom have move , unless they move it . yet signal of peril have pass among they ; they curve their soft ear for the sound of it , and open their sensitive nostril , and pat upon the ground with one little foot to encourage themselves against the panting of their heart and the traitorous length of their shadow . 
 ha ! not for nothing be their fear this day . an active and dangerous speciman of the human race be come , lightly and gracefully skim the moss , above salt - water reach , of the stepping - stone . the step be say to be a thousand year old , and probably be of half that age , belong to a time when sound work be , and a monastery flourish in the valley . even though they come down from great Hercules himself , never have they be cross by a pretty foot or a fair form than now come gayly over they . but the rabbit make no account of that . to the young man with the adze they be quite accustom , and they like he , because he mind his own business , and care nothing about theirs ; but of this wandering maiden they have no safe knowledge , and judge the bad , and all rush away , some tenscore strong , give notice to he as they pass the boat that he also have well be cautious . 
 Daniel be in a sweet temper now , by virtue of hard labor and gratified wit . by skill and persistence and bodily strength he have compass a curve his father have declare impossible without a dock - yard . three plank be fix , he be sure of the rest , and could well afford to stop , to admire the effect , and feel proud of his work , and of himself the worker . then the panic of the cony make he turn his head , and the quick beat of his heart be quicken by bad than bodily labor . 
 Miss dolly Darling be saunter sweetly , as if there be only one sex in the world , and that an entirely divine one . the gleam of spring sunset be bright in her hair , and in the soft garnish of health on her cheek , and the vigorous play of young life in her eye ; while the silvery glance of the slop shore , and breezy ruffle of the darken sea , do nothing but offer a foil for the form of the shell - color frock and the sky - blue sash . 
 Young Daniel fall back upon his half - shape work , and despise it , and himself , and everything , except what he be afraid to look at . in the hollow among the sand - hill where the cradle of the boat be , fine rush grow , and tuft of ragwort , and stalk of last year 's thistle , and sea - osier where the spring ooze down . through these the white rib of the rise boat shine forth like an elephant 's skeleton ; but the builder entertain some hope , as well as some fear , of be unperceive . 
 but a far great power than his own be here . curved and hollow ship be female in almost all language , not only because of their curve and hollow , but also because they be craft so to speak . 
 " oh , Captain Tugwell , be you at work still ? why , you really ought to have go with the smack . but perhaps you send your son instead . I be so glad to see you ! it be such nice company to hear you ! I do not expect to be leave alone , like this . " 
 " if you please , miss , it be not   father at all . Father be go with the fishing long ago . it be only I , Daniel , if you please , miss . " 
 " no , Daniel , I be not pleased at all . I be quite surprised that you should work so late . it scarcely seem respectable . " 
 at this the young man be so much amazed that he could only stare while she walk off , until the clear duty of right himself in her good opinion strike he . then he throw on his coat and run after she . 
 " if you please , Miss dolly will you please , Miss dolly ? " he call , as she make off for the stepping - stone ; but she do not turn round , though her name be " Miss dolly " all over Springhaven , and she like it . " you be bind to stop , miss , " he say , sternly ; and she stop , and cry , " what do you mean by such word to I ? " 
 " not any sort of harm , miss , " he answer , humbly , inasmuch as she have obey he ; " and I ask your pardon for speak so . but if you think twice you be bind to explain what you say concern I , now just . " 
 " oh , about your work so late , you mean . I offer good advice to you . I think it be wrong that you should go on , when everybody else have leave off long ago . but perhaps your father make you . " 
 " Father be a just man , " say young Tugwell , draw up his own integrity ; " now and then he may take a crooked twist , or such like ; but he never goeth out of fair play to his knowledge . he hath a - be hard upon I this day ; but the main of it be to check mother of her way . you understand , miss , how the woman - folk go on in a house , till the other woman hear of it . and then out - of - door they be the same as lamb . " 
 " it be most ungrateful and traitorous of you to your own mother to talk so . your mother spoil you , and this be all the thank she get ! wait till you have a wife of your own , Master Daniel ! " 
 " wait till I be dead then I may , Miss dolly , " he answer , with a depth of voice which frighten she for a moment ; and then he smile and say , " I beg your pardon , " as gracefully as any gentleman could say it ; " but let I see you safe to your own gate ; there be very rough people about here now , and the time be not quite as they use to be , when we be a - fight daily . " 
 he follow she at a respectful distance , and then run forward and open the white gate . " good - night , Daniel , " the young lady say , as he lift his work cap to she , show his bright curl against the darken sea ; " I be very much oblige to you , and I do hope I have not say anything to vex you . I have never forget all you do for I , and you must not mind the way I have of say thing . " 
 " what a shame it do appear what a fearful shame it be , " she whisper to herself as she hurry through the tree " that he should be nothing but a fisherman ! he be a gentleman in everything but birth and education ; and so strong , and so brave , and so good - looking ! " 




 CHAPTER XI 

 NO PROMOTION 

 " do it again now , Captain Scuddy ; do it again ; you know you must . " 
 " you touch the rim with your shoe , last time . you be bind to do it clean , once more . " 
 " no , he do not . you be a liar ; it be only the ribbon of his shoe . " 
 " I will punch your head if you say that again . it be his heel , and here be the mark . " 
 " oh , Scuddy dear , do not   notice they . you can do it fifty time run , if you like . nobody can run or jump like you . do it just once more to please I . " 
 Kitty Fanshawe , a boy with large blue eye and a purely gentle face , look up at Blyth Scudamore so faithfully that to resist he be impossible . 
 " very well , then ; once more for Kitty , " say the sweetest - temper of mankind , as he vault back into the tub . " but you know that I always leave off at a dozen . thirteen thirteen I could never stop at . I shall have to do fourteen at least ; and it be too bad , just after dinner . now all of you watch whether I touch it anywhere . " 
 a barrel almost five foot in height , and less than a yard in breadth , stand under a clump of tree in the play - ground ; and Blyth Scudamore have make a clean leap one day , for his own satisfaction , out of it . sharp eye see he , and sharp wit be pleased , and a strong demand have arise that he should perform this feat perpetually . good nerve , as well as strong spring , and compactness of power be need for it ; and even in this athletic age there be few who find it easy . 
 " come , now , " he say , as he land lightly , with both heel together ; " one of you big fellow come and do it . you be three inch tall than I be . and you have only get to make up your mind . " 
 but all the big fellow hang back , or begin to stimulate one another , and to prove to each other how easy it be , by every proof but practice . " well , then , I must do it once more , " say Blyth , " for I dare not leave off at thirteen , for fear of some great calamity , such as I never could jump out of . " 
 but before he could get into the tub again , to prepare for the clear spring out of it , he behold a man with silver button come across the playing - field . his heart fall into his heel , and no more agility remain in he . he have make up his mind that Admiral Darling would forget all about he by Saturday ; and though the fair image of dolly would abide in that quiet mind for a long while , the balance of his wish   cast by shyness   be heavily against this visit . and the boy , who understand his nature , with a poignant love like that of our friend in this world begin to probe his tender place . 
 " one more jump , Captain Scuddy ! you must ; to show the flunky what you can do . " 
 " oh , do not   I wish I be go ? he will have turtle soup , and venison , and two man behind his chair . " 
 " and the beautiful young lady look at he every time he take a mouthful . " 
 " but he dare not go court after thirteen jump . and he have vow that he will have another . come , Captain Scuddy , no time to lose . " 
 but Scudamore set off to face his doom , with his old hat hang on the back of his head as it generally do and his ruddy face and mild blue eye full of humorous diffidence and perplexity . 
 " if you please , sir , his honour the Hadmiral have send I to fetch ' e and your thing ; and hoss be bait along of the Blue Dragon . " 
 " I be sorry to say that I forget all about it , or , at least , I think that he would . how long before we ought to start ? " 
 " my name be Gregory , sir Coachman Gregory accustom always to a pair , but do not   mind a single hoss , to oblige the Hadmiral , once in a way . about half an hour , sir , will suit I , unless they come down to the skittle - alley , as ought to be always on a Saturday afternoon ; but not a soul there when I look in . " 
 any man in Scudamore 's position , except himself , would have grieve and groan . for the evening dress of that time , though less gorgeous than of the age before , be still an expensive and elaborate affair ; and the young man , in this ebb of fortune , be poorly stock with raiment . but he pass this trouble with his usual calmness and disregard of trifle . " if I wear the good I have get , " he think , " I can not be charge with disrespect . the Admiral know what a sailor be ; and , after all , who will look at I ? " accordingly he go just as he be , for he never wear an overcoat , but take a little canvas kit , with pump and silk stocking for evening wear , and all the good that he could muster of his Volunteer equipment . 
 the Admiral come to the door of the Hall , and meet he with such hearty warmth , and a glance of such kind approval at his open throat and glowing cheek , that the young man feel a bound of love and tender veneration towards he , which endure for lifetime . 
 " your father be my dear friend , and the very good man I ever know . I must call you ' Blyth , ' " say the Admiral , " for if I call you ' Scudamore , ' I shall think perpetually of my loss . " 
 at dinner that day there be no other guest , and nothing to disturb the present one , except a young lady 's quick glance , of which he endeavor to have no knowledge . Faith Darling , a gentle and beautiful young woman , have take a natural liking to he , because of his trouble , and simplicity , and devotion to his widow mother . but to the young , dolly Darling , he be only a visitor , dull and stupid , require , without at all repaying , the trouble of some attention . he be not tall , nor handsome , nor of strike appearance in any way ; and although he be clearly a gentleman , to her judgment he be not an accomplished , or even a clever one . his inborn modesty and shyness place he at great disadvantage , until well know ; and the simple truth of his nature forbid any of the large talk and bold utterance which please she as yet among young officer . 
 " what a plague he will be all day tomorrow ! " she say to her sister in the drawing - room . " Father be oblige , I suppose , to invite he ; but what can we do with he all the day ? sunday be dull enough , I be sure , already , without our have to amuse a gentleman who have scarcely get two idea of his own , and be afraid to say ' bo ' to a goose , I do believe . do you hear what he say when I ask he whether he be fond of rid ? " 
 " yes ; and I think it so good of he , to answer so straightforwardly . he say that he use to be very fond of it , but be afraid that he should fall off now . " 
 " I should like to see he . I tell you what we will do . we will make he ride back on Monday morning , and put he on ' Blue Bangles , ' who will not   have see daylight since Friday . will not   he jump about a bit ! what a shame it be , not to let we ride on Sundays ! " 
 Ignorant of these kind intention , Scudamore be enjoy himself in his quiet , observant way . Mr. Twemlow , the rector of the parish , have chance as he often chance on a Saturday , after buckle up a brace of sermon to issue his mind   with his body outside it   for a little relief of neighbourhood . and these little airing of his chasten love for he love everybody , when he have do his sermon come , whenever there be a fair chance of it , to a glass of the fine old port which be the true haven for an ancient Admiral . 
 " just in time , Rector , " cry Admiral Darling , who have add by many a hardship to his inborn hospitality . " this be my young friend Blyth Scudamore , the son of one of my old friend . you have hear of Sir Edmond Scudamore ? " 
 " and see he and feel he . and to he I owe , under a merciful providence , the power of drink in this fine port the health of his son , which I do with deep pleasure , for the excellence both of end and mean . " 
 the old man bow at the praise of his wine , and the young one at that of his father . then , after the usual pinch of snuff from the Rector 's long gold box , the host return to the subject he have be full of before this interruption . 
 " the question we have in hand be this . what be to be do with our friend Blyth ? he be get on famously , till this vile peace come . Twemlow , you call it that yourself , so that argument about word be useless . Blyth 's lieutenancy be on the book , and the way they carry thing on now , and shoot poor fellow ' head off , he might have be a post - captain in a twelvemonth . and now there seem nothing on earth before he well than Holy - Orders . " 
 " Admiral Darling be kind enough to think , " say Scudamore , in his mild , hesitative way , blush outwardly , but smile inwardly , " that I be too good to be a clergyman . " 
 " and so you be , and Heaven know it , Blyth , unless there be a chance of get on by goodness , which there be in the Navy , but not in the Church . Twemlow , what be your opinion ? " 
 " it would not be modest in I , " say the Rector , " to stand up too much for my own order . we do our duty , and we do not   get on . " 
 " exactly . you could not have put it well . you get no vacancy by shot and shell , and be fit for another world , you keep out of it . have you ever hear I tell the story about Gunner MacCrab , of the Bellerophon ? " 
 " fifty time , and more than that , " reply the sturdy parson , who like to make a little cut at the Church sometimes , but would not allow any other hand to do it . " but now about our young friend here . surely , with all that we know by this time of the character of that Bony , we can see that this peace be a mere trick of his to bamboozle we while he get ready . in six month we shall be at war again , hammer and tong , as sure as my name be Twemlow . " 
 " so be it ! " cry the Admiral , with a stamp on his oak floor , while Scudamore 's gentle eye flash and fall ; " if it be the will of God , so be it . but if it once begin again , God alone know where France will be before you and I be in our graf . they have drain all our patience , and our pocket very nearly ; but they have scarcely put a tap into our energy and endurance . but what be they ? a gang of slave , ram into the cannon by a Despot . " 
 " they seem to like it , and the question be for they . but the struggle will be desperate , mountain of carnage , ocean of blood , universal mourning , lamentation , and woe . and I have have enough trouble with my tithe already . " 
 " tithe be dependent on the will of the Almighty , " say the Admiral , who pay more than he altogether like ; " but a war go by reason and good management . it encourage the good man of the day , and it bring out the difference between right and wrong , which be quite smother up in peace time . it keep out a quantity of foreign rubbish and stuff only make to be look at , and it make people trust one another , and know what country they belong to , and feel how much they have leave to be thankful for . and what be the use of a noble fleet , unless it can get some fighting ? Blyth , what say you ? you know something about that . " 
 " no , sir , I have never be at close quarter yet . and I doubt or at least I be certain that I should not like it . I be afraid that I should want to run down below . " 
 Mr. Twemlow , have never smell hostile powder , gaze at he rather loftily , while the young man blush at his own truth , yet look up bravely to confirm it . 
 " of all I have ever know or meet , " say Admiral Darling , quietly , " there be but three Nelson and two other , and one of those two be half - witted who could fetch up muzzle to muzzle without a feeling of that sort . the true courage lie in resist the impulse , more than be free from it . I know that I be in a precious fright the first time I be shoot at , even at a decent distance ; and I do not   pretend to like it even now . but I be pretty safe now from any further chance , I fear . when we cut our wisdom - tooth , they shelf we . Twemlow , how much wise you be in the Church ! the old a man get , the high they promote he . " 
 " then let they begin with I , " the Rector answer , smile ; " I be old enough now for almost anything , and the only promotion I get be stiff joint , and tooth that crave peace from an olive . Placitam paci , Mr. Scudamore know the rest , be fresh from the learn Stonnington . but , Squire , you know that I be content . I love Springhaven , Springhaven love I , and we chasten one another . " 
 " a man who know all the Latin you know , Rector for I own that you beat I to the spelling - book should be at least an Archdeacon in the Church , which be equal to the rank of Rear - Admiral . but you never have push as you should do ; and you let it all off in quotation . those be very comforting to the mind , but I never know a man do good with they , unless they come out of the Bible . when Gunner Matthew of the Erigdoupos be wait to have his leg off , with no prospect before he except a well world you know what our Chaplain say to he ; and the effect upon his mind be such , that I have get he to this day upon my land . " 
 " of course you have the big old poacher in the county . he shoot half your pheasant with his wooden leg by moonlight . what your Chaplain say to he be entirely profane in the turn of a text of Holy - Writ ; and it show how our cloth be spoil by contact with your " for the Admiral be laugh to himself at this old tale , which he would not produce before young Scudamore , but love to have out with the Rector " and I hope it will be a good warning to you , Squire , to settle no more old gunner on your property . you must understand , Mr. Scudamore , that the Admiral make a sort of Naval Hospital , for all his old salt , on his own Estates . " 
 " I be sure it be wonderfully kind in he , " the young man answer , bravely , " for the poor old fellow be throw to the dog by the country , when it have disable they . I have not see much of the service , but quite enough to know that , Mr. Twemlow . " 
 " I have see a great deal , and I say that it be so . and my good friend know it as well as I do , and be one of the first to lend a help hand . in all such case he do more than I do , whenever they come within his knowledge . but let we return to the matter in hand . here be a young man , a first - rate sailor , who would have be under my guardianship , I know , but for but for sad circumstance . be he to be grind at Virgil and Ovid till all his spirit go out of he , because we have patch up a very shabby peace ? it can never last long . every Englishman hat it , although it may seem to save his pocket . twemlow , I be no politician . you read the paper more than I do . how much long will this wretched compact hold ? you have predict the course of thing before . " 
 " and so I will again , " reply the Rector . " Atheism , mockery , cynicism , blasphemy , lust , and blood - thirstyness can not rage and raven within a few league of a godly and just nation without stink in their nostril . Sir , it be our mission from the Lord to quench Bony , and to conquer the bully of Europe . we do not   look like do it now , I confess . but do it we shall , in the end , as sure as the name of our country be England . " 
 " I have no doubt of it , " say the Admiral , simply ; " but there will be a deal of fight betwixt this and then . Blyth , will you leave I to see what I can do , whenever we get to work again ? " 
 " I should think that I would , sir , and never forget it . I be not fond of fighting ; but how I have long to feel myself afloat again ! " 




 CHAPTER XII 

 AT the YEW - tree 

 all the common - sense of England , more abundant in those day than now , feel that the war have not be fight out , and the way to the lap of peace could only be win by vigorous use of the arm . some few there be even then , as now there be a cackle multitude , besotte enough to believe that fact can be undo by blink they . but our forefather on the whole be wise , and know that nothing be trample more basely than right that will not right itself . 
 therefore they set their face hard , and toughen their heart like knot oak , against all that man could do to they . there be no magnificent proclamation , no big vaunt of victory at the buckling on of armour , but the quiet strength of steadfast will , and the stern resolve to strike when stricken , and try to last the long . and so their mother - land become the mother of man and freedom . 
 in November , 1802 , the speech from the throne apprise the world that England be prepare . the wide , long , and deadly war , since the date of gunpowder , be lower ; and the heart of all who love their kin be heavy , but find no help for it . 
 the sermon which Mr. Twemlow preach in Springhaven church be magnificent . some parishioner , keep memory more alert than conscience , declare that they have receive it all nine , or it might be ten , year since , when the fighting first be call for . if so , that prove it none the bad , but themselves , for again require it . their Rector tell they that they think too much of their own flesh - pot and fish - kettle , and their country might go to the bottom of the sea , if it leave they their own fishing - ground . and he say that they would wake up some day and find themselves turn into Frenchmen , for all thing be possible with the Lord ; and then they might smite their breast , but must confess that they have deserve it . neither would year of prayer and fast fetch they back into decent Englishmen ; the abomination of desolation would be set up over their doorway , and the scarlet woman of Babylon would revel in their sanctuary . 
 " now do not   let none of we be in no hurry , " Captain Tugwell say , after dwelling and sleep upon this form of doctrine ; " a man knoweth his own trade the good , the very same way as the parson doth . and I never know no good to come of any hurry . our life be give we by the Lord . and he never would ' a make them threescore and ten , or for man of any strength fourscore , if his will have be to jerk we over they . never do I see no Frenchman as could be turn to an Englishman , not if he be to fast and pray all day , and cut himself with knife at the go down of the sun . my opinion be that Parson Twemlow be touch up by his own conscience for have a nephew more french than English ; and ' Caryl Carne ' be the name thereof , with more french than English sound to it . " 
 " why , he have be go for year and year , " say the landlord of the Darling Arms , where the village be hold council ; " he have never be see in these part since the death of the last Squire Carne , to my knowledge . " 
 " and what do the old Squire die of , John Prater ? not that he be to be call old young , I dare say , than I be now . what do he die of , but marry with a long outlandish ' ooman ? a femmel as could not   speak a word of English , to be anyhow sure of her meaning ! ah , they be bad time at Carne Castle ; and as nice a place as need be then , until they dip the property . six grey horse they be use to go with to London Parliament every year , before the last squire come of age , as I have heere my father say score of time , and no lie ever come from his mouth , no more than it could from mine , almost . then they drop to four , and then to two , and pretend that the road be easy . " 
 " when I be down the coast , last week , so far as Littlehampton , " say a stout young man in the corner , " a very coorous thing happen I , leastway by my own opinion , and glad shall I be to have the judgment of Cappen Zeb consarne it . there come in there a queer - rig craft of some sixty ton from Halvers , desire to set up trade again , or to do some smoogling , or spy perhaps . her name be the Doctor Humm , which seem a great favorite with they Crappos , and her skipper have a queer name too , as if he be two man in one , for he call himself ' jack ' ; a fellow about forty year old , as I haul out of the sea with a boat - hook one night on the varner . well , he seem to think a good deal of that , though contrary to their nature , and nothing would do but I must go to be fat with he everywhere , if the folk would change his money . he have pick up a decent bit of talk from ship in the oyster line before the war ; and I put his lingo into order for he , for which he be very thankful . " 
 " and so he be bind to be . but you have no call to do it , Charley Bowles . " Captain Tugwell speak severely , and the young man feel that he be wrong , for the elder shake their head at he , as a traitor to the English language . 
 " well , main likely , I go amiss . but he seem to take it so uncommon kind of I hitch he with a boat - hook , that we get on together wonderful , and he call I ' Friar Sharley , ' and he try to take up with our manner and custom ; but his head be outlandish for English grog . one night he be three sheet in the wind , at a snug little crib by the river , and he take to the brag as be bear with they . ' all dis contray in one year now , ' say he , nod over his glass at I , ' shall be of the grand nashong , and I will make a great man of you , Friar Sharley . do you know what prawn be , my good friend ? ' well , I say I have catch a good many in my time ; but he laugh and say , ' prawn will catch you this time . one tousand prawn , all with two hondre man inside he , and the leetle prawn will come to land at your house , Sharley . bootiful place , quiet sea , no bad rock . you look out in the morning , and the white coast be make black with they . ' now what do you say to that , Cappen Tugwell ? " 
 " I have   a - heere that style of talk many time afore , " Master Tugwell answer , solidly ; " and all I can say be that I should have punch his head . and you deserve the same thing , Charley Bowles , unless you have   get more than that to tell we . " 
 " so I might , Cappen , and I will not   deny you there . but the discourse be consarne Squire Carne now just , and the trouble he fall into , before I be come to my judgment yet . why , an uncle of mine serve footman there Jeremiah Bowles , know to every one , until he be no more hear of . " 
 nod of assent to the fame of Jeremiah encourage the stout young man in his tale , and a wedge of tobacco rekindle he . 
 " yes , it be a coorous thing indeed , and coorous for I to hear of it , out of all mast - head of Springhaven . say Moosoo Jacks to I , that night when I bouse he up unpretende : ' you keep your feather eye open , my tear , ' for such be his way of pronounce it , ' and you shall arrive to laglore , laglore and what be still nobler , de monnay . in one two tree month , you shall see a young captain return to his contray dominion , and then you will go to his side and say jack , and he will make present to you a sack of silver . ' well , I hail the chance of this pretty smart , you may suppose , and I ask he what the sailor 's name would be , and surprise I be when he answer Carne , or Carny , for he give it in two syllable . next morning 's tide , the Doctor Humm clear out , and I have no other chance of discourse with Moosoo Jacks . but I want to know what you think , Cappen Zeb . " 
 " so you shall , " say the captain of Springhaven , sternly . " I think you have well call your Moosoo Jacks ' Master Jackass , ' or ' Master Jackanapes , ' and put your own name on the back of he . you be with a Frenchman hob and nobbing , and you do not   even know how they pronounce themselves , unchristian as it be to do so . ' Jarks ' be his name , the very same as Navy beef , and a common one in that country . but to speak of any Carne come nigh we with french plotting , and of prawn land here at Springhaven ' tis as likely as I should drop french money into the till of this baccy - box . and you can see that I be not go to play such a trick as that , John Prater . " 
 " why to my mind there never be big stuff talk , " the landlord speak out , without fear of offence , for there be no other sign - board within three mile , " than to carry on in that way , Charley . what they may do at Littlehampton be beyond my knowledge , never have keep a snug crib there , as you be pleased to call it . but at Springhaven ' twould be the wrong place for hatch of french treachery . we all know one another a deal too well for that , I hope . " 
 " Prater , you be right , " exclaim Mr. Cheeseman , owner of the main shop in the village , and universally respect . " Bowles , you must have an imagination the same as your uncle Jerry have . and to speak of the Carnes in a light way of talk , after all their misfortune , be terrible . why , I pass the old castle one night last week , with the moon to one side of it , and only I in my one - horse shay to the other , and none but a man with a first - rate conscience would have have the stomach to do so . however , I seed no ghost that time , though I do hear some noise as make I use the whip ; and the swing of the ivy be black as a hearse . a little drop more of my own rum , John : it give I quite a chill to think of it . " 
 " I do not   take much account of what people say , " Harry Shanks , who have a deep clear voice , observe , " without it be in my own family . but my own cousin Bob be come home one night from a bit of sweethearte at Pebbleridge , when , to save the risk of rabbit - hole in the dark , for he put out his knee - cap one time , what do he do but take the path inland through the wood below Carne Castle the opposite side to where you be , Master Cheeseman , and the same side as the moon would be , only she be not   up that night . well , he have some misgiving , as anybody must ; still he push along , whistle and swing his stick , and say to himself that there be no such thing as cowardice in our family ; till just at the corner where the big yew - tree be , that we sometimes starboard helm by when the tide be make with a nor'w wind ; there Bob seed a sight as make his hair crawl . but I will not   say another word about it now , and have to go home in the dark by myself arter'ards . " 
 " come , now , Harry ! " " oh , we can not   stand that ! " " we will see you to your door , lad , if you out with it , fair and forcible . " 
 of these and other exhortation Harry take no notice , but fold his arm across his breast , and gaze at something which his mind present . 
 " Harry Shanks , you will have the manner " Captain Tugwell speak impressively , not for his own sake , for he know the tale , and have be consult about it , but from sense of public dignity " to finish the story which you begin . to begin a yarn of your own accord , and then drop it all of a heap , be not respectful to present company . Springhaven never do allow such trick , and will not put up with they from any young fellow . if your meaning be to drop it , you should never have begin . " 
 Glasses and even pipe rang sharply upon the old oak table in applause of this british sentiment , and the young man , with a sheepish look , submit to the voice of the public . 
 " well , then , all of you know where the big yew - tree stand , at the break of the hill about half a mile inland , and how black it loom among the other stuff . but Bob , with his sweetheart in his head , no doubt , be that full of courage that he forget all about the old tree , and the murder do inside it a hundred and twenty year ago , they say , until there it be , over his head a'most , with the gap in it star like rib at he . ' bout ship be the word , pretty sharp , you may be sure , when he come to his wit consarne it , and the purse of his lip , as be whistle a jig , go as dry as a bag with the bottom out . through the grey of the night there be sound come to he , such as have no right to be in the air , and a sort of a shiver lay hold of his heart , like a cold hand fling over his shoulder . as hard as he could lay foot to the ground , away he go down hill , forget of his kneecap , for such be the condition of his mind and body . 
 " you must understand , mate , that he have not   see nothing to skeer he , but only hear sound , which come into his ear to make his hair rise ; and his mind might have put into they more than there be , for the want of intarprete . perhaps this come across he , as soon as he feel at a well distance with his wind short ; anyhow , he bring up again ' a piece of rock - stuff in a hollow of the ground , and begin to look skeerily backward . for a bit of a while there be nothing to distemper he , only the dark of the hill and the tree , and the grey light a - come from the sea in front . but just as he be begin for to call himself a fool , and to pick himself onto his leg for trudge home , he seed a thing as skeere he bad than ever , and fetch he flat upon his low end . 
 " from the black of the yew - tree there burst a big light , bright than a lighthouse or a blue thunder - bolt , and fly with a long streak down the hollow , just as if all the world be a - blazing . three time it come , with three different colour , first blue , and then white , and then red as new blood ; and poor Bob be in a condition of mind must be see before say more of it . if he have be bring up to follow the sea , instead of the shoemaking , maybe his wit would have be more about he , and the narve of his symptom more ship - shape . but it never be bear into his mind whatever , to keep a lookout upon the offing , nor even to lie snug in the fern and watch the yew - tree . all he be up for be to make all sail , the moment his stick would carry it ; and he fear to go nigh his sweetheart any more , till she take up with another fellow . " 
 " and sarve he quite right , " be the judgment of the room , in high fettle with hot rum and water ; " to be skeere of his life by a smuggler 's signal ! eh , Cappen Zebedee , you know that be it ? " 
 but the captain of Springhaven shake his head . 




 CHAPTER XIII 

 WHENCE , and WHEREFORE ? 

 at the rectory , too , ere the end of that week , there be no little shake of head almost as wise as Zebedee Tugwell 's . Mrs. Twemlow , though nearly sixty year of age , and acquaint with many a sorrow , be as lively and busy and notable as ever , and even more determined to be the mistress of the house . for by this time her daughter Eliza , begin to be twenty - five year old a job which take some year in finishing begin at the same time to approve her birth by a vigorous aim at the mastery . for , as everybody say , Miss Eliza be a Carne in blood and breed and fibre . there be little of the Twemlow stock about she for the Twemlows be mild and humorous but plenty of the strength and dash and wildness and contemptuous spirit of the ancient carne . 
 carne a carne , as Mr. Twemlow say , when his wife be inclined to be masterful a derivation confirm by the family motto , " Carne non caret carne . " in the case , however , of Mrs. Twemlow , age , affliction , experience , affection , and perhaps above all her good husband 's large benevolence and placidity , have work a great change for the well , and make a nice old lady of she . she be tall and straight and slender still ; and know how to make the most , by grave attire and graceful attitude , of the bodily excellence entail for age on the lineage of Carne . of moral goodness there have not be an equally strict settlement , at least in male heredity . so that Mrs. Twemlow 's thought about she kith and kindre be rather sad than proud , unless some ignorance be show about they . 
 " poor as I be , " say Mr. Twemlow , now consult with she , " and poor as every benefice clergyman must be , if this war return , I would rather have lose a hundred pound than have hear what you tell I , Maria . " 
 " my dear , I can not quite see that , " his wife make thoughtful answer ; " if he only have money to keep up the place , and clear off those nasty incumbrance , I should rejoice at his come back to live where we have be for century . " 
 " my dear , you be too poetical , though the feeling be a fine one . within the old wall there can scarcely be a room that have a sound floor to it . and as for the roof , when that thunder - storm be , and I take shelter with my pony well , you know the state I come home in , and all my good clothe on for the Visitation . luckily there seem to be no rheumatism in your family , Maria ; and perhaps he be too young as yet to pay out for it till he get old . but if he come for business , and to see to the relic of his property , surely he might have a bedroom here , and come and go at his liking . after all his foreign fanglement , a course of quiet english life and the tone of english principle might be of the great use to he . he would never wish to see the continent again . " 
 " it be not to be think of , " say Mrs. Twemlow . " I would not have he to live in this house for fifty thousand pound a year . you be a great deal wise than I be , Joshua ; but of his nature you know nothing , whereas I know it from his childhood . and Eliza be so strong - willed and stubborn you dislike , of course , to hear I say it , but it be the fact it be , my dear . and I would rather stand by our daughter 's grave than see her fall in love with Caryl Carne . you know what a handsome young man he must be now , and full of french style and frippery . I be sure it be most kind of you to desire to help my poor family ; but you would rue the day , my dear , that bring he beneath our quiet roof . I have lose my only son , as it seem , by the will of the Lord , who afflict we . but I will not lose my only daughter , by any such folly of my own . " 
 tear roll down Mrs. Twemlow 's cheek as she speak of her mysterious affliction ; and her husband , who know that she be not weak - minded , console she by share her sorrow . 
 " it shall be exactly as you like , " he say , after a quiet interval . " you say that no answer be need ; and there be no address to send one to . we shall hear of it , of course , when he take possession , if , indeed , he be allow to do so . " 
 " who be to prevent he from come , if he choose , to live in the home of his ancestor ? the estate be all mortgage , and the park be go , turn into a pound for Scotch cattle - breeding . but the poor old castle belong to we still , because no one would take the expense of it . " 
 " and because of the story concern it , Maria . your nephew Caryl be a brave young fellow if he mean to live there all alone , and I fear he can afford himself no company . you understand he so much well : what do you suppose his motive be ? " 
 " I make no pretence to understand he , dear , any more than his poor father could . my dear brother be of headstrong order , and it do he no good to contradict he , and indeed it be dangerous to do so ; but his nature be as simple as a child 's almost , to any one accustom to he . if he have not marry that grand french lady , who revel in every extravagance , though she know how we all be impoverished , he might have be live and in high position now , though a good many year my senior . and the bad of it be that he do it at a time when he ought to have know so much well . however , he pay for it bitterly enough , and his only child be set against he . " 
 " a very sad case altogether , " say the rector . " I remember , as if it be yesterday , how angry poor Montagu be with I . you remember what word he use , and his threat of attack I with his horsewhip . but he beg my pardon , most humbly , as soon as he see how thoroughly right I be . you be like he in some thing , as I often notice , but not quite so generous in confess you be wrong . " 
 " because I do not   do it as he do , Joshua . you would never understand I if I do . but of course for a man you can make allowance . my rule be to do it both for man and woman , quite as fairly as if one be the other . " 
 " certainly , Maria certainly . and therefore you can do it , and have always do it , even for poor Josephine . no doubt there be much to be plead , by a candid and gentle mind , on her behalf . " 
 " what ! that dreadful creature who ruin my poor brother , and call herself the Countess de Lune , or some such nonsense ! no , Joshua , no ! I have not so entirely lose all english principle as to quite do that . instead of be largeness , that would be mere looseness . " 
 " there be many thing , however , that we never understand , and perhaps never shall in this world , " Mr. Twemlow continue , as if talk to himself , for reason on that subject would be misaddress to she ; " and nothing be more natural than that young Caryl should side with his mother , who so pet he , against his poor father , who be violent and harsh , especially when he have to pay such bill . but perhaps our good nephew have amass some cash , though there seem to be but little on the continent , after all this devastation . be there anything , Maria , in his letter to enable we to hope that he be come home with money ? " 
 " not a word , I be afraid , " Mrs. Twemlow answer , sadly . " but take it , my dear , and read it to I slowly . you make thing so plain , because of practice every Sunday . oh , Joshua , I never can be sure which you be great in the Lessons or the Sermon . but before you begin I will shoot the bolt a little , as if it have catch by accident . eliza do rush in upon we sometimes in the most unbecoming , unladylike way . and I never can get you to reprove she . " 
 " it would be as much as my place be worth , as the maid say when imagine to have steal sugar . and I must not read this letter so loud as the Lessons , unless you wish Lizzie to hear every word , for she have all her mother 's quick sens . there be not much of it , and the scrawl seem hasty . we might have have more for three and fourpence . but I be not the one to grumble about bad measure as the boy say about old Busby . now , Maria , listen , but say nothing ; if feminine capacity may compass it . why , bless my heart , every word of it be french ! " the rector throw down his spectacle , and gaze at his wife reproachfully . but she smile with superior innocence . 
 " what else could you expect , after all his year abroad ? I can not make out the whole of it , for certain . but surely it be not beyond the compass of masculine capacity . " 
 " yes , it be , Maria ; and you know it well enough . no honest Englishman can endure a word of French . Latin , or Greek , or even Hebrew though I take to that rather late in life . but French be only fit for woman , and very few of they can manage it . let we hear what this Frenchman say . " 
 " he be not a Frenchman , Joshua . he be an Englishman , and probably a very fine one . I will not   be sure about all of his letter , because it be so long since I be at school ; and french book be generally unfit to read . but the general meaning be something like this : 
 ' MY BELOVED and HIGHLY valued aunt , since I hear from you there be many year now , but I hope you have hold I in memory . I have the intention of return to the country of England , even in this bad time of winter , when the climate be most funereal . I shall do my good to call back , if possible , the scatter ruin of the property , and to institute again the name which my father make displeasing . in this good work you will , I have faith , afford I your good assistance , and the influence of your high connection in the neighbourhood . accept , dear aunt , the assurance of my high consideration , of the most sincere and the most devoted , and allow I the honour of write myself your most loving and respectful nephew , 
 ' CARYL CARNE . ' 
 now , Joshua , what do you think of that ? " 
 " fine word and no substance ; like all french stuff . and he never even mention I , who give he a top , when he should have have the whip . I will not pretend to understand he , for he always be beyond I . dark and excitable , moody and capricious , haughty and sarcastic , and devoid of love for animal . you remember his pony , and what he do to it , and the little dog that crawl upon her stomach towards he . for your sake I would have put up with he , my dear , and striven to improve his nature , which be sure to be much bad at six - and - twenty , after so many year abroad . but I confess it be a great relief to I that you wisely prefer not to have he in this house , any more at least than we can help it . but who come here ? what a hurry we be in ! Lizzie , my darling , be patient . " 
 " here be this plague of a door bar and bolt again ! be I not to have an atom of breakfast , because I just happen to oversleep myself ? the morning get dark and dark ; it be almost impossible to see to dress oneself . " 
 " there be plenty of tinder in the house , Eliza , and plenty of good tallow candle , " Mrs. Twemlow reply , have put away the letter , while her husband let the complainant in . " for the third time this week we have have prayer without you , and the example be shocking for the servant . we shall have to establish the rule you suggest too late to pray for food , too late to get it . but I have keep your help of bacon hot , quite hot , by the fire . and the teapot be under the cozy . " 
 " thank you , dear mother , " the young lady answer , careless of word , if deed be in her favour , and too clever to argue the question . " I suppose there be no kind of news this morning to reward one for get up so early . " 
 " nothing whatever for you , Miss Lizzie , " say her father , as soon as he have kiss she . " but the paper be full of the prospect of war , and the extent of the preparation . if we be drive to fight again , we shall do it in earnest , and not spare ourselves . " 
 " nor our enemy either , I do hope with all my heart . how long be we to be afraid of they ? we have always invade the French till now . and for they to talk of invade we ! there be not a bit of spirit leave in this island , except in the heart of Lord Nelson . " 
 " what a hot little patriot this child be ! " say the father , with a quiet smile at she . " what would she say to an Englishman , who be more french than English , and would only write french letter ? and yet it might be possible to find such people . " 
 " if such a wretch exist , " cry Miss Twemlow , " I should like to crunch he as I crunch this toast . for a Frenchman I can make all fair allowance , because he can not help his birth . but for an Englishman to turn Frenchman " 
 " however reluctant we may be to allow it , " the candid rector argue , " they be the foremost nation in the world , just now , for energy , valour , decision , discipline , and I fear I must add patriotism . the most wonderful man who have appear in the world for century be their leader , and by land his success have be almost unbroken . if we must have war again , as I fear we must , and very speedily , our chief hope must be that the Lord will support his cause against the scoffer and the infidel , the libertine and the assassin . " 
 " you see how beautifully your father put it , Eliza ; but he never abuse people . that be a habit in which , I be sorry to say , you indulge too freely . you show no good feeling to anybody who differ from you in opinion , and you talk as if Frenchmen have no religion , no principle , and no humanity . and what do you know about they , pray ? have you ever speak to a Frenchman ? have you ever even see one ? would you know one if you even set eye upon he ? " 
 " well , I be not at all sure that I should , " the young lady reply , be thoroughly truthful ; " and I have no wish for the opportunity . but I have see a french woman , mother ; and that be quite enough for I . if they be so , what must the man be ? " 
 " there be a name for this process of feminine reasoning , this cumulative and syncopetic process of the mind , entirely feminine   but regard by itself as rational , a name which I use to know well in the day when I have the ten Fallacies at my finger ' end , more tenaciously perhaps than the Decalogue . strange to say , the name be go from my memory ; but but " 
 " but then you have well go after it , my dear , " his wife suggest with authority . " if your only impulse when you hear reason be to search after hard name for it , you be safe outside of its sphere altogether . " 
 " I be strike with the truth of that remark , " observe the rector ; " and the more so because I descry a male member of our race approach , with a hat at once the emblem and the crown of sound reason . away with all fallacy ; it be church - warden Cheeseman ! " 




 CHAPTER XIV 

 a horrible suggestion 

 " can you guess what have bring I down here in this hurry ? " Lord Nelson ask Admiral Darling , have jump like a boy from his yellow post - chaise , and shake his old friend 's broad right hand with his slender but strenuous leave one , even as a big bell be swing by a thin rope . " I have no time to spare not a day , not an hour ; but I make up my mind to see you before I start . I can not expect to come home alive , and , except for one reason , I should not wish it . " 
 " nonsense ! " say the Admiral , who be saunter near his upper gate , and enjoy the world this fine spring morning ; " you be always in such a confounded hurry ! when you come to my time of life , you will know well . what be it this time ? the Channel fleet again ? " 
 " no , no ; Billy Blue keep that , thank God ! I hate look after a school of herring - boat . the Mediterranean for I , my friend . I receive the order yesterday , and shall be at sea by the twentieth . " 
 " I be very glad to hear it , for your sake . if ever there be a restless fellow in the good old time we be not like that . come up to the house and talk about it ; at least they must take the horse out . they be not like you ; they can not   work forever . " 
 " and they do not   get knock about like I ; though one of they have lose his starboard eye , and he sail and steer all the well for it . let they go up to the stable , Darling , while you come down to the beach with I . I want to show you something . " 
 " what crotchet be in his too active brain now ? " the eld and strong man ask himself , as he find himself hook by the right arm , and lead down a track through the tree scarcely know to himself , and quite out of sight from the village . " why , this be not the way to the beach ! however , it be never any good to oppose he . he get his own way so because of his fame . or perhaps that be the way he get his fame . but to show I about over my own land ! but let he go on , let he go on . " 
 " you be wonder , I dare say , what I be about , " cry Nelson , stop suddenly , and fix his sound eye which be wonderfully keen , though he be always in a fright about it upon the large and peaceful blinker of his ancient commander ; " but now I shall be able to convince you , though I be not a land - surveyor , nor even a general of land - force . if God Almighty prolong my life which be not very likely it will be that I may meet that scoundrel , Napoleon Bonaparte , on dry land . I hear that he be eager to encounter I on the wave , himself command a line - of - battle ship . I should send he to the devil in a quarter of an hour . and ashore I could astonish he , I think , a little , if I have a good army to back I up . remember what I do at Bastia , in the land that produce this monster , and where I be call the Brigadier ; and again , upon the coast of Italy , I show that I understand all their dry - ground business . tush ! I can beat he , ashore and afloat ; and I shall , if I live long enough . but this time the villain be in earnest , I believe , with his trumpery invasion ; and as soon as he hear that I be go , he will make sure of have his own way . we know , of course , there be fifty man as good as myself to stop he , include you , my dear darling ; but everything go by reputation the noise of the people praise - puff . that be all I get ; while the lucky fellow , like Cathcart , get the prize - money . but I do not   want to grumble . now what do you see ? " 
 " well , I see you , for one thing , " the Admiral answer , at his leisure , be quite inured to his friend 's quick fire , " and wear a coat that would be a disgrace to any other man in the navy . and far on I see some land that I never shall get my rent for ; and beyond that nothing but the sea , with a few fishing - craft inshore , and in the offing a sail , an outward - bind East Indiaman some fool who would not   wait for convoy , with war as good as proclaim again . " 
 " nothing but the sea , indeed ? the sweep of the land , and the shelter of the bay , the shoaling of the shore without a rock to break it , the headland that shut out both wind and wave ; and outside the headland , off Pebbleridge , deep water for a fleet of line - of - battle ship to anchor and command the land approach moreover , a stream of the pure water from deep and never - fail spring Darling , the place of all place in England for the French to land be opposite to your front door . " 
 " I be truly oblige to you for predict , and to they for do it , if ever they attempt such impudence . if they find out that you be away , they can also find out that I be here , as commander of the sea defence , from Dungeness to Selsey - Bill . " 
 " that will make it all the more delightful to land at your front door , my friend ; and all the easy to do it . my own plan be to strike with all force at the head - quarter of the enemy , because the most likely to be unprepared . about a year ago , when I be down here , a little before my dear father 's death , without your commission I take command of your fishing - craft come home for their Sunday , and show they how to take the beach , partly to confirm my own suspicion . there be no other landing on all the south coast , this side of Hayling Island , fit to be compare with it for the use of flat - bottom craft , such as most of Boney 's be . and remember the set of the tide , which make the fortune of your fisherman . to be sure , he know nothing of that himself ; but he have sharp rogue about he . if they once make good their landing here , it would be difficult to dislodge they . it must all be do from the land side then , for even a 42 - gun frigate could scarcely come near enough to pepper they . they love shoal water , the skulk and that have enable they to baffle I so often . not that they would conquer the country all brag but still it would be a nasty predicament , and scare the poor cockney like the very devil . " 
 " but remember the distance from Boulogne , Hurry . if they can not cross twenty - five mile of channel in the tooth of our ship , what chance would they have when the distance be near eighty ? " 
 " a much well chance , if they know how to do it . all our cruiser would be to the eastward . one afternoon perhaps , when a haze be on , they make a feint with light craft toward the Scheldt every british ship crowd sail after they . then , at dusk , the main body of the expedition slip with the first of the ebb to the westward ; they meet the flood tide in mid - channel , and use their long sweep be in Springhaven , or at any rate the light of they , by the top of that tide , just when you be shave . you laugh at such a thought of mine . I tell you , my dear friend , that with skill and good luck it be easy ; and do it they should , if they be under my command . " 
 if anybody else have even talk of such a plan as within the bound of likelihood , Admiral Darling would have be almost enraged . but now he look doubtfully , first at the sea   as if it might be thick with prame already , and then at the land which be his own as if the rent might go into a Frenchman 's pocket , and then at his old and admired friend , who have ruin his sleep for the summer . 
 " happily they be not under your command , and they have no man to compare with you ; " he speak rather nervously ; while Nelson smile , for he love the praise which he have so well earn ; " and if it be possible for you to talk nonsense , I should say that you have do it now . but two thing surely you have overlook . in the first place , the French can have no idea of the special opportunity this place afford . and again , if they have , they could do nothing , without a pilot well acquainted with the spot . though the landing be so easy , there be shoal outside , very intricate and dangerous , and know to none except the native of the place , who be jealous to the last degree about their knowledge . " 
 " that be true enough ; and even I should want a pilot here , though I know every spit of sand eastward . but away fly both your difficulty if there should happen to be a local traitor . " 
 " a traitor at Springhaven ! such a thing be quite impossible . you would laugh at yourself , if you only know the character of our people . there never have be , and there never will be , a Springhaven man capable of treachery . " 
 " that be good news , ay , and strange news too , " the visitor answer , with his left hand on his sword , for he be now in full though rather shabby uniform . " there be not many traitor in England , I believe ; but they be as likely to be find in one place as another , accord to my experience . well , well , I be very glad you have no such scoundrel here . I will not   say a single word against your people , who be as fine a lot as any in the south of England , and as obstinate as any I could wish to see . of an obstinate man I can always make good ; with a limp one I can do nothing . but bear in mind every word you have hear I say , because I come down on purpose about it ; and I generally penetrate the device of the enemy , though they lead I on a wild - goose - chase sometimes , but only when our own folk back they up , either by lie or stupidity . now look once more , for you be slow as well as a great deal wise than I be . you see how this land - lock bight of Springhaven seem make by the Almighty for flat - bottom craft , if once they can find their way into it ; while the trend of the coast towards Pebbleridge be equally suit for the cover fleet , unless a gale from southwest come on , in which case they must run for it . and you see that the land force , by crown the hill above your house and across the valley , might defy our noble volunteer , and all that could be bring against they , till a hundred thousand cutthroat be establish here . and Boney would make his head - quarter at the Hall , with a french cook in your kitchen , and a german butler in your cellar , and my pretty godchild to wait upon he , for the rogue love pretty maiden . " 
 " that will do . that be quite enough . no wonder you have write poem , Nelson , as you tell we the last time you be here . if my son have only get your imagination but perhaps you know something more than you have tell I . perhaps you have be tell " 
 " never mind about that , " the great sea - captain answer , turn away as if on spring ; " it be high time for I to be off again , and my chaise have spring on her cable . " 
 " not she . I have order she to be dock . Dine with we you shall this day , if we have to dine two hour early , and though Mother Cloam rage furiously . how much long do you suppose you can carry on at this pace ? look at I . I have double your bodily substance ; but if I go on as you do you remember the twenty - four - pounder old hotcopper put into the launch , and fire it , in spite of all I could say to he ? well , you be just the same . you have not get the scantling for the metal you carry and be always work . you will either blow up , or else scuttle yourself . look here , how your seam be open !
