" here Admiral Darling thrust his thumb through the ravel seam of his old friend 's coat , which make he jump back , for he love his old coat . " yes , and you will go in the very same way . I wonder how any coat last so much as a month , with you inside it . " 
 " this coat , " say Nelson , who be most sweet - temper with any one he love , though hot as pepper when stir up by stranger " this coat be the one I wear at Copenhagen , and a sounder and kind coat never come on a man 's back . Charles Darling , you have make a bad hit this time . if I be no more worn out than this coat be , I be fit to go to sea for a number of year yet . and I hope to show it to a good many frenchman , and take as many ship , every time they show fight , as there be button on it . " 
 " then you will double all your capture at the Nile ; " such a series of button have this coat , though mostly loose upon their mooring , for his guardian angel be not " domestic " ; " but you may be trust not to let they drift so . you have give I a lesson in coast - defence , and now you shall be board by the lady . you possess some gift of the tongue , my friend , as well as great gift of hand and eye ; but I will back my daughter to beat you there . come up to the house . no turning of tail . " 
 " I speak very well in the House of Lords , " say Nelson , in his simple way , " in reply to the speech of his Majesty , and again about the Commissioner 's bill ; or at least everybody tell I so . but in the House of Ladies I hold my tongue , because there be abundance without it . " 
 this , however , he fail to do when the matter come to the issue ; for his godchild Horatia , more commonly call dolly , happen to be in the mood for take outrageous liberty with he . she possess very little of that gift most precious among woman the sense of veneration ; and to she a hero be only a man heroic in act of utility . " he shall do it , " she say to Faith , when she hear that he be come again ; " if I have to kiss he , he shall do it ; and I do not   like kiss those old man . " 
 " hush ! " say her eld sister . " dolly , you do say thing so recklessly . one would think that you like to kiss young man ! but I be sure that be not your meaning . I would rather kiss Lord Nelson than all the young man in the kingdom . " 
 " well do , Faith ! all the young man in the kingdom ! how recklessly you do say thing ! and you can not   kiss he he be MY godfather . but just see how I get round he , if you have wit enough to understand it . " 
 so these two join in their kind endeavour to make the visitor useful , the object be so good that doubtful mean might be excuse for it . in different way and for diver reason , each of these young lady now have take to like Blyth Scudamore . faith , by power of pity first , and of grief for her own misfortune , and of admiration for his goodness to his widow mother which make his good breech shine hard at the knee ; and dolly , because of his shy adoration , and dauntless defence of she against a cow   whose calf be on the road to terminate in veal , as well as his special skill with his pocket - knife in cut out figure that could dance , and almost sing ; also his great gift , when the tide be out , of make rare creature run after he . what avail to explore female reason precisely ? their mind be make up that he must be a captain , if Nelson have to build the ship with his one hand for he . 
 " after that , there be nothing more to be say , " confess the vanquished warrior ; " but the daughter of an Admiral should know that no man can be post until he have serve his time as lieutenant ; and this young hero of yours have never even hold the King 's commission yet . but as he have see some service , and be beyond the age of a middy , in the present rush he might get appoint as junior lieutenant , if he have any stout seconder . your father be the man , he be always at hand , and can watch his opportunity . he know more big - wig than I do , and he have not give offence where I have . get your father , my dear , to attend to it . " 
 but the lady be not to be so put off , for they understand the difference of character . Lord Nelson be as sure to do a thing as Admiral Darling be to drop it if it grow too heavy . hence it come to pass that Blyth Scudamore , though fail of the Victory and amphion which he would have choose , if the choice be his receive with that cheerful philosophy   which have make he so dear to the school - boy , and be largely require among they   his appointment as junior lieutenant to the 38 - gun frigate Leda , attach to the Channel fleet under Cornwallis , whose business it be to deal with the french flotilla of invasion . 




 CHAPTER XV 

 ORDEAL of AUDIT 

 England see the grow danger , and prepare , with an even mind and well - girt body , to confront it . as yet stand up no other country to help or even comfort she , so cowed be all the continent by the lash , and spur of an upstart . alone , encumber with the pack of Ireland , pinch with hunger and dearth of victual , and cramp with the colic of Whiggery , she set her strong shoulder to the wheel of fortune , and so keep it till the hill be behind she . some nation   which owe their existence to she   have forget these thing conveniently ; an Englishman hat to speak of they , through his unjust abhorrence of self - praise ; and so do a Frenchman , by virtue of motif equally respectable . 
 but now the especial danger lie in the special strength of England . scarcely any man along the coast , who have ever come across a Frenchman , could be lead   by quotation from history or even from newspaper   to believe that there be any sense in this menace of his to come and conquer we . even if he land , which be not likely for none of they could box the compass the only thing he take would be a jolly good thrashing , and a few pill of lead for his garlic . this lofty contempt on the part of the seafaring man have be enhance by Nelson , and throve with stoutest vigour in the enlightened breast of Springhaven . 
 yet military man think otherwise , and so do the owner of crop and rick , and so do the dealer in bacon and egg and crockery , and even hardware . Mr. Cheeseman , for instance , who leave nothing unsold that he could turn a penny by , be anything but easy in his mind , and dream such dream as he could not impart to his wife on account of her tendency to hysteric but tell with much power to his daughter Polly , now the recognise belle of Springhaven . this vigilant grocer and butterman , tea , coffee , tobacco , and snuffman , hosi also , and general provider for the outer as well as the inner man , have much of that enterprise in his nature which the country believe to come from London . his possession of this be ascribe by all person of a thoughtful turn to his ownership of that well - build schooner the London Trader . sailing as she do , when the weather be fine , nearly every other week , for London , and return with equal frequency , to the woman who have never be ten mile from home she be a mystery and a watchword . not one of they would allow lad of hers to join this romantic galleon , and tempt the black cloud of the distance ; neither do Mr. Cheeseman yearn   for reason of his own about city price   to navigate this good ship with native . moreover , it be absurd , as he say , with a keen sense of his own cheapness , to suppose that he could find the fund to buy and ply such a ship as that ! 
 truth be a fugitive creature , even when she deign to be visible , or even to exist . the truth of Mr. Cheeseman 's statement have exist , but be long since fly . such be his worth that he could now afford to buy the London Trader three time over , and pay ready money every time . but when he first invest hard cash in she against the solid tear of his prudent wife true enough it be that he could only scrape together one quarter of the sum require . Mrs. Cheeseman , who be then in a condition of absorb interest with Polly , make it her last request in this world for she never expect to get over it that Jemmy should not run in debt on a goose - chase , and fetch her poor spirit from its grave again . James Cheeseman be compel as the noble man may be to dissemble and even deny his intention until the blessed period of caudle - cup , when , the weather be pleasant and the wind along the shore , he find himself encourage to put up the window gently . the tide be come in with a long seesaw , and upon it , like the baby in the cradle full of sleep , lie rock another little strange , or rather a very big one , to the lady 's conception . 
 let bygone be bygone . there be some reproach ; but the weak vessel , Mrs. Cheeseman , at last strike flag , without sink , as she threaten to do . and when little Polly go for her first airing , the London Trader have accomplish her first voyage , and be sail in triumphantly with a box of " top and bottom " from the ancient firm in Threadneedle Street , which have save so many infant from the power that cut the thread . after that , everything go as it should go , include this addition to the commercial strength of Britain , which the lady be enable soon to talk of as " our ship , " and to cite when any question rise of the late London fashion . but even now , when a score of year , save one , have make their score and go , Mrs. Cheeseman only guess and doubt as to the purchase of her ship . James Cheeseman know the value of his own counsel , and so keep it ; and be pat on both shoulder by the world , while he pat his own butter . 
 he wear an apron of the pure white , with shoulder - strap of linen tape , and upon his counter he have a desk , with a carve oak rail in front of it and return at either end . the joy of his life be here to stand , with goodly shirt sleeve shin , his bright cheek also shin in the sun , unless it be hot enough to hurt his good . he be not a great man , but a good one in the opinion of all who owe he nothing , and even in his own estimate , though he owe so much to himself . it be enough to make any one who possess a shilling hungry to see he so clean , so ready , and ruddy among the many good thing which his look and manner , as well as his word , commend . and as soon as he begin to smack his rosy lip , which nature have fit up on purpose , over a rasher , or a cut of gammon , or a keg of good Aylesbury , or a fine red herring , no customer have a penny in his pocket might struggle hard enough to keep it there . for the half - hearted policy of finger one 's money , and ask a price theoretically , would recoil upon the constitution of the strong man , unless he could detach from all cooperation the congenial research of his eye and nose . when the weather be cool and the air full of appetite , and a fine smack of salt from the sea be sparkle on the margin of the plate of expectation , there be Mr. Cheeseman , with a knife and fork , amid a presence of hungrifye good that beat the weak effort of imagination . ham of the first rank and high education , spring of pork sweet than the pure spring of poetry , pat of butter fragrant as the most delicious flattery , chick with breast too ample to require to be break , and sometimes prawn from round the headland , fresh enough to saw one another 's head off , but for be boil already . 
 memory fail to record one - tenth of all the good thing gather there . and why ? because hope be the power arouse , and how seldom can memory endorse it ! even in the case of Mr. Cheeseman 's ware there be people who say , after make short work with they , that short weight have enable they to do so . and every one live in the village be surprised to find his own scale require balance again every time he send his little girl to Cheeseman 's . 
 this upright tradesman be attend to his business one cold day in May , 1803 , soon after Nelson sail from Portsmouth , and he stand with his beloved pound of farm - house butter , bladder of lard , and new - lay egg , and square of cream - cheese behind he , with a broad butter - spathe of white wood in his hand , a long goose - pen tuck over his left ear , and the great copper scale hang handy . so strict be his style , though he be not above a joke , that only his own hand might serve forth an ounce of good butter to the public . and whenever this be weigh , and the beam adjust handsomely to the satisfaction of the purchaser , down go the butter to be pack upon a shelf uninvade by the public eye . person too scantily endow with the great of all christian virtue have the hardihood to say that Mr. Cheeseman here indulge in a process of high art discover by himself . discourse of the weather , or the crop , or perhaps the war , and mourn the dishonesty of statesman nowadays , by dexterous undersweep of keen steel blade , from the bottom of the round , or pat , or roll , he would have away a thin slice , and with that motion jerk it into the barrel which he keep beneath his desk . 
 " be this , then , the establishment of the illustrious Mr. Cheeseman ? " the time be yet early , and the gentleman who put this question be in rid dress . the worthy tradesman look at he , and the rosy hue upon his cheek be marble with a paler tint . 
 " this be the shop of the ' umble James Cheeseman , " he answer , but not with the alacrity of business . " all thing good that be in season , and nothing keep unseasonable . with what can I have the honor of serve you , sir ? " 
 " with a little talk . " the stranger 's manner be not unpleasantly contemptuous , but lofty , and such as the english shopman love , and call " aristocratic . " 
 " to talk with a gentleman be a pleasure as well as an honour , " say Cheeseman . 
 " but not in this public establishment . " the visitor wave both hand as he speak , in a style not then common with Englishmen though they be learn eloquent gesticulation now . " it be fine , Mr. Cheeseman ; but it be not bah , I forget your english word . " 
 " it be fine , sir , as you be good enough to observe " the humble James Cheeseman be proud of his shop " but not , as you remark , altogether private . that can hardly be expect , where business be conduct to suit universal requirement . polly , my dear , if your mother can spare you , come and take my place at the desk a few minute . I have business inside with this gentleman . you may sell almost anything , except butter . if any one want that , they must wait till I come back . " 
 a very pretty damsel , with a cap of foreign lace both adorn and adorn by her beautiful bright hair , come shyly from a little door behind the counter , receive with a quick blush the stranger 's earnest gaze , and return with a curtsey the courteous flourish of his loop - up riding - hat . " what a handsome gentleman ! " say polly to herself ; " but there be something very sad and very wild in his appearance . " her father 's conclusion be the same , and his heart misgave he as he lead in this unexpected guest . 
 " there be no cause for apology . this place be a very good one , " the stranger reply , lay down his heavy whip on the table of a stone - floor room , to which he have be show . " you be a man of business , and I be come upon dry business . you can conjecture be it not so ? who I be by this time , although I be tell that I do not bear any strong resemblance to my father . " 
 he take off his hat as he speak , shake back his long black hair , and fix his jet - black eye upon Cheeseman . that upright dealer have not recover his usual self - possession yet , but manage to look up for he be short by a head than his visitor with a doubtful and enquire smile . 
 " I be Caryl Carne , of Carne Castle , as you be pleased to call it . I have not be in England these many year ; from the death of my father I have be afar ; and now , for cause of my own , I be return , with hope of collect the fragment of the property of my ancestor . it appear to have be their custom to scatter , but not gather up again . my intention be to make a sheaf of the relic spread by squanderer , and snap up by scoundrel . " 
 " to be sure , to be sure , " cry the general dealer ; " this be vastly to your credit , sir , and I wish you all success , sir , and so will all who have so long respect your ancient and honourable family , sir . take a chair , sir please to take a chair . " 
 " I find very little to my credit , " Mr. Carne say , dryly , as he take the offer chair , but keep his eye still upon Cheeseman 's ; " but among that little be a bond from you , give nearly twenty year agone , and of which you will retain , no doubt , a vivid recollection . " 
 " a bond , sir a bond ! " exclaim the other , with his bright eye twinkle , as in some business enterprise . " I never sign a bond in all my life , sir . why , a bond require surety , and nobody ever go surety for I . " 
 " bond may not be the proper legal term . it be possible . I know nothing of the english law . but a document it be , under hand and seal , and your signature be witness , Mr. Cheeseman . " 
 " ah well ! let I consider . I begin to remember something . but my memory be not as it use to be , and twenty year make a great hole in it . will you kindly allow I to see this paper , if you have it with you , sir ? " 
 " it be not a paper ; it be write upon parchment , and I have not bring it with I . but I have write down the intention of it , and it be as follow : 
 " ' this indenture make between James Cheeseman   with a long description , of the one part , and Montagu Carne   treat likewise , of the other part , after a long account of some arrangement make between they , witnesseth that in consideration of the sum of 300 pound well and truly pay by the say Montagu Carne to Cheeseman , he , the say Cheeseman , doth assign , transfer , set over , and so on , to the said Carne , etc . , one equal undivided moiety and one half part of the other moiety of and in a certain vessel , ship , trading - craft , and so forth , know or thenceforth to be know as the London Trader , of Springhaven , in the county of Sussex , by way of security for the interest at the rate of five per cent . per annum , payable half - yearly , as well as for the principal sum of 300 pound , so advanced as aforesaid . ' " 
 " if it should prove , sir , that money be owe , " Mr. Cheeseman say , with that exalt candour which make a weak customer condemn his own eye and nose , " no effort on my part shall be want , bad as the time be , to procure it and discharge it . in every commercial transaction I have find , and my experience be now considerable , that confidence , as between man and man , be the only true footing to go upon . and how can true confidence exist , unless " 
 " unless a man show some honesty . and a man who keep book such as these , " pursue the visitor , suggest a small kick to a pile of ledger , " can hardly help know whether he owe a large sum or whether he have pay it . but that be not the only question now . in continuation of that document I find a condition , a clause provisional , that it shall be at the option of the aforesaid Montagu Carne , and his representative , either to receive the interest at the rate before mention and thereby secure , or , if he or they should so prefer , to take for their own benefit absolutely three - fourth of the net profit , proceed , or other increment realise by the trading venture , or other employment from time to time , of the say London Trader . also there be a covenant for the insurance of the say vessel , and a power of sale , and some other provision about access to trading book , etc . , with which you have , no doubt , a good acquaintance , Mr. Cheeseman . " 
 that enterprise merchant , importer of commodity , and wholesale and retail dealer be fond of assure his numerous friend that " nothing ever come amiss to he . " but some of they now would have doubt about this if they have watch his face as carefully as Caryl Carne be watch it . Mr. Cheeseman could look a hundred people in the face , and with great vigour too , when a small account be run . but the sad , contemptuous , and pierce gaze as if he be hardly worth penetrate and the twirl of the black tuft above the lip , and the firm conviction on the broad white forehead that it be confront a rogue too common and shallow to be worth frown at all these , and the fact that be under they , come amiss to the true James Cheeseman . 
 " I scarcely see how to take this , " he say , be clever enough to suppose that a dash of candour might sweeten the embroilment . " I will not deny that I be under obligation to your highly respected father , who be greatly beloved for his good - will to his neighbour . ' Cheeseman , ' he use to say , ' I will stand by you . you be the only man of enterprise in these here part . whatever you do be for the good of Springhaven , which belong to my family for century before those new - fangle darling come . and , Cheeseman , you may trust to the honour of the Carnes not to grind down a poor man who have his way to make . ' they be his word , sir ; how well I recollect they ! " 
 " too well almost , " reply the young man , coldly , " consider how scanty be your memory just now . but it may save time , and painful effort of your memory , if I tell you at once that I be not concerned in any way with the sentiment of my father . I owe he very little , as you must be well aware ; and the matter betwixt you and I be strictly one of business . the position in which I be leave be such that I must press every legal claim to the extreme . and have the option under this good document , I have determine to insist upon three - quarter of the clear proceed of this trading - ship , from the date of the purchase until the present day , as well as the capital sum invest on this security . " 
 " very well , sir , if you do , there be only one course leave I to go into the Court of Bankruptcy , see all my little stock in trade sell up , and start in life again at the age of fifty - seven , with a curse upon all old family . " 
 " your curse , my good friend , will not add sixpence to your credit . and the heat you exhibit be not well adapt for calculation commercial . there be one other course which I be able to propose , though I will not give a promise yet to do so a course which would relieve I from take possession of this noble ship which have make your fortune , and perhaps from enforce the strict examination of your trading - book , to which I be entitle . but before I propose any such concession , which will be a grand abdication of right , one or two thing become necessary . for example , I must have some acquaintance with your character , some certitude that you can keep your own counsel , and not divulge everything that arrive within your knowledge ; also that you have some courage , some freedom of mind from small insular sentiment , some desire to promote the true interest of mankind , and the destruction of national prejudice . " 
 " certainly , sir ; all of those I can approve of . they be very glorious thing , " cry Cheeseman a man of fine liberal vein , whenever two half - crown be as good as a crown . " we be cramp and trample and down - trodden by the air big people give themselves , and the longing of such of we as think be to speak our mind about it . upon that point of freedom , sir , I can heartily go with you , and every stick upon my premise be well insure . " 
 " include , I hope , the London Trader , accord to your covenant . and that remind I of another question be it well - find , well - man , and a good rapid ship to make the voyage ? no falsehood , if you please , about this matter . " 
 " she be the fast sailer on the english coast , build at Dunkirk , and as sound as a bell . she could show her taffrail , in light weather , to any british cruiser in the Channel . she could run a fine cargo of french cognac and foreign lace any day . " 
 " it be not my desire , " Caryl Carne reply , " to cheat the British Revenue . for that purpose exist already plenty of british tradesman . for the present I impress upon you one thing only , that you shall observe silence , a sacred silence , regard this conversation . for your own sake you will be incline to do so , and that be the only sake a man pay much attention to . but how much for your own sake you be oblige to keep your counsel , you will very soon find out if you betray it . " 




 CHAPTER XVI 

 FOX - HILL 

 when it be know in this fine old village that young Squire Carne from foreign part be come back to live in the ancient castle , there be much large outlay   both of word and thought   about that than about any french invasion . " let they land if they can , " say the able - bodied man , in discussion of the latter question ; " they will not   find it so easy to get away again as they seem to put into their reckoning . but the plague of it all be the damage to the fishing . " 
 not that the squadron of Captain Tugwell be shorn as yet of its number , though all the young man be under notice to hold themselves ready as " Sea - Fencibles . " the injury to their trade lie rather in the difficulty of get to their fishing - ground , and in the disturbance of these by cruiser , with little respect for their net and line . again , as the tiding of french preparation wax more and more outrageous , Zebedee have as much as he could do to keep all his young hand loyal . all their solid interest lie   as he tell they every morning   in stick to the Springhaven flag a pair of sol couchant , herring salient , and mackerel regardant , all upon a bright sea - green rather than in hanker after roll of drum and Union - Jack . what could come of these but hardship , want of victual , wound , and death ; or else to stump about on one leg , and hold out a hat for a penny with one arm ? they feel that it be true ; they have see enough of that ; it have happen in all their own family . 
 yet such be the love of the native land and the yearning to stand in front of it , and such be the hate of be triumph over by fellow who kiss one another and weep , and such be the tingling of the knuckle for a blow when the body have be kick in sore place , that the heart will at last get the well of the head or at least it use to be so in England . wherefore Charley Bowles be in arm already against his country 's enemy ; and Harry Shanks wait for little except a clear proclamation of prize - money ; and even young Daniel be tear at his kedge like a lively craft riding in a brisk sea - way . he have see Lord Nelson , and have speak to Lord Nelson , and that great man would have pat he on the head so patriotic be his sentiment if the great man have be a little tall . 
 but the one thing that keep Dan Tugwell firm to his mooring at Springhaven be the deep hold of his steadfast heart in a love which it know to be hopeless . to die for his country might become a stern duty , about which he would rather not be hurry ; but to die for Miss dolly would be a wild delight ; and how could he do it unless he be at hand ? and now there be so many young officer again , land in boat , come in post - chaise , or charge down the road on horseback , that Daniel , while touch up the finish of his boat with paint and varnish and Venetian Red , be not so happy as an artist should be who know how to place the whole . sometimes , with the paint stir up and creaming , and the ooze of the brush trim warily , through the rush and ragwort and sea - willow his keen , unconquerable eye would spy the only figure that quell they , faraway , show against the shin water , or shadow upon the flat mirror of the sand . but , alas ! there be always another figure near it , big , bulkier , frame with ugly angle , jerk about with the elbow stick out , instead of glide gracefully . likely enough the lovely form , bring near to the eye and heart by love , would flit about beautifully for two sweet moment , fill with rapture all the flash of the sea and calm of the evening sky beyond ; and then the third moment would be hideous . for the figure of the ungainly foe would stride across the delicious vision , huge against the wave like cyclop , and like he gesticulant , but unhappily not so single - eyed that the slippery fair might despise he . then away would fly all sense of art and joy in the touch of perfection , and a very nasty feeling would ensue , as if nothing be worth live for , and nobody could be believe in . 
 that plaguesome Polypheme be Captain Stubbard , begirt with a wife , and endow with a family almost in excess of benediction , and dance attendance upon Miss Dolly , too stoutly for his own comfort , in the hope of procure for his own Penates something to eat and to sit upon . some evil genius have whisper , or rather trumpet , into his ear for he have but one left , and that work very seldom , through alarm about the bullet which have carry off its fellow that if he desire , as he do with heart and stomach , to get a clear widening by 200 pound of his strait way and restrict mean , through Admiral Darling it might be do , and Miss dolly be the proper one to make he do it . for the Inspectorship of Sea - Fencibles from Selsea - bill to Dungeness be worth all that money in hard cash yearly ; and the late Inspector have quit this life through pork boil in a copper kettle the situation be naturally vacant ; and the Admiral be the man for whose check the Inspectorship be appoint , it be needless to say that   in the spirit of fair play   the appointment be vest in the Admiral . 
 the opinion of all who know he be that Captain Stubbard be fairly entitle to look for something high . and he share that opinion , take loftier aim than figure could be make to square with , till the latter prevail , as they generally do , because they can work without victual . for although the brave Captain have lose three rib or at any rate more than he could spare of they   not be a pig   in the service of his country , he require as much as ever to put inside they ; and his child , not have inherit that loss as scientifically as they should have do , be hard to bring up upon the 15 pound yearly allow by Great Britain for each of the go bone . from the ear that be go he derive no income , have rashly compound for 25 pound . 
 in the nature of thing , which the name have follow , the father be the feed ; and the world be full of remark unless he become a good clothier also . but everything go against this father , with nine little Stubbards run after he , and no ninepence in any of his pocket , because he be shelfe upon half - pay , on account of the depression of the time and of his rib . but Miss Dolly Darling be resolve to see he right , for she hat all national meanness . 
 " what be the use of have any influence , " she ask her good father , " unless you employ it for your own friend ? I should be quite ashamed to have it say of I , or think , that I could get a good thing for any one I be fond of , and be mean enough not to do it , for fear of paltry jealousy . Mean be much too weak a word ; it be downright dishonest , and what be much bad , cowardly . what be the government mean for , unless it be to do good to people ? " 
 " certainly , my dear child , certainly . to the people at large , that be to say , and the high interest of the country . " 
 " can there be any people more at large than Captain Stubbard and his wife and child ? their elbow be come out of their clothe , and they have scarcely get a bed to sleep upon . my income be not enough to stop to count , even when I get it pay punctually . but every farthing I receive shall go that be to say , if it ever do come into the lap of Mrs. Stubbard , anonymously and respectfully . " 
 " pay your bill , first , " say the Admiral , take the weather - gage of the discussion : " a little bird tell I that you owe a good trifle , even in Springhaven . " 
 " then the little bird have get a false bill , " reply dolly , who be not very easy to fluster . " who be there to spend sixpence with in a little hole of this kind ? I be not a customer for tea , coffee , tobacco , snuff , or pepper , nor even for whiting , sol , or conger . Old Cheeseman import all the fashion , as he say ; but I go by my own judgment . and trumpery as my income be , very little of it go into his till . but I should like to know who tell you such a wicked story , father ? " 
 " thing be mention in confidence , and I put they together , " say the Admiral . " do not   say another word , or look as if you would be happy if you have something to cry about . your dear mother use to do it ; and it beat I always . I have long have my eye upon Captain Stubbard , and I remember well that gallant action when his three rib fly away . we call he Adam , because of his wife come just when his middle rib go , and his name be Adam Stubbard , sure enough . such man , in the prime of their life , should be promote , instead of be disable , for a scratch like that . why , he walk every bit as well as I do , and his watch - ribbon cover it . and nine child ! Lord bless my heart ! I scarcely know which way to turn , with only four ! " 
 within a short fortnight Captain Stubbard be appoint , with an office establish at the house of Widow shank though his real office naturally be at the public - house and Royal Proclamations arouse the valour of nearly everybody who could read they . nine little Stubbards soon be rig too smart to know themselves , as the style be of all dandy ; and even Mrs. Stubbard have a new belt make to go round she , when the weather be elastic . 
 " these be the thing that prove the eye of an all - wise Providence over we , " say the Captain to the Admiral , point out six pair of short leg , galligaskine from one roll of cloth ; " these be the thing that make one feel the force of the word of David . " 
 " certainly , yes , to be sure ! " reply the gallant senior officer , all at sea as to the passage suggest . " good leg they have get , and no mistake ; like the polished corner of the temple . let they go and dip they in the sea , while you give the benefit of your opinion here . not here , I mean , but upon Fox - hill yonder ; if Mrs. Stubbard will spare you for a couple of hour , most kindly . " 
 of the height that look down with a breezy air upon the snug nest of Springhaven , the fair to see from a distance , and to tread with brisk foot , be Fox - hill . for the down , which be channel with the spring that form the brook , keep this for their own last spring into the air , before bath in the vigorous composure of the sea . all the other hill fall back a little , to let Fox - hill have the first choice of aspect or bear the first brunt , as itself would state the matter . and to anybody come up , and ten time to a stranger , this resolute foreland offer more invitation to go home again , than to come visit . for the bulge of the breast be steep , and rib with hoop come up in denial , concrete with chalk , muricate with flint , and thornily crest with good stout furze . and the forefront of the head , when gain , be stiff with bramble , and stub with sloe , and mitre with a choice band of stanch sting - nettle . 
 " it would take a well frenchman , " say the Admiral , with that brevity which be the happy result of stoutness up steep hill , " than any of ' they flat - bottom , ' as Swipes , my gardener , call they , to get through these prickle , Stubbard , without Sark - blewing . such a wonderfully thin - skinned lot they be ! do I ever tell you the story of our boatswain 's mate ? but that take a well sailing breeze than I have   get now . you see where we be , do not   you ? " 
 " certainly , Admiral , " reply Captain Stubbard , disdain to lay hand to his injure side , painfully as it yearn for pressure ; " we have have a long pull , and we get a fine outlook over the country for league , and the Channel . how close at hand everything look ! I suppose we shall have rain , and we want it . I could thump that old castle among the tree into smash , and your church look as if I could put a shot with a rifle - gun into the bell - chamber . " 
 " and so you could . what I want to show you be that very point , and the importance of it . with a battery of long twenty - four up here , the landing , the bay , and all the road be at our mercy . my dear old friend Nelson draw my attention to it . " 
 " it be plain as a pikestaff to Tom , Dick , or Harry : " Captain Stubbard be a frank , straightforward man , and much as he owe to the Admiral 's aid , not a farthing would he pay in flattery . " but why should we want to command this spot ? there be nothing to protect but a few common house , and some half - score of fishing - craft , and a schooner that trade to London , and yonder old church , and oh yes , to be sure , your own house and property , Admiral . " 
 " those must take their chance , like other . I hope I know well than to think of they in comparison with the good of the country . but if we fail to occupy this important post , the enemy might take we by surprise , and do so . " 
 " possible , but most improbable . this little place lie , by the trend of the coast , quite out of their course from Boulogne to London ; and what be there here to tempt they ? no rich town to sack , no great commerce to rob , no valuable shipping to lay hand on . " 
 " no ; but there be my house and my two girl ; and I do not   want my old roof burn , and my daughter put to wait on Boney . but to think of self - interest be below contempt , with our country go through such trial . neither should we add any needless expense to a treasury already overburden . " 
 " certainly not . it would be absolutely wicked . we have a long and costly war before we , and not a shilling should be spend except in case of clear necessity . " 
 " I be very glad indeed to find your opinion so decide , so untainted with petty self - interest . " as Admiral Darling speak he close a little silver telescope , with which he have be gaze through the woode coronet of the hill . " I think it my duty to consult you , Stubbard , before despatch this letter , which , be back by Nelson 's opinion , would probably have receive attention . if a strong battery be throw up here , as it would be in a fortnight from the receipt of this bit of foolscap , the appointment of commandant would rest with I , and I could appoint nobody but your good self , because of your well - know experience in earthwork . the appointment would have double your present pay , which , though well than nothing , be far below your merit . but your opinion settle the question otherwise , and I must burn my letter . let we lose no more time . Mrs. Stubbard will call I a savage , for keep you away so long . " 
 " important business , " reply the Captain , " will not wait even for lady , or , rather , they must try to wait for it , and give way to more reasonable urgency . some time be require for consider this matter , and decide what be most for the interest of the nation . oblige I with your spy - glass , Admiral . there be one side on which I have neglect to look out , and that may of all be the most important . a conclusion arrive at by yourself and Nelson be not to be hastily set aside . your knowledge of the country be so far beyond mine , though I may have have more to do with land - work . we ought to think twice , sir , if the government will pay for it , about a valuable job of this kind . " 
 with these word Captain Stubbard begin to use the telescope carefully , form his opinion through it , and wisely shake his head , now and then , with a long and long focus . then he close the glass , and his own lip firmly whereby a man announce that no other should open his against they and sternly stride the yard exact , take measurement for the battery . the hill be crown with a ring of Scotch fir , cast a quiet shade upon the warlike haste of the Captain . if Admiral Darling smile , it be to the landscape and the offing , for he know that Stubbard be of rather touchy fibre , and relish no joke unless of home production . his slow , solid face be enough to show this , and the squareness of his outline , and the forward thrust of his knee as he walk , and the larkspur impress of his linger heel . and he seldom say much , without something to say . 
 " well , " cry the Admiral , grow tired of sit so long upon a fall trunk , " what conclusion do you feel inclined to come to ? ' tis a fine breezy place to clear the brain , and a briny air to sharpen the judgment . " 
 " only one tree need come down this crooked one at the southeast corner . " Captain Stubbard begin to swing his arm about , like a windmill uncertain of the wind . " all gentleman hate to have a tree cut down , all blackguard delight in the process . admiral , we will not hurt your tree . they will add to our strength , by mask it . six long twenty - four of the new make , here in front , and two eighteen upon either flank , and I should like to see the whole of the Boulogne flotilla try to take yonder shore by daylight . that be to say , of course , if I command , with good old salt to second I . with your common artillery officer , landlubber , smell - the - wick , cross - the - brace son of gun , there have well not be anything at all put up . they can not   make a fortification ; and when they have make it , they can not   work it . Admiral Darling , you know that , though you have not have the bad luck to deal with they as I have . I may thank one of they for be up here on the shelf . " 
 " of one thing you may be quite certain , " reply the commander of the sea defence ; " if we have any battery on this Fox - hill , it shall be construct and man by blue - jacket . I have a large draft of they now at discretion . every man in Springhaven will lend a hand , if pay for it . it would take at least a twelvemonth to get it do from Woolwich . a seaman do a thing before a landsman think about it . " 




 CHAPTER XVII 

 SEA - SIDE LODGINGS 

 to set a dog barking be easy than to stop he by the sound reasoning . even if the roof above his honest head , grow loose on its nail , be be mend , he come out to ask about the matter , and in strong term proclaim his opinion to the distance . 
 after this kind behave the people about to be protect by this battery . they have dream of no danger till they see their house begin to be protect , and for this though it add to their importance they be not truly thankful . they take it in various way , accord to their rich variety of reflection ; but the way in which nobody take it be that of gratitude and humility . 
 " everything upside down , " they say , " everything go clean topsy - turvy ! and the deep meaning of it be to rob our fishing , under pretence of the national . it may bring a good bit of money to the place , for the lining of one or two pocket , such as John Prater 's and Cheeseman 's ; but I never do hold so much with money , when shattery way come along of it . no daughter of mine stir out - of - door after sundown , I can tell they . " 
 thus be the mind of the man disturb , or at any rate those of the elder one ; while the woman , on the whole , be pleased , although they pretend to be contemptuous . " I will tell you what I think , ma'am , " Mrs. Cheeseman say to Widow Shanks quite early , " if you take a farthing less than half a guinea a week for your dimity - parlour , with the window up the hill , and the little door under the big sweet - briar , I shall think that you be not as you use to be . " 
 " and right you would be , ma'am , and too right there ; " Mrs. Shanks sigh deeply as she think of it . " there be nobody but you can understand it , and I do not   mind say it on that account to you . whenever I have want for a little bit of money , as the nature of lone widow generally do , it have always be out of your power , Mrs. Cheeseman , to oblige I , and quite right of you . but I have a good son , thank the Lord , by the name of Harry , to provide for I ; and a guinea a week be the agreement now for the dimity - parlour , and the three leg'd bed , and cold dinner to be pay for extra , such as I might send for to your good shop , with the money ready in the hand of my little girl , and jug below her apron for refreshment from the Darling . " 
 " well , I never ! my dear soul , you have take all my breath away . why , it must be the captain of all the gunner . how gunpowder do pay , to be sure ! " 
 " Lor , ma'am , why , do not   you know , " reply Mrs. Shanks , with some contempt , " that the man with three rib be the captain of the gunner the man in my back sitting - room ? no dimity - parlour for he with his family , not for a guinea and a half a week . but if I be to tell you who the gentleman be , and one of the high all round these part , truthful as you know I , Mrs. Cheeseman , you would say to yourself , what a liar she be ! " 
 " Mrs. Shanks , I never use coarse expression , even to myself in private . and perhaps I could tell you a thing or two would astonish you more than I , ma'am . suppose I should tell you , to begin with , who your guinea lodger be ? " 
 " that you could never do , Mrs. Cheeseman , with all your time a - counting change . he be not of the rank for a twopenny rasher , or a wedge of cheese pack in old petticoat . " 
 these two lady now look at one another . they have not have a quarrel for almost three month , and a large arrear of little prick on either side be pende . soon or later it would have to be fight out   like a feud between two nation , with a houseful of loss and woe to either side , but a thimbleful of pride and glory . yet so much wise be these woman than the most sagacious nation that they put off to a cheap time their grudge against each other . 
 " his rank may be royal , " say the wife of Mr. Cheeseman , " though a go - downhill kind of royalty , perhaps , and yet he might be glad , Mrs. Shanks , to come where the butter have the milk spot , and none be in the cheese , ma'am . " 
 " if such should be his wish , ma'am , for supper or for breakfast , or even for dinner on a Sunday when the rain come through the Castle , you may trust I to know where to send he , but not to guarantee he at all of his money . " 
 " they high one be very apt to slip in that , " Mrs. Cheeseman answer , thoughtfully ; " they seem to be less particular in pay for a thing than they be to have it good . but a burn child dread the fire , as they say ; and a young man with a castleful of owl and rat , by reason of go for these hundred year on credit , will have it bring home to he to pay ready money . but the Lord be over we ! if I do not   see he a - go your way already ! good - by , my dear soul good - by , and preserve you ; and if at any time short of table or bed linen , a loan from an old friend , and come back well wash , and it sha'n't   be , as the child sing , ' a friend with a loan have the pick of your bone , and he will not   let you very long alone . ' " 
 " many thank to you for friendly meaning , ma'am , " say the widow , as she take up her basket to go home , " and glad I may be to profit by it , with the time commanding . but as yet I have have neither sleeper or feeder in my little house , but the child . though both of they reserve the right to do it , if nature should so compel they the three - rib gentleman with one ear , at five shilling a week , in the sitting - room , and the young man up over he . their meaning be for business , and study , and keeping of account , and have of a quiet place in bad weather , though feed they must , soon or later , I depend ; and then who be there but Mr. Cheeseman ? " 
 " how grand he do look upon that black horse , quite as solid as if he be glue to it ! " the lady of the shop reply , as she put away the money ; " and to do that without victual be beyond a young man 's power . he look like what they use to call a knight upon an errand , in the picture - book , when I be romantic , only for the hair that come under his nose . ah ! his errand will be to break the heart of the young lady that go down upon the sand in their blue gown , I be afraid , if they can only manage with the hair below his nose . " 
 " and do they good , some of they , and be a judgment from the Lord , for the french style in their skirt be a shocking thing to see . what should we have say when you and I be young , my dear ? but quick step be the word for I , for I expect my Jenny home on her day out from the Admiral , and no Harry in the house to look after she . ah ! dimity - parlour be a thing as may happen to cut both way , Mrs. Cheeseman . " 
 Widow Shanks have good cause to be proud of her cottage , which be the prettiest in Springhaven , and one of the most commodious . she have fight a hard fight , when her widowhood begin , and the child be too young to help she , rather than give up the home of her love - time , and the cradle of her little one . some of her neighbour   who want the house   be sadly pain at her stubbornness , and even dishonesty , as they put it , when she know that she never could pay her rent . but " never be a long time , " accord to the proverb ; and with the forbearance of the Admiral , the kindness of his daughter , and the growth of her own child , she stand clear of all debt now , except the sweet one of gratitude . 
 and now she could listen to the moaning of the sea   which use to make she weep all night   with a milder sense of the cruel woe that it have drown her husband , and a lull of sorrow that be almost hope ; until the dark vision of wreck and corps melt into sweet dream of her son upon the water , finish his supper , and get ready for his pipe . for Harry be make his own track well in the wake of his dear father . 
 now if she have go inland to dwell , from the stroke of her great calamity as most people tell she to make haste and do not only the sympathy of the sea , but many of the little care , which be the ant that bury heavy grief , would have be wholly lose to she . and amongst these care the foremost always , and the most distracting , be that of keep her husband 's cottage as she still would call it tidy , comfortable , bright , and snug , as if he be come on Saturday . 
 where the brook run into the first hearing of the sea , to defer its own extinction it take a lively turn inland , leave a pleasant breadth of green between itself and its destiny . at the breath of salt the large tree hang back , and turn their bough up ; but plenty of pretty shrub come forth , and shade the cottage garden . neither have the cottage wall any lack of leafy mantle , where the summer sun work his own defeat by foster cool obstruction . for here be the tamarisk , and jasmin , and the old - fashioned corchorus flower all the summer through , as well as the myrtle that love the shore , with a thicket of stiff young sprig arise , slow of growth , but hide yearly the havoc make in its head and body by the frost of 1795 , when the mark of every wave upon the sand be ice . and a vine , that seem to have be evolve from a miller , or to have prejecte he , clamber with grey silver pointrel through the more glossy and darker green . and over these you behold the thatch , thick and long and parti - colour , eave with little window , where a bird may nest for ever . 
 but it be not for this outward beauty that Widow Shanks , stick to her house , and pay the rent at interval . to her steadfast and well - manage mind , the number of room , and the separate staircase which a solvent lodger might enjoy , be the choice grant of the household god . the time be bad as they always be when conscientious people think of they and poor Mrs. Shanks be desirous of pay her rent , by the payment of somebody . every now and then some well - feed family , hunger   after long carnage   for fish , would come from village pasture or town shamble , to gaze at the sea , and to taste its content . for in those day fish be still in their duty , to fry well , to boil well , and to go into the mouth well , instead of be dissolute as nowadays the good be with dirty ice , and flabby with arrest fermentation . in the pleasant dimity - parlour then , command a fair view of the lively sea and the stream that sparkle into it , be noble dinner of sole , and mackerel , and smell that smell of cucumber , and dainty dory , and pearl - button turbot , and sometimes even the crisp sand - lance , happily for himself , unhappily for whitebait , still unknown in London . then , after long roving ashore or afloat , these diner come back with a new light shed upon they that of the moon outside the house , of the supper candle inside . there be sure to be a crab or lobster ready , and a dish of prawn sprig with parsley ; if the sea be begin to get cool again , a keg of philanthropic oyster ; or if these be not hospitably on their hinge yet , certainly there would be choice - bodied creature , dry with a dash of salt upon the sunny shingle , and lacking of perfection nothing more than to be warm through upon a toasting - fork . 
 by none , however , of these delight be the newly win lodger tempt . all that he want be peace and quiet , time to go through a great trunk full of paper and parchment , which he bring with he , and a breath of fresh air from the down on the north , and the sea to the south , to enliven he . and in good truth he want to be enliven , as Widow Shanks say to her daughter Jenny ; for his eye be gloomy , and his face be stern , and he seldom say anything good - natured . he seem to avoid all company , and to be wrap up wholly in his own concern , and to take little pleasure in anything . as yet he have not use the bed at his lodging , nor break his fast there to her knowledge , though he ride down early every morning and put up his horse at Cheeseman 's , and never ride away again until the dark have fall . neither have he care to make the acquaintance of Captain Stubbarb , who occupy the room beneath his for a Royal Office as the landlady proudly entitle it ; nor have he receive , to the good of her knowledge , so much as a single visitor , though such might come by his private entrance among the shrub unnoticed . all these thing stir with deep interest and wonder the enquire mind of the widow . 
 " and what do they say of he up at the Hall ? " she ask her daughter Jenny , who be come to spend holiday at home . " what do they say of my new gentleman , young squire Carne from the Castle ? the Carnes and the darling be never great friend , as every one know in Springhaven . still , it do seem hard and unchristianlike to keep up they old enmity ; most of all , when the one side be down in the world , with the owl and the bat and the coney . " 
 " no , mother , no . they be not a bit like that , " reply Jenny a maid of good loyalty ; " it be only that he have not call upon they . all gentlefolk have their proper rule of behaviour . you can not   be expect to understand they , mother . " 
 " but why should he go to they more than they should come to he , particular with young lady there ? and he with only one horse to their seven or eight . I be right , you may depend upon it , Jenny ; and my mother , your grandmother , be a lady's - maid in a high family than Darling it depend upon they to come and look he up first , and he have no call to knock at their door without it . why , it stand to reason , poor young man ! and not a bit hath he eat from Monday . " 
 " well , I believe I be right , but I will ask Miss Dolly . she be that sharp , she know everything , and I do not   mind what I say to she , when she think that she look handsome . and it take a very bad dress , I can tell you , to put she out of that opinion . " 
 " she be right enough there : " Mrs. Shanks shake her head at her daughter for speak in this way . " the ugly frock as ever come from France could not   make she any but a booty . and the Lord know the quality have come to queer shape now . Undecent would be the name for it in our rank of woman . why , the last of her frock she give you , Jenny , how much do I put on , at top and bottom , and you three inch short than she be ! and the slip they tie round they oh dear ! oh dear ! as if that be to hold they up and buckle they together ! will not   they have the groaning by the time they come to my age ? " 




 CHAPTER xviii 

 FRENCH and ENGLISH 

 Admiral Darling be now so busy , and so continually call from home by the duty of his commandership , that he could not fairly be expect to call upon Mr. Caryl Carne . yet that gentleman , be rather sensitive which sometimes mean very spiteful resent as a personal slight this failure ; although , if the overture have be make , he would have ascribe it to intrusive curiosity , and a low desire to behold he in his ruin . but truly in the old man 's kindly heart there be no sour corner for ill blood to lurk in , and no dull fibre for ill - will to feed on . he keep on meaning to go and call on Caryl Carne , and he have quite make up his mind to do it , but something always happen to prevent he . 
 neither do he care a groat for his old friend Twemlow 's advice upon that subject . " do not   go near he , " say the Rector , take care that his wife be quite safe out of hearing ; " it would ill become I to say a word against my dear wife 's own nephew , and the representative of her family . and , to the utmost of my knowledge , there be nothing to be say against he . but I can not   get on with he at all . I do not   know why . he have only honor we with a visit twice , and he would not even come to dinner . nice manner they learn on the continent ! but none of we weep when he decline ; not even his good aunt , my wife . though he must have get a good deal to tell we , and an extraordinary knowledge of foreign way . but instead of do that , he seem to sneer at we . I can look at a question from every point of view , and I defy anybody to call I narrow - minded . but still , one must draw the line somewhere , or throw overboard all principle ; and I draw it , my dear Admiral , against infidel and against Frenchmen . " 
 " no rational person can do otherwise " the Admiral 's opinion be decisive " but this young man be of good english birth , and one can not   help feel sorry for his circumstance . and I assure you , Twemlow , that I feel respect as well for the courage that he show , and the perseverance , in come home and face those vile usurer . and your own wife 's nephew ! why , you ought to take his part through thick and thin , whatever you may think of he . from all I hear he must be a young man of exceedingly high principle ; and I shall make a point of call upon he the first half - hour I get to spare . to - morrow , if possible ; or if not , the day after , at the very late . " 
 but the needful half - hour have not yet be find ; and Carne , who be will not to think the bad of everybody , conclude that the Darling race still cherish the old grudge , which have always be on his own side . for this he care little , and perhaps be rather glad of it . for the old dwelling - place of his family   the Carne Castle besiege by the roundhead a hundred and sixty year agone   now threaten to tumble about the ear of any one knock at the gate too hard . or rather the remnant of its wall do so ; the great part , have already fall , lie harmless , and produce fine blackberry . 
 as a castle , it have be well respect in its day , though not of mighty bulwark or impregnable position . stand on a knoll , between the ramp of high land and the slope of shore , it would still have be conspicuous to traveller and to voyager but for the tall tree around it . these hide the moat , and the relic of the drawbridge , the groin archway , and cloven tower of the keep which have twice be strike by lightning as well as the window of the armoury , and the chapel hush with ivy . the banqueting hall be in well repair , for the Carnes have be hospitable to the last ; but the window keep no wind off , neither do the roof repulse the rain . in short , all the front be in a pretty state of ruin , very nice to look at , very nasty to live in , except for toad , and bat , and owl , and rat , and eft , and brindle slug with yellow stripe ; or on a summer eve the cockroach and the carrion - beetle . 
 at the back , however , and above the road which Cheeseman travel in his pony - chaise , be a range of room still fit to dwell in , though poorly furnish , and floor with stone . in well time these have be the domain of the house - keeper and the butler , the cook and the other upper servant , who have mind their duty and heed their comfort more truly than the master and mistress do . for the downfall of this family , as of very many other , have be chiefly cause by unwise marriage . instead of choose sensible and active wife to look after their home affair and regulate the household , the Carnes for several generation now have wed flighty lady of good birth and pretty manner , none of whom bring they a pipkinful of money , while all help to spend a potful . therefore their descendant be now live in the kitchen , and have no idea how to make use of they , in spite of his french education ; of comfort also he have not much idea , which be all the well for he ; and he scarcely know what it be to earn and enjoy soft quietude . 
 one night , when the summer be in full prime , and the weather almost blameless , this young Squire Carne ride slowly back from Springhaven to his wear - out castle . the beauty of the night have keep he back , for he hat to meet people on the road . the linger gossip , the tired fagot - bearer , the youth go home from the hay - rick , the man with a gun who know where the hare play , and beyond they all the truant sweetheart , who can not have enough of one another , and wish " good - night " at every corner of the lane , till they tumble over one another 's cottage step all these to Caryl Carne be a smell to be avoid , an eyesore to shut the eye at . he let they get home and pull their boot off , and set the frying - pan a - bubble for they end the day with a bit of bacon , whenever they could cash or credit it and then he set forth upon his lonely ride , strike fear into the heart of any bad child that lie awake . 
 " almost as good as France be this , " he mutter in French , though for once enjoy the pleasure of good english air ; " and well than France would it be , if only it be not cut short so suddenly . there will come a cold wind by - and - by , or a chilly black cloud from the east , and then all be shiver and rawness . but if it only remain like this , I could forgive it for produce I . after all , it be my native land ; and I see the lovely girl to - day that ever I set eye on . none of their make - up and highly finish demoiselle be fit to look at her such simple beauty , such charm of nature , such enchant innocence ! ah , that be where those french girl fail they be always study how they look , instead of leave we to think of it . bah ! what odd to I ? I have high stake to play for . but accord to old Twemlow 's description , she must be the daughter of that old bear Darling , with whom I shall have to pick a bone some day . ha ! how amusing be that battery to I ! how little John Bull know the nature of french troop ! to - morrow we be to have a grand practice - day ; and I hope they will not   shoot I in my new lodging . nothing be impossible to such an idiot as Stubbard . what a set of imbecile I have find to do with ! they have scarcely wit enough to amuse oneself with . Pest of my soul ! be that you , Charron ? again you have break my order . " 
 " name should be avoid in the open air , " answer the man , who be swing on a gate with the simple delight of a Picard . " the climate be of France so much to - night that I find it my duty to encourage it . for what reason shall not I do that ? it be not so often that I have occasion . my dear friend , scold not , but accept the compliment very seldom truthful to your native land . there be none of your clod - pate about to - night . " 
 " come in at once . the mere sound of your breath be enough to set the neighbourhood wonder . could I ever have be burden with a more french Frenchman , though you speak as good English as I do ? " 
 " it be all of that miserable Cheray , " the french gentleman say , when they sit in the kitchen , and Jerry Bowles be feed the fine black horse . " fruit be a thing that my mouth prepare for , directly there be any warmth in the sun . it put itself up , it be elevate , it will not have meat , or any substance coarse . wine of the softest and fruit of the fine be what it must then have , or unmouth itself . that miserable Cheray , his maledictioned name put I forth to be on fire for the good thing he design . cheray you call they , and for cheray I despatch he , suspend between the leaf in the good sun . Bah ! there be nothing ever fit to eat in England . the cheray look very fine , very fine indeed ; and so many do I consume that to travel on a gate be the only palliation . would you have I stay all day in this long cellar ? no diversion , no solace , no change , no conversation ! Old Cheray may sit with his hand upon his knee , but to renaud Charron that be not sufficient . how much long before I sally forth to do the thing , to fight , to conquer the nation ? where be even my little ship of despatch ? " 
 " captain , " answer Caryl Carne , prepare calmly for his frugal supper , " you be place under my command , and another such speech will despatch you to Dunkirk , bind hand and foot , in the hold of the Little Corporal , with which I be now in communication . unless by the time I have sever this bone you hand I your sword in submission , my supper will have to be postpone , while I march you to the yew - tree , signal for a boat , and lie you strapped beneath the oarsman . " 
 Captain Charron , who have hold the command of a french corvette , star furiously at this man , young than himself , so strongly establish over he . Carne be not concerned to look at he ; all he care about be to divide the joint of a wing - rib of cold roast beef , where some good picking lurk in the hollow . then the french man , whose chance would have be very small in a personal encounter with his chief , arise and take a naval sword , short but rather heavy , from a hook which in well day have hold a big dish - cover , and make a salute rather graceful than gracious , present the fringed handle to the carver . 
 " this behaviour be sensible , my friend , and worthy of your distinguished ability . " Carne 's resolute face seldom yield to a smile , but the smile when it come be a sweet one . " pardon I for speak strongly , but my instruction must be the law to you . if you be my commander   as , but for local knowledge , and question of position here , you would be , do you think then that you would allow I to rebel , to grumble , to wander , to demand my own pleasure , when you know that it would ruin thing ? " 
 " Bravo ! it be well speak . my captain , I embrace you . in you live the spirit of the Grand Army , which we of the sea and of the ship admire always , and always desire to emulate . ah , if England possess many englishman like you , she would be hard to conquer . " 
 the owner of this old English castle shoot a glance at the Frenchman for any sign of irony in his word . see none , he continue , in the friendly vein : 
 " our business here demand the great caution , skill , reserve , and self - denial . we be fortunate in have no man of any keen penetration in the neighbourhood , at least of those in authority and concern with public matter . as one of an ancient family , possess the land for century , I have every right to be here , and to pursue my private business in privacy . but if it once get talk about that a french officer be with I , these stupid people will awake their suspicion more strongly by their own stupidity . in this queer island you may do what you like till the neighbourhood turn against you ; and then , if you revolve upon a pin , you can not suit they . you understand ? you have hear I before . it be this that I never can knock into you . " 
 Renaud Charron , who consider himself as all frenchman do then , and perhaps do now far swifter of intellect than any Englishman , find himself not well pleased at this , and desire to know more about it . 
 " nothing can be simple , " the Englishman reply ; " and therefore nothing surer . you know the old proverb ' everything in turn , except scandal , whose turn be always . ' and again another saying of our own land ' the second side of the bread take less time to toast . ' we must not let the first side of ours be toast ; we will shun all the fire of suspicion . and to do this , you must not be see , my dear friend . I may go abroad freely ; you must hide your gallant head until matter be ripe for action . you know that you may trust I not to keep you in the dark a day long than be needful . I have get the old shopkeeper under my thumb , and can do what I please with his trading - ship . but before I place you in command I must change some more of the crew , and do it warily . there be an obstinate cornishman to get rid of , who stick to the plank like a limpet . if we throw he overboard , we shall alarm the other ; if we discharge he without show cause , he will go to the old Admiral and tell all his suspicion . he must be get rid of in London with skill , and then we ship three or four Americans , first - rate seaman , afraid of nothing , who will pass here as fellow from Lancashire . after that we may run among the cruiser as we like , with the boldness and skill of a certain Captain Charron , who must be ill in his cabin when his ship be board . " 
 " it be famous , it be very good , my friend . the patience I will have , and the obedience , and the courage ; and so much the more readily because my pay be good , and keep itself go on dry land as well as sea . " 




 CHAPTER XIX 

 in the line of FIRE 

 no wonder there have be a great deal of talk in the village all that evening , for the following notice have appear in a dozen conspicuous place , begin with the gate of the church - yard , and end with two of the big mooring - post , and not even spar the Admiral 's white gate , where it flap between the two upper rail . it be not print , but write in round hand , with a liberal supply of capital , on a stiff sheet of official paper , stamp with the Royal Arms at the top . and those who be in the secret know that Master Bob Stubbard , the Captain 's eld son , have accomplish this great literary feat at a guerdon of one shilling from the public service fund every time he suck his pen at the end of it . 
 " by order of his Majesty King George III . to - morrow be Wednesday , and the fishing - boat at sea , Artillery practice from Fox - hill fort will be carry on from twelve at noon until three P.M. at a mark - boat moor half a mile from the shore . therefore his Majesty 's loyal subject be warn to avoid the beach westward of the brook between the white flagstaff , as well as the sea in front of it , and not to cross the line of fire below the village but at their own risk and peril . 
 " sign   ADAM JACKSON STUBBARD , R.N. , command Fox - hill Battery . " 
 some indignation be arouse by this ; for Mrs. Caper junior   who be Mrs. Prater 's cousin   have be confine , out of proper calculation , and for the very first time , the moment the boat be go on Monday ; and her house , be near to the fort , and in a hollow where the noise would be certain to keep go round and round , the effect upon her head , not to mention the dear baby 's , be more than any one dare to think of , with the poor father so far away . and if Squire Darling have only be at home , not a woman who could walk would have think twice about it , but go all together to insist upon it that he should stop this wicked bombardment . and this be most unselfish of all of they , they be sure , because they have so long look forward to put cotton - wool in their ear , and see how all the enemy of England would be demolish . but Mrs. Caper junior , and Caper , natu minimus , fall fast asleep together , as thing turn out , and hear not a single bang of it . 
 and so it turn out , in another line of life , with thing against all calculation , resent to be reckon as they always do , like the countless child of Israel . for Admiral Darling be go far away inspect , leave his daughter to inspect themselves . 
 " you may just say exactly what you consider right , dear , " say Miss dolly Darling to her sister Faith ; " and I dare say it make you more comfortable . but you know as well as I do , that there be no reason in it . Father be a darling ; but he must be wrong sometimes . and how can he tell whether he be wrong or right , when he go away fifty mile to attend to other people ? of course I would never disobey his order , anymore than you would . but fact change accord to circumstance , and I feel convinced that if he be here he would say , ' go down and see it , dolly . ' " 
 " we have no right to speculate as to what he might say , " reply Faith , who be very clear - headed . " his order be definite : ' keep within the ground , when notice be give of artillery practice . ' and those order I mean to obey . " 
 " and so do I ; but not to misunderstand they . the beach be a part of our ground , as I have hear he say fifty time in argument , when people try to come encroach . and I mean to go on that part of his ground , because I can not   see well from the other part . that be clearly what he mean ; and he would laugh at we , if we could tell he nothing when he come home . why , he promise to take we as far as Portsmouth to see some artillery practice . " 
 " that be a different thing altogether , because we should be under his control . if you disobey he , it be at your own risk , and I shall not let one of the servant go with you , for I be mistress of the household , if not of you . " 
 " what trumpery air you do give yourself ! one would think you be fifty year old at least . stay at home , if you be such a coward ! I be sure dear daddy would be quite ashamed of you . they be pop already , and I mean to watch they . " 
 " you will not   go so very far , I be quite sure of that , " answer Faith , who understand her sister . " you know your own value , darle dolly , and you would not go at all , if you have not be forbid . " 
 " when people talk like that , it goad I up to almost anything . I intend to go , and stand , as near as can be , in the middle of the space that be mark off ' dangerous . ' " 
 " do , that be a dear . I will lend you my shell - silk that measure twenty yard , that you may be sure of be hit , dear . " 
 " inhuman , selfish , wicked creature ! " cry dolly , and it be almost cry ; " you shall see what come of your cold - bloodedness ! I shall pace to and fro in the direct line of fire , and hang on my back the king 's proclamation , inside out , and write on it in large letter ' by order of my sister I do this . ' then what will be say of you , if they only kill I ? my feeling might be very sad , but I should not envy your , Faith . " 
 " kiss I , at any rate , before you perish , in token of forgiveness ; " and dolly   who dearly love her sister at the keen height of rebellion   run up and kiss Faith , with a smile for she , and a tear for her own self - sacrifice . " I shall put on my shell - pink , " she say , " and they will not   have the heart to fire shell at it . " 
 the dress of the lady of the present passing period have be largely affect by the recent peace , which allow the " french baby " as the milliner ' doll be call to come in as quickly as they be conceive . in war time score of these " doxy - dummy " as the rough tar call they be toss overboard from capture vessel or set up as a mark for tobacco - juice , while sweet eye in London weep for want of they . and even Mr. Cheeseman have fail to bring any type genuinely french from the wholesale house in St. Mary 's Axe , which be fame for canonical issue . but bless be the patient , if their patience last long enough . the lady of England be now in full enjoyment of all the new french discovery , which prove to be the right name , inasmuch as they banish all reputable form of cover . at least , so Mrs. Twemlow say ; and the Rector go far than she do , obtain for his sympathy a recommendation to attend to his own business . but when he show the Admiral his wife 's last book of pattern from a drawer which he have no right to go to great laughter be hold between the twain , with some glance over shoulder , and much dread of bad example . " whatever you do , do not   let my girl see it ; I will be bind you will not   let your eliza , " say the Admiral , after a pinch of snuff to restore the true balance of his principle ; " faith would pitch it straight into the fire ; but I be not quite so sure that my dolly would . she love a bit of finery , and she look well in it . " 
 " tonnish female , " as the magazine of fashion call the high class of popinjay , would have star with contempt at both Faith and Dolly Darling in their simple walking - dress that day . dowdy would have be the name for they , or frump , or fright , or country gawk , because their attire be not statuesque or classic , as it should have be , which mean that they be not half naked . 
 Faith , the eld sister , have mean to let young dolly take the course of her own stubbornness ; but no soon do she see she go forth alone than she throw on cloak and hat , and follow . the day be unsuited for classic apparel , as english day be apt to be , and a lady of fashion would have look more foolish , and even more indecent , than usual . a brisk and rather crisp east wind have arise , which have no respect for person , and even Faith and dolly in their high - neck country dress have to handle their tackle warily . 
 dolly have a good start , and grow much excited with the petulance of the wind and with her own audacity , cross the mouth of the brook at a very fine pace , with the easterly gust to second she . she could see the little mark - boat well out in the offing , with a red flag flaring merrily , defy all the effort of the gunner on the hill to plunge it into the bright dance of the wave . and now and then she hear what she know to be the rush of a round shoot far above her head , and follow the sound see a little silver fountain leap up into the sunshine and skim before the breeze ; then glance up the hill she see the gray puff drift , and presently feel the dull rumble of the air . at the root of the smoke - puff , once or twice , she descry a stocky figure move leisurely , and in spite of the distance and huddle of vapour could declare that it be Captain Stubbard . then a dense mass of smoke be bring down by an eddy of wind , and set her coughing . 
 " come away , come away this very moment , dolly , " cry Faith , who have hurry up and seize her hand ; " you be past the danger - post , and I meet a man back there who say they be go to fire shell , and they have get two short gun on purpose . he say it will be very dangerous till they get the range , and he beg I most earnestly not to come on here . if I be anybody else , he say , he would lay hand on I and hold I back . " 
 " some old fisherman , no doubt . what do they know about gun practice ? I can see Captain Stubbard up there ; he would rather shoot himself than I , he say yesterday . " 
 while dolly be repeat this assurance , the follow word be be exchange upon the smoky parapet : " if you please , sir , I can see two woman on the beach , half - way between the post a'most .
