" " can not   help it would not   stop for all the petticoat in the kingdom . if they choose to go there , they must take their chance . a bit more up , and to you , my good man . be you sure you put in twenty - three ? Steady ! so , so that be beautiful . " 
 " what a noisy thing ! what do it come here for ? I never see it fall . there must be some mistake . I hope there be nothing nasty inside it . run for your life , faith ; it mean to burst , I do believe . " 
 " down on your face ! " cry a loud , stern voice ; and dolly obey in an instant . but Faith stand calmly , and say to the man who rush past she , " I trust in the Lord , sir . " 
 there be no time to answer . the shell have leave off roll , and sputter more fiercely as the fuse thicken . the man lay hold of this , and try to pull it out , but could not , and jump with both foot on it ; while Faith , who quite expect to be blow to piece , say to herself , " what pretty boot he have ! " 
 " a fine bit of gunnery ! " say the young man , stoop over it , after tread the last spark into the springy sand . " the little artillery man be want here . lady , you may safely stay here now . they will not make two hit in proximity to each other . " 
 " you shall not go , " say Faith , as he be hurry away , " until we know who have be so reckless of his life , to save the life of other . both your hand be burn very seriously , I fear . " 
 " and your clothe , sir , " cry dolly , run up in hot terror , as soon as the danger be over ; " your clothe be spoil sadly . oh , how good it be of you ! and the whole fault be mine or at least Captain Stubbard 's . he will never dare to face I again , I should hope . " 
 " young lady , if I have be of any service to you , " say the stranger , with a smile at their excitement , " I beg you to be silent to the Captain Stubbard concern my share in this occasion . he would not be gratify by the interest I feel in his beautiful little bombardment , especially that of fair lady . ha , there go another shell ! they will make well aim now ; but you must not delay . I beseech you to hasten home , if you would do I kindness . " 
 the fair daughter of the Admiral have enjoy enough of warfare to last they till the end of their honeymoon , and they could not reject the entreaty of a man who have risk his life to save they . tremble and bewildered , they make off at the quick step permit by maiden dignity , with one or two kindly turn of neck , to show that he be mean to follow they . but another sulphurous cloud rush down from the indefatigable Stubbard , and when it have pass they , they look back vainly for the gentleman who have spoil his boot . 




 CHAPTER XX 

 among the lady 

 it would have surprise the stout Captain Stubbard , who think no small beer of his gunnery , to hear that it be hold in very light esteem by the " frenchified young man overhead , " as he call Caryl Carne , to his landlady . and it would have amaze he to learn that this young man be a captain of artillery , in the grand army muster across the sea , and one of the most able among plenty of ability , and favour by the great First Consul . 
 in the gully where the Tugwell boat be build , behind a fringe of rough longshore growth , young Carne have be sit with a good field - glass , observe the practice of the battery . he have also be able to observe unseen the disobedient practice of young lady , when their father be widely out of sight . upon Faith , however , no blame could fall , for she go against her wish , and only to retrieve the rebellious dolly . 
 secure from the danger , these two hold council in the comfort of the Admiral 's Round - house . there Miss Dolly , who consider it her domain , keep sundry snug appliance congenial to young lady , for remove all trace of sudden excitement , and make they fit to be see again . simple and unfashionable as they be in dress , they be sure to have something to do to themselves after the late derangement , ere ever they could run the risk of meet any of the brave young officer , who be so mysteriously fond of come for order to Springhaven Hall . 
 " you look well enough , dear , " say faith at last , " and much well than you deserve to look , after lead I such a dance by your self - will . but one thing must be settle before we go back be we to speak of this matter , or not ? " 
 " how can you ask such a question , faith ? " Miss dolly love a bit of secrecy . " of course we must rather bite our tongue out , than break the solemn pledge which we have give . " she have cry a good deal , and she begin to cry again . 
 " do not   cry , that be a darling , " say the simple - hearted sister . " you make the whole world seem so cruel when you cry , because you look so innocent . it shall be as you please , if I can only think it right . but I can not see how we give a pledge of any sort , consider that we run away without speak . the question be have we any right to conceal it , when father have a right to know everything ? " 
 " he would be in such a sad passion , " plead dolly , with a stock of fresh tear only wait , " and he never would look again at poor Captain Stubbard , and what would become of all his family ? " 
 " Father be a just and conscientious man , " reply the daughter who inherit those quality ; " he would not blame Captain Stubbard ; he would blame we , and no other . " 
 " oh , I could not bear to hear you blame , Faith . I should have to say that it be all my fault . and then how I should catch it , and be punish for a month ! confine to the ground for a month at least , and never have a bit of appetite . but I be not think of myself , I be quite sure of that . you know that I never do that much . I be think of that heroic gentleman , who stamp out the spark so cleverly . all the time I lay on the sand I watch he , though I expect to be blow to piece every single moment . oh ! what a nasty sensation it be ! I expect to find all my hair turn grey . but , thank Heaven , I do not   see a streak in it ! " to make sure of that , she go to the glass again . 
 " if all mine have turn grey , ' twould be no odd to nobody as Captain Zeb say about his income because I be intend for an old maid . " Miss Darling , whose beauty still lack many year of its prime , turn away for a moment , because her eye be glisten , and her sister be tired of the subject . " but for yours there be fifty to weep , dolly . especially perhaps this young gentleman , towards whom you feel so much gratitude . " 
 " how unkind you be , Faith ! all the gratitude I owe he be for save your life . as for myself , I be flat upon the sand , with a heap of sea - weed between I and the thing . if it have go off , it would have go over I ; but you choose to stand up , like a stupid . your life be save , beyond all doubt , by he ; and the way you acknowledge it be to go and tell his chief enemy that he be there observe he ! " 
 " well , I never ! " Faith exclaim , with more vigour than grace of language . " a minute ago you know nothing of he , and even wonder who he be , and now you know all about his enemy ! I be afraid that you stick at nothing . " 
 " I do not   stick thinking , as you do , Miss , " dolly answer , without abashment , and know that the elder hat to be so address ; " but thing come to I by the light of nature , without a twelvemonth of brown - study . when I say what you remind I of , in such a hurry , it be perfectly true so true that you need have no trouble about it , with all your truth . but since that , a sudden idea flash across I , the sort of idea that prove itself . your hero you be in such a hurry to betray can be nobody but the mysterious lodger in Widow Shanks ' dimity - parlour , as she call it ; and Jenny have tell I all she know about he , which be a great deal less than she ought to know . I mean to have tell you , but you be so grand in your lofty contempt of what you call gossip , but which I call good neighbourly intercourse ! you know that he be Mr. Caryl Carne , of course . everybody know that , and there the knowledge seem to terminate . even the Twemlows , his own aunt and uncle , be scarcely ever favour with his company ; and I , who be always on the beach , or in the village , have never have the honour of behold he , until until it come to this " here she imitate with her lip the spluttering of the fuse so well that her sister could not keep from laugh . " he never go out , and he never ask question , any more than he answer they , and he never care to hear what fish they have catch , or anything else , about anybody . he never eat or drink , and he never say a word about the flower they put upon his table ; and what he do all day long nobody know , except that he have a lot of book with he . Widow Shanks , who have the good right to know all about he , have make up her mind that his head have be turn by the trouble of his family , except for his go without dinner , which no lunatic ever do , accord to her knowledge . and he seem to have get ' Butter Cheeseman , ' as they call he , entirely at his beck and call . he leave his black horse there every morning , and rid home at night to his ancestral ruin . there , now , you know as much as I do . " 
 " there be mischief at the bottom of all this , " say Faith ; " in these dangerous time , it must not be neglect . we be bind , as you say , to consider his wish , after all that he have do for we . but the tale about we will be over the place in a few hour , at the late . the gunner will have know where their bad shot fall , and perhaps they will have see we with their glass . how will it be possible to keep this affair from gossip ? " 
 " they may have see we , without see he at all , on account of the smoke that come afterwards . at any rate , let we say nothing about it until we hear what other people say . the shell will be wash away or bury in the sand , for it fall upon the shingle , and then roll towards the sea ; and there need be no fuss unless we choose to make it , and so perhaps ruin Captain Stubbard and his family . and his wife have make such pretty thing for we . if he know what he have do , he would go and shoot himself . he be so excessively humane and kind . " 
 " we will not urge his humanity to that extreme . I hate all mystery , as you know well . but about this affair I will say nothing , unless there be cause to do so , at least until father come back ; and then I shall tell he if it seem to be my duty . " 
 " it will not   be your duty , it can not   be your duty , to get good people into trouble , Faith . I find it my duty to keep out of trouble , and I like to treat other the same as myself . " 
 " you be such a lover of duty , dear dolly , because everything you like become your duty . and now your next duty be to your dinner . Mrs. Twemlow be come I forgot to tell you as well as Eliza , and Mrs. Stubbard . and if Johnny come home in time from Harrow , to be Jack among the lady , we shall hear some wonder , you may be quite sure . " 
 " oh , I vow , I forget all about that wicked Johnny . what a blessing that he be not here just now ! it be my black Monday when his holiday begin . instead of get steady , he grow more plaguesome . and the wonder of it be that he would tie your kid shoe ; while he pull out my jaconet , and sit on my french hat . how I wish he be old enough for his commission ! to - morrow he will be dance in and out of every cottage , boat , or gun , or rabbit - hole , and nothing shall be hide from his eye and ear . let he come . ' I be accustomed to have all thing go awry , ' as somebody say in some tragedy . the only chance be to make he fall in love , deeply in love , with Miss Stubbard . he do it with somebody for his Easter week , and become as harmless as a sucking dove , till he find his nymph eat onion raw with a pocketful of boil limpet . Maggie Stubbard be too perfect in her style for that . she be twelve year old , and have lot of hair , and eye as large as oyster . I shall introduce Johnny to - morrow , and hope to keep he melancholy all his holiday . " 
 " perhaps it will be for his good , " say Faith , " because , without some high idea , he get into such dreadful scrape ; and certainly it will be for our good . " 
 after make light of young love thus , these girl deserve the shaft of Cupid , in addition to Captain Stubbard 's shell . and it would have be hard to find fair mark when they come down dress for dinner . Mrs. Twemlow arrive with her daughter Eliza , but without her husband , who be to fetch she in the evening ; and Mrs. Stubbard come quite alone , for her walkable child as she call they be all up at the battery . " can not   smell powder too young in such day as these , " be the Captain 's utterance ; and , sure enough , they take to it , like son of gun . 
 " I should be so frightened , " Mrs. Twemlow say , when Johnny   who sit at the foot of the table represent his father most gallantly   have say grace in Latin , to astonish their weak mind , ' so nervous all the time , so excessively anxious , the whole time that dreadful din be proceed ! it be over now , thank goodness ! but how can you have endure it , how can you have go about your household duty calmly , with seven of your child I think you say go about in that fiery furnace ? " 
 " because , ma'am , " reply Mrs. Stubbard , who be dry of speech , and fit mother of hero , " the cannon be so make , if you can understand , that they do not shoot out of their back end . " 
 " we be quite aware of that " Miss Twemlow come to her mother 's relief very sharply " but still they be apt to burst , or to be overload , or badly direct , or even to fly back suddenly , as I have hear on good authority . " 
 " very likely , miss , when they be command by young woman . " 
 Eliza Twemlow colour , for she be rather quick of temper ; but she do not condescend to pay rudeness in kind . 
 " it would hardly be a lady - like position , I suppose , " she answer , with a curve of her graceful neck the Carnes have be celebrate for their neck , which be long than those of the darling ; " but even under the command of a most skilful man , for instance Captain Stubbard , little accident will happen , like the fall of a shell upon the beach this afternoon . some people be close to it , accord to the rumour ; but luckily it do not explode . " 
 " how providential ! " cry Mrs. Twemlow ; " but the stupid people would have go without much pity , whatever have befall they , unless they be blind , or too ignorant to read . do not   you think so , Faith , my dear ? " 
 " I do not   believe a single word of that story , " Mrs. Stubbard cut short the question ; " for the simple reason that it never could have happen . my husband be to direct every gun himself . be it likely he would have shell the beach ? " 
 " well , the beach be the proper place for shell ; but if I have only know it , would not   I have come a few hour early ? " say Johnny . " even now there must be something leave to see ; and I be bind to understand that sort of thing . lady , I entreat you not to think I rude , if I go as soon as ever you can do without I . I think I have get you nearly everything you want ; and perhaps you would rather be without I . " 
 with many thank and compliment such a pretty boy he be the lady release he gladly ; and then Mrs. Twemlow , have reason of her own , draw nigh to Mrs. Stubbard with lively interest in her child . at first , she receive short answer only ; for the Captain 's wife have draw more sour juice than sweet us from adversity . but the wife of the man of peace outflank the well half of the man of war , drive in her outpost , and secure the key of all her communication . 
 " I can scarcely believe that you be so kind . my dear Mrs. Twemlow , how good you be ! my Bob be a nice boy , so manly and clever , so gentle and well - behave , even when he know that I be not likely to find he out . but that you should have notice it , be what surprise I so few people now know the difference ! but in the House of God as you so well observe you can very soon see what a boy be . when I tell he that he may ride your grey pony , I wish you could be there to watch the fine expression of his face . how he do love dumb animal ! it be only last Saturday , he knock down a boy nearly three time his own size for poke a pin into a poor donkey with the fish . and Maggie to have a flower - bed on your front lawn ! they will not   let she touch a plant , at our cottage , though she understand garden so thoroughly . she will not   sleep a wink to - night , if I tell she , and I have well keep that for the morning . poor child ! they have have a hard time of it ; but they have come out like pure gold from the fire I mean as many of they as can use their leg . but to be on horseback what will Bob say ? " 
 " you must have meet with very little kindness , Mrs. Stubbard , to attach any importance to such mere trifle . it make I blush to think that there can be a spot in England where such child as yours could pass unnoticed . it be not a question of religious feeling only . far from it ; in fact , quite the opposite ; though my husband , of course , be quite right in insist that all our opinion and action must be refer to that one standard . but I look at thing also from a motherly point of view , because I have suffer such sad trial . three dear one in the churchyard , and the dear of all the Almighty only know where he be . sometimes it be more than I can bear , to live on in this dark and most dreadful uncertainty . my medical man have forbid I to speak of it . but how can he know what it be to be a mother ? but hush ! or darling Faith may hear I . sometimes I lose all self - command . " 
 Mrs. Twemlow 's eye be in need of wiping , and stout Mrs. Stubbard 's in the same condition . " how I wish I could help you , " say the latter , softly : " be there anything in the world that I can do ? " 
 " no , my dear friend ; I wish there be , for I be sure that it would be a pleasure to you . but another anxiety , though far less painful , be worry I as well just now . my poor brother 's son be behave most strangely . he hardly ever come near we , and he seem to dislike my dear husband . he have take room over your brave husband 's Office , and he come and go very mysteriously . it be my duty to know something about this ; but I dare not ask Captain Stubbard . " 
 " my dear Mrs. Twemlow , it have puzzle I too . but think that you know all about it , I conclude that everything must be quite right . what you tell I have surprise I more than I can tell . I shall go to work quietly to find out all about it . mystery and secrecy be such hateful thing ; and a woman be always the good hand at either . " 




 CHAPTER XXI 

 a gracious mercy 

 as a matter of course , every gunner at the fort be ready to make oath by every colour of the rainbow , that never shoot , shell , wad , sponge , or even powder - flake could by any possibility have fall on the beach . and before they have time to grow much more than doubly positive that be to say , within three day ' time the sound of gun fire in earnest drown all question of bad practice . 
 for the following Sunday beheld Springhaven in a state of excitement beyond the memory of the very old inhabitant , or the imagination of the young . Excitement be a crop that , to be large , must grow though it thrive all the well without much root and in this particular field it begin to grow before noon of Saturday . for the man who be too old to go to sea , and the boy who be too young , and the woman who be never of the proper age , all these keep look from the good lookout , but nothing could they see to enable they to say when the kettle , or the frying - pan , or gridiron , would be want . they rub their eye grievously , and spin round three time , if time have bring or leave they the power so to spin ; and they pull an irish halfpenny , with the harp on , from their pocket , and moisten it with saliva which in English mean spat on it and then throw it into the pocket on the other side of body . but none of these accredit appeal to heaven put a speck upon the sea where the boat ought to have be , or cast upon the cloud a shade of any sail approach . uneasily wonder , the granny , wife , and little one go home , when the nightfall quench all eyesight , and tell one another ancient tale of woe . 
 yet there be a salve for every sore , a bung for every bunghole . upon the Sunday morning , when the tide be come in , and a golden haze hang upon the peaceful sea , and the seven bell of the old grey church be speak of the service cheerfully , suddenly a deep boom move the bosom of distance , and palpitate all along the shore . six or seven hale old gaffer   not too stiff to walk , with the help of a staff , a little far than the rest   be come to hear parson by the path below the warren , where a smack of salt would season they for doctrine . they know from long experience , the grandmother of science , that the mist of the sea , come on at breakfast - time , in the month of August   with the wind where it be and the tide as they be , would be sure to hold fast until dinner - time . else , good as they be , and prepare punctually once a week for a well world , the hind button of their Sunday coat would have be towards the church , and the front one to the headland . for the body of their son be dearer to they , substantially dearer , than their own old soul . 
 they be all begin to be deaf , or rather go on with it very agreeably , lose thereby a great deal of disturbance , and gain great room for reflection . and now when the sound of a gun from the sea hang shake in the web of vapour , each of these wise man gaze steadfastly at the rest , to see his own conclusion reflect , or conclude . a gun it be indeed a big well - shot gun , and no deafness could throw any doubt on it . there might not be anything to see , but still there would be plenty to hear at the headland a sound more arouse than the parson 's voice , a roar beyond that of all the gallery . " ' tis a battle ! " say one , and his neighbour cry , " a rare one ! " they turn to the parish church the quarter of farewell , and those of salutation to the battle out at sea . 
 it be all over the village , in the time it take to put a hat on , that the british and the french fleet be hammer and tong at it , within the distance you may throw an apple off Springhaven headland . 
 even the young woman know that this be quite impossible , because there be no water there for a colly - brig to anchor ; nevertheless , in the hurry and scare , the thought of that new battery and Lord Nelson , and above all in the fog , they believe it . so that there be scarcely any room to stand , at the Watch - point , inside the shag - rock ; while in church there be no one who could help be there , by force of holy office , or example . 
 these latter be not in a devout frame of mind , and   but for the look of it   would have do more good by join the other congregation . for the sound of cannon - shot come into their ear , like ball of unadulterated pepper , and every report make they look at one another , and whisper " ah ! there go some poor fellow 's head . " for the sacred building be construct so that the sound outside of it have more power than the good thing offer in the inside . 
 however , as many , or as few , as do their duty , by join the good company of the minister , find themselves all the well for it , and more fresh for a start than the runagate . inasmuch as these latter have nearly get enough of listen without see anything , while the steady church - goer have refresh the entire system by look about without listen . and to show the truant people where their duty should have bind they , the haze have be thicken all over the sea , while the sun keep the time on the old church dial . this be speak of for many year , throughout the village , as a Scriptural token of the proper thing to do . 
 " well , and what have ' e see ? " ask the senior church - warden not Cheeseman , who be only the junior , and have neither be at church nor on the headland but Farmer Graves , the tenant of the Glebe and of up - farm , the Admiral 's good holding ; " what have ' e see , good people all , to leave parson to prache to hisself a'most a sarmon as he be hathn't   prache for five year , to my knowledge ? have ' e see fat bull of Basan ? " 
 " Naw ; but we have heer'd un roar , " reply one who be sure to say something . " Wust of it be , there be no making out what language un do roar in . " 
 " one Englishman , I tell ' e , and two frenchman , " say an ancient tar who have serve under Keppel ; " by the ring of the gun I could swear to that much . and they load they so different , that they do . " 
 before the other have well finish laugh at he , it become his turn to laugh at they . the wind be in the east , and the weather set fair , and but for the sea - mist the power of the sun would have be enough to dazzle all beholder . already this vapour be begin to clear off , coil up in fleecy wisp above the glistening water , but cling still to any bluff or cliff it could lay hold on . 
 " Halloa , Jem ! where be go of now ? " shout one or two voice from the Oar - stone point , the furth outlook of the Havenhead hill . 
 " to see they frenchy hopper get a jolly hiding , " Jem Prater reply , without ease his scull . he be John Prater 's nephew , of the " Darling Arms , " and have stop behind the fishing to see his uncle 's monthly beer in . " you can not   see up there , I reckon , the same as I do here . one english ship have get a job to tackle two crappos . but , by George ! she will do it , mate . good bye , and the Lord defend you ! " 
 he have nobody but his little brother Sam , who be hold the tiller , to help he , and his uncle 's boat   which he have take without leave   be neither stout nor handy . but the stir of the battle have fetch he forth , and he mean to see the whole of it without take harm . every Englishman have a full right to do this , in a case of such french audacity , and the English sea and air begin to give he fair occasion . for now the sun have sweep the mist with a besom of gold wire , widen every sweep , and throw brilliant prospect down it . the gentle heave of the sea flash forth with the white bird hover over it , and the curdle of fugitive vapour glow like pillar of fire as they float off . then out of the drift appear three ship , partly shroud in their own fog . 
 the wind be too light for manoeuvre much , and the combatant swing to their broadside , have take the breath of the air away by the fury of their fire . all three be stand to the north - north - west , under easy sail , and on the starboard tack , but scarcely hold steerage - way , and take little heed of it . close quarter , close and close still , muzzle to muzzle , and beard to beard , clinched tooth , and hard pounding , be the order of the day , with the crash of shattered timber and the cry of die man . and still the ship come onward , forget where they be , heave too much iron to have think of heaving lead , ready to be shipwreck , if they could but wreck the enemy . 
 between the bulky curl of smoke could be see the scar of furious battle , splinter mast and shiver yard , tattere sail and yawn bulwark , and great gap even of the solid side ; and above the ruck of smoke appear the tricolor flag upon the right hand and the left , and the Union - jack in the middle . 
 " She've   a'got more than she can do , I reckon , " say an old man famous in the lobster line ; " other a one of they be as big as she be , and two to one seemeth onfair odd . wish she well out of it that be all as can be do . " 
 " Kelks , you be a fool , " reply the ancient navyman , steady his spy - glass upon a ledge of rock . " in my time we make very little of that ; and the breed may be slack off a little , but not quite so bad as that would be . ah ! you should a ' hear what old Keppel on the twenty - seventh day of July it be , in the year of our Lord 1778 . talk about Nelson ! to my mind old Keppel could have box his compass backward . not but what these man know how to fight quite as well as need be nowadays . why , if I be aboard of that there frigate , I could not   do much more than she have do . she will have one of they , you see if she do not , though she look to have the bad of it , till you come to understand . the Leader her name be , of thirty - eight gun , and she will lead one of they into Portsmouth , to refit . " 
 it be hard to understand the matter , in its present aspect , at all as the ancient sailor do ; for the fire of the Leda cease suddenly , and she fall behind the other , as if hamper with her canvas . a thrill of pain run through all the gaze Britons . 
 " how now , old Navy - Mike ? " cry the lobster man . " strike be the word , and no mistake . and small blame to she either . she hathn't   get a sound thread to draw , I do believe . who be the fool now , Mike ? though vex I be to ask it . " 
 " wait a bit , old lobster - pot . ah , there now , she breeze ! whistle for a wind , lad , whistle , whistle . sure as I be a sinner , yes ! she be lay her course to board the Frenchman on the weather quarter . with a slant of wind she will do it , too , if it only hold two minute . whistle on your nail , my boy , for the glory of old England . " 
 in reply to their shrill appeal for even the woman try to whistle or perhaps in compulsory sequence of the sun , the wind freshen briskly from the sunny side of east . the tattered sail of the brave ship fill , with the light fall through they upon one another , the head swing round at the command of helm , the pennon fly gaily and the ensign flap , and she bear down smoothly on the outer and therefore unwounde side of the enemy . 
 " that be what I call judgmatical , " old Mike shout , with a voice that rival cannon ; " whoever think of that deserve three epulet , one on each shoulder and one upon his head . doubt if old Keppel would have think of that , now . you see , mate , the other Crappo can not   fire at she without first hit of her own consort . and well than that ever so much well the tilt of the charge will throw she over on her wound . Master Muncher hath two great hole ' twixt wind and water on his larboard side , and will not   they suck the briny , with the weight of our bow upon the starboard beam ? ' twill take fifty hand to stop leak , instead of stop boarder . " 
 the smoke be drift off , and the sun shine bravely . the battle have be glide toward the foot of the spectator ; and now from the height of the cliff they could descry the deck , the gun , the coil of rope , the turmoil , and dark rush of man to their fate . small fight , man to man , demand still the power of a telescope , and distance make the trenchant arm of hero , work right and left , appear like the nipper of an earwig . the only thing certain be that man be be kill , and glory be be manufacture largely . 
 " She've   a do it , she've   a do it rarely . there be not a d d froggy leave to go to heaven ; or if there be so he be a ' batten down below , " old Mike shout , flourish his spy - glass , which rattle in its joint as much as he do ; " down come the blood , froth , and blue blaze , as they call the republican emrod , and up go the Union - jack , my heartie . three cheer ! three cheer ! again ! again ! again ! " 
 from the sea far below , and far away , come also the volume of a noble english shout , as the flag begin to flutter in the quickening breeze , and the sea arise and dance with sunshine . no one , who have get all his blood leave in he , could think of anything but glory . 
 " my certy , they have well mind their sounding , though ! " say the old navy - man , with a stitch in his side and a lump in his throat , from loud utterance ; " five fathom be every inch of it where they be now , and the tide make strong , and precious little wind to claw off with . Jem Prater ! Jem Prater ! oar up , and give signal . ah , he be too far off to do any good . in five minute more they will be on the White Pig , where no ship ever get off again . oh , thank the Lord , mate , thank the Lord , for his mercy endureth forever ! the other froggy be stick hard and fast , and our lad will just fetch out in time . " 
 Old Navy - Mike have make no mistake . the consort of the capture frigate , a corvette of twenty - four gun , have boldly stand on with the intention of round to the wind , cross the bow of the other twain , and retrieve the fortune of the day perhaps , by a broadside into the shattered upper work of the terribly hamper british ship . the idea be clever and spirited , and have a very fair chance of success ; but the land below the sea forefend it . full of fine ardour and the noble thirst for fame , speed on for the palm of high enterprise and the glory of the native land , alas , they stick fast in a soft bit of english sand ! it be in their power now to swear by all they disbelieve in , and in everything visible and too tangible ; but their power be limit strictly to that ; and the fast they swear , the fast they be bind to stick . 
 Springhaven din well , with its enemy so place , and a message from the Leda by Jem Prater , that the fishing fleet be rescue , and would be home to early supper , and so much to be talk about all dinner - time , that for once in his life nearly everybody find it more expedient to eat with his fork than his knife . then all who could be spar from wash up , and get ready for further cookery , go duly to church in the afternoon , to hear the good rector return humble thank for a gracious Mercy to the british arm , and to see a young man , who have land with despatch , put a face full of gunpowder in at window , to learn whether Admiral Darling be there . 




 CHAPTER XXII 

 a SPECIAL urgency 

 Admiral Darling be not in church . his duty to his country keep he up the hill , and in close consultation with Captain Stubbard , who be burn to fire his battery . 
 " I never know such bad luck in all my life . the devil have be appoint First Lord of the weather ever since I come to Springhaven . " as Stubbard declare these great truth he stride about in his little fortress , deliver a kick at the heel of thing which have no right to be lumber there . " to think that I should never have see those beggar , when but for the fog I could have smash they right and leave . admiral , these thing make a Christian an infidel . " 
 " Nonsense , sir ! " say the Admiral , sternly , for a man of his kind nature ; " you forget that without the fog , or rather the mist for it be only that those fellow would never have come within range . we have very great blessing to be thankful for , though the credit fall not to our battery . the Frenchmen fight wonderfully well , as well as the good Englishman could have do , and to capture they both be a miracle of luck , if indeed we can manage to secure they . my friend , young Honyman , of the Leda , have prove himself just what I say he would be ; and have perform a very gallant exploit , though I fear he be severely wound . but we shall know more now , for I see a young fellow jump up the hill , like a kangaroo , and probably he come for order . one thing we have learn , Stubbard , and must take the hint to - morrow put a hut on the Haven head , and keep a watchman there . why , bless my heart , it be Blyth Scudamore that be come ! there be nobody else that can skip like that . " 
 the young lieutenant enter between two gun the gunner be dismiss in great disgust to dinner with his pleasant face still a little grime with gunpowder , and flush by his hurry up the steep hill - side . 
 " this for you , sir , " he say , salute the Admiral , present his letter , and then draw back ; " and I be to wait your convenience for reply . " 
 " what next will the service come to , " ask the Admiral of Captain Stubbard , " when a young man just commission give himself such mighty air ? shake hand , Blyth , and promise you will come and dine with we , unless you be order to return on board at once . how be your good captain ? I know he when he wear Nankins . Jem Prater bring word that he be wound . I hope it be not serious . " 
 " no , sir ; not much to speak of . he have only lose three finger . that be why I write this letter or report , I ought to call it , if anybody else have write it . oh , sir ! I can not bear to think of it ! I be fifth luff when the fight begin , and now there be only one leave above I , and he be in command of our big prize , the Ville d'Anvers . but , Admiral , here you will find it all , as I write it , from the lip , when they tie up the finger , of Captain Honyman . " 
 " how could you tie they up when they be go ? " Captain Stubbard enquire , with a sneer at such a youth . he have get on very slowly in his early day , and could not bear to see a young man with such vacancy before he . " why , you be the lucky lad I ever see ! sure to go up at least three step . how well you must have keep out of it ! and how happy you must feel , Lieutenant Scudamore ! " 
 " I be not at all happy at lose dear friend , " the young man answer , gently , as he turn away and pat the breech of a gun , upon which there be a little rust next day ; " that feeling come later in life , I suppose . " 
 the Admiral be not attend to they now , but absorb in the brief account of the conflict , begin by Captain Honyman in his own handwriting , and finish by his voice , but not his pen . any one desirous to read this may do so in the proper place . for the present purpose it be enough to say that the modesty of the language be scarcely surpass by the brilliancy of the exploit . and if anything be need to commend the writer to the deep good will of the reader , it be find in the fact that this enterprise spring from warm zeal for the commerce of Springhaven . the Leda have be order on Friday last to protect the peaceful little fishing fleet from a crafty design for their capture , and this she have do with good effect , have justice on her side , and fortune . the particular of the combat be not so clear , after the captain 's three finger be go ; but if one make proper allowance for that , there be not very much to complain of . the Admiral consider it a very good report ; and then put on his spectacle , and think it still well . 
 " why ! why ! why ! " he say for without affectation many officer have catch the style of his then gracious Majesty " what be this ? what be this ? something on the other side , in a different man 's handwriting , and mighty difficult to read , in my opinion . Stubbard , do you ever see such a scrawl ? make it out for I . you have good eye , like a hawk , or the man who see through a milestone . Scudamore , what be his name ? you know . " 
 " three finger at five pound apiece per annum as long as he live ! " Captain Stubbard compute on his own : " fifteen pound a year perhaps for forty year , as you seem to say how young he be ; that come to just 600 pound , and his hand as good as ever "   " I will be hang if it be , if he write this ! " the Admiral interject   " and well , I must say , from a selfish point of view , because of only two nail leave to clean , and his other hand increase in value ; why , the scale be disgraceful , iniquitous , boobyish , and make without any knowledge of the human frame , and the comparative value of its member . Lieutenant Scudamore , look at I . here you see I without an ear , damage in the fore - hatch , and with the larboard bow stove in and how much do I get , though so much old ? " 
 " well , if you will not   help I , Stubbard , " say the Admiral , who know how long his friend would carry on upon that tack , " I must even get Scudamore to read it , though it seem to have be write on purpose to elude he . Blyth , my dear boy , can you explain it ? " 
 " it be it be only something , sir " the lieutenant blush , and hesitate , and look away unmanfully " which I ask Captain Honyman to leave out , because because it have nothing to do with it . I mean , because it be of no importance , even if he happen to have that opinion . his hand be tie up so , that I do not like to say too much , and I think that he would go to sleep , because the doctor have make he drink a poppy head boil down with pigtail . but it seem as if he have get up after that for he always will have his own way while I be go to put this coat on ; and perhaps he write that with his left hand , sir . but it be no part of the business . " 
 " then we will leave it , " say Admiral Darling , " for young eye than mine to read . Nelson write well with his left hand than ever he do with his right , to my thinking , the very first time that he try it . but we can not   expect everybody to do that . there be no sign of any change of weather , be there , Stubbard ? my order will depend very much upon that . I must go home and look at the quicksilver before I know what be good to do . you have well come with I , Scudamore . " 
 Admiral Darling be quite right in this . everything depend upon the weather ; and although the rough autumn be not come yet , the prime of the hopeful year be past . the summer have not be a grand one , such as we get about once in a decade , but of loose and uncertain character , such as an Englishman have to make the good of . it might be take up for a golden autumn , ripen corn , and fruit , and tree , or it might break up into shower and temp , sodden earth , and welter sky . 
 " your captain refer to I for order , " say Admiral Darling to Scudamore , while they be hasten to the Hall , " as Commander of the Coast Defence , because he have be bring too far inshore , and one of the Frenchmen be strand . the frigate you board and carry be the Ville d'Anvers , of forty gun . the corvette that take the ground , so luckily for you , when half of your hand be aboard the prize , be the blonde , teak - build , and only launch last year . we must try to have she , whatever happen . she will not   hurt where she be , unless it come on to blow . our sand hold fast without nipping , as you know , like a well - breed sheep - dog , and the White Pig be the tough of all of they . she may stay there till the equinox , without much mischief , if the present light air continue . but the bad job will be with the prisoner ; they be the plague of all these affair , and we can not   imitate Boney by poison they . on the whole , it have well not have happen , perhaps . though you must not tell Honyman that I say so . it be a very gallant action , very skilful , very beautiful ; and I hope he will get a fine lift for it ; and you too , my dear Blyth , for you must have fight well . " 
 " but , Admiral , surely you would have be grieve if so many of your tenant , and their boat as well , have be sweep away into a french harbour . what would Springhaven be without its Captain Zebedee ? " 
 " you be right , Blyth ; I forget that for the moment . there would have be weep and wail indeed , even in our own household . but they could not have keep they long , though the loss of their boat would have be most terrible . but I can not make out why the French should have want to catch a few harmless fishing - smack . Aquila non captat muscas , as you teach the boy at Stonnington . and two ship despatch upon a paltry job of that sort ! either Captain Honyman be strangely misinform , or there be something in the background , entirely beyond our knowledge . pay attention to this matter , and let I know what you hear of it as a friend , Blyth , as a friend , I mean . but here we be ! you must want feed . Mrs. Cloam will take care of you , and find all that be needful for a warrior 's cleanup . I must look at the barometer , and consider my despatch . let we have dinner , Mrs. Cloam , in twenty minute , if possible . for we stand in real need of it . " 
 concern that there could be no doubt . glory , as all english officer know , be no durable stay for the stomach . the urgency of mankind for victual may roughly be gauge by the length of the jaw . Captain Stubbard have jaw of tremendous length , and always carry a bag of captain 's biscuit , to which he be oblige to have recourse in the height of the hot engagement . Scudamore have short jaw , well set up , and powerful , without rapacity . but even these , after twelve hour of fasting , demand something well than gunpowder . he could not help think that his host be regard the condition of affair very calmly , until he remember that the day be Sunday , when no Briton have any call to be disturb by any but sacred insistency . at any rate , he be under order now , and those order be entirely to his liking . so he freshen up his cheerful and simple - minded face , put his sailor - knot neckcloth askew , as usual , and with some trepidation go down to dinner . 
 the young lady would not have be young woman if they have not receive he warmly . Kind Faith , who love he as a sister might for she have long discover his good quality have tear in her beautiful eye , as she give he both hand , and smile sweetly at his bashfulness . and even the critical dolly , who look so sharply at the outside of everything , allow her fair hand to stay well in his , and say something which be melody to he . then Johnny , who be of a warlike cast , and hop soon to destroy the french nation , shake hand with this public benefactor already employ in that great work . 
 " I shall scarcely have time for a bit of dinner , " say Admiral Darling , as they sit down . " I have send word to have the Protector launch , and to give little Billy a feed of corn . all you young people may take your leisure . youth be the time that command time and space . but for my part , if I can only manage this plate of soup , and a slice of that fish , and then one help of mutton , and just an apple - fritter , or some trifle of that sort , I shall be quite as lucky as I can hope to be . Duty perpetually spoil my dinner , and I must get some clever fellow to invent a plate that will keep as hot as duty be in these volcanic time . but I never complain ; I be so used to it . eat your dinner , child , and do not   think of mine . " 
 have scarcely afford himself an hour , the Admiral , in full uniform , embark upon little Billy , a gentle - minded pony from the west country , who conduct his own digestion while he consult that of his rider . at the haven they find the Protector ready , a ten - oare galley man by Captain Stubbard 's man , good sample of Sea - Fencibles . and the Captain himself be there , to take the tiller , and do any fighting if the chance should arise , for he have be disappoint all the morning . the boat which bring Scudamore have be recall by signal from the Leda , and that active young officer have seek she vainly , and thereby miss the Protector , follow steadily in Mr. Prater 's boat , with the nephew , Jem , pull the other oar , and Johnny Darling , who rag at the thought of be leave behind , steer vaguely . and just as they round the harbour - head , the long glassy sweep of the palpitating sea bore inward and homeward the peaceful squadron , so wistfully watch for and so dearly welcome . 




 CHAPTER XXIII 

 YOH - HEAVE - OH ! 

 " her condition be very bad , as bad as could be , without go straight to the bottom , " the Admiral say to the Rector that night , as they smoke a pipe together ; " and to the bottom she must have go , if the sea have get up , before we thrum she . Honyman want to have she bring inside the Head ; but even if we could have get there , she would ground at low water and fill with the tide . and what could we do with all those prisoner ? with our fresh hand at the pump , we very soon fetch the water out of she , and make she as tight as we could ; and I think they will manage to take she to Portsmouth . she have beautiful line . I never see a smart ship . how she come to the wind , with all that water in she ! the wind be all right for Portsmouth , and she will be a fine addition to the Navy . " 
 " but what be become of the other vessel , craft , corvette , or whatever you call she ? you say that she be scarcely hurt at all . and if she get off the White Pig 's back in the night , she may come up and bombard we . not that I be afraid ; but my wife be nervous , and the Rectory face the sea so much . if you have order away the Leda , which seem to have conquer both of they , the least you can do be to keep Captain Stubbard under arm all night in his battery . " 
 " I have a great mind to do so ; it would be a good idea , for he be very much inclined to cut up rough to - day . but he never would forgive I , he be such a hog at hammock as we use to say , until we grow too elegant . and he know that the Blonde have haul down her colour , and Scudamore be now prize - captain . I have send away most of her crew in the Leda , and I be not at all sure that we ought not to blow she up . in the end , we shall have to do so , no doubt ; for nothing large than a smack have ever get off that sand , and float . but let our young friend try ; let he have a fair trial . he have the stuff of a very fine seaman in he . and if he should succeed , it would be score with a long leg for he . Halloa ! why , I think the girl be fast asleep long ago ! " 
 " as if we could sleep , papa , with this upon our mind ! " dolly wave an open letter in the air , and then present it . " perhaps Faith might , but I be sure I never could . you defy we to make out this , which be on the other leaf ; and then , without give we fair play , you take it to the desk in your Oak - room , and there you leave it . well , I take the liberty of go there for it , for there can not   be any secret about a thing that will be print ; and how be they to print it , if they can not   contrive to read it ? how much will you pay I for interpreting , papa ? Mr. Twemlow , I think I ought to have a guinea . can you read it , now , with all your learning , and knowledge of dead language ? " 
 " my dear , it be not my duty to read it , and not at all my business . it seem to be write with the end of a stick , by a boy who be learn his letter . if you can interpret it , you must be almost a Daniel . " 
 " do you hear that , papa , you who think I be so stupid ? faith give it up ; she have no perseverance , or perhaps no curiosity . and I be very nearly beat too , till a very fine idea come into my head , and I have make out every word except three , and perhaps even those three , if Captain Honyman be not very particular in his spelling . can you tell I anything about that , papa ? " 
 " yes , dolly , just what you have hear from I before . Honyman be a good officer ; a very good one , as he have just prove . no good officer ever spell well , whether in the army or the navy . look at Nelson 's letter . I be inclined to ascribe my own slow promotion to the unnatural accuracy of my spelling , which offend my lord , because it puzzle they . " 
 " then all be straight sailing , as you say , papa . but I must tell you first how I find it out , or perhaps you will not   believe I . I know that Captain Honyman write this postscript , or whatever it be , with his left hand , so I take a pen in my own left hand , and practise all the letter , and the way they join , which be quite different from the other hand . and here be the copy of the word , as my left hand teach my right to put they down , after ink ever so many finger : 
 " ' we never could have do it without Scudamore . he jump a most wonderful jump from our jib - boom into her mizzen chain , when our grapple have slip , and we could get no nearer , and there he make fast , though the enemy come at he with cutlass , pike , and musket . by this mean we borde and carry the ship , with a loss as above report . when I grow faint from a trifling wound , Luff Scudamore lead the border with a cool courage that discomfit the fo . ' " 
 " Robert Honyman all over ! " cry the Admiral , with delight . " I could swear that he write it , if it be write with his toe . ' twas an old joke against he , when he be lieutenant , that he never could spell his own title ; and he never would put an e after an o in any word . he be far too straightforward a man to spell well ; and now the loss of three finger will cut his word short than ever , and be a fine excuse for he . he be faint again , when I board the Leda , partly no doubt through strong medical measure ; for the doctor , who be an ornament to his profession , have cauterise his stump with a marlinspike , for fear of inflammation . and I hear that he have sing the other finger off . but I hope that may prove incorrect . at any rate , I could not bear to disturb he , but leave write order with Scudamore ; for the senior be on board the prize . dolly , be off to bed , this moment . " 
 " well , now , " say the Rector , draw near , and fill another deliberative pipe , " I have no right to ask what your order be , and perhaps you have no right to tell I . but as to the ship that remain in my parish , or at any rate on its border , if you can tell I anything , I shall be very grateful , both as a question of parochial duty , and also because of the many question I be sure to have to answer from my wife and daughter . " 
 " there be no cause for secrecy ; I will tell you everything : " the admiral hat mystery . " why , the London paper will publish the whole of it , and a great deal more than that , in three day ' time . I have send off the Leda with her prize to Portsmouth . with this easterly breeze and smooth water , they will get there , cripple as they be , in some twenty - four hour . there the wound will be care for , and the prisoner draft off . the Blonde , the corvette which be aground , surrender , as you know , when she find herself helpless , and within range of our new battery . Stubbard 's man long to have a few shot at she ; but of course we stop any such outrage . nearly all her officer and most of her crew be on board the Leda , have give their parole to attempt no rise ; and frenchman be always honourable , unless they have some very wicked leader . but we leave in the corvette her captain , an exceedingly fine fellow , and about a score of hand who volunteer to stay to help to work the ship , upon condition that if we can float she , they shall have their freedom . and we put a prize crew from the Leda on board she , only eight - and - twenty hand , which be all that could be spar , and in command of they our friend Blyth Scudamore . I send he to ask Robert Honyman about it , when he manage to survive the doctor , for a captain be the master of his own luff ; and he answer that it be exactly what he wish . our gallant frigate lose three lieutenant in this very spirited action , two kill and one heavily wound . and the first be in charge of the Ville d'Anvers , so there be nobody for this enterprise except the gentle Scuddy , as they call he . he be very young for such a business , and we must do all we can to help he . " 
 " I have confidence in that young man , " say Mr. Twemlow , as if it be a question of theology ; " he have very sound view , and his principle be high ; and he would have take holy order , I believe , if his father 's asset have permit it . he perceive all the rapidly grow danger with which the Church be surround , and when I be in doubt about a line of Horace , he show the fine diffidence , and yet prove that I be right . the ' White Pig , ' as the name of a submarine bank , be most clearly of classic origin . we find it in Homer , and in Virgil too ; and probably the Romans , who undoubtedly have a naval station in Springhaven , and exterminate the oyster , as they always do " 
 " come , come , Twemlow , " say the Admiral , with a smile which smooth the breach of interruption , " you carry I out of my depth so far that I long to be strand on my pillow . when your great book come out , we shall have in perfect form all the pile of your discovery , which you break up into little bit too liberally . the Blonde on the Pig be like Beauty and the Beast . if gentle Scuddy rescue she , it will not   be by Homer , or Horace , or even holy order , but by hard tug and stout seamanship . " 
 " with the blessing of the Lord , it shall be do , " say the Rector , knock his pipe out ; " and I trust that Providence may see fit to have it do very speedily ; for I dread the effect which so many gallant stranger , all work hard and apparently in peril , may produce upon the female of this parish . " 
 but the Admiral laugh , and say , " Pooh , pooh ! " for he have faith in the maid of Springhaven . 
 for these there be a fine time now in store young man up and down everywhere , people run in and out with some new news , before they could get their hat on , the kettle to boil half a dozen time a day , and almost as much to see as they could talk of . at every high - water that come by daylight and sometimes there be two of they every maid in the parish be bind to run to the top of a sand - hill high enough to see over the neck of the Head , and there to be up among the rush all together , and repulse disdainfully the society of lad . these take the matter in a very different light , and think it quite a pity and a piece of fickle - mindedness , that they might go the round of crab - pot , or of inshore lug - line , without anybody to watch they off , or come down with a basket to meet they . 
 for be it understand that the great fishing fleet have not launch forth upon its labour . their narrow escape from the two french cruiser would last they a long time to think over , and to say the same thing to each other about it that each other have say to they every time they meet . and they know that they could not do this so well as to make a new credit of it every time , when once they be in the same craft together , and could not go asunder more than ten yard and a half . and well , far well , than all these reason for stay at home and enjoy themselves , be the great fact that they could make more money by leisure than by labour , in this nobly golden time . 
 Luck foster skill in this great affair , which deserve to be record for the good of any village gift with like opportunity . it appear that the British Admiralty have long be eager for the capture of the Blonde , because of her speed and strength and beauty , and the mischief she have do to english trade . to destroy she would be a great comfort , but to employ her aright would be glorious ; and her proper employment be to serve as a model for english frigate first , and then to fight against her native land . therefore , no soon do their lordship hear what have happen at Springhaven than they send down a rider express , to say that the ship must be save at any price . and as nothing could be spar from the blockade force , or the fleet in the down , or the cruise squadron , the Commander of the coast - defence be instruct to enrol , impress , or adapt somehow all the man and the matter available . something be say about free use of money in the service of his Majesty , but not a penny be send to begin upon . but Admiral Darling carry out his order , as if he have receive they frame in gold . " they be pretty sure to pay I in the end , " he say ; " and if they do not , it will not   break I . I would give 500 pound on my own account , to carry that corvette to Spithead . and it would be the making of Scudamore , who remind I of his father more and more , every time I come across he . " 
 the fleet under Captain Tugwell have quite lately fall off from seven to five , through the fierce patriotism of some young member , and their sanguine belief in bounty - money . Captain Zeb have present they with his experience in a long harangue nearly fifty word long and they look as if they be convince by it . however , in the morning they be go , have mostly have tiff with their sweetheart which be fervent incentive to patriotism and they charter themselves , and their boat be number for the service of their country . they have do their work well , because they have none to do , except to draw small wage , and they find themselves qualified now for more money , and come home at the early chance of it . 
 two guinea a day for each smack and four hand , be the term offer by the Admiral , whose hard - work conscience be twitch into herring - bone by the strife between native land and native spot . " I have have many tussle with uncertainty before , " he tell dolly , go down one evening , " but never such vexation of the mind as now . all our people expect to get more for a day , than a month of fine fishing would bring they ; while the Government go by the bad time they make , and expect they to throw in their boat for nothing . ' the same as our breech , ' Tugwell say to I ; ' whenever we work , we throw in they , and we ought to do the very same with our boat . ' this make it very hard for I . " 
 but by do his good , he get over the hardship , as people generally do . he settle the daily wage as above , with a bonus of double that amount for the day that see the Blonde upon her leg again . indignation prevail , or pretend to do so ; but common - sense conquer , and all set to work . hawser , and chain , and buoy , and all other needful gear and tackle be provide by the Admiralty from the store - house build not long ago for the Fencibles . and Zebedee Tugwell , by right of position , and without a word say for it because who could say a word against it ? become the commander of the Rescue fleet , and draw double pay naturally for himself and family . 
 " I do it , " he say , " if you ask I why I do it , without any intention of better myself , for the Lord hath place I above need of that ; but mainly for the sake of discipline , and the respectability of thing . suppose I be under you , sir , and know you be get no more than I be , why , my stomach would fly every time that you give I an order without a ' please , Zebedee ! ' but as soon as I feel that you pocket a shilling , in the time I take pocket twopence , the value of your brain ariseth plain before I ; and instead of think what you say , I do it . " 




 CHAPTER XXIV 

 accord to CONTRACT 

 when the Blonde have be on the White Pig for a week , in spite of all the science of Scudamore , ready money of the Admiral , and effort of the native , there begin to be sign of a change in the weather . the sea be as smooth , and the sky as bright , and the land as brown as ever ; but the feel of the air be not the same , and the sound that come through it be different . " rain afore Friday , " say Captain Zeb , " and a blow from sowwest afore Sunday . ' twill break up the Blunder , I reckon , my lad . " 
 with various aspect they look at he , all hold sweet converse at the Darling Arms , after the manifold struggle of the day . the eye of the young man be fill with disappointment and anger , as at a sure seer of evil ; the elder , to whom cash be more important , gaze with anxiety and dismay ; while a pair , old enough to be sire of Zebedee , nod approval , and look at one another , expect to receive , but too discreet to give , a wink . then a lively discourse arise and throve among the young ; and the elder let they hold it , while they talk of something else . 
 on the follow morning two dialogue be hold upon different part of Springhaven shore , but each of great import to the beautiful captive still fast aground in the offing . the first be between Captain Zebedee Tugwell and Lieutenant Scudamore . the gentle Scuddy , still hop against hope , have stick fast to his charge , upon whose fortune so much of his own depended . if he could only succeed in float and carry she into Portsmouth , his mark would be make , his position secure far quick than by ten gallant action ; and that which he care for a hundredfold , the comfort of his widow mother , would be advance and establish . for , upon the valuation of the prize , a considerable sum would fall to he , and every farthing of it would be send to she . bright with youthful hope , and trustful in the rise spring of tide , which have all but release they yesterday , accord to his firm belief , he run from the Hall through the Admiral 's ground , to meet the boat which be wait for he , while he be have breakfast and council with his chief . between the Round - house and the old white gate he hear a low whistle from a clump of shrub , and turn that way , meet Tugwell . with that prince of fisherman he shake hand , accord to the manner of Springhaven , for he have learn to admire the brave habit of the man , his strong mind , and frank taciturnity . and Tugwell on his part have take a liking to the simple and cheerful young officer , who receive his suggestion , be kind to all hand , and so manfully bear the daily disappointment . 
 " nobody in there ? " ask Zeb , with one finger pointing to the Round - house ; " then sit down on this bit of bank , sir , a minute . Less chance to be shoot at by any french ship . " 
 the bit of bank really be a bit of hollow , where no one could see they from the beach , or lane , or even from the Round - house . Scudamore , who understand his man , obey ; and Tugwell come to his bearing on a clump of fern before he . 
 " how much will Government pay the chap as fetch she out of that snug little berth ? for division to self and partner , how much ? for division to self and family , how much ? " 
 " I have think about that , " the lieutenant answer , with little surprise at the question , but much at the secrecy throw around it ; " and I think it would be very unsafe to count upon get a penny beyond the Admiral 's term double pay for the day that we float she . " 
 Captain Zebedee shake his head , and the golden sheaf of his olympian beard ruffle and crisp , as to an adverse wind . 
 " can not   a'most believe it , " he reply , with his bright eye steadily settle on Scudamore 's ; " the English country , as I belong to , can not   quite ' a coom to that yet ! " 
 " I fear that it have indeed , " Blyth answer , very gravely ; " at least I be sure of this , Master Tugwell , that you must not look forward to any bounty , bonus , or premium , or whatever it be call , from the Authorities who should provide it . but for myself , and the difference it will make to I whether we succeed or fail , I shall be happy , and will give my word , to send you 50 pound , to be divide at your discretion among the smack . I mean , of course , as soon as I get pay . " 
 Scudamore be frighten by the size of his own promise ; for he have never yet own 50 pound in the solid . and then he be scared at the wholesale loss of so large a sum to his mother . 
 " never fear , lad , " honest Tugwell reply , for the young man 's face be fair to read ; " we will not take a farden of thy hard airning , not a brass farden , so help I Bob ! gentlefolk have so much call for money , as none of we know nothing of . and thou hast help to save all the lot of we from Frenchies , and be the most forward , as I hear tell . but if we could ' a get 50 pound out of Government , why so much more for we , and none the less for they . but a Englishman must do his duty , in reason , and when ' a do not   hurt his self by the same . there be a change in the weather , as forbid more sport . you shall have the Blunder off to - morrow , lad . would not   do to be too sudden like . " 
 " I fear I be very stupid , Master Tugwell . but I do not   see how you can manage it so surely , after labour nine day all in vain . " 
 Zebedee hesitate half a moment , betwixt discretion and the pride of knowledge . then the latter vanquish and relieve his mind . 
 " I trust in your honour , sir , of course , to keep I clear . I might have bring ' e off the Pig , first day , or second to the late , if it be sound business . but with winter time come , and the week 's fishing lose , our duty to our family and this place be to pull ' e on hard , sir , to pull ' e aground firm ; and with the help of the Lord we have a - do it well . we be not   a - go to kill the goose as lay the golden egg . no offence to you , sir ; it be not   you as be the goose . " 
 Master Tugwell rub his pocket with a very pleasant smile , and then put his elbow on his great square knee , and complacently study the lieutenant 's small mind . 
 " I can understand how you could do such a thing , " say Scudamore , after he have rub his eye , and then look away for fear of laugh , " but I can not understand by what power on earth you be enable to look at I and tell I this . for nine day you have be pay every night , and pay pretty well , as you yourself acknowledge , to haul a ship off a shoal ; and all the time you have be haul she hard upon it ! " 
 " young man , " reply Tugwell , with just indignation , " a hofficer should be above such word . but I forgive ' e , and hope the Lord will do the same , with allowance for youth and ill - convenience . I might ' a know no well , at your age and training . " 
 " but what be you pay for , just answer I that , unless it be to pull the Blonde off the sand - bank ? and how can you pretend that you have do an honest thing by pull she further upon the bank ? " 
 " I will not   ask ' e , sir , to beg my pardon for say what never man say to I , without read the word of the contraction ; " Zeb pull out a paper from his hat , and spread it , and lay a stone at every corner ; " this contraction be sign by yourself and Squire Darling , for and on behalf of the kingdom ; and the word be for we to give our service , to pull , haul , tow , warp , or otherwise as direct , release , relieve , set free , and rescue the aforesaid ship , or bark , or vessel , craft , or " 
 " please not to read all that , " cry Scuddy , " or a gale of wind may come before you be half - way through . it be Admiral Darling 's lawyer , Mr. Furkettle , who prepare it , to prevent any chance of misunderstanding . " 
 " provide always , " continue Tugwell , slowly , " and the meaning , condition , purport , object , sense , and intention of this agreement be , that the aforesaid Zebedee Tugwell shall submit in everything to the order , command , instruction , counsel , direction , injunction , authority , or discretion , whether in writing or otherwise , of the aforesaid " 
 " I would not interrupt you if I could help it " Scudamore have a large stock of patience   enhance by laborious practice at Stonnington , but who might abide , when time be precious , to see Zebedee feel his way with his finger along the bottom and to the end of every word , and then stop to congratulate himself at the conquest of every one over two syllable ? " but excuse I for say that I know all these condition ; and the tide will be lose , if we stop here . " 
 " very good , sir ; then you see how it standeth . who hath break they ? not we ! we be pay for to haul ; and haul we do , accord to superior order . she ground from the south , with the tide make upp'ard , somewhere about three - quarter flow ; and the squire , and you , and all the rest of ' e , without no knowledge of the Pig whatsomever , fasten all your pulley - haulie by the starn , and say , ' now pull ! ' and pull we do , to the tune of sixteen guineas a day for the good of Springhaven . " 
 " and you know all the time that it be wrong ! well , I never come across such people . but surely some one of you would have have the honesty I beg pardon , I mean the good - will to tell we . I can scarcely imagine some forty man and boy preserve such a secret for nine whole day , haul for their life in the wrong direction , and never even by a wink or smile " 
 " Springhaven be like that , " say Master Tugwell , proudly ; " we do a thing one and all together , even if we reason consarne it . and over and above that , sir , there be but two man in Springhaven as understand the White Pig , bar my own self . the young ' uns might ' a smell a rat , but they know well than to say so . where the Blunder ground and she hath airne her name , for the good of the dweller in this village be the chine of the Pig ; and he hath a double back , with the outer side high than the inner one . she come through a narrow nick in his outer back , and then plump , stem on , upon the inner one . you may haul at she forever by the starn , and there she will ' bide , or lie up again on the other back . but bring her weight forrard , and tackle she by the head , and off she come , the very next fair tide ; for she hath berth herself over the big of it , and there bain't   but a basketful under her forefoot . " 
 " then , Master Tugwell , let we lose no time , but have at she at once , and be do with it . " Scudamore jump up , to give action to his word ; but Tugwell sate aground still , as firmly as the Blonde . 
 " beg of your pardon , sir , I would invite of you not to be in no sart of hurry hasting forwardly . we must come off gradual , after hold on so long there , and well to have Squire Darling round the corner first , sir . not that he knoweth much about it , but ' a might make believe to do so . and when ' a hath see we pull wrong way , a hundred and twenty guineas ' worth , a ' might grudge we the reward for pull right way . I have   a - know ' un get into that state of mind , although it be his own tenant . " 
 the lieutenant be at length compel to laugh , though for many reason loth to do so . but the quiet contempt for the Admiral 's skill , and the brief hint about his character , touch his sense of the ludicrous more softly than the explanation of his own mishap . then the Captain of Springhaven smile almost imperceptibly ; for he be a serious man , and his smile be accustom to be interior . 
 " I do hear tell , " he say , stroke his beard , for fear of have discompose it , " that the squire be under compulsion to go a bit westward again to - morrow . and when he cometh back he would be glad to find we have manage the job without he . no fear of the weather break up afore Friday , and she can not   take no harm for a tide or two . if you think well , sir , let we heave at she to - day , as afore , by superior order . then it come into your mind to try t'other end a bit , and you shift all the gun and heavy lumber forrard to give weight to the bow and lift the starn , and off she will glide at the first tug to - morrow , so sure as my name be Zebedee . but mind one thing , sir , that you keep she , when you have   get she . she hath too many furriner native aboard of she , to be any way to my liking . " 
 " oh , there need be no doubt about they , " reply Blyth ; " we treat they like ourselves , and they be all upon their honour , which no Frenchman ever think of breaking . but my man will be tired of wait for I . I shall leave you to your plan , Tugwell . " 
 " ah , I know the natur ' of they young man , " Captain Zebedee muse , as he sate in his hollow , till Scudamore 's boat be far away ; " they be full of scruple for themselves and faith in other fellow . he will never tell Squire , nor no one else here , what I lay he under , and the laugh would go again ' he , if he do . we shall get to - day 's money , I reckon , as well as double pay to - morrow , and airn it . well , it might ' a be well , and it might be wuss . " 
 about two mile westward of the brook , some rock mark the end of the fine Springhaven sand and the beginning of a far more rugged beach , the shingle and flint shelf of Pebbleridge . here the chalk of the Sussex backbone   which have be plump over and sleek by the flesh of the valley   jut forth , like the scrag of a skeleton , and crumble in low but rugged cliff into the flat domain of sea . here the landing be bad , and the anchorage bad , for a slippery shale reject the fluke , and the water be usually keep in a fidget between the order of the west wind and scurry of the tide . 
 this very quiet morning , with the wind off shore , and scarcely enough of it to comb the sea , four smart - look Frenchmen , with red cap on their head , be barely hold way upon the light gig of the Blonde , while their captain be keep an appointment with a stranger , not far from the weed - strew line of wave . in a deep rocky channel where a land - spring rise   which be still - bear except at low water , and laver and dilsk and claw - coral show that the sea have more dominion there than the sky , two man stand face each other ; and their word , though belong to the most polite of tongue , be not so courteous as might be . each man stand with his back to a rock not touch it , however , because it be too wet one be as cold and as firm as the rock , the other like the sea , tumultuous . the passionate man be Captain Desportes , and the cold one Caryl Carne . 
 " then you wish I to conclude , monsieur , " Carne speak as one offer repentance , " that you will not do your duty to your country , in the subject set before you ? I pray you to deliberate , because your position hang upon it . " 
 " never ! never ! once more , Captain , with all thank for your consideration , I refuse . my duty to my own honour have first place . after that my duty to my country . speak of it no more , sir ; it quite be to insult I . " 
 " no , Captain Desportes , it be nothing of that kind , or I should not be here to propose it . your parole be give only as long as your ship continue upon the sand . the moment she float , you be liberate . then be the time for a noble stroke of fortune . be it not so , my dear friend ? " 
 " no , sir . this affair be impossible . my honour have be pledge , not until the ship be float , but until I be myself set free in France . I be sorry not to see thing as you see they for I ; but the question be for my own consideration . " 
 Captain Desportes have resent , as an honest man must do , especially when more advanced in year , the other 's calm settlement , without invitation , of matter which concern his own conscience . and as most mankind if at all perceptive like or dislike one another at a glance , desporte , be very quick and warm of nature , have feel at first sight a strong repulsion from the cold and arrogant man who face he . his age be at least twice that of Carne , he have see much service in the well day of France , and have rise slowly by his own skill and valour ; he know that his future in the service depend upon his decision in this matter , and he have a large family to maintain . but his honour be pledge , and he hold fast by it . 
 " there be one consideration , " Carne reply , with rancour slowly kindling in his great black eye , " which precede all other , even that of honour , in the mind of a trust officer . it be not that of patriotism which have not its usual weight with monsieur but it be that of obedience , discipline , loyalty , faith , towards those who have place faith in he . Captain Desportes , as commander of a ship , be entrust with property ; and that confidence be the first debt upon his honour . " 
 to desporte , as to most man of action , the right be plain than the reason . he know that this final plea be unsound , but he do not see how to contest it . so he come back to fact , which be easy for he . 
 " how be I to know , monsieur , what would be the wish of those who have entrust I with my position ? you be place in authority by some mean here , in your own country , but against it . that much you have prove to I , by paper . but your credential be general only . they do not apply to this especial case . if the Chief of the State know my position , he would wish I to act as I mean to act , for the honour and credit of our nation . " 
 " be you then acquaint with his signature ? if so , perhaps you will verify this , even if you be resolve to reject it . "
