 Carne draw a letter from an inner pocket , and carefully unfold it . there be many word and minute direction upon various subject , write by the hand of the most minute , and yet most comprehensive , of mankind . 
 " there be nothing in this that concern you , " he say , after show the date , only four day old , " except these few word at the end , which perhaps you may like to read , before you make final decision . the signature of the Chief be clear . " 
 Captain Desportes read aloud " it be of the utmost importance to I , that the Blonde should not be capture by the enemy , as the Ville d'Anvers have be . you tell I that it be ashore near you , and the captain and crew upon parole , to be liberate if they assist in the extrication of the vessel . this must not be . in the service of the state , I demand that they consider not at all their parole . the well - know speed and light draught of that vessel have render she almost indispensable to I . when the vessel be free , they must rise upon the enemy , and make for the near of our port without delay . upon this I insist , and place confidence in your establish courage and management , to accomplish it to my satisfaction . " 
 " your order be clear enough , " say Caryl Carne . " what reason can you give , as an officer of the Republic , for disobey they ? " 
 desporte look at his ship in the distance , and then at the sea and the sky , with a groan , as if he be bid farewell to they . Carne feel sure that he have prevail , and a smile shed light , but not a soft light , on his hard pale countenance . 
 " be in no rash haste , " say the french sea - captain , and he could not have find word more annoying to the cold proud man before he ; " I do not recognise in this mandate the voice of my country , of the honourable France , which would never say , ' let my son break their word of honour ! ' this man speak , not as Chief of a grand state , not as leader of noble gentleman , but as Emperor of a society of serfs . France be no empire ; she be a grand nation of spirit , of valour , above all , of honour . the English have treat I , as I would treat they , with kindness , with largeness , with confidence . in the name of fair France , I will not do this thing . " 
 Carne be naturally pale , but now he grow white with rage , and his black eye flash . 
 " France will be an empire within six month ; and your honour will be put upon prison diet , while your family starve for the sake of it . " 
 " if I ever meet you under other circumstance , " reply the brave Frenchman , now equally pale , " I shall demand reparation , sir . " 
 " with great pleasure , " reply Carne , contemptuously ; " meanwhile monsieur will have enough to do to repair his broken fortune . " 
 Captain Desportes turn his back , and give a whistle for his crew , then step with much dignity into his boat . " to the Blonde , lad , " he cry , " to the unsullied blonde ! " then he sate , look at she , and stroke his grizzle beard , into which there come trickle a bitter tear or two , as he think of his wife and family . he have act well ; but , accord to the measure of the present world , unwisely . 




 CHAPTER XXV 

 no concern of ours 

 the very next morning it be know to the faithful of Springhaven that the glory of the place would be treble that day , and its income increase desirably . that day , the fair stranger   which have so long awaken the admiration of the woman , and the jealousy of the man   would by the consummate skill of Captain Zeb who have triumph over all the officer of the british Navy float forth magnificently from her narrow bed , hoist her white sail , and under british ensign salute the new fort , and shape a course for Portsmouth . that she have stick fast and in danger so long be simply because the cocked hat be too proud to give ear to the wisdom in an old otter - skin . now Admiral Darling be baffle and go ; and Captain Tugwell would show the world what he could do , and what stuff his man be make of , if they only have their way . from old Daddy Stakes , the bald father of the village , to Mrs. Caper junior 's baby equally bald , but with a crop as sure of come as mustard and cress beneath his flannel some in arm , some on leg , some upon brave crutch , all be abroad in the soft air from the west , which have steal up under the stiff steel skirt of the east wind , exactly as wise Captain Zeb predict . 
 " my dear , " say Mrs. Twemlow to the solid Mrs. Stubbard , for a very sweet friendship have spring up between these lady , and would last until their interest should happen to diverge , " this will be a great day for my dear husband 's parish . perhaps there be no other parish in the kingdom capable of act as Springhaven have , so obedient , so disciplined , so faithful to their contract ! I be tell that they even pull the vessel more aground , in preference to set up their own opinion . I be tell that as soon as the Admiral be go for between you and I he be a little overbearing , with the very good intention in the world , but too confident in his own sagacity then that clever but exceedingly modest young man , Lieutenant Scudamore , be allow at last to listen to our great man Tugwell , who have long be the oracle of the neighbourhood about the sea , and the weather , and all question of that kind . and between you and I , my dear , the poor old Admiral seem a little bit jealous of his reputation . and what do you think he say before he go , which show his high opinion of his own ability ? Tugwell say something in his rough and ready way , which , I suppose , put his mightiness upon the high rope , for he shout out in everybody 's hearing , ' I will tell you what it be , my man , if you can get she off , by any of your ' something I must not repeat ' device , I will give you fifty guineas , five - and - twenty for yourself , and the rest to be divide among these other fellow . ' then Zebedee pull out a testament from his pocket , for he be a man of deep religious conviction , and can read almost all the easy place , though he think most of the hard one , and he make his son Dan   who be a great scholar , as they say , and a very fine - look youth as well   put down at the end what the Admiral have say . now , what do you think of that , dear Mrs. Stubbard ? " 
 " I think , " reply that strong - minded lady , " that Tugwell be an arrant old fox ; and if he get the fifty guineas , he will put every farthing into his own pocket . " 
 " oh , no ! he be honest as the day itself . he will take his own twenty - five , and then leave the rest to settle whether he should share in their twenty - five . but we must be quick , or we shall lose the sight . quite a number of people be come from inland . how wonderfully quickly these thing spread ! they come the first day , and then make up their mind that nothing could be do , and so they stop at home . but now , here they be again , as if by magic ! if the ship get off , it will be know halfway to London before nightfall . but I see Captain Stubbard go up the hill to your charming battery . that show implicit faith in Tugwell , to return the salute of the fair captive ! it be indeed a proud day for Springhaven ! " 
 " but it be not   do yet . and perhaps it will not   be do . I would rather trust officer of the navy than people who catch crab and oyster . I would go up to the battery , to laugh at my husband , but for the trick the child play I . my authority be go , at the very first puff of smoke . how child do delight in that vile gunpowder ! " 
 " so they ought , in the present state of our country , with five hundred thousand of Frenchmen come . my dear Mrs. Stubbard , how thankful we should be to have child who love gunpowder ! " 
 " but not when they blow up their mother , ma'am . " 
 " oh , here come Eliza ! " cry Mrs. Twemlow . " I be so glad , because she know everything . I think we have miss she . my dear child , where be Faith and Dolly Darling go ? there be so many stranger about to - day that the well class should keep together . " 
 " here be three of we at any rate , " reply the young lady , who consider her mother old - fashioned : " enough to secure one another 's sanctity from the low order . faith have go on to the headland , with that heroic mannikin , Johnny . dolly be to follow , with that shank maid to protect she , as soon as her hat be trim , or some such era . but I will answer for it that she lose herself in the crowd , or some fib of that sort . " 
 " Eliza ! " say her mother , and very severely , because Mrs. Stubbard be present , " I be quite astonished at your talking so . you might do the great injury to a very lively and harmless , but not over - prudent girl , if any one hear you who would repeat it . we all know that the Admiral be so wrap up in dolly that he let she do many thing which a mother would forbid . but that be no concern of our ; and once for all , if such thing must be say , I beg that they may not be say by you . " 
 in the present age , Mrs. Twemlow would have get sharp answer . but her daughter only look aggrieved , and glance at Mrs. Stubbard , as if to say , " well , time will show whether I deserve it . " and then they hasten on , among the bad class , to the headland . 
 not only all the fishing - smack , and Captain Stubbard 's galley , but every boat half as sound as a hat , might now be see near the ground vessel , prepare to labour or look on . and though the White Pig be allow to be three - quarter of a mile from the near point , the mighty voice of Captain Zeb ride over the flicker breadth of sea , and through the soft babble of the wave ashore . the wind be light from southwest , and the warp be nearly in the same direction now , the Blonde begin to set her course , to catch a lift of air , when the tide should come busily work under she . and this would be the good tide since she take the ground , last Sunday week , when the spring be go off . as soon as the hawser be make fast , and the shout of Zebedee redouble with great strength   both of sound and of language , and the long rope lift with a flash of splash , and a creak of heavy wood , and the cry be , " with a will ! with a will , my gay lad ! " every body have a sound eye in it be gaze intently , and every heart be flutter , except the lovely eye and quick heart in all Springhaven . 
 Miss dolly have make up her mind to go , and would have have warm word ready for any one rash enough to try to prevent she . but a very short note which be put into her hand about 10 A.M. distract she . 
 " if you wish to do I a real service , accord to your kind word of Saturday , be in the upper shrubbery at half past eleven ; but tell no one except the bearer . you will see all that happen well there than on the beach , and I will bring a telescope . " 
 dolly know at once who have write this , and admire it all the more because it be follow by no signature . for year she have long for a bit of romance ; and the common - sense of all the world irk she . she know as well as possible that what she ought to do be to take this letter to her sister Faith , and be guide by her advice about it . faith be her eld by three year or more , and as steadfast as a rock , yet as tender as young moss . there be no fear that Faith would ride the high horse with she , or lie down the law severely ; she be much more likely to be too indulgent , though certain not to play with wrong . 
 all this the young sister know , and therefore resolve to eschew that knowledge . she like her own way , and she mean to have it , in a harmless sort of way ; her own high spirit should be her guide , and she be old enough now to be her own judge . Mr. Carne have save her sister 's life , when she stand up in that senseless way ; and if Faith have no gratitude , dolly must feel , and endeavour to express it for she . 
 reasoning thus , and much well than this , she be very particular about her hat , and french pelerine of fluted lawn , and frock of pale violet trim on either side with gather muslin . her little heart flutter at be draw in , when it should have be plump up to her neck , and very nearly display to the public ; but her father be stern upon some point , and never would hear of the classic discovery . she have not even grecian sandal , nor a ' surprise fan " to flutter from her wrist , nor hair oil into flat lesbian coil , but freedom of rich young tress , and of graceful figure , and taper limb . there be no one who could say her nay , of the lover of maiden nature . 
 however , maidens must be discreet , even when most adventurous ; and so she take another maid to help she , of respected but not romantic name Jenny Shanks , who have bring she that letter . Jenny be much pretty than her name , and the ground she trod on be worship by many , even when her shoe be down at heel . especially in this track remain the fine part of Charley Bowles 's heart   while the coarser be up against the Frenchmen , as well as a good deal of Mr. Prater 's nephew 's , and of several other sole - fisher . this enable Jenny to enter kindly into tender question . and she fetch her Sunday bonnet down the trap - ladder where she keep it because the other maid be so nasty as soon as her letter be deliver . 
 " your place , Jenny , be to go behind , " Miss Dolly say , with no small dignity , as this zealous attendant keep step for step with she , and swing her red arm against the lady 's fair one . " I be come upon important business , Jenny , such as you can not understand , but may stay at a proper distance . " 
 " Lor , miss , I be sure I beg your pardon . I think it be a kind of coorting - match , and you might be glad of my experience . " 
 " such thing I never do , and have no idea what you mean . I shall be much oblige to you , Jenny , if you will hold your tongue . " 
 " oh yes , miss ; no fear of my tell anybody . wild horse would never pull a syllable out of I . the young man be so aggravating that I keep my proper distance from they . but the mind must be make up , at one time or other . " 
 dolly look down at she with vast contempt , which she would not lower herself by express , even with favour of time and place . then turn a corner of the grassy walk , between ground - ash and young larch , they come upon an opening plant round with ilex , arbutus , juniper , and laurel , and back by one of the rock which form the outwork of the valley . from a niche in this rock , like the port - hole of a ship , a rill of sparkle water pour , and begin to make a noise already , cut corner 's of its own production short , in its hurry to be a brook , and then to help the sea . and across its exit from the rock   like a measure of its insignificance   a very comfortable seat be fix , so that any gentleman or even a lady with divide skirt might freely sit with one foot on either bank of this menacing but not yet very formidable stream . so that on the whole this nook of shelter under the coronet of rock be a favourite place for a sage cock - pheasant , or even a woodcock in wintry weather . 
 upon that bench   where the Admiral love to sit , in the afternoon of peace and leisure , observe with a spy - glass the manoeuvre of his tranquil fishing fleet   Caryl Carne be sit now , with his long and strong leg well spread out , his shoulder comfortably settle back , and his head cast a little on one side , as if he be try to compute his property . then , as dolly come into the opening , he arise , make a bow beyond the compass of any true Briton , and swing his hat , come to meet she . dolly make a curtsey in the style impress upon she by her last governess but one a french lady of exceedingly high ancestry and manner and Carne recognise it as a fine thing out of date . 
 " Jenny , get away ! " say dolly word not mean for he to hear , but he have grave command of countenance . 
 " this lay I under one more obligation : " Carne speak in a low voice , and with a smile of diffidence which remind she of Scudamore , though the two smile be as different as night and day . " I have take a great liberty in ask you to come , and that multiply my gratitude for your good - will . for my own sake alone I would not have dare to sue this great favour from you , though I put it so , in terror of alarm you . but it be for my own sake also , since anything evil to you would be terrible to I . " 
 " no one can wish to hurt I , " she answer , look up at he bravely , and yet frighten by his gaze , " because I have never harm any one . and I assure you , sir , that I have many to defend I , even when my father be go from home . " 
 " it be beyond doubt . who would not rush to do so ? but it be from those who be least suspect that the danger come the bad . the most modest of all gentleman , who blush like a damsel , or the gallant officer devote to his wife and child , or the simple veteran with his star , and scar , and downright speech these be the people that do the wrong , because no one believe it be in they . " 
 " then which of the three be to carry I off from home , and friend , and family Lieutenant Scudamore , Captain Stubbard , or my own godfather , Lord Nelson ? " 
 this young man nourish a large contempt for the intellect of woman , and be therefore surprised at the quickness and spirit of the girl whom he wish to terrify . a sterner tone must be use with she . 
 " I never deal in joke , " he say , with a smile of sad sympathy for those who do ; " my life be one perpetual peril , and that restrain facetiousness . but I can make allowance for those who like it . " 
 Miss dolly , the pet child of the house , and all the people round it except the gardener , Mr. Swipes , who find she too inquisitive quick as she be , could not realise at once the possibility of be look down upon . 
 " I be sorry that you have to be so grave , " she say , " because it prevent all enjoyment . but why should you be in such continual danger ? you promise to explain it , on Saturday , only you have no time then . we be all in danger from the French , of course , if they ever should succeed in landing . but you mean something more than that ; and it seem so hard , after all your loss , that you should not be safe from harm . " 
 with all her many fault many more than she dream of fair dolly have a warm and gentle heart , which fill her eye with tender loveliness , whenever it obtain command of they . Carne , who be watch they steadfastly for his own purpose , forget that purpose , and drop his dark eye , and lose the way to tell a lie . 
 " if I may ask you , " he say , almost stammer , and longing without knowledge for the blessing of her touch , " to to allow I just to lead you to this seat , I may perhaps be able I will not take the liberty of sit at your side but I may perhaps be able to explain as much of my affair as you can wish to hear of they , and a great deal more , I fear , a great deal more , Miss Darling . " 
 dolly blush at the rich tone in which he pronounce her name , almost as if it be an adjective ; but she allow he to take her hand , and lead she to the bench beneath the rock . then , regardless of his breech , although of fine padusoy , and his coat , though of purple velvet , he sate down on the bank of the rill at her foot , and wait for she to say something . the young lady love mainly to take the lead , but would liefer have follow suit just now . 
 " you have promise to tell I , " she say , very softly , and with an unusual timidity , which add to her face and manner almost the only charm they lack , ' some thing which I do not understand , and which I have no right to ask you of , except for your own offer . why should you , without injure any one , but only have suffer loss of all your family property , and of all your right and comfort , and live in that lonely place which use to be full of company why should you be in danger now , when you have nothing more to be rob of ? I beg your pardon I mean when all your enemy must have do their bad . " 
 " you be too young yet to understand the world , " he answer , with a well - draw sigh ; " and I hope most truly that you may never do so . in your gentle presence I can not speak with bitterness , even if I could feel it . I will not speak harshly of any one , however I may have be treat . but you will understand that my life alone remain betwixt the plunderer and their prey , and that my errand here prevent they from legally swallow up the spoil . " 
 Miss dolly 's idea of the law , in common with that of most young lady , suggest a horrible monster ravening to devour the fall . and the fall of the Carnes have long be a subject of romantic interest to she . 
 " oh , I see ! " she exclaim , with a look of deep wisdom . " I can quite understand a thing like that , from what I have hear about witness . I hope you will be very careful . my sister owe so much to you , and so do I. " 
 " you must never speak of that again , unless you wish to grieve I . I know that I have say too much about myself ; but you alone care to know anything about I ; and that beguile one out out of one 's wit . if I speak bad English , you will forgive I . I have pass so many year on the continent , and be pick up the language of my childhood very slowly . you will pardon I , when I be mislead by by my own signification . " 
 " well do ! " cry the innocent dolly . " now that be the very first piece of bad English you have use , to the good of my belief , and I be rather quick in that . but you have not yet explain to I my own danger , though you ask I to come here for that purpose , I believe . " 
 " but you shall not be so ; you shall not be in danger . my life shall be give for your defence . what import my peril compare with your ? I be not of cold blood . I will sacrifice all . have faith in I purely , and all shall be do . " 
 " all what ? " dolly ask , with a turn of common - sense , which be the most provoking of all thing sometimes ; and she look at he steadily , to follow up her question . 
 " you can not be persuade that you be in any danger . it be possible that I have be too anxious . do you speak the french language easily ? do you comprehend it , when speak quickly ? " 
 " not a word of it . I have have to learn , of course , and can pronounce very well , my last mistress say ; but I can not make it out at all in the way the french people pronounce it , when one come to talk with they . " 
 " it be very wrong of they , and the loss be theirs . they expect we to copy they even in their language , because we do it in everything else . pardon I one moment . may I look at the great enterprise which be to glorify Springhaven ? it be more than kind of you to be here instead of there . but this , as I venture to say , be a far well place to observe the operation . your word remind I of Captain Desportes , who have be , I think , your father 's guest . a very gallant sailor , and fame for the most unexpected exploit . without doubt , he would have capture all three ship , if he have not contrive to run his own aground . " 
 " how could he capture his own ship ? I think that you never deal in joke . but if you dislike they , you seem to be fond of a little mystery . I like the french captain very much , and he take the trouble to speak slowly for I . my father say that he bear his misfortune nobly , and like a perfect gentleman . Mr. Scudamore admire he , and they be great friend . and yet , sir , you seem inclined to hint that I be in danger from Captain Desportes ! " 
 " ha ! she be afloat ! they have succeed . I think that they have so arrange it . the brave ship spread her pinion . how clever the people of Springhaven be ! if you will condescend to look through this glass , you will see much embracing of the Saxon and the Gaul , or rather , I should say , of the Saxon by the Gaul . Old Tugwell be not fond to be embrace . " 
 " oh , let I see that ! I must see that ! " cry dolly , with all reserve and caution fly ; " to see Capp'en Zeb in the arm of a Frenchman yes , I declare , two have get he , if not three , and he put his great back against the mast to disentangle it . oh , what will he do next ? he have knock down two , in reply to excessive cordiality . what wonderful creature frenchman be ! how kind it be of you to show I this ! but excuse I , Mr. Carne ; there will be twenty people come to the house before I can get back almost . and the ship will salute the battery , and the battery will return it . look ! there go a great puff of smoke already . they can see I up here , when they get to that corner . " 
 " but this spot be not private ? I trust that I have not intrude . your father allow a sort of foot - path through this upper end of his ground ? " 
 " yes , to all the villager , and you be almost one of they ; there be no right of way at all ; and they very seldom come this way , because it lead to nowhere . faith be fond of sit here , to watch the sea , and think of thing . and so be I sometimes , I mean . " 




 CHAPTER XXVI 

 long - PIPE TIMES 

 Daily now the roar and clank of war grow loud and louder , across the narrow sea , and up the river , and around the quiet home of England . if any unusual cloud of dust , any move shade , appear afar , if the tramp of horse in the lane be hear , or neigh of a colt from the four - cross road , people at dinner would start up and cry , " the French , the French have land ! " while the man in the field would get near the hedge to peep through it , and then run away down the ditch . 
 but the nation at large , and the govern power , certainly be not in any great fright . Nay , rather they err , if at all , on the side of tranquillity and self - confidence ; as one who have be fire at with blank - cartridge forget that the click of the trigger will not tell he when the bullet have be drop in . the bullet be there this time ; and it miss the heart of Britannia , only through the failure of the powder to explode all at once . 
 it be some year before all this be know ; even Nelson have no perception of it ; and although much alarm be indulge in on the sly , the few who give voice to it be condemn as faint - hearted fellow and " alarmist . " how then could Springhaven , which never have fear any enemy , or even neighbour , depart from its habit , while still an eye - witness of what have befall the Frenchman ? and in this state of mind , have plenty to talk of , it do not   as otherwise must have be do   attach any deep importance to the strange vagary of the London Trader . 
 that great Institution , and Royal Exchange , as well as central embassy of Fashion , have lately become most uncertain in its date , which for year have announce to loose - reckon housewife the day of the week and the hour to buy candle . instead of come home on a Saturday eve , in the van of all the fishing fleet , return their cheer and those of customer on the beach , the London Trader arrive anywhen , as often in the dark as daylight , never take the ground at all , and give a very wide berth to Captain Zeb Tugwell , his craft , and his crew . at time she land package big and bulky , which would have be search   in spite of London bill of lading   if there have be any Custom - house here , or any keen Officer of Customs . but these be deliver by daylight always , and cart by Mr. Cheeseman 's horse direct to his master 's cellar ; and Cheeseman have tell everybody that his wife , have come into a little legacy , be resolve in spite of his advice to try a bit of speculation in hardware , through her sister mile away at Uckfield . Most of the neighbour like Mrs. Cheeseman , because she give good weight   scarcely half an ounce short , with her conscience to her family throw in against it , as well as the sound piece of gossip to be have for the money in Springhaven . and therefore they wish she well , and box their child 's ear if they find they poke nose into her package . Mrs. Cheeseman shake her head when enquire of on the subject , and say with grave truth that the Lord alone can tell how any of poor people 's doing may turn out . 
 some other thing puzzle the village , and would in more sensible time have produce a sensation . why do Mr. Cheeseman now think nothing of as much as three spot on his white linen apron , even in the first half of the week ? why be he seldom at John Prater 's now , and silent in a corner even when he do appear ? what be become of the ruddy polish , like that of a Winter Redstrake , on his cheek , which make a man long for a slice of his ham ? why , the only joke he have make for the last three month be a terrible one at his own expense . he have rush down the street about ten o'clock one morning , at a pace quite insane for a middle - aged man , with no hat on his head and no coat on his back , but the string of his apron dash wild on the breeze , and his big ham - carver make flash in his hand . it be think that some boy must have run off with a penny , or some visitor change a bad shilling ; but no , there be no such good reason to give for it . 
 the yearning of all age , especially dotage , be for a relapse to the infantile state when all plaything be hold in common . and this wise of all place   in its own opinion   have a certain eccentric inclination towards the poetic perfection when it will be impossible to steal , because there will be nothing leave worth steal . still everybody here stick to his own right , and would knock down anybody across they , though find it very nice to talk as if other could have no such standing - point . moreover , they have sufficient common - sense to begin with the right end foremost , and to take a tender interest in one another 's good , moveable , handy , and divisible ; instead of hunger after hungry land , which feed nobody , until itself well feed and tend , and be as useless without a master as a donkey or a man be . the knowledge of these rudiment of civilization be not yet lose at Springhaven ; and while everybody feel and even prove his desire to share a neighbour 's trouble , nobody meddle with any right of his , save his right to be assist . 
 among they throve the old english feeling of respect for ancient family , which be nowadays call " toadyism " by those whom it baulk of robbery . to trade upon this good - will be almost as low a thing as any man can do , even when he do it for good us . but to trade upon it , for the harm of those who feel it , and the ruin of his country , be without exception the very low and this be what Caryl Carne be at . 
 he look at the matter in a wholly different light , and would have stab any man who put it as above ; for his sense of honour be as quick and hot as it be crook and misguide . his father have be a true Carne , of the old stamp hot - blooded , headstrong , stubborn , wayward , narrow - minded , and often arrogant ; but to balance these fault and many other truthful , generous , kind - hearted , affectionate , staunch to his friend , to his inferior genial , loyal to his country , and respectful to religion . and he might have do well , but for two sad evil he take a burdened property , and he plunge into a bad marriage . 
 his wife , on the other hand , might have do well , if she have marry almost anybody else . but her nature be too like his own , with feminine vanity and caprice , french conceit , and the pride of noble birth in the proud age of nobility harden all her fault , and hammer the rivet of her strong self - will . to these little difficulty must be add the difference of religion ; and though neither of they care two pin for that , it be a matter for crossed dagger . a pound of feather weigh as much as   and in some poise more than   a pound of lead , and the leaden - head squire and the feather - head Madame swing always at opposite end of the beam , until it break between they . tale of rough conflict , imprisonment , starvation , and even vile blow , be tell about they for several year ; and then " Madame la Comtesse "   as her husband disdainfully call she   disappear , carry off her one child , Caryl . she be still of very comely face and form ; and the Squire make known to all whom it concern , and many whom it do not concern , that his french wife have run away with a young Frenchman , accord to the habit of her race and kind . in support of this charge he have nothing whatever to show , and his friend disbelieve it , know he to be the last man in the world to leave such a wrong unresented . 
 during the last three generation the fortune of the Carnes have be decline , slowly at first , and then fast and fast ; and now they fall with the final crash . the lady of high birth and great beauty have bring nothing else into the family , but rather have impoverish it by her settlement , and wild extravagance afterwards . her husband Montagu Carne stave off the evil day just for the present , by raise a large sum upon second mortgage and the security of a trustful friend . but this sum be dissipate , like the rest ; for the squire , be deeply wound by his wife 's desertion , prove to the world his indifference about it by plunge into still more reckless way . he have none to succeed he ; for he vow that the son of the adulteress as he call she should never have Carne Castle ; and his last mad act be to buy five - and - twenty barrel of powder , wherewith to blow up his ancestral home . but ere he could accomplish that stroke of business he stumble and fall down the old chapel step , and be find the next morning by faithful Jeremiah , as cold as the ivy which have catch his foot , and as dead as the stone he would have send to heaven . 
 no marvel that his son have no love for his memory , and little for the land that give he birth . in very early day this boy have show that his french blood be predominant . he would bite , and kick , and scratch , instead of strike , as an English child do , and he never care for dog or horse , neither worship he the gamekeeper . France be the proper land for he , as his mother always say with a sweet proud smile , and his father with a sneer , or a brief word now condemn . and France be the land for he   as fact ordain   to be nourish , and teach , and grow into tall manhood , and form into the principle and habitude and character which every nation stamp upon the nature of its member . 
 however , our strong point like that of all other be absolute freedom from prejudice ; and the few english people who meet Caryl Carne be well pleased with his difference from themselves . even the enlightened fisherman , imbue with a due contempt for Crappos , feel a kindly will towards he , and be touch by his return to a ruin home and a lonely life . but the woman , romantic as they ought to be , feel a tender interest in a young man so handsome and so unlucky , who lift his hat to they , and pay his way . 
 among the rise spirit of the place , who like to take a large view , on the strength of more education , than their father have find confirm by life , Dan Tugwell be perhaps the foremost . in the present day he might have be a hot radical , even a socialist ; but thing be not come to that pass yet among people bring up to their duty . and Dan 's free sentiment have not be work by those who make a trade of such work now . so that he be pleased and respectful , instead of carp and contradictory , when person of high position than his own would discuss the condition of the time with he . Carne have discover this , although as a rule he say little to his neighbour , and for reason of his own he be strive to get a good hold upon this young fellow . he know that it could not be do in a moment , nor by any common corruption ; the mind of the youth be keen , clear - sight , and simple by reason of soundness . then Carne accidentally hear of something , which encourage and help he in his design upon Dan . 
 Business be slack upon the sea just now , but unusually active upon land , a tide of gold have flow into Springhaven , and bubble up in frying - pan and sparkle in new bonnet . the fishing fleet have capture the fine french frigate accord to feminine history that ever endeavour to capture they . after such a prisoner , let the fish go free , till hunger should spring again in the human breast , or the part that stand up under it . the hero of the whole   unlike most hero   have not succeed in ruin himself by his service to his country , but be able to go about pat his pocket , with an echo in his heart , every time it tinkle , that a quantity more to come into it be lie lock up in a drawer at home . these be the thing that breed present happiness in a noble human nature , all else be either of the future or the past ; and this be the reason why gold outweigh everything that can be say against it . 
 Captain Tugwell , in his pithy style , be will not to divide all human life into two distinctive tense the long - pipe time and the short - pipe time . the long - pipe time be of ease and leisure , comfort in the way of hot victual and cool pot , the stretching of leg without strain of muscle , and that ever - fresh well - spring of delight to the hard worker , the censorial but not censorious contemplation of equally fine fellow , equally lazy , yet peg hard , because of nothing in their pocket to tap . such be the golden period of standing , or , still well , sit with his back against a tree , and a cool yard of clay between his gently smile lip , shave with his girdle - knife a cake of rich tobacco , and then mill it complacently betwixt his horny palm , with his resolute eye relax into a gentle gaze at the labour sea , and the part   where his supper soon would be   warm into a fine condition for it , by good - will towards all the world . as for the short - pipe time , with a bitter gale dash the cold spray into his eye , leg drench with sleet , and shiver to the fork , and shoulder rack with rheumatism against the groaning mast , and the stump of a pipe keep chatter with his tooth away with all thought of such hardship now , except what would serve to fatten present comfort . 
 but fatherly feeling and sense of right compel Captain Zeb to check idle enjoyment from go too far i. e. , far than himself . every other member of his family but himself , however good the time might be , must work away as hard as ever , and earn whatever victual it should please the Lord to send they . there be always a job to be find , he know that , if a young man or maid have a mind for it ; and " no silver no supper " be the order of his house . his eld son Dan be the first to be drive for a good example to the young one and now he be set to work full time and overtime , upon a heavy job at Pebbleridge . 
 Young Daniel be not at all afraid of work , whenever there be any kind of skill to be show , or bodily strength to be prove by it . but the present task be hateful to he ; for any big - armed yokel , or common wood - hewer , might have do as much as he could do , and perhaps more , at it , and could have take the same wage over it . Mr. Coggs , of Pebbleridge , the only wheelwright within ten mile of Springhaven , have take a Government contract to supply within a certain time five hundred speak - wheel for ammunition tumbril , and as many block - wheel for small artillery ; and to hack out these latter for well man to finish be the daily task of Dan Tugwell . 
 this job swell his muscle and enlarge his calf , and fetch away all the fat he have be enable to form in lofty walk of art ; but these outward improvement be make at the expense of his inner and nobler quality . to hack and hew timber by the cubic foot , without any grow pleasure of proportion or design , to knit the brow hard for a struggle with knot , and smile the stern smile of destruction ; and then , after a long and rough walk in the dark for the equinox now be impend to be joke at by his father   who have lounge about all day , and have all his money tell into the paternal pocket , with narrow enquiry , each Saturday night . but bad of all to know that because he be not bear with a silver spoon in his mouth , he have no heart no heart that he could offer where he lay it ; but there it must lie , and be tread on in silence , while rakish - look popinjay but this reflection stop he , for it be too bitter to be think out , and fetch down his quiver hand upon his axe . enough that these thing do not tend to a healthy condition of mind , or the proper worship of the British Constitution . however , he be not quite a Radical yet . 




 CHAPTER XXVII 

 FAIR in theory 

 one Saturday evening , when the dusk be just begin to smoothe the break of billow and to blunt the edge of rock , young Dan Tugwell swing his axe upon his shoulder , with the flag basket hang from it in which his food have be , and in a rather crusty state of mind set forth upon his long walk home to Springhaven . as Harry Shanks have say , and almost everybody know , an ancient foot - path , little use , but never yet obstruct , cut off a large bend of the shore , and save half a mile of plod over rock and shingle . this path be very lonesome , and infest with dark place , as well as waylay with a very piteous ghost , who never would keep to the spot where he be murder , but might appear at any shady stretch or woody corner . Dan Tugwell know three courageous man who have see this ghost , and would take good care to avoid any further interview , and his own faith in ghost be as stanch as in gold ; yet such be his mood this evening that he determine to go that way and chance it , not for the saving of distance , but simply because he have be tell in the yard that day that the foot - path be stop by the landowner . " we will see about that , " say Dan ; and now he be go to see about it . 
 for the first field or two there be no impediment , except the usual stile or gate ; but when he have cross a little woodland hollow , where the fence of the castle ground run down to the brow of the cliff , he find entrance bar . three stout oak rail have be nail across from tree to tree , and on a board above they be roughly paint : " no thoroughfare . Tresspassers will be prosecute . " for a moment the young man hesitate , his dread of the law be virtuously deep , and his mind well assure that his father would not back he up against settled authority . but the shame of turn back , and the quick sense of wrong , which have long be demand some outlet , conquer his calm judgment , and he cast the basket from his back . then swing his favourite axe , he rush at the oaken bar , and with a few stroke send they roll down the steep bank - side . 
 " that for your stoppage of a right of way ! " he cry ; " and now perhaps you will want to know who do it . " 
 to gratify this natural curiosity he draw a piece of chalk from his pocket , and write on the notice - board in large round hand , " Daniel Tugwell , son of Zebedee Tugwell , of Springhaven . " but suddenly his smile of satisfaction flee , and his face turn as white as the chalk in his hand . at the next turn of the path , a few yard before he , in the gray gloom cast by an ivy - mantled tree , stand a tall dark figure , with the right arm raise . the face be indistinct , but   as Dan 's conscience tell he   hostile and unforgiving ; there be nothing to reflect a ray of light , and there seem to be a rustle of some departure , like the spirit flee . 
 the ghost ! what could it be but the ghost ? ghost ought to be white ; but terror scorn all prejudice . probably this murder one be bury in his breech . Dan 's heart beat quick than his axe have strike ; and his foot be off to beat the ground still quick . but no Springhaven lad ever leave his baggage . Dan leap aside first to catch up his basket , and while he stoop for it , he hear a clear strong voice . 
 " who be you , that have dare to come and cut my fence down ? " 
 no ghost could speak like that , even if he could put a fence up . the inborn courage of the youth revive , and the shame of his fright make he hardier . he step forward again , catch breath as he speak , and eager to meet any man in the flesh . 
 " I be Daniel Tugwell , of Springhaven . and no live man shall deny I of my right . I have a right to pass here , and I mean to do it . " 
 Caryl Carne , look stately in his suit of black velvet , draw sword and stand behind the shattered barrier . " be you ready to run against this ? " he ask . " poor peasant , go back ; what be your right worth ? " 
 " I could smash that skewer at a blow , " say Daniel , flourish his axe as if to do it ; " but my right , as you say , be not worth the hazard . what have a poor man to do with right ? would you stop a man of your own rank , Squire Carne ? " 
 " ah , that would be a different thing indeed ! justice wear a sword , because she be of gentle birth . work - people with axis must not prate of right , or a prison will be their next one . your right be to be disdain , young man , because you be not bear a gentleman ; and your duty be to receive scorn with your hat off . you like it , probably , because your father do . but come in , Daniel ; I will not deny you of the only right an english peasant have the right of the foot to plod in his father 's footstep . the right of the hand , and the tongue , and the stomach even the right of the eye be deny he ; but by some freak of law he have some little right of foot , doubtless to enable he to go and serve his master . " 
 Dan be amazed , and his well sense arouse . why should this gentleman step out of the rank of his birth , to talk in this way ? now and then Dan himself have indulge in such idea , but always with a doubt that they be wicked , and not long enough to make they seem good in his eye . he know that some fellow at " the Club " talk thus ; but they be a lot of idle stranger , who come there chiefly to corrupt the native , and work the fish trade out of their hand . these wholesome reflection make he doubt about accept Squire Carne 's invitation ; and it would have be good for he if that doubt have prevail , though he trudge a thousand mile for it . 
 " what ! break down a fence , and then be afraid to enter ! that be the style of your race , friend Daniel . that be why you never get your right , even when you dare to talk of they . I think you be make of different stuff . go home and boast that you shatter my fence , and then fear to come through it , when I ask you . " Carne smile at his antagonist , and wave his hand . 
 Dan leap in a moment through the hang splinter , and stand before the other , with a frown upon his face . " then mind one thing , sir , " he say , with a look of defiance , while touch his hat from force of habit , " I pass here , not with your permission , but of right . " 
 " very well . let we not split word , " say Carne , who have now quite recover his native language . " I be glad to find a man that dare to claim his right , in the present state of England . I be go towards Springhaven . give I the pleasure of your company , and the benefit of your opinion upon politic . I have hear the high praise of your ability , my friend . speak to I just as you would to one of your brother fisherman . by the accident of birth I be place differently from you ; and in this country that make all the difference between a man and a dog , in our value . though you may be , and probably be , the well man more truthful , more courageous , more generous , more true - hearted , and certain to be the more humble of the two . I have be bring up where all man be equal , and the thing I see here make a new world to I . very likely these be right , and all the rest of the world quite wrong . englishman always be certain of that ; and as I belong to the privileged class , my great desire be to believe it . only I want to know how the low order the dreg , the scum , the dirt under our foot , the slave that do all the work and get starve for it how these trample wretch regard the question . if they be happy , submissive , content , delight to lick the boot of their better , my conscience will be clear to accept their homage , and their money for any stick of mine they look at . but you have amaze I by a most outrageous act . because the low order have own a path here for some century , you think it wrong that they should lose their right . explain to I , Daniel , these extraordinary sentiment . " 
 " if you please , sir , " say Dan , who be follow in the track , though invite to walk by the side , of Caryl Carne , " I can hardly tell you how the low order feel , because father and I do not   belong to they . our family have always own their own boat , and work for their own hand , this two hundred year , and , for all we know , ever since the Romans be here . we call they the low order , as come round to pick up job , and have no settlement in our village . " 
 " a sound and very excellent distinction , Dan . but as against those who make the law , and take good care to enforce they , even you   though of the upper rank here   must be count of the low order . for instance , can you look at a pheasant , or a hare , without be put into prison ? can you dine in the same room with Admiral Darling , or ask how his gout be , without be star at ? " 
 " no , sir . he would think it a great impertinence , even if I dare to do such a thing . but my father might do it , as a tenant and old neighbour . though he never get the gout , when he rid about so much . " 
 " what a matter - of - fact youth it be ! but to come to thing every man have a right to . if you save the life of one of the Admiral 's daughter , and she fall in love with you , as young people will , would you dare even lift your eye to she ? would you not be kick out of the house and the parish , if you dare to indulge the right of every honest heart ? would you dare to look upon she as a human being , of the same order of creation as yourself , who might one day be your wife , if you be true and honest , and help to break down the absurd distinction build up by vile tyranny between you ? in a word , be you a man as every man be on the continent or only an english slave , of the low class ? " 
 the hot flush of wrath , and the soft glow of shame , meet and deepen each other on the fair cheek of this ' slave " ; while his mind would not come to he to make a fit reply . that his passion for dolly , his hopeless passion , should thus be discover by a man of her own rank , but not scorn or ridicule , only pity , because of his want of manly spirit ; that he should be call a ' slave " because of honest modesty , and even encourage in his wild hope by a gentleman , who have see all the world , and look down from a lofty distance on it ; that in his true estimate of thing there should be nothing but prejudice , low and selfish prejudice , between Well , he could not think it out ; that would take he many hour ; let this large - minded man begin again . it be so dark now , that if he turn round on he , unless he be a cat , he would be no wiser . 
 " you do well to take these thing with some doubt , " continue Carne , too sagacious to set up argument , which inure even young man in their own opinion ; " if I be in your place , I should do the same . century of oppression have stamp out the plain light of truth in those who be not allow it . to I , as an individual , it be well so . Chance have ordain that I should belong to the order of those who profit by it . it be against my interest to speak as I have do . be I likely to desire that my fence should be break , my property invade , the distinction so pleasing to I set aside , simply because I consider it a false one ? no , no , friend Daniel ; it be not for I to move . the present state of thing be entirely in my favour . and I never give expression to my sense of right and wrong , unless it be surprised from I by circumstance . your bold and entirely just proceeding have force I to explain why I feel no resentment , but rather admiration , at a thing which any other land - owner in England would not rest in his bed until he have avenge . he would drag you before a bench of magistrate and fine you . your father , if I know he , would refuse to pay the fine ; and to prison you would go , with the taint of it to lie upon your good name forever . the penalty would be wrong , outrageous , ruinous ; no rich man would submit to it , but a poor man must . be this the truth , Daniel , or be it what it ought to be a scandalous misdescription of the law of England ? " 
 " no , sir ; it be true enough , and too true , I be afraid . I never think of consequence , when I use my axe . I only think of what be right , and fair , and honest , as between a man who have a right , and one who take it from he . " 
 " that be the natural way to look at thing , but never permit in this country . you be fortunate in have to deal with one who have be bring up in a juster land , where all mankind be equal . but one thing I insist upon ; and remember it be the condition of my forbearance . not a single word to any one about your dashing exploit . no gentleman in the county would ever speak to I again , if I be know to have put up with it . " 
 " I be sure , sir , " say Daniel , in a truly contrite tone , " I never should have do such an impudent thing against you , if I have only know what a nice gentleman you be . I take you for nothing but a haughty land - owner , without a word to fle at a poor fisherman . and now you go ever so far beyond what the Club doth , in speak of the right that every poor man have not . I could listen to you by the hour , sir , and learn the difference between we and abroad . " 
 " Tugwell , I could tell you thing that would make a real man of you . but why should I ? you be well as you be ; and so be we who get all the good out of you . and besides , I have no time for politic at present . all my time be occupy with stern business collect the ruin of my property . " 
 " but , sir but you come down here sometimes from the castle in the evening ; and if I might cross , without claim right of way , sometimes I might have the luck to meet you . " 
 " certainly you may pass , as often as you please , and so may anybody who set value on his right . and if I should meet you again , I shall be glad of it . you can open my eye , doubtless , quite as much as I can your . good - night , my friend , and well fortune to you ! " 
 " it be worth my while to nail up those rail , " Carne say to himself , as he go home to his ruin . " I have hook that clod , as firm as ever he hook a cod . but , thousand thunder ! what do he mean , by go away without touch his hat to I ? " 




 CHAPTER XXVIII 

 FOUL in practice 

 " I hope , my dear , that your ride have do you good , " say the Rector 's wife to the Rector , as he come into the hall with a wonderfully red face , one fine afternoon in October . " if colour prove health , you have gain it . " 
 " Maria , I have not be so upset for many year . Unwholesome indignation dye my cheek , and that be almost as bad as indigestion . I have have quite a turn as you woman always put it . I be never move by little thing , as you know well , and sometimes to your great disgust ; but to - day my trouble have conspire to devour I . I be not so young as I be , Maria . and what will the parish come to , if I give in ? " 
 " exactly , dear ; and therefore you must not give in . " Mrs. Twemlow reply with great spirit , but her hand be tremble as she help he to pull off his new riding - coat . " remember your own exhortation , Joshua I be sure they be beautiful last Sunday . but take something , dear , to restore your circulation . a reaction in the system be so dangerous . " 
 " not anything at present , " Mr. Twemlow answer , firmly ; " these mental care be beyond the reach of bodily refreshment . let I sit down , and be sure where I be , and then you may give I a glass of treble x. in the first place , the pony nearly kick I off , when that idiot of a Stubbard begin fire from his battery . what have I do , or my peaceful flock , that a noisy set of gun should be set up amidst we ? however , I show Juniper that he have a master , though I shall find it hard to come down - stair tomorrow . well , the next thing be that I see James Cheeseman , Church - warden Cheeseman , Buttery Cheeseman , as the bad boy call he , in the lane , in front of I not more than thirty yard , as plainly as I now have the pleasure of see you , Maria ; and while I say ' kuck ' to the pony , he be go ! I particularly wish to speak to Cheeseman , to ask he some question about thing I have observe , and especially his sad neglect of public worship a most shameful example on the part of a church - warden and I be think how to put it , affectionately yet firmly , when , to my great surprise , there be no Cheeseman to receive it ! I call at his house on my return , about three hour afterwards , have make up my mind to have it out with he , when they positively tell I or at least Polly Cheeseman do that I must be mistake about her ' dear papa , ' because he be go in the pony - shay all the way to Uckfield , and would not be back till night . " 
 " the nasty little story - teller ! " Mrs. Twemlow cry . " but I be not at all surprised at it , when I see how she have get her hair do up , last Sunday . " 
 " no ; Polly believe it . I be quite sure of that . but what I want to tell you be much strange and more important , though it can not have anything at all to do with Cheeseman . you know , I tell you I be go for a good long ride ; but I do not tell you where , because I know that you would try to stop I . but the fact be that I have make up my mind to see what Caryl Carne be at , among his owl and ivy . you remember the last time I go to the old place I knock till I be tired , but could get no answer , and the window be stop with some rusty old spiked railing , where we use to be able to get in at the side . all the other be out of reach , as you know well ; and be of a yield nature , I come sadly home . and at that time I still have some faith in your friend Mrs. Stubbard , who promise to find out all about he , by mean of Widow Shanks and the dimity - parlour . but nothing have come of that . poor Mrs. Stubbard be almost as stupid as her husband ; and as for Widow shank I be quite sure , Maria , if your nephew be plot the overthrow of King , Church , and Government , that delude woman would not listen to a word against he . " 
 " she call he a model , and a blessed martyr " Mrs. Twemlow be smile at the thought of it ; " and she say she be a woman of great penetration , and never will listen to anything . but it only show what I have always say , that our family have a peculiar power , a sort of attraction , a superior gift of knowledge of their own mind , which make they but there , you be laugh at I , Joshua ! " 
 " not I ; but smile at my own good fortune , that ever I get my own way at all . but , Maria , you be right ; your family have always be distinguish for have its own way a masterful race , and a mistressful . and so much the more do the rest of mankind grow eager to know all about they . in an ordinary mind , such as mine , that feeling become at last irresistible ; and find no other way to gratify it , I resolve to take the bull by the horn , or rather by the tail , this morning . the poor old castle have be break up most grievously , even within the last twenty year , and you , who have play as a child among the ruin of the rampart , would scarcely know they now . you can not bear to go there , which be natural enough , after all the sad thing that have happen ; but if you do , you would be surprised , Maria ; and I believe a great part have be knock down on purpose . but you remember the little way in from the copse , where you and I , five - and - thirty year ago " 
 " of course I do , darling . it seem but yesterday ; and I have a flower now which you gather for I there . it grow at a very giddy height upon the wall , full of crack and place where the evening - star come through ; but up you go , like a rocket or a race - horse ; and what a fright I be in , until you come down safe ! I think that must have make up my mind to have nobody except my Joshua . " 
 " well , my dear , you might have do much bad . but I happen to think of that way in , this morning , when you put up your elbow , as you make the tea , exactly as you use to do when I might come up there . and that set I think of a quantity of thing , and among they this plan which I resolve to carry out . I take the trouble first to be sure that Caryl be down here for the day , under the roof of Widow Shanks ; and then I set off by the road up the hill , for the stronghold of all the Carnes . without further peril than the fight with the pony , and the strange apparition of Cheeseman about half a mile from the back entrance , I come to the copse where the violet use to be , and the sorrel , and the lord and lady . there I tether our friend Juniper in a quiet little nook , and cross the soft ground , without make any noise , to the place we use to call our little postern . it look so sad , compare with what it use to be , so desolate and bramble up and ruinous , that I scarcely should have know it , except for the gray pedestal of the prostrate dial we use to moralise about . and the ground inside it , that be nice turf once , with the rill run down it that perhaps supply the moat all stony now , and overgrown , and tangle , with ugly - look elder - bush sprawl through the ivy . to a painter it might have prove very attractive ; but to I it seem so dreary , and so sombre , and oppressive , that , although I be not sentimental , as you know , I actually turn away , to put my little visit off , until I should be in well spirit for it . and that , my dear Maria , would in all probability have be never . 
 " but before I have time to begin my retreat , a very extraordinary sound , which I can not describe by any word I know , reach my ear . it be not a roar , nor a clank , nor a boom , nor a clap , nor a crash , nor a thud , but if you have ever hear a noise combine all those element , with a small percentage of screech to enliven they , that come as near it as I can contrive to tell . we know from Holy Scripture that there use to be such creature as dragon , though we have never see they ; but I seem to be hear one as I stand there . it be just the sort of groan you might have expect from a dragon , who have swallow something highly indigestible . " 
 " my dear ! and he might have swallow you , if you have stop . how could you help run away , my Joshua ? I should have insist immediately upon it . but you be so terribly intrepid ! " 
 " far from it , Maria . quite the contrary , I assure you . in fact , I do make off , for a considerable distance ; not rapidly as a youth might do , but with self - reproach at my tardiness . but the sound cease come ; and then I remember how wholly we be in the hand of the Lord . a sense of the power of right rise within I , back up by a strong curiosity ; and I say to myself that if I go home , with nothing more than that to tell you , I should not have at all an easy time of it . therefore I resolve to face the question again , and ascertain , if possible , without self - sacrifice , what be go on among the ruin . you know every stick and stone , as they use to be , but not as they be at present ; therefore I must tell you . the wall at the bottom of the little Dial - court , where there use to be a sweet - briar hedge to come through , be entirely go , either tumble down or knock down the latter I believe to be the true reason of it . also , instead of sweet - briar , there be now a very flourishing crop of sting - nettle . but the wall at the side of the little court stand almost as sound as ever ; and what surprise I most be to see , when I get far , proceed of course very quietly , that the large court beyond   which use to be the servant ' yard , and the dry - ground , and general lounging - place   have a timber floor lay down it , with a rope on either side , a long heavy rope on either side ; and these rope be still quiver , as if from a heavy strain just loosen . all this I could see , because the high door with the spike , that use to part the dial - court from this place of common business , be fall forward from its upper hinge , and splay out so that I could put my fist through . 
 " by this time I have quite recover all my self - command , and be as calm as I be now , or even calm , because I be under that reaction which ensue when a sensible man have make a fool of himself . I perceive , without think , that the sound which have so scar I proceed from this gangway , or timberway , or staging , or whatever may be the right word for it ; and I make up my mind to stay where I be , only stoop a little with my body towards the wall , to get some idea of what might be go forward . and then I hear a sort of small hubbub of voice , such as foreigner make when they be order to keep quiet , and have to carry on a struggle with their noisy nature . 
 " this be enough to settle my decision not to budge an inch , until I know what they be up to . I could not see round the corner , mind though lady seem capable of do that , Maria and so these fellow , who seem to be in two lot , some at the top and some at the bottom of the plankway , be entirely out of my sight as yet , though I have a good view of their slide - plane . but presently the rope begin to strain and creak , draw taut as our fisherman express it either from the upper or the low end , and I see three barrel come slide down slide , not roll   you must understand , and not as a brewer deliver beer into a cellar . these pass by I ; and after a little while there come again that strange sepulchral sound , which have make I feel so uneasy . 
 " Maria , you know that I can hold my own against almost anybody in the world but you ; and although this place be far outside my parish boundary , I feel that as the Uncle of the present owner so far at least as the lawyer have not snap he up and the brother - in - law of the previous proprietor , I possess an undeniable legal right quo warranto , or whatever it be call to look into all proceeding on these premise . next to Holy Scripture , Horace be my guide and guardian ; and I call to mind a well - know passage , which may roughly be render thus : ' if the crushed world tumble on he , the ruin shall strike he undismayed . ' with this in my head , I go softly down the side - wall of the Dial - court   for there be no getting through the place where I have be peep   to the bottom , where there use to be an old flint wall , and a hedge of sweet - briar in front of it . you remember the pretty conceit I make quaint and wholesome as one of Herrick 's when you say something but I verily believe we be well in those day than we ever have be since . now do not   interrupt I about that , my dear . 
 " some of these briar still be there , or perhaps some of their descendant , straggle weakly among the nettle , and mullein , and other wild stuff , but make all together a pretty good screen , through which I could get a safe side - view of the bottom of the timber gangway . so I take off my hat , for some ruffian fellow like foreign sailor be stand below , throw out their arm , and make noise in their throat , because not allow to scream as usual . it be plain enough at once to any one who know the place , that a large hole have be cut in the solid castle wall , or rather , a loophole have be enlarge very freely on either side , and bring down almost to the level of the ground outside . on either side of this great opening stand three heavy musket at full cock , and it make my blood run cold to think how likely some fatal discharge appear . if I have be bring up to war , Maria , as all the young people be bind to be now , I might have be more at home with such matter , and able to reconnoitre calmly ; but I think of myself , and of you , and Eliza , and what a shocking thing it would be for all of we but a merciful Providence be over I . 
 " too late I regret the desire for knowledge , which have lead I into this predicament , for I durst not rush off from my very sad position , for my breath would soon fail I , and my low limb be thick from the exercise of hospitality . how I long for the wing of a dove , or at any rate for the leg of Lieutenant Blyth Scudamore ! and my dark apprehension gain double force when a stone be dislodge by my foot   which may have tremble , and roll with a sharp echo down into the ballium , or whatever it should be call , where these desperado stand . in an instant three of they have their long gun point at the very thicket which shelter I , and if I have move or attempt to make off , there would have be a vacancy in this preferment . but luckily a rabbit , who have be lie as close as I have , and as much afraid of I perhaps as I be of those ruffian , set off at full speed from the hop of the stone , and they see he , and take he for the cause of it . this enable I to draw my breath again , and consider the good way of make my escape , for I care to see nothing more , except my own house - door . 
 " happily the chance be not long in come . at a shout from below which seem to I to be in English , and sound uncommonly like ' now , then ! ' all those fellow turn their back to I , and begin very carefully to low , one by one , the barrel that have be let down the incline . and other thing be stand there , besides barrel : packing - case , crate , very bulky - look box , and low massive wheel , such as you often see to artillery . you know what a vast extent there be of cellar and vault below your old castle , most of they nearly as sound as ever , and occupy mainly by empty bottle , and the refuse of past hospitality . well , they be go to fill these with something french wine , smuggle brandy , contraband good of every kind you can think of , so long as high profit can be make of they . that be how your nephew Caryl mean to redeem his patrimony . no wonder that he have be so dark and distant ! it never would have do to let we get the least suspicion of it , because of my position in the Church , and in the Diocese . by this light a thousand thing be clear to I , which exceed all the power of the Sphinx till now . " 
 " but how do you get away , my darling Joshua ? " Mrs. Twemlow enquire , as behove she . " so fearless , so devoted , so alive to every call of duty how could you stand there , and let the wretch shoot at you ? " 
 " by take good care not to do it , " the Rector answer , simply . " no soon be all their back towards I , than I say to myself that the human race happily be not spiderine . I girt up my loin , or rather fetch my tail up under my arm very closely , and glide away , with the silence of the serpent , and the craft of the enemy of our fallen race . great care be needful , and I exercise it ; and here you behold I , unshot and unshot - at , and free from all anxiety , except a press urgency for a bowl of your admirable soup , Maria , and a cut from the saddle I see hang in the cellar . " 




 CHAPTER XXIX 

 MATERNAL ELOQUENCE 

 Sufficient for the day be the evil thereof ; and more than sufficient with most of we . Mr. Twemlow and his wife resolve discreetly , after a fireside council , to have nothing to say to Carne Castle , or about it , save what might be force out of they . they perceive most clearly , and very deeply feel , how exceedingly wrong it be for anybody to transgress , or even go aside of , the law of his country , as by Statute settle . still , if his ruin have be chiefly legal ; if he have be bring up under different law , and in place where they make those thing which he desire to deal in ; if it be clear that those thing be good , and their benefit might be extend to person who otherwise could have no taste of they ; above all , if it be the first and good desire of all who hear of it to have their own finger in the pie then let other stop it , who by duty and interest be so minded ; the Rector be not in the Commission of the Peace though he ought to have be there year ago and the breach of the law , if it come to that , be outside of his parish boundary . the voice of the neighbourhood would be with he , for not turn against his own nephew , even if it ever should come to be know that he have reason for suspicion . 
 it be hard to see thing in their proper light , if only one eye have a fly in it ; but if both be in that sad condition , who shall be blame for wink ? not only the pastor , but all his flock , be in need of wire spectacle now , to keep their vision clear and their forehead calm . Thicker than fly around the milk - pail , rumour come flit daily ; and even the night that fair time of thinking be busy with buzz multitude . 
 " long time have I live , and a sight have I seed , " say Zebedee Tugwell to his wife , " of thing as I could not   make no head nor tail of ; but nothing to my knowledge ever coom nigh the sort of way our folk have take to go on . Parson Twemlow tell we , when the war begin again , that the Lord could turn we all into Frenchmen , if we sin against he more than he could bear . I be fool enough to laugh about it then , not intake how it could be on this side of Kingdom Come , where no distinction be of person . but now , there it be a thing the Almighty hath in hand ; and who shall say he nay , when he layeth his hand to it ? " 
 " I reckon , ' a hath begin with you too , Zeb , " Mrs. Tugwell would answer , undesirably . " to be always go on so about trash trifle , as a woman hath a right to fly up at , but no man ! surely Dan hath a right to his politic and his parable , as much as any lame old chap that sitteth on a bench . he work hard all day , and he airn his money ; and any man hath a right to wag his tongue of night - time , when his arm and his leg have be wag all day . " 
 " depend upon how he wag ' un . " the glance of old Tugwell be stern , as he speak , and his eyebrow knit over it . " if for a yarn , to plaise child or maiden , or a bit of argyment about his business , or talk about his neighbour , or aught that consarn he why , lad must be fool , and I can smoke my pipe and think that at his age I be like he . but when it come to talk of his better , and the Government , and the right of everybody to command the ship , and the soup soup , what be it ? " 
 " superior position of the work class , dignity of labour , undefeasible right of mankind to the soil as they be bear in , and soshallistick something . " 
 " so shall I stick equality , " Mr. Tugwell amend , triumphantly ; " and so shall I stick he , by the holy poker , afore the end of the week be out . I have   a - be fool enough to leave off ropesending of he now for a matter of two year , because ' a be good , and outgrow of it like , and because you always coom between we . but mind you , mother , I will have none of that , next time . business I mean , and good measure it shall be . " 
 " Zeb Tugwell , " say his wife , long greatly to defy he , but frighten by the steadfast gaze she meet , " you can never mean to say that you would lay your hand on Dan a grown man , a'most as big as yourself , and a good half - head taller ! suppose he be to hit you back again ! " 
 " if he do , I should just kill he , " Zeb answer , calmly . " he would be but a jellyfish in my two hand . but there , I will not talk about it , mother . no need to trouble you with it . ' tis none of my seek the Lord in heaven know but a job as he hath dutifie for I to do . I will go out , and have my pipe , and dwell on it . " 
 " and I may lay a deal of it on myself , " Mrs. Tugwell begin to moan , as soon as he be go ; " for I have cocker Dan up , and there be no deny it , afore Tim , or Tryphena , or Tabby , or Debby , or even little Solomon . because he be the first , and so like his dear father , afore he get on in the world so . oh , it all come of that , all the trouble come of that , and of lay up of money , apart from your wife , and forget almost of her christian name ! and the very same thing of it money , money , and the getting on with breech that requireth no mending , and the look over church - book at gay young lady all of it leadeth to the same bad end of his better , and the Government , and the Soshallistick Quality . 
 " why , with all these mercy , " continue Mrs. Tugwell , though not in a continuous frame of mind , as Daniel come in , with a slow heavy step , and sit down by the fire in silence , " all these mercy , as be buy and pay for , from one and sixpence up to three half - crown , and give no more trouble beyond dust once a week how any one can lay his eye on other people 's property , without consideration of his own , as will be after his poor mother 's time , be to I quite a puzzle and a pin - prick . not as if they be owe for , or buy at auction , or so much as beat down by sixpence , but all at full price and own judgment , pay for by airning of labour and peril of the deep . and as Widow Shanks say , the last time she be here , by spoil of the enemy of England , who make we pay tremenjious for ' most everything we live on . and I know who would understand they crackerie , and dust they when I be go to dust , and see her own pretty face in they , whenever they have the back - varnish . " 
 Dan know that the future fair owner and duster design by his mother be Miss Cheeseman , towards whom he have cherish tender yearning in the sensible and wholesome day . and if Polly Cheeseman have hang herself on high which she might have do without a bit of arrogance perhaps she would still have be to this young man the star of fate and glory , instead of a dip , thirty - two to the pound ; the like whereof she sell for a farthing . distance make the difference . " he that will not   allow heed shall pay dear in his need ; " the good mother grow warm , as the son begin to whistle ; " and to my mind , Master Dan , it will not   be long afore you have homer thing to think of than politic . ' politic be fiddle - stick ' be what man of my age use to say ; sensible man with a house and freehold , and a pig of their own , and experience . and such a man I might have have , and sensible child by he , child as never would have whistle at their mother , if it have not   be for your poor father , Dan . misguided he may be , and too much of his own way , and not well enough in his own mind to take in a woman 's but for all that he hath a right to be honour by his child , and to lead their mind in matter touch of the king , and Church , and true religion . why only last night , no , the night afore last , I meet Mrs. Prater , and I say to she " 
 " you tell I all that , mother ; and it must have be a week ago ; for I have hear it every night this week . what be it you desire that I should do , or say , or think ? " 
 " holy mercy ! " cry Mrs. Tugwell , " what a way to put thing , Dan ! all I desire be for your good only , and so lead on to the comfort of the rest . for the whole place go wrong , and the cat sit in the corner , when you go on with politic as your dear father grunt at . no doubt it may all be very fine and just , and worth a man give his life for , if he do not   care about it , nor nobody else but even if it be to keep the French out , and yourn goeth near to let they in , what difference of a button would it make to we , Dan , compare to our stick together , and feed with a knowledge and a yielding to the fancy of each other ? "
