 " I be sure it be no fault of mine , " say Daniel , move from his high rope by this last appeal ; " to I it never matter twopence what I have for dinner , and you see I give Tim all the brown of the baked potato the very last time I have my dinner here . but what come above all those little bother be the necessity for insist upon freedom of opinion . I do not   pretend to be so old as my father , nor to know so much as he know about the world in general . but I have read a great deal more than he have , of course , because he take a long time to get a book with the right end to he ; and I have think , without know it , about what I have read , and I have hear very clever man   who could have no desire to go wrong , but quite the other way   carry on about these high subject , beyond I , but full of plain language . and I will not   be force out of a word of it by fear . " 
 " but for love of your mother you might keep it under , and think it all inside you , without bring of it out , in the presence of your elder . you know what your father be a man as never yet lay his tongue to a thing without do of it right or wrong , right or wrong ; and this time he hath right , and the law , and the Lord , and the King himself , to the side of he . and a rope's - end in his pocket , Dan , as I try to steal away , but he be too wide - awake . such a big hard one you never do see ! " 
 " a rope 's end for I , well turn twenty year of age ! " cry Daniel , with a laugh , but not a merry one ; " two can play at that game , mother . I will not be rope end by nobody . " 
 " then you will be rope - noosed ; " the poor mother fall into the settle , away from the fire - light , and put both hand over her eye , to shut out the spectacle of Dan dangle ; " or else your father will be , for you . ever since the Romans , Dan , there have be Tugwells , and respect ten time more than they be . oh do ' e , do ' e think ; and not bring we all to the grave , and then the gallow ! why I can mind the time , no more agone than last Sunday , when you use to lie here in the hollow of my arm , without a stitch of clothe on , and kind people be tempt to smack you in pleasure , because you do stick out so prettily . for a well - form baby there never be see , nor a finer - temper one , when he have his way . and the many night I walk the floor with you , Dan , when your first tooth be come through , the size of a horse - radish , and your father most wonderful to put up with my coo to you , when he have not have a night in bed for nigh three week oh , Dan , do ' e think of thing as consarneth your homer life , and thing as be above all reason ; and let they bless politic go home to they as trade in they . " 
 Mrs. Tugwell 's tender recollection have give she a pain in the part where Dan be nurse , and drive she out of true logical course ; but she come back to it , before Dan have time to finish the interesting picture of himself which she have suggest . 
 " now can you deny a word of that , Dan ? and if not , what be there more to say ? you be smack as a little babe , by many people kindly , when ever so much tender than you now can claim to be . and in those day you never could have deserve it yet , not have frame a word beyond ' Mam , ' and ' da , ' and both of those make much of , because doubtful . there be nothing about the Constitooshun then , but the colour of the tongue and the condition of the bowel ; and if any fool have ask you what politic be , you would have suck your thumb , and offer they to suck it ; for generous you always be , and just come after . and what cry have big folk , grow upright and wicked , to make about be smack , when they deserve it , for meddle with matter outside of their business , by those in authority over they ? " 
 " well , mother , I daresay you be right , though I do not   altogether see the line of it . but one thing I will promise you whatever father do to I , I will not lift a hand against he . but I must be off . I be late already . " 
 " where to , Dan ? where to ? I always use to know , even if you be go court . go a - courting , Dan , as much as ever you like , only do not   make no promise . but whatever you do , keep away from that bad , wicked , Free and Frisky Club , my dear . " 
 " Mother , that be the very place I be just bind to . after all you have say , I would have stay away to - night , except for be on the list , and pledge in honour to twenty - eight question , all bear upon the grand issue of the age . " 
 " I do not   know no more than the dead , what that mean , Dan . but I know what your father have get in his pocket for you . and he say the next time you go there , you should have it . " 




 CHAPTER XXX 

 paternal DISCIPLINE 

 " the Fair , free , and Frisky " as they call themselves , be not of a violent order at all , neither treasonable , nor even disloyal . their Club , if it deserve the name , have not be of political , social , or even convivial intention , but have lapse unaware into all three us , and most of all that last mention . the hard the time be , the more confidential   and therefore convivial   do Englishmen become ; and if free - trade survive with we for another decade , it will be the death of total abstinence . but now they have bad time , without free - trade that Goddess be still in the goose - egg and when two friend meet , without a river between they , they be bind to drink one another 's health , and do it , without the unstable and cold - blooded element . the sense of this duty be paramount among the " Free and Frisky , " and without it their final cause would have vanish long ago , and therewith their formal one . 
 none of the old - establish folk of the blue blood of Springhaven , such as the Tugwells , the Shankses , the Praters , the Bowleses , the Stickfasts , the Blocks , or the Kedgers , would have anything to do with this Association , which have form itself among they , like an anti - corn - law league , for the destruction of their right and property . its origin have be commercial , and its principle aggressive , no less an outrage be contemplate than the purchase of fish at low figure on the beach , and the speedy distribution of that slippery ware among the near village and town . but from time immemorial the trade have be in the hand of a few staunch factor , who pay a price govern by the season and the weather , and send the commodity as far as it would go , with soundness , and the hope of freshness . Springhaven believe that it supply all London , and be proud and blest in so believe . with these barrowman , huckster and pedlar of fish , it would have no manifest dealing ; but if the factor who manage the trade choose to sell their refuse or surplus to they , that be their own business . in this way perhaps , and by bargain on the sly , these petty dealer manage to procure enough to carry on their weekly enterprise , and for a certain good reason take a room and court - yard handy to the Darling Arms , to discuss other people 's business and their own . the good reason be that they be not allow to leave the village , with their barrow or truck or basket , until the night have fall , on penalty of be pelt with their own ware . such be the dignity of this place , and its noble abhorrence of anything low . 
 the vision of lofty institution , which one may not participate , inspire in the low human nature more jealousy than admiration . these higgler may have be very honest fellow , in all but pecuniary question , and possibly continue to be so in the bosom of their own family . but here in Springhaven , by the force of circumstance they be almost compel to be radical : even as the sweetest cow 's milk turn sour , when she can just reach red clover with her breath , but not her lip . but still they be not without manner , and reason , and good - will to people who have patience with they . this enable they to argue lofty question , without black eye , or kicking , or even tweak of nose ; and a very lofty question be now before they . 
 to get once into Admiral Darling 's employment be to obtain a vested interest ; so kind be his nature and so forgiving , especially when he have scold anybody . Mr. Swipes , the head gardener for so many year , hold an estate of freehold in the garden although he have no head , and would never be a gardener , till the hang garden of Babylon should be hang on the top of the tower of Babel with a vested remainder to his son , and a contingent one to all descendant . yet this man , although his hand be generally in his pocket , have not enough sense of their lining to feel that continuance , usage , institution , orderly sequence , heredity , and such like , be the button of his coat and the texture of his breech , and the warmth of his body inside they . therefore he never could hold aloof from the Free and Frisky gathering , and accept the chair upon Bumper - night , when it be a sinecure benefice . 
 this be a bumper - night , and in the chair sit Mr. Swipes , discharge gracefully the arduous duty of the office , which consist mainly in call upon member for a speech , a sentiment , or a song , and in default of mental satisfaction , bodily amendment by a pint all round . but as soon as Dan Tugwell enter the room , the Free and Friskies with one accord return to lofty business . Mr. Swipes , the gay Liber of the genial hour , retire from the chair , and his place be take by a Liberal though the name be not yet invent estranged from his own godfather . this be a hard man , who make salt herring , and long to cure everything fresh in the world . 
 Dan , be still a very tender youth , and quite unaccustomed to public speaking , be abash by these token of his own importance , and heartily wish that he have stop at home . it never occur to his simple mind that his value be not political , but commercial ; not " anthropological , " but fishy , the main ambition of the Free and Frisky Club have long be the capture of his father . if once Zeb Tugwell could be bring to treat , a golden era would dawn upon they , and a boundless vision of free - trade , when a man might be pay for refuse to sell fish , as he now be for keep to himself his screw . Dan know not these thing , and his heart misgave he , and he wish that he have never hear of the twenty - eight question set down in his name for solution . 
 however , his disturbance of mind be needless , concern those great issue . all the member , except the chairman , have forget all about they ; and the only matter they care about be to make a new member of Daniel . a little flourish go on about large thing   which nobody know , or care to know , then the table be hammer with the heel of a pipe , and Dan be make a Free and Frisky . an honorary member , with nothing to pay , and the honour on their side , they tell he ; and every man rise , with his pot in one hand and his pipe in the other , yet able to stand , and to thump with his heel , be careful . then the President make entry in a book , and bow , and Dan be request to sign it . in the fervour of good - will , and fine feeling , and the pride of popularity , the young man be not old enough to resist , but set his name down firmly . then all shake hand with he , and the meeting be declare to be festive , in honour of a new and noble member . 
 it be altogether wrong to say though many people say it that young Dan Tugwell be even a quarter of a sheet in the wind , when he steer his way home . his head be as solid as that of his father ; which , instead of grow light , increase in specific , generic , and differential gravity , under circumstance which tend otherwise , with an age like our , that insist upon sobriety , without allow practice . all Springhaven folk have long practice in the art of keep sober , and if ever a man walk with his leg outside his influence , it be always from defect of proper average quite lately . 
 be that as it may , the young man come home with an enlarged map of the future in his mind , a brisk and elastic rise in his walk , and his head much encourage to go on with liberal and indescribable feeling . in accordance with these , he expect his mother to be ready to embrace he at the door , while a saucepan simmer on the good - night of the wood - ash , with just as much gentle breath of onion from the cover as a youth may taste dreamily from the lip of love . but oh , instead of this , he meet his father , spread out and yet solid across the doorway , with very large arm bare and lumpy in the gleam of a fireplace uncrowned by any pot . Dan 's large idea vanish , like a blaze without a bottom . 
 " rather late , Daniel , " say the captain of Springhaven , with a nod of his great head , make gigantic on the ceiling . " all the rest be abe , the proper place for honest folk . I suppose you have   be airne money , overtime ? " 
 " not I , " say Dan ; " I work hard enough all day . I just look in at the Club , and have a little talk of politic . " 
 " the Club , indeed ! the stink barrow - grinder ! do I tell you , or do I forget to tell you , never to go there no more ? " 
 " you tell I fast enough , father ; no doubt about that . but I be not aboard your boat , when I happen on dry land , and I be old enough now to have opinion of my own . " 
 " oh , that be it , be it ? and to upset all the State , the King , the House of Lords , and the Parliamentary House , and all as be descend from the Romans ? well , and what do their Wusships say to you ? do they anoint you king of slooshing ? " 
 " Father , they do this and you have a right to know it ; " Dan speak with a grave debative tone , though his voice become doubtful , as he see that his father be quietly seek for something ; " almost before I know what be come , they have make I a member , and I sign the book . they have no desire to upset the kingdom ; I hear no talk of that kind ; only that every man should have his own opinion , and be free to show what can be say for they . and you know , father , that the world go on by reason , and justice , and good - will , and fair play " 
 " no , it do not , " cry the captain , who have find what he want ; " if it have to wait for they , it would never go on at all . it go on by government , and management , and discipline , and the stopping of younker from their bless foolery , and by the ten commandment , and the proverb of King Solomon . you to teach your father how the world go on ! off with your coat , and I will teach you . " 
 " Father , " say Dan , with his milder nature tremble at the stern resolution in his father 's eye , as the hearth - fire flash up show their strong flash , " you will never do such a thing , at my age and size ? " 
 " will not   I ? " answer Zebedee , crack in the air the three knot tail of the stout hempen twist . " as for your age , why , it ought to know well ; and as for your size , why , the more room for this ! " 
 it never come into Daniel 's head that he should either resist or run away . but into his heart come the deadly sense of disgrace at be flog , even by his own father , at full age to have a wife and even child of his own . 
 " Father , " he say , as he pull off his coat and red striped shirt , and show his broad white back , " if you do this thing , you will never set eye on my face again so help I God ! " 
 " do not   care if I do not , " the captain shout . " you be never son of mine , to be a runagate , and traitor . how old be you , Master Free and Frisky , to larn I how the world go on ? " 
 " as if you do not   know , father ! the fifteenth of last March I be twenty year of age . " 
 " then one for each year of your life , my lad , and another to make a man of thee . this little tickler hath three tail ; seven three be twenty - one come just right . " 
 when his father have do with he , Dan go softly up the dark staircase of old ship timber , and enter his own little room , strike a light . he see that his bed be turn down for he , by the love hand of his mother , and that his favourite brother Solomon , the young of the tugwell race , be sleep sweetly in the opposite cot . then he catch a side view of his own poor back in the little black - frame look - glass , and be quite amazed ; for he have not feel much pain , neither flinch , nor wince , nor speak . in a moment self - pity do more than pain , indignation , outrage , or shame could do ; it bring large tear into his softened eye , and a long sob into his swell throat . 
 he have bear himself like a man when flog ; but now he behave in the manner of a boy . " he shall never hear the last of this job , " he mutter , " as long as mother have a tongue in her head . " to this end he fill a wet sponge with the red proof of his scourging , lay it where it must be see , and beside it a leaf tear from his wage - book , on which he have write with a trembling hand : " he say that I be no son of his , and this look like it . sign , Daniel Tugwell , or whatever my name ought to be . " 
 then he wash and dress with neat' - foot oil all of his wound that he could reach , and tie a band of linen over they , and , in spite of increase smart and pang , dress himself carefully in his Sunday clothe . from time to time he listen for his father 's step , inasmuch as there be no bolt to his door , and to burn a light so late be against all law . but nobody come to disturb he ; his mother at the end of the passage sleep heavily , and his two child - sister in the room close by , Tabby and Debby , be in the land of dream , as far go as little solly be . have turn out his tool from their flat flag basket , or at least all but three or four favourite , he fill it with other clothe likely to be need , and buckle it over his hatchet - head . then the beating of his heart be like a flail inside a barn , as he steal along silently for one terrible good - bye . 
 this be to his darling pet of all pet , Debby , who worship this brother a great deal more than she worship her heavenly Father ; because , as she say to her mother , when rebuke " I can see Dan , mother , but I can not   see he . can I sit in his lap , mother , and look into his face , and be tell pretty story , and eat apple all the time ? " Tabby be of different grain , and her deity be Tim ; for she be of the Tomboy kind , and have no imagination . but Debby be enough to make a sound and seasoned heart to ache , as she lie in her little bed , with the flush of sleep deepen the delicate tint of her cheek , shed bright innocence fresh from heaven on the tranquil droop of eyelid and the smile curve of lip . her hair lie flutter , as if by play with the angel that protect she ; and if she could not see her heavenly Father , it be not because she be out of his sight . 
 a well tear than be ever shed by self - pity , or any other selfishness , run down the cheek she have kiss so often , and fall upon her coax , nestling neck . then Dan , with his candle behind the curtain , set a long light kiss upon the forehead of his darling , and with a heart so full , and yet so empty , take one more gaze at she , and then be go . with the basket in his hand , he drop softly from his window upon the pile of seaweed at the back of the house collect to make the wall wholesome and then , care little what his course might be , be lead perhaps by the force of habit down the foot - path towards the beach . so late at night , it be not likely that any one would disturb he there , and no one in the cottage which he have leave would miss he before the morning . the end of October now be near , the night be long , and he need not hurry . he might even lie down in his favourite boat , the good of her size in Springhaven , the one he have build among the rabbit . there he could say good - bye to all that he have know and love so long , and be off before dawn , to some place where he might earn his crust and think his thought . 




 CHAPTER XXXI 

 SORE TEMPTATION 

 when a man 's spirit and heart be low , and the world seem turn against he , he have well stop both ear than hearken to the sound of the sad sea wave at night . even if he can see their movement , with the moon behind they , draw path of ripple light , and boat   with white sail plume shadow , or thin oar that dive for gem , and perhaps a merry crew with music , come home not all sea - sick well , even so , in the summer sparkle , the long low fall of the wave be sad . but how much more on a winter night , when the moon be away below the sea , and weary water roll unseen from a vast profundity of gloom , fall unreckoned , and be no more than a wistful moan , as man be ! 
 the tide be at quarter - ebb , and a dismal haze lie thick on shore and sea . it be not enough to be call a fog , or even a mist , but quite enough to deaden the gray light , always flow along the boundary of sky and sea . but over the wet sand and the white frill of the gently gurgle wave more of faint light , or rather perhaps , less of heavy night , prevail . but Dan have keen eye , and be well accustomed to the trick of darkness ; and he come to take his leave forever of the fishing squadron , with a certainty of know all the five , as if by daylight for now there be only five again . 
 as the tide withdraw , the fishing - smack   which have scarcely earn their name of late   be compel to make the good of the world until the tide come back again . to judge by creaking , straining , groaning , and even grinding of timber millstone   if there yet live in Ireland the good - will for a loan to we , all these little craft be make dreadful hardship of the abandonment which man and nature inflict on they every thirteenth hour . but all thing do make more noise at night , when they get the chance   perhaps in order to assert their own prerogative , and they seem to know that noise go far , and assume a high character , when man have leave off make it . 
 the poor young fisherman 's back be get very sore by this time , and he begin to look about for the white side - streak which he have paint along the water - line of that new boat , to distract the meddlesome gaze of rival from the peculiar curve below , which even Admiral Darling have not notice , when he pass she on the beach ; but Nelson would have spy it out in half a second , and know all about it in the other half . Dan know that he should find a very fair berth there , with a roll or two of stuff to lay his back on , and a piece of tarpaule to draw over his leg . in the faint light that hover from the breaking of the wavelet he soon find his boat , and see a tall man stand by she . 
 " Daniel , " say the tall man , without move , " my sight be very bad at night , but unless it be bad than usual , you be my admired friend Daniel . a young man in a thousand one who dare to think . " 
 " yes , Squire Carne , " the admired friend reply , with a touch of hat protest against any claim to friendship : " Dan Tugwell , at your service . and I have think too much , and be pay out for it . " 
 " you see I in a melancholy attitude , and among melancholy surrounding . " Caryl Carne offer his hand as he speak , and Dan take it with great reverence . " the truth be , that anger at a gross injustice , which have just come to my knowledge , drive I from my book and sad family paper , in the room beneath the roof of our good Widow shank . and I need must come down here , to think beside the sea , which seem to be the only free thing in England . but I little expect to see you . " 
 " and I little expect to be here , Squire Carne . but if not make too bold to ask be it anybody that be beat ? " 
 " Beaten be not the right word for it , Dan ; cruelly flog and lash , a dear young friend of mine have be , as fine a young fellow as ever live and now he have not get a sound place on his back . and why ? because he be poor , and dare to lift his eye to a rich young lady . " 
 " but he be not flog by his own father ? " ask Dan , deeply interested in this romance , and rub his back , as the pain increase with sympathy . 
 " not quite so bad as that , " reply the other ; ' such a thing would be impossible , even in England . no ; his father take his part , as any father in the world would do ; even if the great man , the young lady 's father , should happen to be his own landlord . " 
 a very black suspicion cross the mind of Dan , for Carne possess the art of suggest vile suspicion : might Admiral Darling have discover something , and request Dan 's father to correct he ? it be certain that the Admiral , so kind of heart , would never have desire such severity ; but he might have tell Captain Tugwell , with whom he have a talk almost every time they meet , that his eld son want a little discipline ; and the Club might have serve as a pretext for this , when the true crime must not be declare , by reason of its enormity . Dan close his tooth , and english air grow bitter in his mouth , as this belief run through he . 
 " good - night , my young friend ; I be begin to recover , " Carne continue , briskly , for he know that a nail snap in good oak , when the hammer fall too heavily . " what be a little bit of outrage , after all ? when I have be in England a few year more , I shall laugh at myself for have love fair play and self - respect , in this innocent young freshness . we must wag as the world do ; and you know the proverb , what make the world wag , but the weight of the bag ? " 
 " but if you be more in earnest , sir or at least I mean , if you be not bind here by property and business , and an ancient family , and thing you could not get away from , and if you want only to be allow fair play , and treat as a man by other man , and be able to keep your own money when you earn it , or at least to buy your own victual with it what would you try to do , or what part of the country would you think well to go to ? " 
 " Dan , you must belong to a very clever family . it be useless to shake your head you must ; or you never could put such question , so impossible to answer . in all this blessed island , there be no spot yet discover , where such absurd vision can be realize . Nay , nay , my romantic friend ; be content with more than the average blessing of this land . you be not starve , you be not imprison , you be not even beat ; and if you be not allow to think , what harm of that ? if you think all day , you would never dare to act upon your thought , and so you be well without they . tush ! an Englishman be never bear for freedom . good - night . " 
 " but , sir , Squire Carne , " cry Dan , pursue he , " there be one thing which you do not seem to know . I be drive away from this place to - night ; and it would have be so kind of you to advise I where to go to . " 
 " drive away ! " exclaim Carne , with amazement . " the pride of the village drive out of it ! you may be drive yourself away , Tugwell , through some scrape , or love affair ; but when that blow over you will soon come back . what would Springhaven do without you ? and your dear good father would never let you go . " 
 " I be not the pride , but the shame , of the village . " Dan forget all his home - pride at last . " and my dear good father be the man who have do it . he have leather I bad than the gentleman you speak of , and without half so much to be say against he . for nothing but go to the Club to - night , where I be sure we drink King George 's health , my father have lash I so , that I be ashamed to tell it . and I be sure that I never mean to tell it , until your kindness , in a way of speak , almost drive it out of I . " 
 " Daniel Tugwell , " Carne answer , with solemnity , " this be beyond belief , even in England . you must have fall asleep , Dan , in the middle of large thought , and dream this great impossibility . " 
 " my back know whether it have be a dream , sir . I never hear of dream as leave one - and - twenty line behind they . but whether it be one , or whether it be twenty , make no odd of value . the disgrace it be that drive I out . " 
 " be there no way of heal this sad breach ? " Carne ask , in a tone of deep compassion ; " if your father could be bring to beg your pardon , or even to say that he be sorry " 
 " he , sir ! if such a thing be put before he , his answer would be just to do it again , if I be fool enough to go near he . you be too mild of nature , sir , to understand what father be . " 
 " it be indeed horrible , too horrible to think of " the voice of this kind gentleman betray that he be shudder . " if a Frenchman do such a thing , he would be tear to piece . but no french father would ever dream of such atrocity . he would rather flog himself within an inch of his own life . " 
 " be they so much well , then , and kinder , than we Englishmen ? " in spite of all his pain and grief , Dan could not help smile at the thought of his father ropesende himself . " so superior to we , sir , in every way ? " 
 " in almost every way , I be sorry to confess . I fear , indeed , in every way , except bodily strength , and obstinate , ignorant endurance , miscall ' courage , ' and those rough quality whatever they may be which seem needful for the making of a seaman . but in good manner , justice , the sense of what be due from one man to another , in dignity , equality , temperance , benevolence , largeness of feeling , and quickness of mind , and above all in love of freedom , they be very , very sadly far beyond we . and indeed I have be lead to think from some of your fine perception , Dan , that you must have a share of french blood in your vein . " 
 " I , sir ! " cry Dan , jump back , in a style which show the distance between faith and argument ; " no , sir , thank God there be never none of that ; but all English , with some of the Romans , who be pretty near equal to we , from what I hear . I suppose , Squire Carne , you think that low of I because I make a fuss about be larrup , the same as a Frenchman I pull out of the water do about my doing of it , as if I could have help it . no Englishman would have say much about that ; but they seem to make more fuss than we do . and I dare say it be french - like of I , to go on about my hiding . " 
 " Daniel , " answer Caryl Carne , in alarm at this british sentiment ; " as a man of self - respect , you have only one course leave , if your father refuse to apologise . you must cast off his tyranny ; you must prove yourself a man ; you must begin life upon your own account . no more of this drudgery , and slavery for other , who allow you no right in return . but a nobler employment among free people , with a chance of assert your courage and manhood , and a certainty that no man will think you his bondslave because you be bear upon his land , or in his house . my father behave to I well , it do not matter . he might have repent of it , if he have live long ; and I feel ashamed to speak of it , after such a case as your . but behold , how greatly it have be for my advantage ! without that , I might now have be a true and simple Englishman ! " 
 Carne   who have take most kindly to the fortune which make he an untrue Englishman   clap his breast with both hand ; not proudly , as a Frenchman do , nor yet with that abashment and contempt of demonstration which make a true Briton very clumsy in such doing ; while Daniel Tugwell , be very solid , and by no mean " emotional " as people call it nowadays be look at he , to the utmost of his power   which would have be great by daylight , with gratitude , and wonder , and consideration , and some hesitation about his foreign sentiment . 
 " well , sir , " say Dan , with the usual impulse of the british workman , " be there any sort of work as you could find for I , to earn my own living , and be able to think afterwards ? " 
 " there be work of a noble kind , such as any man of high nature may be proud to share in , to which it be possible that I might get an entrance for you , if there should be a vacancy ; work of high character , such as admit of no higgling and haggling , and splitting of halfpence , but an independent feeling , and a sense of advance the liberty of mankind , without risk a penny , but put many guineas into one 's own pocket , and so become fit for a loftier line of life . " 
 " be it smuggling , sir ? " Daniel ask , with sore misgiving , for he have be bring up to be very shy of that . " many folk consider that quite honest ; but father call it roguery though I never shall hear any more of his opinion now . " 
 " Sigh not , friend Daniel ; sigh not so heavily at your own emancipation . " carne never could resist the chance of a little bit of sarcasm , though it often injure his own plot . " Smuggling be a very fine pursuit , no doubt , but petty in comparison with large affair like ours . no , Dan Tugwell , I be not a smuggler , but a high politician , and a polisher of mankind . how soon do you think of leave this outrageous hole ? " 
 despite the stupid outrage upon himself , Dan be too loyal and generous of nature to be pleased with this description of his native place . but Carne , too quick of temper for a really fine intriguer , cut short his expostulation . 
 " call it what you please , " he say ; " only make your mind up quickly . if you wish to remain here , do so : a man of no spirit be useless to I . but if you resolve to push your fortune among brave and lofty comrade , stir scene , and brisk adventure , meet I at six to - morrow evening , at the place where you chop down my rail . all you want will be provide , and your course of promotion begin at once . but remember , all must be honour bright . no shilly - shallying , no lukewarmness , no indifference to a noble cause . faint heart never win fair lady . " 
 the wan moon have rise , and now shine upon Carne 's face , light up all its gloomy beauty , and strange power of sadness . Dan seem to lose his clear keen sight beneath the dark influence of the other 's gaze ; and his will , though not a weak one , drop before a large and strong . " he know all about I and Miss dolly , " say the poor young fisherman to himself ; " I think so before , and I be certain of it now . and , for some reason beyond my knowledge , he wish to encourage it . oh , perhaps because the Carnes have always be against the darling ! I never think of that before . " 
 this be a bitter reflection to he , and might have incline he the right way , if time have allow he to work it out . but no such time be afford ; and in the confusion and gratitude of the moment , he answer , " Sir , I shall be always at your service , and do my very good in every way to please you . " Caryl Carne smile ; and the church clock of Springhaven solemnly strike midnight . 




 CHAPTER XXXII 

 the trial of faith 

 he follow day , the 27th of October , be a dark one in the calendar of a fair and good young lady . two year would then have pass since Faith Darling , at the age of twenty , have receive sad tiding , which would make the rest of her life flow on in shadow . so at least she think , forgetful   or rather perhaps unconscious , for she have not yet learn the fact of life   that time and the tide of year submerge the lofty youthful sorrow . to a warm and stedfast heart like her , and a nature strong but self - control , no casual change , or light diversion , or sudden interest in other matter , could take the place of the motive lose . therefore , be of a deep true faith , and staunch in the belief of a great God , good to all who seek his goodness , she never go away from what she mean , that faith and hope should feed each other . 
 this save she from be a trouble to any one , or damp anybody 's cheerfulness , or diminish the gaiety around she . she take a lively interest in the affair of other people , which a " blighted being " decline to do ; and their pleasure minister to her own good cheer without , or at any rate beyond , her knowledge . therefore she be like by everybody , and belove by all who have any heart for a brave and pitiful story . thus a sweet flower , half close by the storm , continue to breathe forth its sweetness . 
 however , there be time when even Faith be lose in sad remembrance , and her bright young spirit become depressed by the hope defer that maketh sick the heart . as time grow long , hope grow less ; and even the cheerful Admiral , well verse in peril of the deep , and acquaint with many a wandering story , have make up his mind that Erle Twemlow be dead , and would never more be hear of . the rector also , the young man 's father , could hold out no long against that conclusion ; and even the mother , disdain the mention , yet understand the meaning , of despair . and so among those to whom the subject be the most interesting in the world , it be now the strict rule to avoid it with the lip , though the eye be often fill with it . 
 Faith Darling at first scorn this hard law . " it do seem so unkind , " she use to say , " that even his name should be interdict , as if he have disgrace himself . if he be dead , he have die with honour . none who ever see he can doubt that . but he be not dead . he will come back to we , perhaps next week , perhaps to - morrow , perhaps even while we be afraid to speak of he . if it be for my sake that you behave thus , I be not quite so weak as to require it . " 
 the peculiar circumstance of the case have not only baffle enquiry , but from the very beginning preclude it . the man with the keen eye , sharp nose , big ear , and long head , of all the many sneak who now conduct what they call ' special enquiry , " could have do nothing with a case like this , because there be no beginning it . even now , in fair peace , and with large knowledge add , the matter would not have be easy ; but in war universal , and blank ignorance , there be nothing to be do but to sit down and think . and the story invite a good deal of thinking , because of its disappointing turn . 
 during the negotiation for peace in 1801 , and before any article be sign , order be send to the Cape of Good Hope for the return of a regiment of the line , which have not be more than three month there . but the cape be likely to be restore to Holland , and two empty transport return from India be to call under convoy , and bring home these troop . one of the officer be Captain Erle Twemlow , then about twenty - five year of age , and under probation , by the Admiral 's decree , for the hand of the maiden whose heart have be his from a time to itself immemorial . after tiresome day of impatience , the transport arrive under conduct of a frigate ; and after another week , the soldier embark with fine readiness for their native land . 
 but before they have clear the Bay , they meet a brig - of - war direct from Portsmouth , carry despatch for the officer in command of the troop , as well as for the captain of the frigate . some barbarous tribe on the coast of Guinea , the part that be call the Ivory Coast , have plunder and burn a british trading station within a few mile of Cape Palmas , and have kill and devour the trader . these native must be punish , and a stern example make , and a negro monarch of the name of Hunko Jum must have his palace burn , if he possess one ; while his rival , the king of the Crumbo tribe , whose name be Bandeliah , who have strive to protect the trader , must be reward , and have a treaty make with he , if he could be bring to understand it . both sailor and soldier be ready enough to undertake this little spree , as they call it , expect to have a pleasant run ashore , a fine bit of sport with the negro , and perhaps a few nosering of gold to take home to their wife and sweetheart . 
 but , alas ! the reality be not so fine . the negro who have do all the mischief make off , carry most of their house with they ; and the palace of Hunko Jum , if he possess one , be always a little way far on . the Colonel be a stubborn man , and so be the sea - captain good Tories both , and not desirous to skulk out of scrape , and leave well man to pick up their clumsy breakage . blue and red vie with one another to scour the country , and punish the native if only they could catch they and to vindicate , with much strong language , the dignity of Great Britain , and to make an eternal example . 
 but white bone be what the white man make , under that slimy sunshine and putrefy moon . weary , slack - joint , low - hearted as they be , the deadly coast - fever fall upon they , and they shiver , and burn , and groan , and rave , and leap into hole , or roll into camp fire . the Colonel die early , and the Naval Captain follow he ; none stand upon the order of their go ; but man follow man , as in a funeral , to the grave , until there be no grave to go to . the hand of the Lord be stretch out against they ; and never would one have come back to England , out of more than five hundred who land , except for the manhood and vigour of a seaman , Captain Southcombe , of the transport Gwalior . 
 this brave and sensible man have be leave with his ship lie off to be signal for , in case of mishap , while his consort and the frigate be despatch in advance to a creek , about twenty league westward , where the land - force triumphant be to join they . Captain Southcombe , with every hand he could muster , trace the unfortunate party inland , and find they lead many league in the wrong direction , lose among quagmire breathe death , wear out with vermin , venom , and despair , and hem in by savage lurk for the night , to rush in upon and make an end of they . what need of many word ? this man , and his comrade , do more than any other man on the face of this earth could have do without british blood in they . they bury the many who have die without hope of the decent concealment which our life have have , and therefore our death long for ; they take on their shoulder , or on cane wattle , the many who have make up their mind to die , and be in much doubt about have do it , and they rouse up and work up by the scruff of their loose place the few who could get along on their own leg . and so , with great spirit , and still great patience , they manage to save quite as many as deserve it . 
 because , when they come within signal of the Gwalior , Captain Southcombe , march slowly with his long limp burden , find ready on the sand the little barrel , about as big as a kilderkin , of true and unsullied Stockholm pitch , which he have take , as his brother take Madeira , for ripeness and for betterance , by right of change of climate . with a little of this give choicely and carefully at the back of every sick man 's tongue , and a little more spread across the hollow of his stomach , he find they so enable in the afternoon that they be glad to sit up in the bottom of a boat , and resign themselves to an all - wise Providence . 
 many survive , and bless Captain Southcombe , not at first cordially for the man yet remain to be discover who be grateful to his doctor but gradually more and more , and with that healthy action of the human bosom which be call expectoration , whenever grateful memory be rekindle by the smell of tar . but this be a trifle ; many useful life be save , and the nation should have thank Captain Southcombe , but do not . 
 after these sad incident , when sorrow for old friend be temper by the friendly warmth afford by their shoe , a muster be hold by the Major in command , and there be only one officer who could neither assert himself alive , nor be certify as dead . that one be Erle Twemlow , and the regiment would rather have lose any other two officer . urgent as it be , for the safety of the rest , to fly with every feather from this pestilential coast , sail be hand , boat despatch , and dealing try with Hunko Jum , who have reappear with promptitude , the moment he be not want . from this noble monarch , and his chief , and all his nation , it be hard to get any clear intelligence , because their own be absorb in absorbing . they have find upon the sand a cask of Admiralty rum , as well as a stout residue of unadulterated pitch . nose , and tongue , and historical romance for a cask have be wash ashore five generation since , and set up for a god , when the last drop be lick induce this brave nation to begin upon the rum ; and fashion   as powerful with they as with we   compel they to drink the tar likewise , because they have see the white man do it . this would have make it hard to understand they , even if they have be english scholar , which their ignorance of rum prove they not to be ; and our sailor very nearly go their way , after sadly ascertain nothing , except that the cask be empty . 
 but luckily , just as they be push off , a very large , black head appear from behind a vegetable - ivory tree , less than a quarter of a mile away , and they know that this belong to Bandeliah , the revere king of the Crumbos , who have evidently smell rum far inland . with he they be enable to hold discourse , partly by sign , and partly by mean of an old and highly polished negro , who have be the rat - catcher at the factory now consume ; and the conclusion , or perhaps the confusion , arrive at from sign , grunt , grin , nod , wagging of finger and twisting of toe , translate grandiloquently into break English , be not far from be to the follow effect : 
 to wit , that two great king reign inland , either of they able to eat up Hunko Jum and Bandeliah at a mouthful , but both of they too proud to set foot upon land that be flat , or in water that be salt . they rule over two great nation call the Houlas , and the Quackwas , go out of sight among great river and land with clear water stand over they . and if the white man could not understand this , it be because they drink salt - water . 
 moreover , they say that of these two king , the king of the Houlas be a woman , the most beautiful ever see in all the world , and able to jump over any man 's head . but the king of the Quackwas be a man , and although he have more than two thousand wife , and be tall by a joint of a bamboo than Bandeliah whose stature be at least six foot four yet nothing would be of any use to he , unless he could come to an agreement with Mabonga , the queen of the Houlas , to split a durra straw with he . but Mabonga be coy , and understand man , as well as jump over they , would grant they no other favour than the acceptance of their present . however , the other great king be determined to have she for his wife , if he abolish all the rest , and for this reason he have catch and keep the lost Englishman as a medicine - man ; and it be not likely that he would kill he , until he fail or succeed . 
 to further enquiry Bandeliah answer that to rescue the prisoner be impossible . if it have be his own new wife , he would not push out a toe for she . the great king Golo live up in high place that overlook the ground , as he would these white man , and his army go like wind and spread like fire . none of his warrior eat white man 's flesh ; they be afraid it would make they cowardly . 
 a brave heart be generally tender in the middle , to make up for be so firm outside , even as the durian fruit be . Captain Southcombe have walk the poop - deck of the Gwalior many a time , in the cool of the night , with Erle Twemlow for his companion , and have take a very warm liking to he . so that when the survivor of the regiment be land at Portsmouth , this brave sailor travel at his own cost to Springhaven , and tell the Rector the whole sad story , make it clear to he beyond all doubt , that nothing whatever could be do to rescue the poor young man from those savage , or even to ascertain his fate . for the Quackwas be an inland tribe , inhabit vast region wholly unknown to any European , and believe to extend to some mighty river , and lake resemble inland sea . 
 therefore Mr. Twemlow , in a deep quiet voice , ask Captain Southcombe one question only whether he might keep any hope of ever have , by the mercy of the Lord , his only son restore to he . and the sailor say yes ; the mistake would be ever to abandon such a hope , for at the moment he least expect it , his son might stand before he . he pretend to no experience of the western coast of Africa , and nigger he know be a very queer lot , act accord to their own light , which differ accord to their nature . but he be free to say , that in such a condition he never would think of despair , though it might become very hard not to do so , as time go on without bring any news . he himself have be in sad peril more than once , and once it appear quite hopeless ; but he think of his wife and his child at home , and the Lord have be pleased to deliver he . 
 the parson be rebuke by this brave man 's faith , who make no pretence whatever to piety ; and when they say Goodbye , their eye be bright with the goodwill and pity of the human race , who know trouble not inflict as yet upon monkey . Mr. Twemlow 's heart fall when the sailor be go , quite as if he have lose his own mainstay ; but he brace himself up to the heavy duty of impart sad news to his wife and daughter , and bad of all to Faith Darling . but the latter surprise he by the way in which she bear it ; for while she make no pretence to hide her tear , she be speak as if they be needless . and the strange thing of all , in Mr. Twemlow 's opinion , be her curious persistence about Queen Mabonga . could any black woman and she suppose she must be that be consider by white people to be beautiful ? have Captain Southcombe ever even see she ; and if not , how could he be in such rapture about her attraction ? she do not like to say a word , because he have be so kind and so faithful to those poor soldier , whom it be his duty to bring home safe ; but if it have not be for that , she might have think that with so many child and a wife at Limehouse , he should not have allow his mind to dwell so fondly on the personal appearance of a negress ! 
 the Rector be astonish at this injustice , and begin to revise his opinion about Faith as the fairest and sweetest girl in all the world ; but Mrs. Twemlow smile , when she have leave off cry , and say that she like the dear child all the well for conclude that Ponga or whatever her name be must of necessity and at the first glance fall desperately in love with her own Erle . then the Rector cry , " oh , to be sure , that explain it ! but he never could have think of that , without his wife 's assistance . " 
 two year now , two year of quiet patience , of busy cheerfulness now and then , and of kindness to other always , have make of Faith Darling a lady to be love for a hundred year , and for ever . the sense of her sorrow be never far from she , yet never bring near to any other by herself ; and her smile be as warm , and her eye as bright , as if there have never be a shadow on her youth . to be greet by she , and to receive her hand , and one sweet glance of her large goodwill , be enough to make an old man feel that he must have be good at some time , and a young man hope that he should be so by - and - by ; though the tendency be generally content with the hope . 




 CHAPTER XXXIII 

 FAREWELL , DANIEL 

 thoughtful for other as she always be , this lovely and loveable young woman go alone , on the morning of the day that be so sorrowful for she , to bear a little share of an eld lady 's sorrow , and comfort she with hope , or at any rate with kindness . they have shed tear together when the bad news arrive , and again when a twelvemonth have weaken feeble hope ; and now that another year have well - nigh kill it in old heart too conversant with the cruelty of the world , a little talk , a tender look , a gentle repetition of thing that have be say at least a hundred time before , might enter by some subtle passage to the cell of comfort . who know how the welt vine leaf , when we give it shade and moisture , crisp its curve again , and breathe new bloom upon its veinage ? and who can tell how the flagging heart , beneath the cool mantle of time , revive , shape itself into keen sympathy again , and spread itself congenially to the altered light ? 
 without think about it , but only desire to do a little good , if possible , Faith take the private way through her father 's ground lead to the rectory , eastward of the village . it be scarcely two o'clock , and the sun be shin , and the air clear and happy , as it can be in October . she be walk rather fast , for fear of drop into the brood vein , when in the little fir plantation a man come forth on her path , and stand within a few yard in front of she . she be startle for an instant , because the place be lonely , and Captain Stubbard 's battery crew have establish their power to repulse the French by pound their fellow - countryman . but presently she see that it be Dan Tugwell , look as unlike himself as any man can do   without the aid of an artist , and with some surprise she go on to meet he . 
 instead of look bright , and bold , and fearless , with the freedom of the sea in his open face , and that of the sun in his cluster curl , young Daniel appear careworn and battered , not only unlike his proper self , but afraid of and ashamed of it . he stand not firmly on the ground , nor lightly poise like a gallant sailor , but loosely and clumsily like a ploughman who leave off at the end of his furrow to ease the cramp . his hat look as if he have sleep in it , and his eye as if he have not sleep with they . 
 Miss Darling have always be fond of Dan , from the day when they play on the beach together , in childhood 's contempt of social law . her old nurse use to shut her eye , after look round to make sure that there be " nobody come to tell on they , " while as pretty a pair of child as the benevolent sea ever prattle with be make mirth and music and romance along its margin . and though in ripe boyhood the unfaithful Daniel transfer the hot part of his homage to the more coquettish dolly , Faith have not make any grievance of that , but rather think all the more of he , especially when he save her sister 's life in a very rash boat adventure . 
 so now she go up to he with a friendly mind , and ask he softly and pitifully what trouble have fall upon he . at the sweet sound of her voice , and the bright encouragement of her eye , he feel as if he be get well . 
 " if you please , miss , " he say , with a meek salutation , which prove his panisic idea to be not properly work into his system as yet " if you please , miss , thing be very hard upon I . " 
 " be it money ? " she ask , with the true british instinct that all common woe have their origin there ; " if it be , I shall be so glad that I happen to have a good bit put by just now . " 
 but Dan shake his head with such dignified sadness that Faith be quite afraid of have hurt his feeling . " oh , I might have know , " she say , " that it be nothing of that kind . you be always so industrious and steady . but what can it be ? be it anything about Captain Stubbard and his man , because I know you do not like they , and none of the old Springhaven people seem to do so ? have you be oblige to fight with any of they , Daniel ? " 
 " no , miss , no . I would not soil my hand by lay it on any of such chap as those . unless they should go for to insult I , I mean , or any one belong to I . no , miss , no . it be ten time bad than money , or assault and battery . " 
 " well , Daniel , I would not on any account , " say Faith , with her desire of knowledge grow hotter by delay , as a kettle boil by wait " on no account would I desire to know anything that you do not seem to think my advice might help you to get out of . I be not in a hurry , but still my time be get rather late for what I have to do . by the time I come back from the rectory , perhaps you will have make up your mind about it . till then , good - bye to you , Daniel . " 
 he step out of the path , that she might go by , and only say , " then goodbye , miss ; I shall be far away when you come back . " 
 this be more than the well - regulate , or large which generally be the worst - regulate feminine mind could put up with . Miss Darling come back , with her mind make up to learn all , or to know the reason why . 
 " Dan , this be unworthy of you , " she say , with her sweet voice full of sorrow . " have I ever be hard or unkind to you , Dan , that you should be so afraid of I ? " 
 " no , miss , never . but too much the other way . that make it so bad for I to say good - bye . I be go away , miss . I must be off this evening . I never shall see Springhaven no more , nor you , miss nor nobody else . " 
 " it be quite impossible , Dan . you must be dream . you do not   look at all like yourself to - day . you have be do too much over - time . I have hear all about it , and how very hard you work . I have be quite sorry for you on Sundays , to see you in the gallery , without a bit of rest , still oblige to give the time with your elbow . I have often be astonish that your mother could allow it . why , Dan , if you go away , you will break her heart , and I do not   know how many more in Springhaven . " 
 " no , miss , no . they very soon mend they . it be the one as go away that get a deal the bad of it . I be sure I do not   know whatever I shall do , without the old work to attend to . but it will get on just as well without I . " 
 " no , it will not , " reply Faith , look at he very sadly , and shake her head at such cynical view ; " nothing will be the same , when you be go , Daniel ; and you ought to have more consideration . " 
 " I be go with a good man , at any rate , " he answer , " the free - minded gentleman that ever come to these part . Squire Carne , of Carne Castle , if you please , miss . " 
 " Mr. Caryl Carne ! " cry Faith , in a tone which make Daniel look at she with some surprise . " be he go away ? oh , I be so glad ! " 
 " no , miss ; not Squire Carne himself . only to provide for I work far away , and not to be behold any more to my own people . and work where a man may earn and keep his own money , and hold up his head while adoe of it . " 
 " oh , Dan , you know more of such thing than I do . and every man have a right to be independent , and ought to be so , and I should despise he otherwise . but do not   be drive by it into the opposite extreme of dislike the people in a different rank " 
 " no , miss , there be no fear of that the only fear be like some of they too much . " 
 " and then , " continue Faith , who be now upon one of her favourite subject past interruption , " you must try to remember that if you work hard , so do we , or nearly all of we . from the time my father get up in the morning , to the time when he go to bed at night , he have not get five minute as he tell we every day for attend to anything but business . even at dinner , when you get a good hour , and will not   be disturb now will you ? " 
 " no , miss ; not if all the work be tumble down . no workman as respect himself would take fifty - nine minute for sixty . " 
 " exactly so ; and you be right . you stand up for your right . your dinner you have earn , and you will have it . and the same with your breakfast , and your supper too , and a good long night to get over it . do you jump up in bed , before you have shut both eye , hear or fancy you have hear the bell , that call you out into the cold , and the dark , and a wet saddle , from a warm pillow ? and put that by , as a trouble of the war , and the chance of be shoot at by dark tall man " here Faith shudder at her own presentment , as the image of Caryl Carne pass before she " have you to consider , at every turn , that whatever you do though you mean it for the good will be twist and turn against you by some one , and make into wickedness that you never dream of , by envious people , whose grudge against you be that they fancy you look down on they ? though I be sure of one thing , and that be that my father , instead of look down upon any honest man because he be poor , look up to he ; and so do I ; and so do every gentleman or lady . and any one who go about to persuade the working - people as they be call , because they have to use their hand more that people like my father look down upon they , and treat they like dog , and all those wicked story all I can say be , any man who do it deserve to be put in the stock , or the pillory , or even to be transport as an enemy to his country . " 
 Dan look at the lady with great surprise . he have always know she to be kind and gentle , and what the old people call " mannersome , " to every live body that come near she . but to hear she put , well than he could put they , his own bud sentiment   which he think to be new , with the timeworn illusion of young Liberals , and to know from her bright cheek , and bright eye , that her heart be in every word of it , and to feel himself rebuke for the evil he have think , and the mischief he have give ear to all this be enough to make he angry with himself , and uncertain how to answer . 
 " I be certain that you never think of such thing , " Miss Darling continue , with her gentle smile return ; " you be much too industrious and sensible for that . but I hear that some person be now in our parish who make it their business , for some reason of their own , to spread ill - will and jealousy and hatred everywhere , to make we all stranger and foe to one another , and foreigner to our own country . we have enemy enough , by the will of the Lord   as Mr. Twemlow say , for a sharp trial to we , and a lesson to our pride , and a deep source of gratitude , and charity , and good - will though I scarcely understand how they come in and , above all , a warning to we to stick together , and not exactly hate , but still abhor , everybody who have a word to say against his own country at a time like this . and ten thousand time as much , if he be afraid to say it , but crawl with crafty poison into simple english bosom . " 
 " there be nothing of that , miss , to my knowledge , here , " the young fisherman answer , simply ; " Springhaven would never stand none of that ; and the club drink the health of King George every night of their meeting , and stamp on the floor for he . but I never shall help to do that any more . I must be go , miss and thank you . " 
 " then you will not tell I why you go ? you speak of it as if it be against your will , and yet refuse to say what drive you . have you be poach , Dan ? ah , that be it ! but I can beg you off immediately . my father be very good even to stranger , and as for his do anything to you have no fear , Dan ; you shall not be charge with it , even if you have be in Brown Bushes . " 
 Brown Bushes , a copse about a mile inland , be the Admiral 's most sacred spot , when peace allow he to go shoot , because it be belove by woodcock , his favourite bird both for trigger and for fork . but Daniel only shake his head ; he have not be near Brown Bushes . few thing perhaps will endure more wear than feminine curiosity . but when a trap have be set too long , it get tongue - bind , and grow content without content . 
 " Daniel Tugwell , " say Miss Darling , severely , " if you have not be fight , or conspire against society , or even poach , I can well understand that you may have reason for not desire my assistance or advice . and I only wonder that under such circumstance you take the trouble to wait for I here , as you appear to have do . good - bye . " 
 " oh , do not   be cross , miss ! please not to be cross , " cry Daniel , run after she ; " I would tell you all about it this very instant moment , if it be behove to I . you will hear all about it when you get to Parson Twemlow 's , for I see mother go there , afore she have her breakfast , though I be not concernable to let she see I . if the squire have be home , she would have go up to Hall first . no , miss , no . I do nothing to be ashamed of ; and if you turn back on I , you will be sorry afterwards . " 
 Faith be more apt to think that she have be too sharp than to be so in behaviour to any one . she begin at once , with a blush for her bad idea , to beg Dan 's pardon , and he see his way to say what he be come to say . 
 " you always be too good , Miss Faith , too good to be hard upon any one , and I be sure you have not be hard upon I ; for I know that I look disrespectable . but I could not   find word to say what I want , until you speak so soft and kind . and perhaps , when I say it , you will be angry with I , and think that I trespass upon you . " 
 " no , I will not , Dan ; I will promise you that . you may tell I , as if I be Mr. Swipes , who say that he never lose his temper in his life , because he be always right , and other people wrong . " 
 " well , miss , I be afraid that I be not like that , and that make I feel so uncomfortable with the difference between we . because it be all about Miss dolly , and I might seem so impudent . but you know that I would go through fire and water to serve Miss Dolly , and I durstn't   go away forever without one message to she . if I be in her own rank of life , God Almighty alone should part we , whether I be rich or whether I be poor , and I would like to see any one come near she ! but be only an ignorant fellow without any birth or book - learning , I be not such a fool as to forget that the breadth of the world lie between we . only I may wish she well , all the same I may wish she well and happy , miss ? " 
 " certainly you may . " Faith blush at the passion of his word , and sigh at their despair . " you have save her life . she respect and like you , the same as my father and I do . you may trust I with your message , Dan . " 
 " I suppose it would not be the proper thing for I to see she once before I go ; just for one minute , with you stand by she , that I might that she might " 
 " no , " answer Faith , though it grieve she to say it ; " we must not think of that , Dan . it could do you no good , and it might do her harm . but if you have any message , to be useful to she " 
 " the useful part of it must be through you , miss , and not send to she at all , I think , or it would be very impertinent . the kind part be to give she my good - bye , and say that I would die to help she . and the useful part be for yourself . for God 's sake , miss , do keep Miss dolly out of the way of Squire Carne ! he hath a tongue equal to any woman , with the mind of a man beneath it . he hath get I body and soul ; because I care not the skin of a dab what befall I . but oh , miss , he never must get Miss dolly . he may be a very good man in some way , and he be wonderful free - minded ; but any young lady as marrie he have well have leap into the Culver Hole . farewell , miss , now that I have tell you . " he be go before Faith could even offer he her hand , but he take off his hat and put one finger to his curl , as he look back from the clearing ; and her eye fill with tear , as she wave her hand and answer , " Farewell , Daniel ! " 




 CHAPTER XXXIV 

 cauliflower 

 " they cock and hen , " Mr. Swipes use to say in the early day of his empire " bless you , my lord , they cock and hen know a good bit of gardening as well as I do . they call one another , and they come to see it , and they put their head to one side and talk about it , and they say to one another , ' must be something good there , or he would not   have make it so bootiful ' ; and then up go their comb , and they tear away into it , like a passel of Scotchmen at a scratch - match . if your lordship will not   put a lock on the door , you will never taste a bit of good vegetable . " 
 Admiral Darling be at length persuade to allow Mr. Swipes the privilege of lock himself in the kitchen - garden ; and then , for the purpose of get at he , a bell be put in the gable of the tool - house , with a long handle hang outside the door in the courtyard towards the kitchen . thus he be able to rest from his labour , without incur unjust reproach ; and gradually as he decline , with increase decision , to answer the bell when it ring , accord to the high law of nature it leave off ring altogether . so Mr. Swipes in the walled kitchen - garden seek peace and ensue it . 
 one quiet November afternoon , when the disappearance of Dan Tugwell have be talk out and do with , a sad mishap befell this gardener , during the performance , or , to speak more correctly , the contemplation of his work . a yawn of such length and breadth and height and profundity take possession of he that the space it have so well occupy still retain the tender memory . in plainer word , he have rick his jaw , not from general want of usage , but from the momentary excess . 
 " sarves I right , " he mutter , " for carry on so , without nothing inside of ' un . must go to doctor , quick step , and no mistake . " 
 in this strait he set off for John Prater 's   for it be a matter of luck to get ale at the Hall , and in such emergency he must not trust to fortune , and pass hastily through the door , leave it unlocked behind he . go down the hill he remember this , and have a great mind to go back again , but the unanimous demand of his system for beer impel he downwards . he never could get up that hill again without hydraulic pressure . 
 all might have go well , and all would have go well , except for the grievous mistake of nature in furnish woman with eye whose keenness be only exceed by that of their tongue . the cook at the Hall , a superior person though lightly esteem by Mrs. Cloam have long be ambitious to have a voice in the selection of her raw material . if anything be good , who get the credit ? Mr. Swipes , immediately . but if everything be bad , as more often happen , who receive the blame ? Mary Knuckledown . her lawful name be " Knuckleup , " but early misfortune have reduce she to such mildness that her name become convert as she express it in harmony with her nature . fact have generally be adverse to she , she find some comfort in warm affection for their natural enemy and ever - victorious rival word . any word come with a brave rush be able to scatter to the wind the strong fact ; but big word as all our great orator know knock they at once on the head and cremate they . but the cook be a kind - hearted woman , and like both little and big word , without think of they . 
 she have put down her joint , a good aitch - bone , for roast than which , if well treat , be few well treat to revolve in the distant salute of the fire   until it should ripen for the close embrace , where the tint of gold and chestnut vie , when it come into her provident mind with a flash that neither horse - radish nor cauliflower have yet be deliver by Mr. Swipes . she must run out and pull the long handle in the yard , and remind he gently of her need , for she stand in some awe of his character , as a great annalist of little people 's life . 
 leave the small dog Dandolo with stern order to keep the jack steadily go , with a stick on the dresser to intimidate one eye , and a sop in the dripping - pan to encourage the other , Mrs. Knuckledown run into the court - yard , just in time to see the last swing of the skirt of that noble gardener 's coat , as he turn the wall corner on his march towards the tap . she long to call he back , but remember just in time how fearfully cross that have make he once before , and she be yield with a sigh to her usual bad luck , when an eager and triumphant cluck make she look about . the monarch and patriarch of cock , a magnificent old dorking , not idly endow with five claw for the scratch , have discover something great , and be call all his wife , and even his son , as many as yet crow not against he , to share this special luck of fortune , or kind mood of Providence . in a minute or two he have levy an army , some half - hundred strong , and all spur the land , to practise their liberal claw betime for the gorgeous joy of scatter it . then the grand old cock , whose name be " Bill , " make they all fall in behind he , and strut till he almost tumble on his head , lead the march of destruction to the garden door . 
 but , alas , he have wait for his follower too long , eager as they be for rapine . when he come to his portal of delight , there stand , stout as Britannia herself , and sweep a long knife for her trident , the valiant cook , to protect her cauliflower . " you be off , Bill , " she cry . " I do not   want to hurt you , because you have be a good bird in your time , but now you be grow outrageous . " Bill make a rush for it , but lose a slice of his top - heavy comb , retire . 
 " now be my opportunity , " say Mary to herself , " for to cut my own cabbage for once in my life , and to see what that old beast do in here . oh my ! the old villain , and robber that he be ! bamboozlement be the language for it . " embezzlement she should have say , and to one who know as she do how badly the table of the master be supply , the suspicion be almost unavoidable . for here she see in plenteous show , and appetise excellence , a many many of the very thing she have vainly crave from Mr. Swipes . and if it be so now in November , what must it have be two month ago ? why , poor Miss Faith Mary Knuckledown 's idol , because of her kindness and sad disappointment have ask a little while ago for a bit of salsify , not for herself she never think of herself but for a guest who be fond of it ; also the Admiral himself have call out for a good dish of skirret . but no ; Mr. Swipes say the weather and the black blight have destroy they . yet here they be ; Mary could swear to they both , with their neck above - ground , as if wait for the washing ! cauliflower also   as the cook call broccoli of every kind , here they be in abundance , ten long row all across the middle square , very beautiful to behold . some be just curl in their crinkle coronet , to conceal the young heart that be form , as Miss in her teen draw her tress around the first peep of her own palpitation ; other be show their broad candid bosom , with bold sprig of nature 's green lace crisp round ; while other have their ripe breast shield from the air by the breakage of their own broad fringe upon they . 
 Mary know that this be do by Mr. Swipes himself , because he have bring she some in that condition ; but the unsuspicious master have accept his assurance that " they be only fit for pig as soon as the break - stalk blight come on them " ; and then the next day he have buy the very same , perhaps at ninepence apiece , from Mr. Cheeseman 's window , trim and shorn close , like the head of a monk . " I will see every bit of ' un , now that I be here . " Mrs. Knuckledown speak aloud , to keep up her courage . " too bad for that old beast to keep we lock out from the very place we ought to have for pommylarding , because he saith all the fruit would go into our pocket . and what go into his'en , I should like to know ? suppose I lock he out , as he hath lock we out . he will not   be back yet for half an hour , anyway . Wish I could write what a list I would make , if it be only of the thing he denieth he hath get ! " 
 strong in her own honesty and loyalty to her master , the cook turn the key in the lock , and leave Swipes to ring himself into his own garden , as he always call it . that be to say , if he should return , which be not very likely , before she have time for a good look round . but she see such a sight of thing she have long for , to redeem her repute in the vegetable way , as well as such herb for dainty stuffing , of which she know more than cook generally do , that her cap nearly come off her head with amazement , and time fly by unheeded . until she be startled and terrify sadly by the loud , angry clang of the bell in the gable . not only be Mr. Swipes come back , but he be in a furious rage outside , though his fury be chill with some shiver of fear . at first , when he find the door lock against he , he think that the Admiral must have come home unexpected , and fail to find he at work , have turn the key against he , while himself inside . if so , his situation would be in sad peril , and many acre of lie would be require to redeem it . for trust in his master 's long time of absence , and full time of public duty when at home , Mr. Swipes have grow more private stock , as he call it , and deny the kitchen more , than he have ever do before , in special preparation for some public dinner about to be give at the Darling Arms , by military officer to naval , and in turn by the latter to the former ; for those be hospitable day , when all true Britons stick their country 's enemy with knife and fork , as well as sword . 
 but learn , as he soon do at the stable , that the Admiral be still away , and both the young lady be go for a ride with Miss Twemlow , the gardener come back in a rage , and ring the bell . " oh , whatever shall I do ? " the trembling Mary ask herself . " good take the upper hand if I can . he be a thief , and a rogue , and he ought to be fright . do he know I can not   write ? no , for certain he dothn't . one of his big lie about I be a letter I write to poor Jonadab . " 
 with her courage renew by the sense of that wrong , she open the door , and stand face Mr. Swipes , with a piece of paper in her hand , which a woman 's quick wit bid her fetch from her pocket . 
 " Halloa , madam ! " the gardener exclaim , with a sweep of his hat and a low salute , which he mean to be vastly satirical ; ' so your ladyship have come to take the air in my poor garden , instead of tend the spit . and what do your ladyship think of it , so please you ? sorry as I have any dung about , but have not   no warning of this royal honour . " 
 " Sir , " say Mrs. Knuckledown , pretend to be frighten a great deal more than she be " oh , sir , forgive I ! I be sure I mean no harm . but the fowl be run in , and I run up to stop they . " 
 " oh , that be how your ladyship condescend ; and to keep out the fowl , you lock out I ! allow I the royal and unapparelled honour of show your ladyship to her carriage ; and if I ever catch she in here again , I will pitch you down the court - yard pretty quick . be off , you dirty baggage , or I will not   answer for it now ! " 
 " oh , you be too kind , Mr. Swipes ; I be sure you be too gentle , to forgive I , like of that ! and the little list I make of the flower in your garden , I shall put it in a teapot till the Quality want something . " 
 Mr. Swipes give a start , and his over - water eye could not meet those of Mary , which be mildly set upon they . " list ! " he mutter " little list ! what do you please to mean , Miss ? " 
 " well , the ' dirty baggage ' mean nothing unparalleled , sir , but just the same as anybody else might do . some people call it a Inventionary , and some an Emmarandum , and some a Catalogue . it do not   interfere with you , Mr. Swipes ; only the next time as Miss dolly ask , the same as she be do the other day " 
 " oh , she be , be she ? the little - ! " Mr. Swipes use a word concern that young lady which would have insure his immediate discharge , together with one from the Admiral 's good toe . " and pray , what be her observation , ma'am ? "
