 Carne   whose dealing be quite unknown to all of the french authority save one , and that the supreme one   be come by appointment to meet his commander in a quiet and secluded spot . it be early February now , and although the day be wan , and the wind , which be draw to the north of west , deliver a cold blow from the sea , yet the breath of Spring be in the air already , and the beat of her pulse come through the ground . almost any man , except those two concerting to shed blood and spread fire , would have look about a little at the pleasure of the earth , and feel a touch of happiness in the goodness of the sky . 
 Caryl Carne wait in the shelter of a tree , scarcely deserve to be call a tree , except for its stiff tenacity . all the branch be drive by the western gale , and scourge flat in one direction that in which they well could hold together , and try to believe that their life be their own . like the wing of a sea bird strive with a temp , all the spray be fray alike , and all the twig hackle with the self - same pile . whoever observe a tree like this should stop to wonder how ever it manage to make itself any sort of trunk at all , and how it be persuade to go up just high enough to lose the chance of ever come down again . but Carne care for nothing of this sort , and heed very little that do not concern himself . all he think of be how he might persuade his master to try the great issue at once . 
 while he lean heavily against the tree , with his long sea - cloak flap round his leg , two horseman strike out of the Ambleteuse road , and come at hand - gallop towards he . the foremost , who ride with short stirrup , and sit his horse as if he despise he , be the foremost man of the world just now , and for ten year yet to come . 
 Carne run forward to show himself , and the master of France dismount . he always look well upon horseback , as short man generally do , if they ride well ; and his face   which help to make his fortune   appear even more commanding at a little distance . an astonishing face , in its sculptured beauty , set aspect , and stern haughtiness , calm with the power of transcendant mind , and a will that never meet its equal . even Carne , void of much imagination , and contemptuous of all the human character he share , be the slave of that face when in its presence , and could never meet steadily those pierce eye . and yet , to the study of a neutral dog , or a man of abstract science , the face be as bad as it be beautiful . 
 Napoleon as he be soon to be call by a cringe world smile affably , and offer his firm white hand , which Carne barely touch , and bent over with deference . then the foaming horse be send away in charge of the attendant trooper , and the master begin to take short quick step , to and fro , in front of the weather - beat tree ; for to stand still be not in his nature . Carne , be beckon to keep at his side , lose a good deal of what he have mean to say , from the trouble he find in time his wonte stride to the brisk pace of the other . 
 " you have do well on the whole very well , " say Napoleon , whose voice be deep , yet clear and distinct as the sound of a bell . " you have keep I well informed ; you be not suspect ; you be enlarge your knowledge of the enemy and of his resource ; every day you become more capable of conduct we to the safe landing . for what , then , this hurry , this demand to see I , this expose of yourself to the risk of capture ? " 
 Carne be about to answer ; but the speaker , who undershoot the thought of other before they be shape as the shuttle of the lightning underweave a cloud raise his hand to stop he , and go on : 
 " because you suppose that all be ripe . because you believe that the slow beast of islander will strengthen their defence more by delay than we shall strengthen our attack . because you be afraid of incur suspicion , if you continue to prepare . and most of all , my friend , because you be impatient to secure the end of a long enterprise . but , Captain , it must be long yet . it be not for you , but for I , to fix the time . behold I ! I be come from a grand review . we have again rehearse the embarkation . we have again put two thousand horse on board . the horse do it well ; but not the man . they be as brave as eagle , but as clumsy as the ostrich , and as fond of the sand without water . they will all be sea - sick . it be in their countenance , though many have be practise in the mouth of river . those infamous English will not permit we to proceed far enough from our native land to acquire what they call the leg of the sea . if our brave be sea - sick , how can they work the cannon , or even navigate well for the accursed island ? they must have time . they must undergo more wave , and a system of diet before embarkation . Return , my trust Captain , and continue your most esteemed service for three month . I have write these new instruction for you . you may trust I to remember this addition to your good work . " 
 Carne 's heart fall , and his face be gloomy , though he do his good to hide it . so well he know the arrogance and fierce self - will of his command officer that he durst not put his own opposite view of the case directly before he . this arrogance grow with the growth of his power ; so that in many important matter Napoleon lose the true state of the case through the terror feel by his subordinate . so great be the mastery of his presence that Carne feel himself guilty of impertinence in carry his head above the level of the General 's plume , and stoop unconsciously as hundred of tall man be say to have do to lessen this anomaly of Nature . 
 " all shall be do to your order , my General , " he reply , submissively . " for my own position I have no fear . I might remain there from year to year without any suspicion arise , so stupid be the people all around , and so well be my name know among they . the only peril be in the landing of store , and I think we should desist from that . a few people have be wonder about that , though hitherto we have be most fortunate . they have set it down so far to smuggle operation , with which in that tyrannical land all the low order sympathise . but it would be wise to desist awhile , unless you , my General , have anything of moment which you still desire to send in . " 
 " what sort of fellow be that Sheeseman ? " ask Napoleon , with his wonderful memory of detail . " be he more to be confide in as a rogue or as a fool ? " 
 " as both , sir ; but more especially as a rogue , though he have the compunction of a fool sometimes . but he be as entirely under my thumb , as I be under that of my Commander . " 
 " that be very good , " answer the First Consul , smile with the sense of his own power ; " and at an hour 's notice , with fifty choose man land from the London Trader ah , I love that name ; it be appropriate you could spike all the gun of that pretentious little battery , and lock the Commander of the Coast - Defence in one of his own cellar . be it not so , my good Captain ? answer I not . that be enough . one question more , and you may return . be you certain of the pilotage of the proud young fisherman who know every grain of sand along his native shore ? surely you can bribe he , if he hesitate at all , or hold a pistol at his ear as he steer the lead prame into the bay ! Charron would be the man for that . between you and Charron , there should be no mistake . " 
 " he require to be handle with much delicacy . he have no idea yet what he be mean to do . and if I understand his nature , neither bribe nor fear would move he . he be stubborn as a Breton , and of that simple character . " 
 " one can always befool a Breton ; but I hate that race , " say Napoleon . " if he can not be make useful , tie a round shot to he , throw he overheard , and get a gentle native . " 
 " alas , I fear that we can not indulge in that pleasure , " say Carne , with a smile of regret . " it cost I a large outlay of skill to catch he , and the native of that place be all equally stubborn . but I have a plan for make he do our work without be at all aware of it . be it your wish , my General , that I should now describe that plan ? " 
 " not now , " reply Napoleon , pull out a watch of English make , " but in your next letter . I start for Paris in an hour 's time . you will hear of thing soon which will add very greatly to the weight and success of this grand enterprise . we shall have perfidious Albion catch in her own noose , as you shall see . you have not hear of one Captain Wright , and the landing - place at Biville . we will have our little Biville at Springhaven . there will be too many of we to swing up by a rope . Courage , my friend ! the future be with you . our regiment be cast dice for the fair english county . but your native county be reserve for you . you shall possess the whole of it I swear it by the god of war and command the southern army . be brave , be wise , be vigilant , and above all thing be patient . " 
 the great man hold up his hand , as a sign that he want his horse , and then offer it to Caryl Carne , who touch it lightly with his lip , and bend one knee . " my emperor ! " he say , " my Emperor ! " 
 " wait until the proper time , " say Napoleon , gravely , and yet well please . " you be not the first , and you will not be the last . observe discretion . Farewell , my friend ! " 
 in another minute he be go , and the place look empty without he . Carne stand gloomily watch the horseman as their figure grow small in the distance , the large man behind pound heavily away , like an english dragoon , on the scanty sod , of no importance to anybody unless he have a wife or child the little man in front   with the white plume wave , and the well - breed horse go easily , the one whose body would affect more body , and certainly send more soul out of they , than any other bear upon this earth as yet , and we hope as long as ever it endureth . 
 Caryl Carne care not a jot about that . he be anything but a philanthropist ; his weakness , if he have any , be not dispersive , but thoroughly concentric . he gather his long cloak round his body , and go to the high spot within his reach , about a mile from the watch - tower at Cape Grisnez , and thence he have a fine view of the vast invasive fleet and the vaster host behind it . 
 an Englishman who love his Country would have turn sick at heart and faint of spirit at the sight before he . the foe be gather together there to eat we up on every side , to get we into his net and rend we , to tear we asunder as a lamb be tear when its mother have drop it in flight from the wolf . for forty square mile there be not an acre without a score of tent upon it , or else of hut throw up with slab of wood to keep the powder dry , and the steel and iron bright and sharp to go into the vital of England . mighty dock have be scoop out by warlike hand , and shine with ship crowd with gun and alive with man . and all along the shore for league , wherever any shelter lay , and great battery protect they , hundred of other ship tear at their mooring , to dash across the smooth narrow line , and blacken with fire and redden with blood the white cliff of the land they loathe . 
 and what be there to stop they ? the steam of the multitude rise in the air , and the clang of armour fill it . number irresistible , and relentless power urge they . at the beck of the hand that have call the horse , the grey sea would have be black with ship , and the pale wave would have be red with fire . Carne look at the water way touch with silver by the soft descent of the winter sun , and upon it , so far as his gaze could reach , there be but a dozen little object move , puny creature in the distance mice in front of a lion 's den . and much as he hat with his tainted heart the land of his father , the land of his birth , some reluctant pride arise that he be by right an Englishman . 
 " it be the dread of the english seaman , it be the fame of Nelson , it be the habit of be beat when England meet they upon the sea nothing else keep this mighty host like a set of tremble captive here , when they might launch forth irresistibly . and what be a great deal bad , it will keep I still in my ruin dungeon , a spy , an intriguer , an understrapper , when I be fit to be one of the foremost . what a fool I be so to be cow and enslave , by a man no well endow than myself with anything , except self - confidence ! I should have look over his head , and tell he that I have have enough of it , and if he would not take advantage of my toil , I would toil for he no long . why , he never even thank I , that I can remember , and my pay be no more than Charron 's ! and a pretty strict account I have to render of every republican coin he send . he will have his own head on they within six month , unless he be assassinate . his manner be not those of a gentleman . while I be speak to he , he actually turn his back upon I , and clear his throat ! every one hat he as much as fear he , of all who be in the rank of gentleman . how would it pay I to throw he over , denounce my own doing , excuse they as those of a Frenchman and a french officer , and bow the knee to Farmer George ? truly if it be not for my mother , who have sacrifice her life for I , I would take that course , and have do with it . such all - important news would compel they to replace I in the property of my forefather ; and if neighbour look coldly on I at first , I could very soon conquer that nonsense . I should marry little dolly , of course , and that would go half - way towards do it . I hate that country , but I might come to like it , if enough of it belong to I . aha ! what would my mother say , if she dream that I could have such idea ? and the whole of my life belong to she . well , let I get back to my ruin first . it would never do to be capture by a british frigate . we have a narrow shave of it last time . and there will be a vile great moon to - night . " 
 with these reflection which be upon the whole more to his credit than the wonte web of thought Carne with his long stride strike into a path towards the beach where his boat be wait . although he know where to find several officer who have once be his comrade , he keep himself gladly to his loneliness ; less perhaps by reason of Napoleon 's order than from the grow charm which Solitude have for all who begin to understand she . 




 CHAPTER XXXIX 

 run the GAUNTLET 

 though Carne have make light , in his impatient mood , of the power of the blockade fleet , he feel in his heart a sincere respect for its vigilance and activity . La Liberte   as the unhappy Cheeseman 's schooner be call within gunshot of France   be glad enough to drop that pretentious name , and become again the peaceful London Trader , when she find herself beyond the reach of french battery . the practice of her captain , the lively Charron , be to give a wide berth to any british cruiser appear singly ; but whenever more than one hove in sight , to run into the midst of they and dip his flag . from the speed of his schooner he could always , in a light wind , show a clean pair of heel to any single heavy ship , and he have not yet come across any cutter , brig of war , or light corvette that could collar the Liberte in any sort of weather . Renaud Charron be a brave young Frenchman , as fair a speciman as could be find , of a truly engaging but not overpower type , kindly , warm - hearted , full of enterprise , lax of moral   unless honour their veneer be touch , love excitement , and capable of anything , except skulk , or sulking , or run away slowly . 
 " none of your risky trick to - night ! " say Carne , as he stand on the schooner 's deck , in the dusk of the February evening , himself in a dark mood grow dark for his english blood supply the element of gloom , and he feel a dull pleasure in goad a Frenchman , after be trample on by one of french position . " you will just make straight , as the tide and shoal allow , for our usual landing - place , set I ashore , and follow I to the old quarter . I have order to give you , which can be give only there . " 
 " my command officer shall be obey , " the Frenchman answer , with a light salute and smile , for he be not endow with the power of hating , or he might have indulge that bad power towards Carne ; " but I fear that he have not find thing to his liking . " 
 " what concern be that of your ? your duty be to carry out my order , to the utmost of your ability , and offer opinion when ask for . " 
 the light - hearted Frenchman shrug his shoulder . " my command officer be right , " he say ; " but the sea be get up , and there will be wind , unless I mistake the arising of the moon . my command officer have well retire , until his command be need . he have be know to feel the effect of high tossing , in spite of his unequalled constitution . be it not so , my commander ? I ask with deference , and anxiety . " 
 Carne , who like to have the joke on his side only , swear at the moon and the wind , in clear English , which be short and more efficacious than French . he long to say , " try to keep I out of rough water , " but his pride , and the fear of suggest the opposite to this sailor who love a joke , keep he silent , and he withdraw to his little cuddy , chew a biscuit , to feed , if it must be so , the approach malady . 
 " we shall have some game , and a fine game too , " say Renaud Charron to himself , as he order more sail to be make . " Milord give himself such mighty air ! we will take he to the cross - run off the Middle Bank , and offer he a basin through the key - hole . to make sea - sick an Englishman for , after all , what other be he ? will be a fine piece of revenge for fair France . " 
 Widow shank have remark with tender sorrow more perhaps because she admire the young man , and be herself a hearty soul , than from any loss of profit in victual he that " he be one of they folk as seem to go about their business , and do their job , and keep their skin as full as other people , without put nort inside of they . " she know one of that kind before , and he be shoot by the Coast - guard , and when they postmartyre he , an eel twenty foot long be find inside he , double up for all the world like a love - knot . Squire Carne be of too high a family for that ; but she would give a week 's rent to know what be inside he . 
 there be no little justice in these remark , as be pretty sure to be the case with all good - natured criticism . the good cook that ever be roasted can not get out of a pot more than be put in it ; and the weight of a cask , as a general rule , diminish if the tap be turn , without any redress at the bung - hole . Carne run off his content too fast , before he have arrange for fresh receipt ; and all who have feel what come of that will be able to feel for he in the result . 
 but a further decrease be in store for he now . as the moon arise , the wind get high , and chop round to one point north of west , raise a perkish head - sea , and grin with white tooth against any flapping of sail . the schooner be put upon the starboard tack as near to the wind as she would lie , bear so for the french coast more than the English , and make for the Vergoyers , instead of the Varne , as intend . this carry they into wide water , and a long roll from the southwest crossing the point squabble of the strong new wind . 
 " General , " cry Charron , now as merry as a grig , and skip to the door of Carne 's close little cabin , about an hour before midnight , " it would afford we pleasure if you would kindly come on deck and give we the benefit of your advice . I fear that you be a little confine down here , and in need of more solid sustenance . my General , arise ; there be much briskness upon deck , and the wave be dance beautifully in the full moon . two sail be in sight , one upon the weather bow , and the other on the weather quarter . ah , how superior your sea - word be to ours ! if I be bear an Englishman , you need not seek far for a successor to Nelson , when he get shoot , as he be sure to be before very long . " 
 " get out ! " mutter Carne , whose trouble be faintly illuminate by a sputtering wick . " get out , you scoundrel , as you love plain english . go direct to the devil only let I die in peace . " 
 " all language be excusable in those affect with the malady of the sea , " reply the Frenchman , dance a little to encourage his friend . " Behold , if you would get up and do this , you would be as happy inside as I be . but stay I know what will ease you in an instant , and enable you to order we right and leave . the indefatigable Sherray put a fine piece of fat pork in store before we sail ; I have just have it cook , for I be almost starve . it float in brown liquor of the rich order , such as no Englishman can refuse . take a sip of pure rum , and you will enjoy it surely . Say , my brave General , will you come and join I ? it will cure any little disquietude down here . " 
 with a pleasant smile Charron lay his hand on the part of his commander which he suppose to be blameable . Carne make an effort to get up and kick he , but fall back with everything whirl around , and all human standard invert . then the kindly Frenchman tuck he up , for his face be blue and the chill of exhaustion strike into he . " I wish you could eat a little bit , " say Charron , gently ; but Carne give a push with his elbow . " well , you will be bad before you be well , as the old woman say in your country . but what be I to do about the two british ship for they be sure to be british now in sight ? " but Carne turn his back , and his black boot dangle from the rim of his bunk as if there be nothing in they . 
 " this be go a little too far , " cry Charron ; " I must have some order , my commander . you understand that two english ship be manifestly bear down upon we " 
 " let they come and send we to the bottom the soon the well , " his commander groan , and then raise his limp knuckle with a final effort to stop his poor ear forever . 
 " but I be not ready to go to the bottom , nor all the other people of our fourteen hand " the Frenchman speak now to himself alone " neither will I even go to prison . I will do as they do at Springhaven , and doubtless at every other place in England . I will have my dish of pork , which be now just crackle I be capable of smell it even here and I will give some to Sam Polwhele , and we will put head together over it . to outsail friend Englishman be a great delight , and to out - gun he would be still great ; but if we can not accomplish those , there will be some pleasure of outwit he . " 
 Renaud Charron be never dispose to make the bad of anything . when he go upon deck again , to look out while his supper be wait , he find no change , except that the wind be freshen and the sea increase , and the stranger whose company he do not covet seem wait for no invitation . with a light wind he would have have little fear of give they the go - by , or on a dark night he might have contrive to slip between or away from they . but everything be against he now . the wind be so strong , blow nearly half a gale , and threaten to blow a whole one , that he durst not carry much canvas , and the full moon , approach the meridian now , spread the white sea with a broad flood of light . he could see that both enemy have descry he , and be act in concert to cut he off . the ship on his weather bow be a frigate , rid the wave in gallant style , with the wind upon her beam , and travel two foot for every one the close - haul schooner could accomplish . if the latter continue her present course , in another half - league she would be under the port - hole of the frigate . 
 the other enemy , though far off , be far more difficult to escape . this be a gun - brig , not so very much big than La Liberte herself for gun - brig in those day be very small craft and for that very reason more dangerous . she bear about two point east of north from the greatly persecute Charron , and be hold on steadily under easy sail , neither gain much upon the chase nor lose . 
 " carry on as we be for about ten minute , " say Charron to his mate , Sam Polwhele ; " that will give we period to eat our pork . come , then , my good friend , let we do it . " 
 Polwhele as he be call to make believe that he and other hand be Cornishmen , whereas they be Yankees of the sharp order , owe no allegiance and unhappily no good - will to their grandmother this man , whose true name be Perkins , give the needful order , and follow down . Charron could talk , like many frenchman , quite as fast with his mouth full as empty , and he have a man to talk to who do not require anything to be say twice to he . 
 " no fear of I ! " be all he say . " you keep out of sight , because of your twang . I will teach they a little good English well than ever come out of Cornwall . the good of all English be not to say too much . " 
 the captain and his mate enjoy their supper , while Carne in the distance bear the pang of a malady call bulimus , that be to say , a giant 's ravening for victual , without a babe 's power of receive they . for he be turn the corner of his sickness now , but prostrate and cold as a fall stalactite . 
 " aha ! we have do well . we have warm our wit up . one glass of what you call the grog ; and then we will play a pleasant game with those englishman ! " Carne hear he say it , and in his heart hop that the English would pitch he overboard . 
 it be high time for those two to finish their supper . the schooner have no wheel , but steer as light craft do then , and long afterwards with a bulky ash tiller , have iron eye for lash it in heavy weather . three strong man stand by it now , obedient , yet mutter to one another , for another cable 's length would bring they into danger of be run down by the frigate . 
 " all clear for stay ! " cry Polwhele , under order from Charron . " down helm ! Helm 's alee ! Steady so . let draw ! Easy ! easy ! there she fill ! " and after a few more rapid order the handy little craft be dash away , with the wind abaft the beam , and her head about two point north of east . " Uncommon quick in stay ! " cry Polwhele , who have take to the helm , and now stand there . " wonder what britisher will think of that ? " 
 the british ship soon let he know her opinion , by a roar and a long streak of smoke blow toward he , as she put up her helm to consider the case . it be below the dignity of a fine frigate to run after little smuggling craft , such as she vote this to be , and a large ship have be sight from her top down channel , which might afford her nobler sport . she content herself with a harmless shot , and leave the gun - brig to pursue the chase , bear away for more important business . 
 " nonplus the big ' un ; shall have trouble with the little ' un , " say Master Polwhele to his captain . " she do not   draw half a fathom more than we do . no good run inside the shoal . and with this wind , she have the foot of we . " 
 " bear straight for she , and let she board we , " Charron answer , pleasantly . " down with all french hand into the forepart of the hold , and stow the spare foresail over they . show our last bill of lading , and ask they to trade . you know all about Cheeseman ; double his price . if we make any cash , we will divide it . say we be out of our course , through supply a cruiser that want our good for nothing . I shall keep out of sight on account of my twang , as you politely call it . the rest I may safely leave to your invention . but if you can get any ready rhino , Sam Polwhele be not the man to neglect it . " 
 " bully for you ! " cry the Yankee , look at he with more admiration than he expect ever to entertain for a Frenchman . " there be five ton of cheese that have be seven voyage , and a hundred firkin of irish butter , and five - and - thirty case of russian tongue , as old as old Nick , and ne'er a sign of weevil ! Lor ' no , never a tail of weevil ! Skipper , you deserve to go to heaven out of West Street . but how about he , down yonder ? " 
 " Captain Carne ? leave he to I to arrange . I shall be ready , if they intrude . announce that you have a sick gentleman on board , a passenger afflict with a foreign illness , and have a foreign physician . Mon Dieu ! it be good . every Englishman believe that anything foreign will kill he with a vault . arrange you the trading , and I will be the doctor a German ; I can do the German . " 
 " and I can do the trading , " the American reply , without any rash self - confidence ; " any fool can sell good stuff ; but it requireth a good man to sell bad good . " 
 the gun - brig bear down on they at a great pace , feel happy certitude that she have get a prize not a very big one , but still worth catch . she see that the frigate have fire a shot , and believe that it be do to call her own attention to a matter below that of the frigate . on she come , heel to the lively wind , very beautiful in the moonlight , toss the dark sea in white shower , and with all her taut canvas arch and gleaming , hover with the shade of one another . 
 " heave to , or we sink you ! " cry a mighty voice through a speak trumpet , as she luff a little , bring her port broadside to bear ; and the schooner , which have hoist british colour , obey the command immediately . in a very few second a boat be man , and dance on the hillock of the sea ; and soon , with some danger and much care , the visitor stand upon the London Trader 's deck , and Sam Polwhele come to meet they . 
 " we have no wish to put you to any trouble , " say the officer in command , very quietly , " if you can show that you be what you profess to be . you sail under british colour ; and the name on your stern be London Trader . we will soon dismiss you , if you prove that . but appearance be strongly against you . what have bring you here ? and why do you run the risk of be fire at , instead of submit to his Majesty 's ship Minerva ? " 
 " because she have not   get any ready money , skipper , and we do not   like three month ' bill , " say the tall Bostonian , look loftily at the british officer . " such thing be nothing but piracy , and we have well be shoot at than lose such good as we carry fresh ship , and in prime condition . come and see they , all with Cheeseman 's brand , the celebrate Cheeseman of Springhaven name guarantee the quality . but one thing , mind you no use to hanker after they unless you come provide with the ready . " 
 " we do not   want your good ; we want you , " answer Scudamore , now first luff of the brig of war Delia , and star a little with his mild blue eye at this man 's effrontery . " that be to say , our duty be to know all about you . produce your paper . prove where you clear from last , and what you be do here , some thirty mile south of your course , if you be a genuine british trader . " 
 " paper all in order , sir . first - chop wafer , as they put on now , to save sealing - wax . charter - party , and all the rest . last bill of lading from Gravesend , but you must not   judge our good by that . bulk of they from St. Mary Axe , where Cheeseman hath freight from these thirty year . if ever you have be at Springhaven , Captain , you would jump at anything with Cheeseman 's brand . but have you bring that little bag of guineas with you ? " 
 " once more , we want none of your good . you might praise they as much as you like , if time permit . show I to the cabin , and produce your paper . after that we shall see what be in the hold . " 
 " Supercargo very ill in good cabin . Plague , or black fever , the german doctor say . none of our hand will go near he but myself . but you will not   be like that , will you ? " 
 Less for his own sake than his mother 's who have none but he to help she Scudamore dread especially that class of disease which be now call " zymotic . " his father , an eminent physician , have observe and have write a short work to establish that certain family and type of constitution lie almost at the mercy of such contagion , and find no mercy from it . and among those family be his own . " Fly , my boy , fly , " he have often say to Blyth , " if you ever come near such subject . " 
 " captain , I will fetch they , " continue Mr. Polwhele , look grave at his hesitation . " by good right they ought to be smoke , I dare say , though I do not   hold much with such stuff myself . and the doctor keep do a heap of herb hot . you can see he , if you just come down these few step . perhaps you would not   mind look into the hold , to find something to suit your judgment quality combine with low figure there while I go into the infected den , as the cleverest of my chap call it . why , it make I laugh ! I have   be in and out , with this stand - up coat on , fifty time , and you can not   smell a flue of it , though wonderful strong down there . " 
 Scudamore shudder , and draw back a little , and then steal a glance round the corner . he see a thick smoke , and a figure prostrate , and another tie up in a long white robe , wave a pan of burn stuff in one hand and a bottle in the other , and plainly conjure Polwhele to keep off . then the latter return , quite complacently . 
 " can not   find all of they , " he say , present a pile of paper big enough to taint Sahara . " that doctor go on as bad as open a coffin . say he understand it , and I do not . the old figure - head ! what do he know about it ? " 
 " much more than you do , perhaps , " reply Blyth , stand up for the profession , as he be bind to do . " perhaps we have well look at these on deck , if you will bring up your lantern . " 
 " but , Captain , you will have a look at our hold , and make we a bid we need not take it , any more than you need to double it for as prime a lot of cheese , and side of bacon " 
 " if your paper be correct , it will not be my duty to meddle with your cargo . but what be you do the wrong side of our fleet ? " 
 " why , that be a bad job . there be no fair trade now , no sort of deal on the square nohow . we run all this risk of be catch by Crappos on purpose to supply british ship gorgeous , soweastern station ; and blow I tight if I could not   swear she have be supply chock - full by a Crappo ! only take ten cheese and fifteen side of bacon , though she never know nought of our black fever case ! but , Captain , sit down here , and overhaul our flimsy . not like rag , you know ; do not   hold plague much . " 
 the young lieutenant compel himself to discharge his duty of inspection behind a combing , where the wind be break ; but even so he take good care to keep on the weather side of the document ; and the date perhaps fly away to leeward . " they seem all right , " he say , " but one thing will save any further trouble to both of we . you belong to Springhaven . I know most people there . have you any Springhaven hand on board ? " 
 " I should think so . send Tugwell aft ; pass the word for Dan Tugwell . Captain , there be a family of that name there settle as long as we have be at Mevagissey . ah , that sort of thing be a credit to the place , and the people too , in my opinion . " 
 Dan Tugwell come slowly , and with a heavy step , look quite unlike the spruce young fisherman whom Scudamore have notice as first and smart in the rescue of the strand Blonde . but he could not doubt that this be Dan , the Dan of happy time and thought ; in whom , without use his mind about it , he have feel some likeness to himself . it be not in his power to glance sharply , because his eye be kindly open to all the little incident of mankind , but he manage to let Dan know that duty compel he to be particular . Dan Tugwell touch the slouched hat upon his head , and stand wait to know what he be want for . 
 " Daniel , " say Scudamore , who could not speak condescendingly to any one , even from the official point of view , because he feel that every honest man be his equal , " be you here of your own accord , as one of the crew of this schooner ? " 
 Dan Tugwell have a hazy sense of be put upon an untrue balance . not by this kind gentleman 's word , but through his own proceeding . in his honest mind he long to say : " I fear I have be bamboozle . I have cast my lot in with these fellow through passion , and in hasty ignorance . how I should like to go with you , and fight the French , instead of get mix up with a lot of thing I can not   make out ! " 
 but his equally honest heart say to he : " you have be well treat . you be well pay . you ship of your own accord . you have no right to peach , even if you have anything to peach of ; and all you have see be some queer trading . none but a sneak would turn against his shipmate and his ship , when overhaul by the Royal Navy . " 
 Betwixt the two voice , Dan say nothing , but look at the lieutenant with that gaze which the receiver take to mean doubt of his meaning , while the doubt more often be what to do with it . 
 " be you here of your own accord ? do you belong to this schooner of your own accord ? be you one of this crew , of your own free - will ? " 
 Scudamore ring the change on his simple question , as he have often be oblige to do in the Grammar - school at Stonnington , with the slow - witted boy , who could not , or would not , know the top from the bottom of a sign - post . " do you eat with your eye ? " he have ask they sometimes ; and they have put their thumb into their mouth to enquire . 
 " S'pose I be , " say Dan at last , assume stupidity , to cover hesitation ; " yes , sir , I come aboard of my own free - will . " 
 " very well . then I be glad to find you comfortable . I shall see your father next week , perhaps . shall I give he any message for you ? " 
 " no , sir ! for God 's sake , do not   let he know a word about where you have see I . I come away all of a heap , and I do not   want one of they to bother about I . " 
 " as you wish , Dan . I shall not say a word about you , until you return with your earning . but if you find the fishing business dull , surely you might have come to we , Dan . any volunteer here for his Majesty 's service ? " Scudamore raise his voice , with the usual question . " good pay , good victual , fine promotion , and prize - money , with the glory of fight for their native country , and provision for life if disable ! " 
 not a man come forward , though one man long to do so ; but his sense of honour , whether true or false , forbid he . Dan Tugwell go heavily back to his work , try to be certain that it be his duty . but sad doubt arise as he watch the brave boat , lift over the wave in the moonlight , with loyal arm tug towards a loyal british ship ; and he feel that he have throw away his last chance . 




 CHAPTER XL 

 shelfe the question 

 there be a time of day   as everybody must have notice who be kind enough to attend to thing   not to be tell by the clock , nor measure to a nicety by the position of the sun , even when he have the manner to say where he be a time of day dependent on a multiplicity of thing unknown to we   who have make our own brain , by perceive that we have none , and work away till we get they , yet palpable to all those less self - exalt being , who , or which , be of infinitely nobler origin than we , and have show it , by humility . at this time of day every decent and good animal feel an unthought - of and untraced desire to shift its position , to come out and see its fellow , to learn what be happen in the humble grateful world out of which man have hoist himself long ago , and be therefore a spectre to they to breathe a little sample of the turn the world be take , and sue their share of pleasure in the quiet earth and air . 
 this time be more observable because it follow a period of the opposite tendency , a period of heaviness , and rest , and silence , when no bird sing and no quadrupe play , for about half an hour of the afternoon . then suddenly , without any alteration of the light , or weather , or even temperature , or anything else that we know of , a change of mood flash into every live creature , a spirit of life , and activity , and stir , and desire to use their own voice and hear their neighbour 's . the usual beginning be to come out first into a place that can not knock their head , and there to run a little way , and after that to hop , and take a peep for any people around , and espy none or only one of the very few admit to be friend speedily to dismiss all misgiving , take a very little bit of food , if handy   more as a duty to one 's family than oneself , for the all - important supper - time be not come yet , and then , if gift by the Lord with wing for what bird can stoop at such a moment to believe that his own grandfather make they ? up to the topmost spray that feather in the breeze , and pour upon the grateful air the voice of free thanksgiving . but an if the blade behind the heart be still unplumed for fly , and only gentle flax or fur blow out on the wind , instead of beat it , do the owner of four leg sit and sulk , like a man defraud of his merit ? he answer the question with a skip and jump ; ere a man can look twice at he he have cut a caper , frolic an intricate dance upon the grass , and brighten his eye for another round of joy . 
 at any time of year almost , the time of day command these deed , unless the weather be outrageous ; but never more undeniably than in the month of April . the growth of the year be well establish , and its manner begin to be school by then ; childish petulance may still survive , and the tear of penitence be frequent ; yet upon the whole there be or use to be a sense of responsibility form , and an elemental inkling of true duty towards the earth . even man   the least observant of the power that walk the ground , go for the sign of weather to the cow , or crow , or pig , swallow , spider , gnat , and leech , or the final assertion of his own corn   sometimes be move a little , and enlarge by influence of life beyond his own , and tickle by a pen above his thought , and touch for one second by the hand that make he . then he see a brother man who owe he a shilling , and his soul be swallow up in the resolve to get it . 
 but well in the sky - like period of youth , when the wind sit lightly , and the cloud go by in puff , these little jump of inspiration take the most respectable young man sometimes off his leg , and the young maid likewise if she continue in these fine day to possess such continuation . Blyth Scudamore have be appoint now , partly through his own good desert , and wholly through good influence for Lord St. Vincent be an ancient friend of the excellent Admiral Darling to the command of the Blonde , refit , thoroughly overhaul at Portsmouth , and pronounce by the dock - yard people to be the fast and sound corvette afloat , and in every way a credit to the british navy . " the man that float she shall float in she , " say the Earl , when somebody , who want the appointment , suggest that the young man be too young . " he have see sharp service , and do sharp work . it be waste of time to talk of it ; the job be do . " " Job be the word for it , " think the other , but wisely reserve that great truth for his wife . however , it be not at all a bad job for England . and Scudamore have now see four year of active service , count the former year of volunteering , and be more than twenty - five year old . 
 none of these thing exalt he at all in his own opinion , or , at any rate , not very much . because he have always regard himself with a proper amount of self - respect , as modest man be almost sure to do , desire less to know what the world think of they than to try to think rightly of it for themselves . his opinion of it seem to be that it be very good just now , very kind , and fair , and gentle , and a thing for the heart of man to enter into . 
 for dolly Darling be close beside he , sit on a very pretty bench , make of twisted oak , and turn up at the back and both end , so that a gentleman could not get very far away from a lady without frighten she . not only in this way be the spot well adapt for tender feeling , but itself truly ready to suggest they , with nature and the time of year to help . there be no stream issue here , to puzzle and perpetually divert the human mind   whose origin clearly be spring - water pour into the frame of the jelly - fish , neither be there any big rock , like an obstinate barrier rise ; but gentle slope of daisie pasture lead the eye complacently , sleek cow sniff the herbage here and there , and brush it with the underlip to fetch up the blade for supper - time , and placable tree , forget all the rudeness of the winter wind , begin to disclose to the fond deceive breeze , with many a glimpse to attract a glance , all the cream of their summer intention . and in full enjoyment of all these doing , the poet of the whole stand sing the simple - minded thrush , proclaim that the world be good and kind , but himself perhaps the kind , and his nest , beyond doubt , the good of it . 
 " how lovely everything be to - day ! " Blyth Scudamore speak slowly , and gaze shyly at the lovely thing of all , in his opinion the face of Dolly Darling . " no wonder that your brother be a poet ! " 
 " but he never write about this sort of thing , " say dolly , smile pleasantly . " his poem be all about liberty , and the right of man , and the wrong of war . and if he ever mention cow or sheep , it be generally to say what a shame it be to kill they . " 
 " but surely it be much bad to kill man . and who be to be blame for that , Miss Darling ? the Power that want to overrun all the rest , or the Country that only defend itself ? I hope he have not convert you to the worship of the new Emperor ; for the army and all the great city of France have beg he to condescend to be that ; and the King of Prussia will add his entreaty , accord to what we have hear . " 
 " I think anything of he ! " cry dolly , as if her opinion would settle the point . " after all his horrible murder bad of all of that very handsome and brave young man shoot with a lantern , and bury in a ditch ! I be tell that he have to hold the lantern above his poor head , and his hand never shake ! it make I cry every time I think of it . only let Frank come back , and he will not   find I admire his book so very much ! they do the same sort of thing when I be a little girl , and could scarcely sleep at night on account of it . and then they seem to get a little well , for a time , and fight with their enemy , instead of one another , and make everybody wild about liberty , and citizen , and the noble march of intellect , and the dignity of mankind , and the right of labour when they would not   work a stroke themselves and the black superstition of believe anything , except what they choose to make a fuss about themselves . and thousand of people , even in this country , who have be bring up so much well , be foolish enough to think it very grand indeed , especially the poet , and the one that be too young . but they ought to begin to get wise now ; even Frank will find it hard to make another poem on they . " 
 " how glad I be to hear you speak like that ! I have no idea at least I do not understand " 
 " that I have so much common - sense ? " enquire dolly , with a glance of subtle yet humble reproach . " oh yes , I have a great deal sometimes , I can assure you . but I suppose one never do get credit for anything , without claim it . " 
 " I be sure that you deserve credit for everything that can possibly be imagine , " Scudamore answer , scarcely know , with all his own common - sense to help he , that he be talk nonsense . " every time I see you I find something I have never find before to to wonder at if you can understand and to admire , and to think about , and to to be astonish at . " 
 dolly know as well as he do the word he long to use , but fear . she like this state of mind in he , and she like he too for all his kindness , and his humble worship ; and she could not help admire he for his bravery and simplicity . but she do not know the value yet of a steadfast and unselfish heart , and her own be not quite of that order . so many gallant officer be now to be see at her father 's house , half a cubit tall than poor Blyth , and a hundred cubit high in rank , and wealth , and knowledge of the world , and the power of make their wife great lady . moreover , she like a dark man , and Scudamore be fair and fresh as a rose call Hebe 's Cup in June . another thing against he be that she know how much her father like he ; and though she love her father well , she be not bind to follow his leading . and yet she do not wish to lose this useful and pleasant admirer . 
 " I be not at all ambitious , " she reply , without a moment 's hesitation , for the above reflection have long be deal with , " but how I wish I could do something to deserve even half that you say of I ! but I fear that you find the air get rather cold . the weather be so changeable . " 
 " be you sure that you be not ambitious ? " Scudamore be too deeply plunge to get out of it now upon her last hint ; and to - morrow he must be far away . " you have every right to be ambitious , if such a word can be use of you , who be yourself the height of so many ambition . it be the only fault I could imagine you to have , and it seem too bad that you should have none at all . " 
 " you do not   know anything about it , " say dolly , with a lovely expression in her face of candour , penitence , and pleasantry combine ; " I be not only full of fault , but entirely make up of they . I be tell of they too often not to know . " 
 " by miserably jealous and false people . " it be impossible to look at she and not think that . " by people who can not have a single atom of perception , or judgment , or even proper feeling . I should like to hear one of they , if you would even condescend to mention it . tell I one only one if you can think of it . I be not at all a judge of character , but but I have often have to study it a good deal among the boy . " 
 this make Miss dolly laugh , and drop her eye , and smoothe her dress , as if to be sure that his penetration have not be bring to bear on she . and the gentle Scuddy blush at his clumsiness , and hop that she would understand the difference . 
 " you do say such thing ! " she also be blush beautifully as she speak , and take a long time before she look at he again . " thing that nobody else ever say . and that be one reason why I like you so . " 
 " oh , do you like I do you like I in earnest ? I can hardly dare to dream even for one moment " 
 " I be not go to talk about that any more . I like Mr. Twemlow , I like Captain Stubbard , I like old Tugwell though I should have like he well if he have not be so abominably cruel to his son . now I be sure it be time to go and get ready for dinner . " 
 " ah , when shall I dine with you again ? perhaps never , " say the young man , endeavour to look very miserable and to inspire sadness . " but I ought to be very happy , on the whole , to think of all the pleasure I have enjoy , and how much well I have get on than I have any right in the world to hope for . " 
 " yes , to be the Commander of a beautiful ship , little more than a year from the date of your commission . Captain Stubbard be in such a rage about it ! " 
 " I do not   mean about that though that of course be rare luck I mean a much more important thing ; I mean about get on well with you . the first time I see you in that fine old school , you do not even want to shake hand with I , and you think what a queer kind of animal I be ; and then the first time or two I din at the Hall , nothing but fine hospitality stop you from laugh at my want of practice . but gradually , through your own kind nature , and my humble endeavour to be of use , I begin to get on with you well and well ; and now you be begin almost to like I . " 
 " not almost , but altogether , " she answer , with quite an affectionate glance . " I can tell you there be very few , outside of my own family , that I like half so well as I like you . but how can it matter to you so much ? " 
 she look at he so that he be afraid to speak , for fear of spoil everything ; and be a very good - natured girl , and please with his deep admiration , she sigh just enough to make he think that he might hope . 
 " we be all so sorry to lose you . " she say ; " and no one will miss you so much as I shall , because we have have such pleasant time together . but if we can carry out our little plot , we shall hear of you very often , and I dare say not very unfavourably . faith and I have be put our head together , and for our own benefit , and that of all the house , if we can get you to second it . my father jump at the idea , and say how stupid we be not to think of it before . you know how very little he can be at home this summer , and he say he have to sacrifice his child to his country . so we suggest that he should invite Lady Scudamore to spend the summer with we , if she can be persuade to leave home so long . we will do our very utmost to make her comfortable , and she will be a tower of strength to we ; for you know sometimes it be very awkward to have only two young lady . but we dare not do anything until we ask you . do you think she would take compassion upon we ? a word from you perhaps would decide she ; and Faith would write a letter for you to send . " 
 Scudamore redden with delight , and take her hand . " how can I thank you ? I have well not try , " he answer , with some very tender play of thumb and fore - finger , and a strong impulse to bring lip too into action . " you be almost as clever as you be good ; you will know what I mean without my tell you . my mother will be only too glad to come . she know what you be , she have hear so much from I . and the reality will put to shame all my description . " 
 " tell I what you tell she I be like . the truth , now , and not a word of afterthought or flattery . I be always so irritated by any sort of flattery . " 
 " then you must let I hold your hand , to subdue your irritation ; for you be sure to think that it be flattery you be so entirely ignorant of yourself , because you never think of it . I tell my dear mother that you be the good , and sweetest , and wise , and loveliest , and most perfect , and exquisite , and innocent , and unselfish of all the human being she have ever see , or hear , or read of . and I say it be quite impossible for any one after one look at you to think of himself any more in this world . " 
 " well do ! " exclaim dolly , show no irritation , unless a gleam of pearl inside an arch of coral show it . " it be as well to do thing thoroughly , while one be about it . I can understand now how you get on so fast . but , alas , your dear mother will only laugh at all that . lady be so different from gentleman . perhaps that be why gentleman never understand they . and I would always a great deal rather be judge by a gentleman than a lady . lady pick such a lot of hole in one another , whereas gentleman be too large - minded . and I be very glad upon the whole that you be not a lady , though you be much more gentle than they make believe to be . oh dear ! we must run ; or the lady will never forgive we for keep they starve all this time . " 




 CHAPTER XLI 

 listener hear no good 

 " not that there be anything to make one so very uneasy , " say Mr. Twemlow , " only that one have a right to know the meaning of what we be expect to put up with . nothing be clear , except that we have not one man in the Government who know his own mind , or at any rate dare to pronounce it . Addington be an old woman , and the rest oh , when shall we have Pitt back again ? People talk of it , and long for it ; but the Country be so slow . we put up with everything , instead of demand that the right thing shall be do at once . here be Boney , a fellow raise up by Satan as the scourge of this island for its manifold sin ; and now he be to be the Emperor forsooth not of France , but of Europe , continental Europe . we have only one man fit to cope with he at all , and the voice of the nation have be shout for he ; but who pay any attention to it ? this state of thing be childish simply childish ; or perhaps I ought to say babyish . why , even the child on the sea - shore know , when they make their little sand wall against the tide , how soon they must be sweep away . but the difference be this , that they do not   live inside they , and they have not   get all that belong to they inside they . nobody must suppose for a moment that a clergyman 's family would fail to know where to look for help and strength and support against all visitation ; but , in common with the laity , we ask for Billy Pitt . " 
 " and in another fortnight you will have he , " reply Captain Stubbard , who be din there that day . " allow I to tell you a little thing that happen to my very own self only yesterday . you know that I be one of the last people in the world to be accuse of any what be the proper word for it ? Mrs. Stubbard , you know what I mean Jemima , why the deuce do not   you tell they ? " 
 " Captain Stubbard always have more meaning than he can well put into word , " say his wife ; " his mind be too strong for any dictionary . hallucination be the word he mean . " 
 " exactly ! " cry the Captain . " that express the whole of what I want to say , but go aside of it . I be one of the last man in the world to become the victim of any there , I have   lose it again ! but never mind . you understand now ; or if you do not , Mrs. Stubbard will repeat it . what I mean be that I see all thing square , and straight , and with their own corner to they . well , I know London pretty well ; not , of course , as I know Portsmouth . still , nobody need come along with I to go from Charing Cross to St. Paul 's Church - yard ; and pretty tight I keep all my hatch batten down , and a sharp pair of eye in the crow' - nest for to have they in the foretop will not   do there . it be strictly on duty that I go up the duty of get a fresh stock of powder , for gun be not much good without it ; and I have write three time , without answer or powder . but it seem that my letter be go the round , and would turn up somewhere , when our gun be storm , without a bit of stuff to make answer . " 
 " ah , that be the way they do everything now ! " interrupt Mr. Twemlow . " I think you have be very quiet lately ; but I do not know what a good reason you have . we might all have be shoot , and you could not have fire a salute , to inform the neighbourhood ! " 
 " well , never mind , " reply the Captain , calmly ; " I be not complain , for I never do so . young man might ; but not old hand , whose duty it be to keep their situation in life . well , you must understand that the air of London always make I hungry . there be so many thousand of people there that you can not   name a time when there be nobody eat , and this make a man from the country long to help they . anyhow , I smell roast mutton at a place where a little side street come up into the Strand ; and although it be scarcely half past twelve , it remind I of Mrs. Stubbard . so I call a halt , and stand to think upon a grating , and the scent become flavour with baked potato . this be always more than I can resist , after all the heavy trial of a chequer life . so I push the door open , and see a lot of little cabin , right and leave of a fore and aft gangway , all rig up alike for victual . Jemima , I tell you all about it . you describe it to the Rector and Mrs. Twemlow . " 
 " do not   let we trouble Mrs. Stubbard , " say the host ; " I know the sort of thing exactly , though I do not   go to that sort of place myself . " 
 " no , of course you do not . and I be a little scared at first , for there be sawdust enough to soak up every drop of my blood , if they have pistol I . Mrs. Twemlow , I beg you not to be alarm . my wife have such nerve that I often forget that all lady be not like she . now do not   contradict I , Mrs. Stubbard . well , sir , I go to the end of this cockpit if you like to call it so and get into the starboard berth , and shout for a ration of what I have smell outside . and although it be far from be equal to its smell as the character be of everything you might have think it uncommon good , if you have never taste Mrs. Stubbard 's cooking , after she have be to the butcher herself . very well . I do not   care for kickshaw , even if I could afford they , which have never yet be my destiny . so I call for another ration of hot sheep beg your pardon , lady , what I mean be mutton and half a dozen more of baked potato ; and they remind I of be at home so much that I call for a pint of good pine - apple rum and a brace of lemon , to know where I be to remind I that I be not   where I could not   get they . " 
 " oh , Adam ! " cry Mrs. Stubbard , " what will you say next ? not on weekday , of course , but nearly every Sunday and the sample of his powder in his pocket , Mr. Twemlow ! " 
 " Jemima , you be spoil my story altogether . well , you must understand that this room be low , scarcely high than the cabin of a fore - and - after , with no skylight to it , or wind - sail , or port - hole that would open . and so , with the summer come on , as it be now though a precious long time about it and the smell of the meat , and the thought of the grog , and the feeling of be at home again , what do I do but fall as fast asleep as the captain of the watch in a heavy gale of wind ! my back be to the light , so far as there be any , and to make sure of the top of my head , I fetch down my hat the soft - edged one , the same as you see I wear on fine sunday . 
 " well , I may have go on in that way for an hour , not snore , as Mrs. Stubbard call it , but breathe to myself a little in my sleep , when I seem to hear somebody call I , not properly , but as people do in a dream ' Stoobar Stoobar Stoobar , ' be the sound in my ear , like my conscience haul I over the coal in bad English . this make I wake up , for I always have it out with that part of I when it mutiny ; but I do not move more than to feel for my glass . and then I perceive that it be nothing more or less than a pair of Frenchmen talk about I in the berth next to mine , within the length of a marlin - spike from my blessed surviving ear . 
 " some wiseacre say that listener never hear good of themselves , and upon my word he be right enough this time , so far as I make out . the french language be beyond I , so far as speak go , for I never can lay hold of the word I want ; but I can make out most of what those queer people say , from be a prisoner among they once , and twice in command of a prize crew over they . and the sound of my own name prick I up to listen sharply with my one good ear . you must bear in mind , Rector , that I could not see they , and durst not get up to peep over the quarter - rail , for fear of scar they . but I be wear a short hanger , like a middy 's dirk the one I always carry in the battery . " 
 " I make Adam promise , before he go to London , " Mrs. Stubbard explain to Mrs. Twemlow , " that he would never walk the street without steel or firearm . Portsmouth be a very wicked place indeed , but a garden of Eden compare with London . " 
 " well , sir , " continue Captain Stubbard , " the first thing I hear those frenchman say be : ' stoobar be a stupid beast , like the ox that take the prize up here , except that he have no claim to good look , but the contrary wholly the contrary . ' Mrs. Stubbard , I beg you to preserve your temper ; you have hear other say it , and you should now despise such falsehood . ' but the ox have his horn , and Stoobar have none . for all his great gun there be not one little cup of powder . ' the villain laugh at this , as a very fine joke , and you may well suppose that I almost boil over . ' you have then the command of this beast stoobar ? ' the other fellow ask he , as if I be a jackass . ' how then have you so very well obtain it ? ' ' in a manner the most simple . our chief have he by the head and heel : by the head , by be over he ; and by the heel , because nothing can come in the rear without his knowledge . Behold ! you have all . ' ' it be very good , ' the other villain answer ; ' but when be it to be , my most admirable Charron ? how much long ? how many month ? ' ' behold my finger , ' say the one who have abuse I ; ' I put these into those , and then you know . it would have be already , except for the business that you have be employ upon in this black hole . Hippolyte , you have do well , though crookedly ; but all be straight for the native land . you have make this Government appear more treacherous in the eye of France and Europe than our own be , and you have give a good jump to his instep for the saddle . but all this throw we back . I be tired of trick ; I want fight ; though I find they quite a jolly people . ' ' I do not , ' say the other , who be clearly a low scoundrel , for his voice be enough to settle that ; ' I hate they ; they be of thick head and thick hand , and would come in sabot to catch their enemy asleep . and now there be no chance to entangle any more . their government will be of the old brutal kind , hard knock , and no stratagem . in less than a fortnight Pitt will be master again . I know it from the very good authority . you know what access I have . ' ' then that be past , ' the other fellow answer , who seem to speak more like a gentleman , although he be the one that run down I ; ' that be the Devil . they will have their wit again , and that very fat Stoobar will be supply with powder . Hippolyte , it be a very grand joke . within three mile of his head   which be empty , like his gun   we have nearly two hundred barrel of powder , which we fear to bring over in those flat - bottom for fear of a volley among they . ha ! ha ! stoobar be one fine fat ox ! ' 
 " this be all I hear , for they begin to move , have have enough sugar and water , I suppose ; and they saunter away to pay their bill at the hatch put up at the doorway . it be hopeless to attempt to follow they ; but although I be not so quick in stay as I be , I slew myself round to have a squint at they . one be a slight little active chap , with dapper leg , and jerk like a Frenchman all over . I could pardon he for call I a great fat ox , for want of a bit of flesh upon his own bone . but he know more about I than I do of he , for I never clap eye on he before , to my knowledge . the other be well build , and of some substance , but a nasty , slouchy - look sort of cur , with high fur collar and a long grey cloak . and that be the one call Hippolyte , who know all about our government . and just the sort of fellow who would do so in these day , when no honest man know what they be up to . " 
 " that be true , " say the Rector " too true by half . but honest man soon will have their turn , if that vile spy be well informed . the astonishing thing be that England ever put up with such shameful anarchy . what have be do to defend we ? nothing , except your battery , without a pinch of powder ! with Pitt at the helm , would that have happen ? how could we have sleep in our bed , if we have know it ? fourteen gun , and not a pinch of powder ! " 
 " but you use to sleep well enough before a gun be put there . " Mrs. Stubbard 's right to spare nobody be well establish by this time . " well have the gun , though they could not be fire , than no gun at all , if they would frighten the enemy . " 
 " that be true , ma'am , " reply Mr. Twemlow ; " but until the gun come , we have no sense of our danger . have teach we that , they be bind to act up to their teaching . it be not for ourselves that I have any fear . we have long since learn to rest with perfect faith in the Hand that overruleth all . and more than that if there should be a disturbance , my nephew and my godson Joshua have a house of fourteen room in a Wiltshire valley , quite out of the track of invader . he would have to fight , for he be Captain in the Yeomanry ; and we would keep house for he till all be over . so that it be for my parish I fear , for my people , my school , and my church , ma'am . " 
 " need not   be afraid , sir ; no call to run away , " cry the Captain of the battery , have now well man his own porthole with the Rector 's sound wine ; " we shall have our powder in to - morrow , and the French can not   come to - night ; there be too much moon . they never dare show their nose nor'ard of their sand , with the man in the moon the John Bull in the moon look at they . and more than that , why , that curse Boney " 
 " Adam , in Mr. Twemlow 's house ! you must please to excuse he , all good people . he have sate such a long time , without say what he like . " 
 " Jemima , I have use the right word . the parson will back I up in every letter of it , have say the same thing of he , last Sunday week . but I beg Mrs. Twemlow 's pardon , if I say it loud enough to disturb she . well , then , this bless Boney , if you prefer it , be a deal too full of his own dirty trick for mount the throne of the King they murder , to get into a flat - bottomed boat at Boulogne , and a long sight too jealous a villain he be , to let any one command instead of he . why , the man who set foot upon our shore , and beat we if such a thing can be suppose would be ten time big than Boney in a month , and would sit upon his crown , if he get one . " 
 " well , I do not   believe they will ever come at all , " the solid Mrs. Stubbard pronounce , with decision . " I believe it be all a sham , and what they want be to keep we from attack they in France . however , it be a good thing on the whole , and enable poor officer , who have fight well for their country , to keep out of the Workhouse with their family . " 
 " Hearken , hearken to Mrs. Stubbard ! " the veteran cry , as he pat his waistcoat a well one than he could have wear , and a large one than he could have want , except for the promise invasion . " I will back my wife against any lady in the land for common - sense , and for put it plainly . I be not ashamed to say thank God for the existence of that bless Boney . all I hope be that he will only try to land at Springhaven I mean , of course , when I have   get my powder . " 
 " keep it dry , Captain , " say the Rector , in good spirit . " your confidence make we feel comfortable ; and of course you would draw all their fire from the village , and the house stand near it , as this do . however , I pray earnestly every night that they may attempt it in some other parish . but what be it you hear that Frenchman say about two or three hundred barrel of powder almost within three mile of we ? suppose it be to blow up , where should we be ? " 
 " oh , I do not   believe a word of that . it must be brag and nonsense . to begin with , there be no place where they could store it . I know all the neighbourhood , and every house in it . and there be no cave on this coast in the cliff , or hole of that kind such as smuggler use . however , I shall think it my duty to get a search - order from Admiral Darling , and inspect large farm - building , such as Farmer Graves have get , and another man the other side of Pebbleridge . those be the only place that could accommodate large store of ammunition . why , we can take only forty barrel in the fire - proof magazine we have build . we all know what liar those Frenchmen be . I have no more faith in the 200 barrel of powder than I have in the 2000 ship prepare on the opposite coast to demolish we . " 
 " well , I hope you be right , " Mr. Twemlow answer . " it do seem a very unlikely tale . but the lady be go . let we have a quiet pipe . a man who work as hard as you and I do be entitle to a little repose now and then . " 




 CHAPTER XLII 

 answer the question 

 if Scudamore have not see Dan Tugwell on board of the London Trader , and hear from his own lip that he be one of her crew , it be certain that he would have make a strict search of her hold , accord to his order in suspicious case . and if he have do this , it be probable that he never would have set his nimble foot on deck again , for Perkins   the American who pass as Sam Polwhele   have a heavy ship - pistol in his great rough pocket , ready for the back of the young officer 's head if he have probe below the cheese and firkin of butter . only two man have follow the lieutenant from their boat , the rest be need for her safety in the strong sea running , and those two at the signal would have be fling overboard , and the schooner   put about for the mouth of the Canche , where heavy battery be mount   would have have a fair chance of escape , with a good start , while the gun - brig be pick up her boat . unless , indeed , a shot from the Delia should carry away an important spar , which be not very likely at night , and with a quick surf to baffle gunnery . however , none of these thing come to pass , and so the chance require no measurement . 
 Carne land his freight with his usual luck , and resolve very wisely to leave off that dangerous work until further urgency . he have now a very fine stock of military store for the ruin of his native land , and especially of gunpowder , which the gallant Frenchmen be afraid of stow largely in their flat - bottom craft . and know that he owe his success to moderation , and the good - will of his neighbour towards evasion of the revenue , he think it much well to arrange his magazine than to add to it for a month or two . 
 moreover , he be vex at the neglect of his advice , on the part of his arrogant Commander , a man who be never know to take advice from any mind external to his own body , and not even from that clear power sometimes , when his passionate heart get the uppermost . Carne , though of infinitely small mind , have one great advantage he seldom allow it to be curdle or cross in its clear operation by turbulent bodily element . and now , when he hear from the light - hearted Charron , who have lately be at work in London , that the only man they fear be about to take the lead once more against the enemy of Great Britain , Caryl Carne grow bitter against his chief , and begin for the first time to doubt his success . 
 " I have a great mind to go to Mr. Pitt myself , tell he everything , and throw myself upon his generosity , " he think , as he sate among his ruin sadly . " I could not be bring to trial as a common traitor . although by accident of birth I be an Englishman , I be a french officer , and within my duty in act as a pioneer for the french army . but then , again , they would call I at the good a spy , and in that capacity outside the rule of war . it be a toss - up how they might take it , and the result would depend perhaps on popular clamour . the mighty Emperor have snub I . he be not a gentleman . he have not even invite I to Paris , to share in the festivity and honour he proclaim . I would risk it , for I believe it be the safe game , except for two obstacle , and both of those be woman . matter be grow very ticklish now . that old bat of a Stubbard have get scent of a rat , and be hunt about the farm - house . it would be bad for he if he come prowl here ; that step for inspector be well contrive . twenty foot fall on his head for my friend ; even his bull - neck would get the bad of that . and then , again , there be that wretch of a Cheeseman , who could not even hang himself effectually . if it be not for Polly , we would pretty soon enable he , as the Emperor enable poor Pichegru . and after his own bona fide effort , who would be surprised to find he sus . per coll . ? but Polly be a nice girl , though become too affectionate . and jealous good lack ! a grocer 's daughter jealous , and a Carne compel to humour she ! what idiot woman be in the hand of a strong man ! only my mother my mother be not ; or else my father be a weak one ; which I can well believe from my own remembrance of he . well , one point at least shall be settle to - morrow . " 
 it be early in May , 1804 , and Napoleon have make away to the good of his ability which in that way be pre - eminent with all possible rival and probable foe , be receive address , and appoint dummy , and establish foolscap guarantee against his poor fallible and flexible self as he have the effrontery to call it with all the gravity , grand benevolence , confidence in mankind   as fool , immensity of yearn for universal good , and intensity of planning for his own , which have hoodwink the zany in every age , and never more than in the present age and country . and if France lick the dust , she could plead more than we can it have not be cast off from her enemy 's shoe . 
 Carne 's love of liberty , like that of most people who talk very largely about it , be about as deep as beauty be declare to be ; or even less than that , for he would not have imperille the gloss of his epiderm for the fair goddess . so that it irk he very little that his Chief have smash up the Republic , but very greatly that his own hand should be out in the cold , and have nothing put inside it to restore its circulation . " if I have stick to my proper line of work , in the Artillery , which have make his fortune " he could not help say to himself sometimes " instead of lose more than a year over here , and perhaps another year to follow , and all for the sake of these dirty old ruin , and my mother 's revenge upon this country , I might have be a General by this time almost for nothing depend upon age in France and worthy to claim something lofty and grand , or else to be buy off at a truly high figure . the little gunner have make a great mistake if he think that his flat thumb of low breed can press I down shudder , and starving , and crouch , just until it suit he to hold up a finger for I . my true course be now to consider myself , to watch event , and act accordingly . my honour be free to go either way , because he have not keep his word with I ; he promise to act upon my advice , and to land within a twelvemonth . " 
 there be some truth in this , for Napoleon have promise that his agent 's perilous commission in England should be discharge within a twelvemonth , and that time have elapse without any renewal . but Carne be clear - minded enough to know that he be bind in honour to give fair notice , before throw up the engagement ; and that even then it would be darkest dishonour to betray his confidence . he have his own sense of honour still , though warp by the underhand work he have stoop to ; and even while he reason with himself so basely , he feel that he could not do the thing he threaten . 
 to a resolute man it be a misery to waver , as even the most resolute must do sometimes ; for instance , the mighty Napoleon himself . that great man feel the misery so keenly , and grow so angry with himself for let in the mental pain , that he walk about vehemently , as a horse be walk when cold water upon a hot stomach have make colic only there be nobody to hit he in the rib , as the groom serve the nobler animal .
