Carne do not stride about in that style , to cast his wrath out of his toe , because his body never tingle with the sting - nettling of his mind as it be bind to do with all correct Frenchmen and his leg be long , he might have fall down a hole into ancestral vault before he know what he be up to . be as he be , he sate still , and think it out , and resolve to play his own game for a while , as his master be play for himself in Paris . 
 the next day he reappear at his seaside lodging , look as comely and stately as of old ; and the kind Widow Shanks be so glad to see he that he feel a rare emotion good - will towards she ; as the hard man must do sometimes , especially if other have be hard upon he . he even chuck little Susy under the chin , which amaze she so much that she stroke her face , to make sure of its be her own , and run away to tell her mother that the gentleman be come home so nice . then he order a special repast from John Prater 's for John , on the strength of all his winter dinner , have now paint on his sign - board " Universal Victualler , " care not a fig for the offence to Cheeseman , who never come now to have a glass with he , and have spoil all the appetite inspire by his window through the dismal suggestion of his rash act on the premise . instead of flatten their nose and open their mouth , and exclaim , " oh , should not   I like a bit of that ? " the child , if they venture to peep in at all , now do it with an anxious hope of horror , and a stealthy glance between the ham and bacon for something that might be hang up among the candle . and the bad of it be that the wise man in the village have fail to ascertain as yet " the reason why ' a do it . " until that be know , the most charitable neighbour could have no hope of forgive he . 
 Miss Dolly Darling have not see her hero of romance for a long time ; but something tell she or perhaps somebody that he be now at hand ; and to make sure about it , she resolve to have a walk . faith be very busy , as the lady of the house , in prepare for a visitor , the mother of Blyth Scudamore , whom she , with her usual kindness , intend to meet and bring back from the coach - road that evening ; for no less than three coach a day pass now within eight mile of Springhaven , and several of the native have see they . dolly be not to go in the carriage , because nobody know how many box the visitor might bring , inasmuch as she be to stop ever so long . " I be tired of all this fuss , " cry dolly ; " one would think Queen Charlotte be come , at the least ; and I dare say nearly all her luggage would go into the door - pocket . they be dreadfully poor ; and it serve they right , for be so dreadfully honest . " 
 " if you ever fall into poverty , " say Faith , " it will not be from that cause . when you get your money , you do not   pay your debt . you think that people should be proud to work for you for nothing . there be one house I be quite ashamed to pass by with you . how long have you owe poor Shoemaker Stickfast fifteen shilling and sixpence ? and you take advantage of he , because he dare not send it in to father . " 
 " fashionable lady never pay their debt , " dolly answer , as she spin round on one light heel , to float out a new petticoat that she be very proud of ; " this be not   pay for , nor this , nor this ; and you with your slow head have no idea how it add to the interest they possess . if I be not allow to have a bit of fashion in my dress , I can be in the fashion by not pay for it . " 
 " it be a most happy thing for you , dear child , that you be keep under some little control . what you would do , I have not the least idea , if you be not afraid of dear father , as you be . the bad of it be that he be never here now for as much as two day together . and then he be so glad to see we that he can not attend to our discipline or take notice of our dress . " 
 " ha ! you have inspire I ! " exclaim dolly , who rejoice in tease Faith . " the suggestion be your , and I will act upon it . from the village of Brighthelmstone , which be grow very fine , I will procure upon the strict credit a new Classic dress , with all tackle complete as dear father so well express it and then I will promenade I on the beach , with Charles in good livery and a big stick behind I . how then will springhaven rejoice , and every one that hath eye clap a spy - glass to they ! and what will old Twemlow say , and that frump of an Eliza , who condescend to give I little hint sometimes about tighten up so , perhaps , and let out so , and permit a little air to come in HERE " 
 " do be off , you wicke little animal ! " cry Faith , who in spite of herself could not help laugh , so well be dolly mimic Eliza Twemlow 's voice , and manner , and attitude , and even her figure , less fit by nature for the Classic attire ; " you be waste all my time , and do bad with your own . be off , or I will take a stick to ' e , as old Daddy Stakes say to the boy . " 
 take advantage of this state of thing , the young Miss Darling set forth by herself to dwell upon the beauty of the calm May sea , and her own pretty figure glass in tidal pool . she know that she would show to the utmost of her gift , with her bright complexion softly gleam in the sun , and dark gray eye through their deep fringe receive and return tenfold the limpid glimmer of the shore . and she feel that the spring of the year be with she , the bound of old time that renew his youth and power of go at any pace ; when the desire of the young be to ride he at full gallop , and the pleasure of the old be to stroke his nose and think . 
 dolly , with everything in her favour , youth and beauty , the time of year , the time of day , and the power of the place , as well as her own wish to look lovely , and to be love beyond reason , nevertheless come along very strictly , and keep herself most careful not to look about at all . at any rate , not towards the house , where people live , and therefore must look out . at the breadth of sea , with distant ship jot against the sky like chip , or dot with boat like bit of stick ; also at the playing of the little wave that run at the bottom of the sand , just now , after one another with a lively turn , and then jostle into white confusion , like a flock of sheep huddle up and hurry from a dog at these and at the warm cloud loiter in the sun she might use her bright eye without prejudice . but soon she have to turn they upon a near object . 
 " how absorbed we be in distant contemplation ! a happy sign , I hope , in these turbulent time . Miss Darling , will you condescend to include I in your view ? " 
 " I only understand simple English , " answer dolly . " Most of the other come from France , perhaps . we believe that you be go abroad again . " 
 " I wish that the subject have more interest for you , " Carne answer , with his keen eye fix on her , in the manner that half angered and half conquer she . " my time be not like that of happy young lady , with the world at their foot , and their chief business in it , to discover some new amusement . " 
 " you be not at all polite . but you never be that , in spite of your french education . " 
 " ah , there it be again ! you be so accustomed to the flattery of great people that a simple - minded person like myself have not the small chance of please you . ah , well ! it be my fate , and I must yield to it . " 
 " not at all , " reply dolly , who could never see the beauty of that kind of resignation , even in the case of Dan Tugwell . " there be no such thing as fate for a strong - willed man , though there may be for poor woman . " 
 " may I tell you my idea about that matter ? if so , come and rest for a moment in a quiet little shelter where the wind be not so cold . for there be no such thing as Spring in England . " 
 dolly hesitate , and with the proverbial result . to prove himself more polite than she suppose , Caryl Carne , hat in hand and with low bow preserve a respectful distance , conduct she to a little place of shelter , so pretty and humble and seclude by its own want of art , and simplicity of skill , that she be equally pleased and surprised with it . 
 " why , it be quite a little bower ! " she exclaim ; " as pretty a little nest as any bird could wish for . and what a lovely view towards the west and beyond Pebbleridge ! one could sit here forever and see the sun set . but I must have pass it fifty time without the least suspicion of it . how on earth have you manage to conceal it so ? that be to say , if it be your doing . surely the child must have find it out , because they go everywhere . " 
 " one brat do . but I give he such a scare that he never stop roar till next Sunday , and it frighten all the rest from look round that corner . if any other come , I shall pitch - plaster he , for I could not endure that noise again . but you see , at a glance , why you have fail to see it , as we always do with our little oversight , when humbly point out to we . it be the colour of the ground and the background too , and the grayness of the scanty growth that hide it . nobody find it out by walk across it , because of this swampy place on your side , and the shoot of flint down from the cliff on the other , all sharp as a knife , and as rough as a saw . and nobody come down to this end of the warren , neither be it see from the battery on the hill . only from the back be it likely to be invade , and there be nothing to make people look , or come , up here . so you have I altogether at your mercy , Miss Darling . " 
 dolly think within herself that it be much the other way , but could not well express her thought to that effect . and be of a brisk and versatile not to say volatile order , she go astray into a course of wonder concern the pretty little structure she behold . structure be not the proper word for it at all ; for it seem to have grow from the nature around , with a little aid of human hand to guide it . branch of sea - willow radiant with spring , and supple spray of tamarisk recover from the winter , be lightly inweave and arch together , with the soft compliance of reed and rush from the marsh close by , and the stout assistance of hazel rod from the westward cliff . the back be afford by a grassy hillock , with a tuft or two of brake - fern throwing up their bronzy crocket among the spray russet of last year 's pride . and beneath they a ledge of firm turf afford as fair a seat as even two sweet lover need desire . 
 " how clever he be , and how full of fine taste ! " think the simple - minded dolly ; " and all this time I have be take he for a gloomy , hard - hearted , unnatural man . Blyth Scudamore never could have make this lovely bower . " 
 in this conclusion she be altogether wrong . Scudamore could have make it , and would have make it gladly , with bright love to help he . but Carne never could , and would have scorn the pleasant task . it be Charron , the lively Frenchman , who , with the aid of old Jerry , have achieve this pretty feat , work to relieve his dull detention , with a Frenchman 's playful industry and tasteful joy in nature . but Carne be not likely to forego this credit . 
 " I think I have do it pretty well , " he say , in reply to her smile of admiration ; " with such scanty material , I mean , of course . and I shall think I have do it very well indeed , if you say that you like it , and crown it with new glory by sit for a moment in its unpretentious shade . if your brother come down , as I hope he will , next week , I shall beg he to come and write a poem here . the place be fitter for a poet than a prosy vagabond like I . " 
 " it be very hard that you should be a a wanderer , I mean , " dolly answer , look at he with a sweet thrill of pity ; " you have do nothing to deserve it . how unfairly fortune have always treat you ! " 
 " Fortune could make I a thousand time more than the just compensation even now , if she would . such a glorious return for all my bitter loss and outcast condition , that I should but it be useless to think of such thing , in my low state . the fate have be hard with I , but never shall they boast that they drive I from my pure sense of honour . oh yes , it be damp . but let I cure it thus . " 
 for dolly , grow anxious about his meaning , yet ready to think about another proposal , be desirous to sit down on the sweet ledge of grass , yet uneasy about her pale blue sarsenet , and uncertain that she have not see something of a little sea - snail   live in a yellow house , dado with red , whom to crush would be a cruel act to her dainty fabric . but if he be there , he be sit upon unavenged ; for Carne , pull off his light buff cloak , fling it on the seat ; after which the young lady could scarcely be rude enough not to sit . 
 " oh , I be so sorry now ! perhaps it will be spoil , " she say ; " for you say that the fate be against you always . and I be sure that they always combine against I , when I wear anything of that colour . " 
 " I be go the wrong way to work , " think Carne . " what a little vixen it be ; but what a beauty ! " for his love for she be chiefly a man 's admiration . and bodily she look worthy now of all that could be do in that way , with the light flow in through the bud arch and flash upon the sweet flush of her cheek . carne gaze at she without a word or thought , simply admire , as he never have admire anything , except himself , till now . then she feel all the meaning of his gaze , and turn away . 
 " but you must look at I and tell I something , " he say , in a low voice , and take both her hand ; " you shall tell I what my fate must be . whether you can ever come to love I , as I have love you , long and long . " 
 " you have no right to speak to I like that , " she answer , still avoid his eye , and strive to show proper anger ; " no gentleman would think of take advantage of a lady so . " 
 " I care not what be right or wrong . look up , and tell I that you hate I . dolly , I suppose you do . " 
 " then you be quite wrong " she give he one bright glance of contradiction ; " no . I have always be so sorry for you , and for all your trouble . you must not ask I to say more . " 
 " but I must ; I must . that be the very thing that I must do . only say that you love I , dolly . dolly darling , tell I that . or let your lovely eye say it for you . " 
 " my lovely eye must not tell story " they be gaze softly at he now " and I do not   think I can say it yet . " 
 " but you will you shall ! " he exclaim , with passion grow as he draw she near ; " you shall not slip from I , you shall not stir , until you have answer I one question be there anybody else , my dolly ? " 
 " you frighten I . you forget who I be . of course there be a great many else , as you call it ; and I be not to be call , for a moment , your DOLLY . " 
 " no , not for a moment , but forever . " Carne be accustom to the way of girl , and read all their word by the light of their eye . " your little heart begin to know who love it well than all the world put together . and for that reason I will leave you now . Farewell , my darling ; I conquer myself , for the sake of what be worth a thousand of it . " 
 dolly be in very sad confusion , and scarcely know what she might do next that be to say , if he still go on . pleasant conceit and bright coquetry ill supply the place of honest pride and gentle self - respect , such as Faith be bl with . carne might have kiss dolly a hundred time , without much resistance , for his strong will have master her ; but she would have hat he afterwards . he do not kiss she once ; and she almost wish that he have offer one one little tribute of affection   as the Valentines express it   as soon as he be go , and the crisis of not know what to do be past . " I should have let he I believe I should , " she reflect , sagely recover herself ; " but how glad I ought to be that he do not ! and I do hope he will not   come back again . the next time I meet he , I shall sink into the earth . " 
 for her hat have fall off , and her hair be out of order , and she see two crinkle near the buckle of her waist ; and she have not so much as a look - glass to be sure that she look nice again . with a heavy sigh for all these woe , she gather a flossy bud of willow , and fix it on her breast - knot , to defy the world ; and then , without heed of the sea , sun , or sand , go home with short breath , and quick blush , and some wonder ; for no man 's arm , except her father 's , have ever be round her waist till now . 




 CHAPTER XLIII 

 little and great PEOPLE 

 if ever a wise man depart from wisdom , or a sober place from sobriety , the man be John Prater , and the place Springhaven , towards the middle of June , 1804 . there have be some sharp rumour of great thing before ; but the good people , have be mislead so often , shake their head without produce of their content ; until Captain Stubbard come out in his shirt sleeve one bright summer morning at half past nine , with a large print paper in one hand and a slop basin full of hot paste in the other . his second boy , George , in the absence of Bob   who be now draw ration at Woolwich , follow , with a green baize apron on , and carry a hearth - brush tie round with a string to keep the hair stiff . 
 " lay it on thick on the shutter , my son . never mind about any other notice , except the one about young man want . no hurry ; keep your elbow up ; only do not   dab my breech , nor the shirt you have on Sunday . " 
 by this time there be half a dozen people wait ; for this shutter of Widow Shanks be now accept as the central board and official panel of all public business and authorise intelligence . not only because all Royal Proclamations , offer of reward , and issue of menace be post on that shutter and the one beyond the window   which serve as a postscript and glossary to it , but also inasmuch as the kind - hearted Captain , begin now to understand the native which be not to be do pugnaciously , as he have first attempt it , neither by any show of interest in they   than which they detest nothing more , but by take they coolly , as they take themselves , and gradually slide , without any thought about it , into the wholesome contagion of their mind , and the divine gift of take thing easily our Captain Stubbard may be fairly now declare to have make himself almost as good as a native , by the way in which he minister to their content . 
 for nothing delight they more than to hear of great wonder go on in other place of battle , plague , pestilence , famine , and fire ; of people whose wife run away with other people , or highwayman stop the coach of a bishop . be full of good - nature , they enjoy these thing , because of the fine sympathy call out to their own credit , and the sense of pious gratitude arouse towards Heaven , that they never permit such thing among they . perceive this genial desire of theirs , the stout Captain of the Foxhill battery be kind enough to meet it with worthy subject . receive officially a London newspaper almost every other day , as soon as it have tread the round of his friend , his regular practice be to cut out all the piece of lofty public interest the first - rate murder , the exploit of highwayman , the episode of high life , the gallant execution , the embezzlement of demagogue , in a word , whatever quiet people find a fond delight in ruminate and these he paste   sometimes upside down   upon his shutter . Springhaven have a good deal of education , and enjoy most of all what be hard to read . 
 but this great piece of news , that should smother all the rest , seem now to take a terrible time in come . all the gaffer be wait who have wait to see the result of Mr. Cheeseman 's suicide , and their patience be less on this occasion . at length the great Captain unfold his broad sheet , but even then hold it upside down for a minute . it be below their dignity to do anything but grunt , put their spec on their nose , and lean chin upon staff . they deserve to be reward , and so they be . 
 for this grand poster , which overlap the shutter , be a Royal Proclamation , all print in red ink , announce that his Majesty King George the 3rd would on the 25th of June then ensue hold a grand review upon shotbury down of all the Volunteer force and Reserve , mount , footman , or artillery , of the four county form the Southeast Division , to wit , Surrey , Kent , Sussex , and hant . certain regiment of the line would be appoint to act with they ; and officer in command be order to report at once ,   c. ,   c. God save the king . 
 if shotbury Down have be ten mile off , Springhaven would have think very little of the matter ; for no one would walk ten mile inland to see all the sojer that ever be shoot , or even the " King and Queen , and their fifteen little one . " Most of the little one be very large now ; but the village have see they in a travel show , and expect they to continue like it . but Shotbury Down be only three mile inland ; and the people   who think nothing of twenty mile along the coast   resolve to face a league of peril of the solid earth , because if they only turn round upon their trudge , they could see where they live from every corner of the road . they always do all thing with one accord ; the fishing fleet all should stand still on the sand , and the house should have to keep house for themselves . that be to say , perhaps , all except one . 
 " do as you like , " say Mrs. Tugwell to her husband ; " nothing as you do make much differ to I now . if you feel you can be happy with they thousand of young man , and I without one leave fit to lift a big crock , go your way , Zeb ; but you do not   catch I go , with the tear come into my eye every time I see a young man to remind I of Dan though there will not   be one there fit to stand at his side . and he perhaps fight against his own King now ! " 
 " whatever hath coom to Dannel be all along of your own fault , I tell ' e. " Captain Tugwell have scarcely enjoy a long pipe since the night when he discharge his paternal duty , with so much vigour , and such sad result . not that he feel any qualm of conscience , though his heart be sometimes heavy , but because his good wife be a good wife no long , in the important sphere of the pan , pot , and kettle , or even in listen to his adventure with the proper exclamation in the proper place . and not only she , but all his child , from Timothy down to Solomon , instead of a pleasant chatter around he , and little attention , and a smile to catch a smile , seem now to shrink from he , and hold whisper in a corner , and watch he with timid eye , and wonder how soon their own time would come to be lash and turn away . and as for the woman , whether up or down the road but as he would not admit , even to himself , that he care twopence what they think , it be useless to give voice to their opinion , which they do quite sufficiently . Zebedee Tugwell feel sure that he have do the right thing , and therefore admire himself , but would have enjoy himself more if he have do the wrong one . 
 " what fault of mine , or of his , poor lamb ? " Mrs. Tugwell ask , with some irony . she know that her husband could never dare to go to see the King without she for no married man in the place would venture to look at he twice if he do such a thing and she have make up her own mind to go from the first ; but still , he should humble himself before she do it . " be it I as colte he ? or be it he as gash himself , like the prophet of Baal , when ' a be go hunt ? " 
 " no ; but you cocker he up , the same as be do to they , by the wicked king , and his wife the bad woman as ever live . if they have not   gash theirselve , I reckon , the true man of God would ' a do it for they , the same as he cut their throat into the brook Kishon . Solomon be the wise man as ever live , and Job the most patient the same as I be and Elijah , the Tishbite , the most justest . " 
 " you well finish up with all the Psalms of David , and the Holy Children , and the Burial Service . no more call for Parson Twemlow , or the new Churchwarden come in place of Cheeseman , because ' a try to hang his self . Zebedee Tugwell in the pulpit ! Zebedee , come round with the plate ! Parson Tugwell , if you please , a - read out the ten commandment ! but ' un ought to leave out the sixth , for fear of spoil 's own dinner afterwards ; and the seventh , if ' a hope to go to see King George the third , with another man 's woman to his elbow ! " 
 " when you begin to go on like that , " Captain Tugwell reply , with some dignity , " the only thing as a quiet man can do be to go out of houze , and have a half - pint of small ale . " he put his hat on his head and go to do it . 
 notwithstanding all this and much more , when the great day come for the Grand Review , very few people see more of the king , or enter more kindly into all his thought or rather the thought that they make he think than Zebedee Tugwell and his wife Kezia . the place be so near home , and the smoke of their own chimney and mast of their smack as good as in sight if you know where to look it be natural for they to regard the King as a stranger require to be teach about their place . this sense of proprietary right be strong in dog and bird and cow and rabbit , and everything that act by nature 's law . when a dog sit in front of his kennel , fast chain , every strange dog that come in at the gate confess that the premise be his , and all the treasure they contain ; and if he hunt about which he be like enough to do , unless full of self - respect and fresh victual for any bone invest in the earth to ripen , by the vested owner , he do it with a low tail and many prick of conscience , perhaps hop in his heart that he may discover nothing to tempt he into breach of self - respect . but now man be order , in this matter , to be of low principle than their dog . 
 King George the third , who hat pomp and show , and have in his blood the old german sense of patriarchal kingship , would have enjoy a good talk with Zebedee and his wife Kezia , if he have meet they on the down alone ; but , alas , he be surround with great people , and oblige to restrict himself to the upper order , with whom he have less sympathy . Zebedee , perceive this , make all allowance for he , and buy a new Sunday hat the very next day , for fear of wear out the one he have take off to his Majesty , when his Majesty look at he , and her Majesty as well , and they manifestly say to one another , what a very fine subject they have find . such be loyalty aye , and royalty in those time that we despise . 
 but large event demand our heed . there be forty thousand gallant fellow , from the age of fifteen upwards , do their good to look like soldier , and some almost succeed . true it be that their leg and arm be not all of one pattern , nor their hat put on their head alike any more than the head on their shoulder be neither do they swing together , as they would have do to a good swathe of grass ; but for all that , and make due allowance for the necessity they be under of star incessantly at the King , any man who understand they would have praise they wonderfully . and they go about in such wide formation , and occupy so much of their native land , that the well - drill regiment Napoleon possess would have look quite small among they . 
 " they understand furze , " say a fine young officer of the staff , who have ride up to Admiral Darling 's carriage and salute three lady who keep watch there . " I doubt whether many of the regular force would have get through that brake half so well ; certainly not without double gaiter . if the French ever land , we must endeavour to draw they into furzy ground , and then set the Volunteers at they . no Frenchman can do much with prickle in his leg . " 
 Lady Scudamore smile , for she be think of her son , who would have jump over any furze - bush there and the fir - tree too , accord to her conviction ; dolly also show her very beautiful tooth ; but Faith look at he gratefully . 
 " it be very kind of you , Lord Dashville , to say the good of we that you can find to say . but I fear that you be laugh to yourself . you know how well they mean ; but you think they can not do much . " 
 " no , that be not what I think at all . so far as I can judge , which be not much , I believe that they would be of the great service , if the Country should unfortunately need they . man for man , they be as brave as train troop , and many of they can shoot well . I do not   mean to say that they be fit to meet a french army in the open ; but for act on their flank , or rear , or in a woode country however , I have no right to venture an opinion , have never see active service . " 
 Miss Darling look at he with some surprise , and much approval of his modesty . so strongly do most of the young officer who come to her father 's house lie down the law , and criticise even Napoleon 's tactic . 
 " how beautiful Springhaven must be look now ! " he say , after dolly have offer her opinion , which she seldom long withhold . " the cottage must be quite cover with rose , whenever they be not too near the sea ; and the tree at their good , full of leaf and blossom , by the side of the brook that feed they . all the rest of the coast be so hard and barren , and cover with chalk instead of grass , and the shore so straight and star . but I have never be there at this time of year . how much you must enjoy it ! surely we ought to be able to see it , from this high ground somewhere . " 
 " yes , if you will ride to that shattered tree , " say Faith , " you will have a very fine view of all the valley . you can see round the corner of Foxhill there , which shut out most of it just here . I think you have meet our Captain Stubbard . "

 Faith have a lovely moss - rise in her hat a rose just peep through its lattice at mankind , before it should open and blush at they and she know what it be that he admire more than the sweetest rise that ever gem itself with dew .  this fine young woman , to use the word of Mr. Swipes , colour softly , at his noble lordship 's gaze , to the tint of the rose - bud in her hat ; and then speak coldly to countervail her blush .

Lord Dashville will tell we where we ought to go . "

' and so , if you please , lady , be quite ready , and allow I the honour of conduct you . " 
 with a low bow , he set off about his business , leave the lady in a state of sweet disturbance . Blyth Scudamore 's mother weep a little , for ancient trouble and present pleasure .  Coachman Gregory smile in his sleeve . he know that the Admiral have that day a duty far beyond his power to bring up his Sea - Fencibles to see the King upon which they have insist and then to fetch they all back again , and send they on board of their several craft in a state of strict sobriety . and Gregory mean to bear a hand , and lift it pretty frequently towards the most loyal part of man , in the large festivity of that night . he smack his lip at the thought of this , and give a little flick to his horse . 
 after a long time , long enough for two fair drive to Springhaven and back , and when even the young be grow weary of glare , and dust , and clank , and din , and blare , and roar , and screech music , Lord Dashville ride up through a cloud of rove chalk , and after a little talk with the lady , order the coachman to follow he . then stop the carriage at a proper distance , he lead the three lady towards the King , who be thoroughly tired , and have forget all about they . his Majesty 's sole desire be to get into his carriage and go to sleep ; for he be threescore year and six of age , and his health not such as it use to be . ever since twelve o'clock he have be sit in a box make of feather - edge board , which the newspaper call a pavilion , have two little curtain   both of which stick fast   for his only defence against sun , noise , and dust . moreover , his seat be a board full of knot , with a strip of thin velvet throw over it ; and her Majesty sit towards the other end   that the public might see between they , and weigh more than he do , every time she jump up , he go down , and every time she plump down , he go up . but he never complain , and only slowly get tired . " thank God ! " he say , gently , " it be all over now . my dear , you must be monstrous tired ; and scarcely a bit to eat all day . but I lock some in the seat - box this morning no trust anybody but oneself . let we get into the coach and have at they .  but as soon as he see Lady Scudamore , the King 's good - nature overcome the weariness of the moment . he take she kindly by the hand , and look at her face , which bear the mark of many heavy trial ; and she , who have often see he when the world be bright before she , could not smother one low sob , as she think of all that have be since . 
 " do not   cry , do not   cry , my dear , " say the King , with his kind heart show in his eye ; " we must bow to the will of the Lord , who give sad trial to every one of we . we must think of the good , and not the evil . bless I , keep your spirit up . your son be do very well indeed , very well indeed , from all I hear . good chip of the old block , very good chip . will cure my grandchild , as soon as they want it ; and nobody be ever in good health now . " 
 " no , your Majesty , if you please , my son be in the Royal Navy , fight for his Country and his king . and he have already capture " 
 " three french frigate . to be sure , I know . well than cure three hundred people . fine young officer very fine young officer . must come to see I when he get old . there , you be laugh ! that be as it should be . goodbye , young lady . forty mile to go tonight , and very rough road very rough indeed . monstrous pretty girl ! Uncommon glad that George be not   here to see they . well stay in the country too good for London . must be off ; sha'n't   have a bit o ' sleep to - night , because of sleep the whole way there , and then sure to be late in the morning , not a bit of breakfast till eight o'clock , and all the day throw upside down ! darling , darling the right name for they ! but they must not   come to London . no , no , no . too much wickedness there already . very glad George be not   here to - day ! " 
 his Majesty be talk , as he always do , with the firm conviction that his word intend for the public ear would reach it , while those address , without change of tone , to himself , would be strictly private . but instead of offend any one , this on the whole give great satisfaction , and impress nine people out of ten with a strong and special regard for he , because almost every one suppose himself to be admit at first sight to the inner confidence of the king . and to what could he attribute this ? he would do his own merit great demerit unless he attribute it to they , and to the King an unusual share of sagacity in perceive they . 




 CHAPTER XLIV 

 down among the dead MEN 

 that grand review at Shotbury be declare by all who take part in it , or at all understand the subject , to have be a most remarkable and quite unparalleled success . not only do it show what noble stuff there be in Englishmen , and how naturally they take to arm , but also it inspire with martial feeling and happy faith the wife and mother of all the gallant warrior there . it would make the blood - stain despot cower upon his throne of murder , and teach he the madness of invade any land so fortified . 
 however , Napoleon fail to see the matter in that wholesome light , and smile a grim and unkind smile as he read Caryl Carne 's report of those " left - handed and uncouth manoeuvre . " " one of your Majesty 's feeble regiment would send the whole of those lout to the devil ; and I be bind to impress once more , with all deference to your infallible judgment , the vast importance of carry out your grand design at the first moment . all be prepared on my part . one day 's notice be all I need . " 
 so write Carne ; and perhaps the truth , as usual , lie about half - way between the two opinion . even Carne be not admit to a perfect knowledge of his master 's scheme . but to keep thing move and man alert , the Emperor come to the coast at once , busy as he be in Paris , and occupy for several week , with short interval of absence , the house prepare for he near Boulogne , whence he watch and quicken the ripening of his mighty plan against we . 
 now carne himself , while work with new vigour and fresh enterprise , have a narrow escape from invasion . Captain Stubbard , stir up now and again by Mr. Twemlow , have thoroughly search all cover place , likely to harbour gunpowder , within at least six mile of his fort , that be to say , all likely place , save and except the right one . by do this he have do for himself as regard sweet hospitality among all the lead farmer , maltster , tanner , and miller for mile around . even those whose premise be not enter , as if they have be Frenchmen , have a brother - in - law , or at least a cousin , whose wooden bar have be knock up . and the most atrocious thing of all , if there could be anything bad than bad , be that the Captain din one day , at a market - ordinary , with Farmer , or you might say Squire Hanger for the good part of his land follow to he from his father and have rum and water with he , and speak his health , and tuck Mrs. Hanger up into the shay , and ride alongside to guarantee they ; and then the next day , on the very same horse , up he come at Hanger - dene , and overhaul every tub on the premise , with a parchment as big as a malt - shovel ! such a man be not fit to lay a knife and fork by . 
 some sense of the harm he have do to himself , without a bit of good to any one , dwelt heavily in the Captain 's mind , as he ride up slowly upon the most amiable of the battery - horse for all sailor can ride , from long practice on the wave and strike a stern stroke , with a stick like a linstock , upon the old shutter that serve for a door and the front entrance to Carne Castle . there use to be a fine old piece of workmanship in solid and bold oak here , a door divide in the middle else no man might swing it back and even so pierce with a wicket , for small people to get through . that mighty door be not wear out , for it be not three hundred year old yet , and therefore scarcely in middle life ; but the mortgagee who have sack the place of all that be worth a sack to hold it , these have a very fine offer for that door , from a rich man come out of a dust - bin . and this be one of the many little thing that make Caryl Carne unpleasant . 
 " I do not require production of your warrant . the whole place be open to your inspection , " say Carne , who have long be prepare for this visit ; " open to all the wind and rain , and the low part sometimes fill with water . the upper room , or rather the few that remain of they , be scarcely safe for a person of any weight to walk in , but you be most welcome to try they , if you like ; and this gentleman , I think , might not fall through . here be my quarter ; not quite so snug as my little room at the widow 's ; but I can offer you some bread and cheese , and a glass of country cider . the vault or cellar have hold good wine in their time , but only empty cask and break bottle now . " 
 Captain Stubbard have know for many year the silent woe of poverty , and now he observe with some good - will the young man 's sad but haughty smile . then he order his young subaltern , his battery - mate , as he call he , to ascend the broad crumbling staircase , and glance into the dismantled chamber , while himself with the third of the party a trusty old gunner should inspect the cellarage . 
 " we will not keep you long , sir , " he say to Carne ; " and if you be kind enough to show we the way , which be easily lose in a place of this kind , we shall be all the quick . wilkin , when you have do up there , wait here for we . shall we want a light , sir ? " 
 " in the winter , you could hardly do without one , but at this time of year , I think you may . at any rate I will bring a lantern , and we can light it if want . but the truth be that I know next to nothing of those sepulchral place . they would not be very tempting , even without a ghost , which they be say to have . " 
 " a ghost ! " cry the Captain ; " I do not   like that . not that I have much faith in they ; although one never can be sure . but at this time of day what be it like ? " 
 " I have never see she , and be quite content without it . it be say to be an ancestress of mine , a Lady Cordelia Carne , who be murder , when her husband be away , and bury down there , after be throw into the moat . the old people say that whenever her ghost be walk , the water of the moat burst in and cover the floor of the vault , that she may flit along it , as she use to do . but of course one must not listen to that sort of fable . " 
 " perhaps you will go in front , sir , because you know the way . it be my duty to inspect these place ; and I be devilish sorry for it ; but my duty must be do . " 
 " you shall see every hole and corner , include the stone that be put up to commemorate her murder and keep she quiet . but I should explain that these vault extend for the entire length of the building , except just in the middle , where we now stand . for a few yard the centre of the building seem to have never be excavate , as to which you will convince yourself . you may call the cellar east and west , or right and left , or north and south , or uphill and downhill , or anything else , for really they be so much alike , and partition into cell so much alike , that I scarcely know which be which myself , come suddenly from the daylight . but you understand those thing much well . a sailor always know his bearing . this lead to the entrance of one set . " 
 Carne lead the Captain and old Gunner Bob as he be call in the battery along a dark and narrow passage , whose mouth be browe with ivy . half - way through , they find an archway on the right - hand side , open at right angle into long and badly light vault . in this arch there be no door ; but a black step - ladder   make of oak , no doubt , very steep and rather rickety , be plant to tempt any venturesome foot . 
 " be you sure this ladder be safe ? " the Captain be by no mean in love with the look of it . " my weight have increase remarkably in the fine air of Springhaven . if the bottom be rotten , the top will not   help we . " 
 " let I go first . it be my duty , as the owner ; and I have no family dependent on I . my neck be of no value , compare to your , Captain . " 
 " how I have mistake this young man ! " think the brave yet prudent Stubbard . " I call he a frenchified fool , whereas he be a downright Englishman ! I shall ask he to dinner next week , if Jemima can get a new leg for the dripping - pan . " 
 follow warily , with Gunner Bob behind he , and not disdain the strong arm of the owner , the Captain of Foxhill be land in the vault , and be there , make a strict examination . he even poke his short sword into the bung - hole of three or four empty barrel , that Bob might be satisfied also in his conscience . " Matter of form , " he say , " matter of form , sir , when we know who people be ; but you might have to do it yourself , sir , if you be in the service of your king . you ought to be that , Mr. Carne ; and it be not too late , in such day as these be , to begin . take my advice such a fine young man ! " 
 " alas , my dear sir , I can not afford it . what officer can live upon his pay for a generation ? " 
 " Gospel truth ! " cry the Captain , warmly ; " Gospel truth ! and more than that he must be the last of his generation , or else send his young ' uns to the workhouse . what thing I could tell you , Mr. Carne ! but here we be at the end of the vault ; all empty , as I can certify ; and I hope , my dear sir , that you may live to see they fill with good wine , as they use to be . " 
 " thank you , but there be no hope of that . shall we take the vault of the other end next , or examine the chapel , and the outer building outer ruin , I should say ? " 
 " oh , a little open air first , for goodness sake ! " say the Captain , go heavily up the old step ; " I be pretty nearly choke with all this mildew . a little fresh air , before we undertake the other lot . " 
 as soon as the echo of their step be dead , Charron , old Jerry , and another man jump down from a loop - hole into the vault they have leave , pile up a hoarding at the entrance , and with a crowbar swing back a heavy oak hatch in the footing of the outer wall . a volume of water pour in from the moat , or rather from the stream which have once supply it . see this , they disappear with a soft and pleasant chuckle . 
 the owner keep Stubbard such a time among the ruin , tell he some fine old legend , and otherwise lead he in and out , that when a bit of food and a glass of old Cognac be propose by way of interlude , the Captain heartily embrace the offer . then Carne conduct his three visitor , for Wilkins have now rejoin they , into a low room poorly furnish , and regale they beyond his promise . " rare stuff ! " exclaim Stubbard , with a wink at Carne . " ah , I see that free - trade still exist . no concern of mine , except to enjoy its benefit . here be to your very good health , sir , and I be proud to have make your acquaintance . " 
 " have another drop ; it can hurt no one , " Carne declare , and the Captain acquiesce . 
 " well , I suppose we must finish our job , " the official visitor at length pronounce ; " a matter of form , sir , and no offence ; but we be bind to carry out our duty . there be nothing leave , except the other lot of vault ; but the light begin to fail we , for underground work . I hope they be not so dark as those we have be through . " 
 " just about the same . you would hardly know one set from the other , as I tell you , except for the stone that record the murder . perhaps we have well light the lantern now ? " 
 " by all mean . I do not   half like that story of the lady that walk on the water . it do seem so gashly and unchristian altogether . not that I have any fear of ghost not likely , for I have never even see one . " 
 " I have , " say Gunner Bob , in a deep voice , which make they all glance through the ivy . " I have , and a fearful one it be . " 
 " do not   be a fool , Bob , " the Captain whisper ; " we do not   want to hear about that now . allow I to carry the lantern , Mr. Carne ; it throw such shadow from the way you hold it . why , surely , this be where we be before ! " 
 " you might easily fancy so , " Carne answer , smile , " especially with a mind at all excited " 
 " my mind be not excited , sir ; not at all excite ; but as calm as it ever be in all its life . " 
 " then two thing will show you that these be the other vault . the arch be on your left hand , instead of on your right " he have bring they in now from the other end of the passage " and this entrance , as you see , have a door in it , which the other have not . perhaps the door be to keep the ghost in " his laugh sound hollow , and like a mocking challenge along the dark roof " for this be the part she be suppose to walk in . but so much for the door ! the money - lender have not leave we a door that will stand a good kick . you may find our old door in Wardour Street . " 
 as he speak , he set foot against the makeshift door , and away it go , as he have predict . crash on the step as it fall , it turn over , and a great splash arise at the bottom . 
 " why , bless my heart , there be a flood of water there ! " cry Stubbard , peep timidly down the step , on which   if the light have be clear , and that of his mind in the same condition   he might have see the mark of his own boot . " a flood of water , perhaps six foot deep ! I could scarcely have believe , but for that and the door , that these be not the very vault that we have examine . but what business have the water there ? " 
 " no business at all , any more than we have , " Carne answer , with some rudeness , for it do not suit he to encourage too warmly the friendship of Captain Stubbard ; " but I tell you that the place become cover with water whenever the ghost intend to walk . probably there be not more than a foot of water " there be in fact about three inch " and as you be bind to carry out your duty " 
 " my dear sir , I be satisfied , perfectly satisfied . who could keep gunpowder under water , or even in a flooded cellar ? I shall have the great pleasure in report that I search Carne Castle not of course suspiciously , but narrowly , as we be bind to do , in execution of our warrant " 
 " if you would not mind look in this direction , " whisper Carne , who could never be content , " I think I could show you , just beyond the murder - stone yes , and it seem to be come towards we , as white as a winding - sheet ; do come and look . " 
 " no , sir , no ; it be not my duty " the Captain turn away , with his hair upon the rise . " I be send here to look for saltpetre , not spectre . no officer in his Majesty 's service can be expect Bob , and Wilkins , be you there ? " 
 " yes , sir , yes we have have quite enough of this ; and unless you give the order " 
 " here she come , I do declare ! " whisper Carne , with extraordinary calmness . 
 " Bob , and Wilkins , give I one arm each . make for daylight in close order . you may be glad to see your grandmother , young man ; but I decline to have anything to say to she . Bob , and Wilkins , bear a hand ; I feel a little shaky in my low timber . run for your life , but do not   leave I behind . run , lad , like the very devil ! " for a groan of sepulchral depth , and big enough to lift a granite tombstone , issue from the vault , and wail along the sombre archway . all the Artillerymen flee , as if the muzzle of their big gun be slew upon they , and very soon the sound of horse ' heel , urge at a perilous pace down the hill , ring back as the echo of that grand groan . 
 " I think I do that pretty well , my Captain , " cry Charron , ascend from the vault with dripping boot ; " I deserve a glass of Cognac , if they have leave I any . happy be stoobar that he be content , without break his neck at the inspector 's step . " 
 " he have satisfy his conscience , " Carne answer , grimly ; " yet it can not be blameless , to make he run so fast . I be glad we have be save from kill they . it would have be hard to know what to do next . but he will never trouble we here again . " 




 CHAPTER XLV 

 FATHER , and child 

 " tell Miss Faith , when she come in , that I shall be glad to see she , " say Admiral Darling to his trusty butler , one hot afternoon in August . he have just come home from a long rough ride , to spend at least one day in his own house , and after overhaul his correspondence , go into the dining - room , as the cool in the house , to refresh himself a little with a glass of light wine before go up to dress for dinner . there he sit in an arm - chair , and look at his hand , which be brown by the sun , and tremble from a long period of heavy work and light sleep . he be get too old to endure it with impunity , yet angry with himself for show it . but he be not think of himself alone . 
 " I hope she will be sensible " he be talk to himself , as elderly people be apt to do , especially after be leave to themselves ; " I hope she will see the folly of it of live all her life as the bride of a ghost ; and herself such a beautiful , cheerful darling ! loving , warm - hearted , sweet - temper , adore child , and adore by they ; obedient , gentle I can not   think of anything good that she have not   get , except common - sense . and even for that , I like she all the more ; because it be so different from all the other girl . they have get too much one lover out of sight , even for a month or two , go fight for his Country , what do they do but take up with another , as I very greatly fear our dolly would ? but Faith why , my darling , how well you look ! " 
 " how I wish that I could say the same of you , dear father ! " say the lovely young woman , while kiss he , and smooth with her soft hand his wrinkle forehead ; " you never use to have these little tuck and gather here . I would rather almost that the French should come and devour we all , than see my father , whenever we do see he , once in a month , say , gauffre like this as their laundress do it and get reduce to the classical shape , so that I can put one arm round he . " 
 " my darling , " say the Admiral , though proud at heart of the considerable reduction of his stomach , " you should not say such thing to I , to remind I how very old I be ! " 
 father be crafty , and daughter childish , as behove the both of they . the Admiral know , as well as if he have order it , what Faith would do . and she must have perceive his depth , if only she have take a moment to think of it . because when she plump , like a child , into his arm , how come his arm to be so wide open ? and when two great tear roll down her cheek , how spring his handkerchief so impromptu out from beneath his braid lappet ? 
 " tell I what harm I have do , " she ask , with a bright smile dawn through the dew of her dark eye ; " what have I do to vex you , father , that you say thing fit to make I cry ? and yet I ought to laugh , because I know so well that you be only fish for compliment . you be get so active that I shall be frightened to go for a walk or a ride with you . only I do love to see you look fat , and your darling forehead smooth and white . " 
 " my dear child , I must get up my substance . this very day I begin in earnest . because I be to be a great man , Faith . how would you like to have to call I ' Sir Charles ' ? " 
 " not at all , darling ; except when you deserve it , by be cross to I ; and that never , never happen . I wish there be more chance of it . " 
 " well , dear , if you will not , the other people must ; for his Majesty have be graciously pleased to turn I into a Baronet . he say that I have earn it ; and perhaps I have ; at any rate , he put it so nicely that without be churlish I could not refuse . and it will be a good thing for Frank , I hope , by bring he back from his democratic stuff . to myself it be useless ; but my child ought to like it . " 
 " and so they will , father , for your own dear sake . let I be the first to salute you , father . oh , dolly will be in such a rage because you tell I , without tell she ! " 
 " I never think of that , " say the Admiral , simply ; " I be afraid that I shall get in for it . however , I have a right to please myself , and you need not tell she until I do . but that be not all my news , and not by any mean the good of it . the King be remind , the other day , of all that he and his family owe to the late Sir Edmond Scudamore , and well late than never , he have order your governess , as he call she , to be put on the list for a pension of 300 pound a year . nothing that once get into his head can ever be get out of it , and he be shock at see his old physician 's widow ' go out as a governess go out as a governess great disgrace to the royal family ! ' I be very glad that it happen so . " 
 " and so be I. she ought to have have it long and long ago , especially after the sad misfortune of her husband . you will let I tell she ? it will be such a pleasure . " 
 " certainly , my dear ; you be the very one to do it . tell she that her eld pupil be come with a little piece of news for she ; it will make her smile she have a very pretty smile , which remind I of the gallant Blyth . and now , my child , the third piece of news concern yourself your good , and dutiful , and exceedingly sensible self . ahem ! " cry the Admiral , as he always do , when he fear that he might have overstep the truth . 
 " I know what it be ; you need not tell I , " Faith answer , confirm her fear at once . " it be no use , father ; it be no good at all unless you intend to forget your own promise . " 
 " that I shall never do , " he reply , while look at she sadly ; " no , my dear child , I shall never attempt to drive instead of lead you . but you have not hear I out as yet . you do not   even know who it be I mean . " 
 " oh yes , I do ; I know well enough , father . I be not like dolly , universally admire . because I do not want to be . you mean Lord Dashville can you tell I that you do not ? " 
 " no , my dear " Sir Charles be a little surprised that Faith should be so quick , for   like most people of gentle nature   she be take to be slow , because she never snap " I can not deny that it be Lord Dashville , because that be the man , and no other . but how you could tell surpass I , and it show that he must be very often in your mind : " the Admiral think he have catch she there . " now can you say anything against he ? be he not honest , manly , single - minded , faithful as yourself , I do believe , good - look , well - breed , a Tory , and a gentleman , certain to make any woman happy whom he love ? can you say a syllable against all that ? " 
 " no , " reply Faith a very long , slow " no , " as if she only wish she could say something hard about he . 
 " very well , " her father go on , with triumph , " and can you deny that he be just the person you might have take a great liking to fall in love with , as they call it if only he have come before your mind be full of somebody else a very fine young fellow , no doubt ; but my darling , I will not   say a word against he , only you know what I mean too well . and be you forever to be like a nun because it have please the Lord to take he from you ? " 
 " Lord Dashville have not advance himself in my good opinion , if he care for that , " say Faith , start sideways , as a woman always do , from the direct issue , " by go to you , when I decline to have anything more to say to he . " 
 " my dear , you be unjust , " reply Sir Charles ; " not purposely , I know , for you be the most upright darling that can be , in general . but you accuse young Dashville of what he never do . it be his good mother , the Countess of Blankton , a most kind - hearted and lady - like person , without any nonsense about she , who give I the good cup of tea I ever taste , and speak with the very good feeling possible . she put it so sweetly that I only wish you could have be there to hear she . " 
 " Father , what be the good of it all ? you hate turncoat even bad than traitor . would you like your daughter to be one ? and when she would seem to have turn her coat for the lady wear coat now , the horrid ugly thing ! for the sake of position , and title , and all that . if Lord Dashville have be a poor man , with his own way to make in the world , a plain Mister , there might have be more to be say for it . but to think that I should throw over my poor darling because he will come home without a penny , and perhaps tattoed , but at any rate turn black , for the sake of a coronet , and a heap of gold oh , father , I shall break down , if you go on so ! " 
 " my dear girl , I will not say a word to vex you . but you be famous for common - sense , as well as every other good quality , and I would ask you to employ just a little of it . can you bear I to speak of your trouble , darling ? " 
 " oh yes , I be so well accustomed to it now ; and I know that it be nothing compare to what thousand of people have to bear . sometimes I be quite ashamed of give way to it . " 
 " you do not give way to it , Faith . no person can possibly say that of you . you be my brave , unselfish , cheerful , sweet - natured , upright , and love child . nobody know , but you and I and perhaps I know it even more than you do the greatness of the self - command you use , to be pleasant and gay and agreeable , simply for the sake of those around you . " 
 " then , father , " cry Faith , who be surprised at this , for the Admiral have never say a word about such matter , " you think , after all , that I be that I be almost as good as dolly ! " 
 " you jealous little vixen , I shall recall every word I have say in your favour ! my child , and my pride , you be not only as good as dolly , but my good hope be that when dolly grow old she may be like you . do not   cry , darling ; I can not   stand cry , when it come from eye that so seldom do it . and now that you know what I think of you , allow I to think a little for you . I have some right to interfere in your life ; you will allow that will not   you ? " 
 " Father , you have all right , and a thousand time as much , because you be so gentle about use it . " 
 " I call that bad English , as Zeb Tugwell say when he do not   want to understand a thing . but , my pretty dear , you must remember that you will not have a father always . who will look after you , when I be go , except the Almighty ? and he do not do it , except for the few who look after themselves . it be my duty to consider these point , and they override sentimentality . to I it be nothing that Dashville will be an Earl , and a man of great influence , if he keep up his present high character ; but it be something to I that I find he modest , truthful , not lead away by phantom , a gentleman which be more than a nobleman and with his whole heart give to my dear child faith . " 
 Faith sigh heavily , partly for herself , but mainly , perhaps , for the sake of a fine heart sadly throw away on she . " I believe he be all that , " she say . 
 " in that case , what more can you have ? " pursue the triumphant Admiral . " it be one of the clear thing I ever know , and one of the most consistent " consistent be a great word in those day " as well as in every way desirable . consider , not yourself which you never do but the state of the Country , and of Dolly . they have make I a baronet , for be away from home nearly every night of my life ; and if I have Dashville to see to thing here , I might stay away long enough to be a lord myself , like my late middy the present Duke of Bronte . " 
 Faith laugh heartily . " you call I jealous ! my dear father , I know that you could have do a great deal more than Lord Nelson have , because he learn all that he know from you . and now who be it that really defend the whole south coast of England against the French ? be it Lord Nelson ? he have as much as he can do to look after their fleet in the Mediterranean . Admiral Cornwallis and Sir Charles Darling be the real defender of England . " 
 " no , my dear , you must never say that , except of course in private . there may be some truth in it , but it would be laugh at in the present condition of the public mind . history may do I justice ; but after all it be immaterial . a man who do his duty should be indifferent to the opinion of the public , which begin more and more to be form less by fact than by the newspaper of the day . but let we return to more important matter . you be now in a very sensible frame of mind . you see what my wish be about you , and how reasonable they be . I should be so happy , my darling child , if you would consider they sensibly , and yield some little of your romantic view . I would not ask you unless I be sure that this man love you as you deserve , and in his own character deserve your love . " 
 " then , father , will this content you , dear ? unless I hear something of Erle Twemlow , to show that he be live , and still hold to I , in the course of another twelvemonth , Lord Dashville , or anybody else , may try may try to take his place with I . only I must not be worry I mean , I must not hear another word about it , until the time have quite expire . " 
 " it be a very poor concession , faith . surely you might say half a year . consider , it be nearly three year now " 
 " no , papa , I should despise myself if I be so unjust to one so unlucky . and I only go so much from my own wish because you be such a dear and good father . not a bit of it for Lord Dashville 's sake . " 
 " well , my poor darling , " the Admiral reply , for he see that she be upon the brink of tear , and might hate Lord Dashville if far urge , " half a loaf be well than no bread . if Dashville be worthy of your constant heart , he will stand this long trial of his constancy . this be the tenth day of August , 1804 . I hope that the Lord may be pleased to spare I till the 10th of August , 1805 . high time for they to come and lay the cloth . I be as hungry as a hunter . " 




 CHAPTER XLVI 

 CATAMARANS 

 Napoleon have show no proper dread of the valiant british volunteer , but keep his festival in August , and carry on his sea - side plan , as if there be no such fellow . not content with that , he even flout our blockade fleet by come out to look at they . and if one of our frigate have shoot straight , she might have save million of life and billion of money , at the cost of one greatly bad life . but the poor ship know not her opportunity , or she would rather have go to the bottom than waste it . 
 now the French make much of this affair , accord to their nature ; and history of it , full of life and growth , run swiftly along the shallow shore , and even to Paris , the navel of the earth . frenchman of letter or rather of paper declare that all England be smite with dismay ; and so she might have be , if she have hear of it . but as our neighbour go home again , as soon as the water be six fathom deep , few englishman know that they have try to smell a little of the sea - breeze , outside the smell of their inshore powder . they be pleased to get ashore again , and talk it over , with vivid description of the thing that do not happen . 
 " such scene as these tend much to agitate England , " write a great french historian . " the British Press , arrogant and calumnious , as the Press always be in a free country , rail much at Napoleon and his preparation ; but rail as one who tremble at that which he would fain exhibit as the object of his laughter . " it may have be so , but it be not to be see in any serious journal of that time . he seem to have confound coarse caricaturist with refined and thoughtful journalist , even as , in the account of that inshore skirmish , he turn a gun - brig into a british frigate . however , such matter be too large for we . 
 it be resolve at any rate to try some sort of a hit at all these very gallant Frenchmen , moor under their own battery , and make horse - marine of themselves , whenever Neptune , the father of the horse , permit . the jolly english tar , rid well upon the wave , send many a broad grin through a spy - glass at Muncher Crappo tug hard to get his nag into his gun - boat and then to get he out again , because his present set of shoe would not be wear out in England . every sailor love a horse , regard he as a boat on leg , and therefore know more about he than any landlubber may feign to know . 
 but although they would have be loth to train a gun on the noble animal , who be duly keep beyond their range , all the british sailor long to have a bout with the double tier of hostile craft moor off the shore within shelter of french battery . every day they could reckon at least two hundred sail of every kind of rig invent since the time of Noah , but all prepare to destroy instead of succour the godly . it be truly grievous to see they there and not be able to get at they , for no ship of the line or even frigate could get near enough to tackle they . then the British Admiral , Lord Keith , resolve after much consultation to try what could be do with fire - ship . 
 Blyth Scudamore , now in command of the Blonde , have do much excellent service , in cut off straggler from the french flotilla , and drive ashore near Vimereux some prame and lugger come from Ostend . he begin to know the french coast and the run of the shoal like a native pilot ; for the post of the Blonde , and some other light ship , be between the blockade fleet and the blockaded , where perpetual vigilance be need . this sharp service be the very thing require to improve his character , to stamp it with decision and self - reliance , and to burnish his quiet , contemplative vein with the very frequent friction of the trick of mankind . these he now be strictly bind not to study , but anticipate , take it as first postulate that every one would cheat he , if permit . to a scrimpy and screwy man , of the type most abundant , such a position would have do a deal of harm , shut he up into his own shell hard , and flinte its muricate horn against the world . but with the gentle Scuddy , as the boy at school have call he , the process of harden be beneficial , as it be with pure gold , which can not stand the wear and tear of the human race until it have be reduce by they at least to the mark of their twenty carat . 
 and now it be a fine thing for Scudamore even as a man too philanthropic be strengthen in his moral tone   as his wife find out   by be compel to discharge the least pleasant of the duty of a county sheriff or if not a fine thing , at least it be a wholesome and durable corrective to all excess of lenience , that duty to his country and mankind compel the gentle Scuddy to conduct the western division of this night - attack . 
 at this time there be in the public mind , which be quite of full feminine agility , a strong prejudice against the use of fire - ship . red - hot cannon - ball , and shrapnel , langrage , chain - shot , and greek - fire these and the like be all fair warfare , and France might use they freely . but England   which never be allow to do , without hoot and execration , what every other country do with loud applause   England must rather burn off her right hand than send a fire - ship against the ship full of fire for her house , her cottage , and church . Lord Keith have the sense to laugh at all that stuff , but he have not the grand mechanical power which have now enable the human race , not to go , but to send one another to the star . a clumsy affair call a catamaran , the acephalous ancestor of the torpedo , be expect to relieve the sea of some thousand of people who have no business there . this catamaran be a water - proof box about twenty foot long , and four foot wide , narrow at the end , like a coffin for a giant . it be fill with gunpowder , and ballast so that its lid , or deck , be almost awash ; and near its stern be a box contain clock movement that would go for about ten minute , upon the withdrawal of a peg outside , and then would draw a trigger and explode the charge . this wondrous creature have neither oar nor sail , but demand to be tow to the tideward of the enemy , then have the death - watch set going , and be cast adrift within hail of the enemy 's line . then as soon as it come across their mooring cable , its duty be to slide for a little way along they in a friendly manner , lie hold of they kindly with its long tail , which consist of a series of grappling - hook buoy with cork , and then bring up smartly alongside of the gun - boat , blow itself up , and carry they up with it . how many there be of these catamaran be not quite certain , but perhaps about a score , the intention be to have ten time as many , on the next occasion , if these do well . and no doubt they would have do well , if permit ; but they fail of their purpose , like the great Guy Fawkes , because they be prevent . 
 for the French , by mean of treacherous agent of whom perhaps Caryl Carne be one , though his name do not appear in the despatch know all about this neat little scheme beforehand , and set their wit at work to defeat it . moreover , they know that there be four fire - ship , one of which be the Peggy of Springhaven , intend to add to the consternation and destruction work by the catamaran . but they do not know that , by some irony of fate , the least destructive and most gentle of mankind be order to take a leading part in shatter man , and horse , and even good dog , into vapour . 
 many quiet horse , and sweet - natured dog , whose want of breeding have improve their manner , live in this part of the great flotilla , and be satisfied to have their home where it please the Lord to feed they . the horse be lead to feed out of the gun , that they might not be afraid of they ; and they struggle against early prejudice , to like wood as well as grass , and to get sea - leg . Man put they here to suit his own idea ; of that they be quite aware , and take it kindly , accept superior power , and inferior use of they , without a shade of question in their eye . to their innocent mind it be never bring home that they be tethered here , and cropping clot instead of clover , for the purpose of inspire in their timid friend ashore the confidence a horse repose in a brother horse , but very wisely doubt about invest in mankind . for instance , whenever a wild young animal , a new recruit for the cavalry , be hale against his judgment by a man on either side to the hollow - sound gangway over dance depth of peril , these veteran salt of horse would assure he , with a neigh from the billowy distance , that they be not drown yet , but be walk on a sort of gate , and get their victual regular . on the other hand , as to the presence of the dog , that require no explanation . be there ever a time or place in which a dog grudge his sprightly and disinterested service , or fail to do his good when call upon ? these french dog , whom the mild english mastiff would have look upon , or rather would have shut his eye at , as a lot of cur below contempt , be as full of fine ardour for their cause and country as any noble hound that ever sate like a statue on a marble terrace . 
 on the first of October all be ready for this audacious squibbing of the hornet 's nest , and the fleet of investment   which keep its distance accord to the weather and the tide   stand in , not bodily so as to arouse excitement , but a ship at a time sidle in towards the coast , and traverse one another 's track , as if they be simply exchange station . the french pretend to take no heed , and do not call in a single scouting craft , but show every sign of have all eye shut . nothing , however , be do that night , by reason perhaps of the weather ; but the follow night be favourable , and the british fleet bring as nigh as it durst come , the four fire - ship be despatch after dark , when the enemy be likely to be engage with supper . the sky be conveniently overcast , with a faint light wander here and there , from the lift of the horizon , just enough to show the rig of a vessel and her length , at a distance of about a hundred yard . nothing could be well think the Englishmen ; and the French be of that opinion too , especially as Nelson be not there . 
 Scudamore have nothing to do with the loose adventure of the fire - ship , the object of which be to huddle together this advanced part of the flotilla , so that the catamaran might sweep unseen into a goodly thicket of vessel , and shatter at least half a dozen at once . 
 but somehow the scheme be not well carry out , though it look very nice upon paper . one very great drawback , to begin with , be that the enemy be quite aware of all our kind intention ; and another scarcely less fatal be the want of punctuality on our part . all the float coffin should have come together , like a funeral of fifty from a colliery ; but instead of that they dribble in one by one , and be cast off by their tow - boat promiscuously . Scudamore do his part well enough , though the whole thing go against his grain , and the four catamaran under his direction be the only one that do their duty . the boat of the Blonde have these in tow , and cast they off handsomely at the proper distance , and draw the plug which set their clock - spring go . but even of these four only two explode , although the clock be not american , and those two make a tremendous noise , but only sing a few french beard off . except , indeed , that a fine old horse , with a white roman nose and a bright chestnut mane , who be live in a flat - bottom boat , break his halter , and rush up to the bow , and give vent to his amazement , as if he have be gift with a trumpet . 
 Hereupon a dog , loth to be behind the time , scamper up to his side , and with his forefeet on the gunwale , contribute a howl of incalculable length and unfathomable sadness . 
 in the hurly of the combat and confusion of the night , with the dimness streak with tumult , and the water gash with fire , that horse and this dog might have go on for ever , bewail the nature of the son of man , unless a special fortune have put power into their mouth . one of the fire - ship , as scandal do declare , be that very ancient tub indeed that could not float on its bottom the Peggy of Springhaven , buy at thrice her value , through the influence of Admiral Darling . if one have to meet every calumny that arise , and deal with it before go far , the battle that last for a fortnight and then turn into an earthquake would be a quick affair compare with the one now in progress . enough that the Peggy prove by the light she give , and her grand style of burn to the water 's edge before she blow up , that she be worth at least the hundred pound Widow Shanks receive for she . she startle the French more than any of the other , and the strong light she afford in her last moment shine redly on the anguish of that poor horse and dog . there be no sign of any one to help they , and the flame in the background redouble their woe . 
 now this apparently deserted prame , near the centre of the line , be the Ville de Mayence ; and the flag of Rear - Admiral Lacrosse be even now fly at her peak . " we must have she , my lad , " cry Scudamore , who be wonder what to do next , until he descry the horse and dog and that fine flag ; " let we board she , and make off with all of they . " 
 the crew of his launch be delighted with that . to destroy be very good ; but to capture be still well ; and a dash into the midst of the enemy be the very thing they long for . " ay , ay , sir , " they cry , set their back to their oar , and through the broad light that still shine upon the wave , and among the thick crowd of weltering shadow , the launch shoot like a dart to the side of the foe . 
 " easy all ! throw a grapple on board , " cry the young commander ; and as the stern swung round he leap from it , and over the shallow bulwark , and stand all alone on the enemy 's fore - deck . and alone he remain , for at that moment a loud crash be hear , and the launch fill and sink , with her crew of sixteen plunge wildly in the wave . 
 this come to pass through no fault of their own , but a clever device of the enemy . Admiral Lacrosse , be call away , have leave his first officer to see to the safety of the flag - ship and her immediate neighbour , and this brave man have obtain permission to try a little plan of his own , if assail by any adventurous british boat in charge of the vessel explosive . in the bow of some stout but handy boat he have rig up a mast with a long spar attach , and by mean of a guy at the end of that spar , a brace of heavy chain - shot could be swing up and pitch headlong into any boat alongside . while the crew of Scudamore 's launch be intent upon board the prame , one of these boat come swiftly from under her stern , and with one fling swamp the enemy . then the Frenchmen laugh heartily , and offer oar and buoy for the poor british seaman to come up as prisoner .
