 Scudamore see that he be trap beyond escape , for no other british boat be anywhere in hail . his first impulse be to jump overboard and help his own drown man , but before he could do so an officer stand before he , and say , " Monsieur be my prisoner . his man will be safe , and I can not permit he to risk his own life . Mon Dieu , it be my dear friend Captain Scudamore ! " 
 " and you , my old friend , Captain Desportes ! I see it be hopeless to resist " for by this time a score of Frenchmen be round he " I can only congratulate myself that if I must fall , it be into such good hand . " 
 " my dear friend , how glad I be to see you ! " reply the french captain , embrace he warmly ; " to you I owe more than to any man of your nation . I will not take your sword . no , no , my friend . you shall not be a prisoner , except in word . and how much you have advance in the knowledge of our language , chiefly , I fear , at the expense of France . and now you will grow perfect , at the expense of England . " 




 CHAPTER XLVII 

 ENTER and exit 

 the summer have be fine upon the whole , and a very fair quantity of fish bring in , Miss Twemlow have pick up a sweetheart , as the unromantic mother of the place express it . and the circumstance be of such a nature that very large interest be arouse at once , and not only so , but be feed well and grow fast . 
 the most complete of chronicle be no well than a sponge of inferior texture and with many mouth shut . part that be full of suctive power get no chance of sucking ; other part have a flood of juice bubble at they , but be waterproof . this be the only excuse except one for the shameful neglect of the family of Blocks , in any little treatise pretend to give the dull of glimpse at Springhaven . 
 the other excuse if self - accusation do not poke a finger through it be that the Blockses be mainly of the dry land , and never go to sea when they could help it . if they have live beyond the two tree and the stile that mark the parish boundary upon the hill towards London , they might have be spotless , and grand , and even honest , yet must have be the depth of the hill below contempt . but they dwelt in the village for more generation than would go upon any woman 's finger , and they do a little business with the fish catch by the other , which enable it to look after three day ' journey as if it swam into town upon its own fin . the invention for wrong mankind pay a great deal well than those for right they . 
 now the news come from John Prater 's first , that a gentleman of great renown be come down from London city to live on fish fresh out of the sea . his doctor have order he to leave off butcher 's meat , and baker 's bread , and tea - grocer 's tea , and almost every kind of inland victual , because of the state of his something big , which even Springhaven could not pronounce . he must keep himself up , for at least three month , upon nothing but breeze of the sea , and malt - liquor , and farm - house bread and milk and new - lay egg , and anything he fancy that come out of the sea , shelly , or scaly , or jellified , or weedy . news from a public - house grow fast as seed come up quick for soak and a strong competition for this gentleman arise ; but he know what he be do , and bring down his cook and house - maid , and dislike the noise at the Darling Arms , take no less than five room at the house of Matthew Blocks , on the rise of the hill , where he could see the fish come in . 
 he be call at once Sir Parsley Sugarloaf , for his name be Percival shargeloe ; and his cook rebuke his housemaid sternly , for meddle with matter beyond her sphere , when she tell Mrs. Blocks that he be not Sir Percival , but only Percival Shargeloes , Esquire , very high up in the Corporation , but too young to be Lord Mayor of London for some year . he appear to be well on the right side of forty ; and every young lady on the wrong side of thirty possess a pony , or even a donkey , with leg enough to come down the hill , immediately begin to take a rose - colour view of the many beauty of Springhaven . 
 if Mr. Shargeloes have any ambition for title , it lie rather in a military direction . he have join a regiment of City Volunteers , and must have be a Captain , if he could have stand the drill . but this , though not arduous , have outgo his ambition , nature have gift he with a remarkable power of extract nourishment from food , which be now call assimilation . he be not a great feeder people so blessed seldom be but nothing short of painful starvation would keep he lean . he have consult all the foremost physician about this , and one say , " take acid , " another say , " walk twenty mile every day with two Witney blanket on , " a third say , " thank God for it , and drink before you eat , " and a fourth   a man of wide experience   bid he marry the worst - temper woman he know . then they all give he pill to upset his stomach ; but such be its power that it assimilate they . despair of these , he consult a Quack , and receive the direction which bring he to Springhaven . and a lucky day for he it be , as he confess for the rest of his life , whenever any lady ask he . 
 because Miss Twemlow be intend for he by the nice adjustment of nature . how can two round thing fit together , except superficially ? and in that case one must be upper and the other under ; which be not the proper thing in matrimony , though generally the prevail one . but take a full - moon and a half - moon , or even a square and a tidy triangle with manner enough to have one right angle and when you have put they into one another 's arm , there they stick , all the firmer for friction . Jack Spratt and his wife be a case in point ; and how much more point the case become when the question be not about what be on the plate , but the gentleman be in his own body fat , and the lady in her elegant person lean ! 
 Mr. Sugarloaf which he could not bear to be call be an ardent admirer of the Church , and aware that her minister know what be good , return with great speed the Rector 's call , have earnest hope of some heart - feel word upon the difference between a right and left hand sole . one of these be ever so much well than the other accord to our evolutionist , because when he be a cod , a few milliard of year back , he choose the right side to begin lie down on , that his descendant in the thirty - millionth generation might get flat . his wife , from sheer perversity , lie down upon the other side , and this explain how some of their descendant pull their eye through their head to one side , and some   though comparatively few   to the other . and the bad of it be that the fit for the frying - pan do not survive this well - intend involution , except at a very long figure in the market . 
 as it fall out upon that day , Miss Twemlow be sit in the drawing - room alone , wait till her mother 's hair be quite do up , her own abundant lock be not do up at all , for she have lately take to set her face against all foreign fashion . " I have not be introduce to the king , " she say , " nor even to the Queen , like those forward darling , and I shall do my hair to please myself . " when her father object , she quench he with St. Paul ; and even her mother , though shock , begin to think that Eliza know what she be about . the release of her fine hair , which fall in natural wave about her stately neck , make she look nearly ten year young than she be , for by this time she must have be eight - and - twenty . the lady of the Carne race , as their picture show   until they be sell to be the grandmother of dry - salter , have always be endow with shapely neck , fit column for their small round head . and this young lady 's hair , with no constraint but that of a narrow band across the forehead , cluster and gleam like a bower of acanthu round that parian column . 
 Mr. Shargeloes , have obey his order always to dine early , be thrill with a vision of poetry and romance , as he cross the first square of the carpet . the lady sit just where the light fall well from a filter sunbeam to illumine she , without enter into the shady part ; and the poetry of her attitude be inspire by some very fine poetry upon her lap . " I do not   care what the doctor say , I shall marry that girl , " say Mr. Shargeloes to himself . 
 he be a man who know his own mind , and a man with that gift make other know it . Miss Twemlow clench in the coat upon his back the nail she have drive through his heart , by call he , at every other breath , " Colonel Shargeloes . " he say he be not that ; but she feel that he be , as indeed every patriotic man must be . her contempt for every man who forsook his country in this bitter , bitter strait be at once so ruthless and so bewitch that he be quite surprised into confessing that he have give 10,000 pound , all in solid gold , for the comfort of the Royal Volunteers , as soon as the autumnal damp come on . he could not tell such an elegant creature that what he have pay for be flannel drawer , though she have so much strength of mind that he be enable to tell she before very long . 
 a great deal of nonsense be talk about lady who be get the well of their first youth , as if they then hang themselves out as old slate for any man to write his name on . the truth be that they have well judgment then , less trouble in their heart about a gentleman 's appearance , and more enquiry in their mind as to his temper , taste , and principle , not to mention his prospect of support they . and even as concern appearance , Mr. Shargeloes be very good . nature have give he a fine stout frame , and a very pleasant countenance ; and his life in the busy world have add that quickness of decision and immediate sense of right which a clever woman know to be the very thing she want . moreover , his dress , which go a very long way into the heart of a lady , be most correct and particular . for his coat be of the late Bond Street fashion , the " Jean de Brie , " improve and beautify by suggestion from the Prince of Wales himself . bright claret be the colour , and the button be of gold , bright enough to show the road before he as he walk . the shoulder be pad , as if a jam pot stand there , and the waist button tight , too tight for any happiness , to show the bright laticlave of brocade waistcoat . then follow breech of rich purple padusoy , have white satin bow at the knee , among which the little silver bell of the hessian boot jingle . 
 Miss Twemlow be superior to all small feeling , but have great breadth of sympathy with the sterling truth in fashion . the volume of love , like a pattern - book , fall open , and this well - dress gentleman be engrave upon her heart . the most captious young chit , such as dolly herself , could scarcely have call he either corpulent or old . every day he could be see to be grow young , with the aid of fresh fish as a totally novel ingredient in his system ; his muscle increase with the growth of brain - power , and the shoemaker be punch a fresh hole in his belt , an inch far back , every week he stop there . after buckle up three hole , he propose . Miss Twemlow refer he to her dear papa ; and the Rector take a week to enquire and meditate . " take a month , if you like , " say Mr. Shargeloes . 
 this reply increase the speed . Mr. Twemlow have the deep respect for the Corporation , and to live to be the father of a Lord Mayor of London become a new ambition to lead on his wan year . " come and dine with we on Saturday , and we will tell you all about it , " he say , with a pleasant smile , and warm shake of the hand ; and Shargeloes know that the neck and the curl would bend over the broad gold chain some day . 
 how grievous it be to throw a big stone into a pool which have plenty of depth and length and width for the ring to travel pleasantly , yet not to make one ring , because of wind upon the water ! in the day that be not more than two year old , Springhaven could have take all this news , with a swiftly expand and smoothly fluent circle , with a lift of self - importance at the centre of the movement , and a heave of gentle interest in the far reflective corner . even now , with a tumult of thing to consider , and a tempest of judgment to do it in , people contrive to be positive about a quantity of thing still pende . Sir Parsley Sugarloaf have buy Miss Twemlow for 50,000 pound , they say , and he make she let her curl down so outrageous , because she be to be marry at Guildhall , with a guinea at the end of every hair . Miss Faith would be dirt - cheap at all that money ; but as for Miss Eliza , they wish he well knowledge , which be sure to come , when it be no good to he . 
 " what a corner of the world this be for gossip ! " Mr. Shargeloes say , pleasantly , to his Eliza , have hear from his cook , who desire no new mistress , some few of the thing say about he . " I be not such a fool as to care what they say . but I be greatly surprised at one thing . you know that I be a thorough englishman ; may I tell you what I think , without offend you ? it be a delicate matter , because it concern a relative of your own , my dear . " 
 " I know what you mean . you will not offend I . Percival , I know how straightforward you be , and how keen of perception . I have expect this . " 
 " and yet it seem presumptuous of I to say that you be all blind here , from the high to the low . except indeed yourself , as I now perceive . I will tell you my suspicion , or more than suspicion my firm belief about your cousin , Mr. Carne . I can trust you to keep this even from your father . Caryl Carne be a spy , in the pay of the French . " 
 " I have long think something , though not quite so bad as that , " Miss Twemlow answer , calmly ; " because he have behave to we so very strangely . my mother be his own father 's sister , as you know , and yet he have never din with we more than once , and then he scarcely say a word to any one . and he never yet have ask we to visit he at the castle ; though for that we can make all allowance , of course , because of its sad condition . then everybody think he have take to smuggling , and after all his loss , no one blame he , especially as all the Carnes have do it , even when they be the owner of the land . but ever since poor Mr. Cheeseman , our church - warden , try to destroy himself with his own rope , all the parish begin to doubt about the smuggling , because it pay so well and make the people very cheerful . but from something he have see , my father feel quite certain that the true explanation be smuggle . " 
 " indeed ! do you know at all what it be he see , and when , and under what circumstance ? " Mr. Shargeloes put these question with more urgency than Miss Twemlow like . 
 " really I can not tell you all those thing ; they be scarcely of general interest . my dear father say little about it : all knowledge be deny in this good world to woman . but no doubt he would tell you , if you ask he , when there be no lady present . " 
 " I will , " say Mr. Shargeloes . " he be most judicious ; he know when to speak , and when to hold his tongue . and I think that you combine with beauty one of those two gift which be the utmost to be expect . " 
 " Percival , you put thing very nicely , which be all that could be expect of a man . but do take my advice in this matter , and say no more about it . " 
 Mr. Shargeloes feign to comply , and perhaps at the moment mean to do so . but unluckily he be in an enterprise temper , proud of recovered activity , and determine to act up to the phosphate supply by fish diet . therefore when the Rector , rejoice in an outlet for his long pen - up discovery , and regard this sage man as one of his family , repeat the whole of his adventure at Carne Castle , Mr. Shargeloes say , briefly , " it must be see to . " 
 " Stubbard have be there , " reply Mr. Twemlow , repent perhaps of his confidence ; " Stubbard have make an official inspection , which relieve we of all concern with it . " 
 " Captain Stubbard be an ass . it be a burn shame that important affair should be entrust to such fellow . the country be in peril , deadly peril ; and every Englishman be bind to act as if he be an officer . " 
 that very same evening Carne ride back to his ruin in a very grim state of mind . he have receive from the Emperor a curt and haughty answer to his last appeal for immediate action , and the prospect of another gloomy winter here , with danger thicken round he , and no motion to enliven they , be almost more than he could endure . the night be draw in , and a damp fog from the sea have drizzle the tree , and the ivy , and even his own moustache with cold misery . 
 " bring I a lantern , " he say to old Jerry , as he swing his stiff leg from the back of the jade horse , " and the little flask of oil with the feather in it . it be high time to put the Inspector 's step in order . " 
 Jerry Bowles , whose back and knee be bent with rheumatism and dull service , trot   like a horse who have become too stiff to walk   for the thing command , and come back with they . then his master , without a word , strode towards the passage give entry to the vault which Stubbard have not see the vault contain all the powder , and the weapon for arm the peasantry of England , whom Napoleon fondly expect to rise in his favour at the sight of his eagle . 
 " how do it work ? quite stiff with rust . I think so . nothing be ever in order , unless I see to it myself . give I the lantern . now oil the bearing thoroughly . put the feather into the socket , and work the pin in and out , that the oil may go all round . now pour in some oil from the lip of the flask ; but not upon the treadle , you old blockhead . now do the other end the same . ah , now it would go with the weight of a mouse ! I have a great mind to make you try it . " 
 " what would you do , sir , if my neck be break ? who would do your work , as I do ? " 
 they be under an arch of mouldy stone , open into the deep dark vault , where the faint light of the lantern glance on burnish leather , brass , and steel , or fall without flash upon dull round bulk . the old man , kneel on the round chalk - flint set in lime for the flooring of the passage , be handle the first step of narrow step - ladder lead to the cellar - depth . this top step have be take out of the old oak mortice , and cut short , and then replace in the frame , with an iron pin work in an iron collar , just as the gudgeon of a wheelbarrow revolve . any one step upon it unawares would go down without the aid of any other step . 
 " go like spittle now , sir , " say old Jerry ; " but I do not   want no more harm in this crick of life . the Lord be pleased to keep all they examiner at home . might have none to find their corpusse until next leap - year . I hope with all my heart they will not   come poke their long nose here . " 
 " well , I rather hope they will . they want a lesson in this neighbourhood , " mutter Carne , who be shiver , and hungry , and unsweetene . 




 CHAPTER XLVIII 

 MOTHER SCUDAMORE 

 if we want to know how a tree or flower have bear the gale that flog last night , or the frost that sting the morning , the only sure plan be to go and see . and the only way to understand how a friend have take affliction be to go if it may be do without intrusion and let he tell you , if he like . 
 Admiral Darling be so much vex when he hear of Blyth Scudamore 's capture by the french , and duty compel he to inform the mother , that he would rather have ride a thousand mile upon barley - bread than face she . he know how the whole of her life be now bind up with the fortune of her son , and he long to send Faith with the bad news , as he have send she with the good before ; but he fear that it might seem unkind . so he go himself , with the hope of put the good complexion upon it , yet fully expect sad distress , and perhaps a burst of weeping . but the lady receive his tiding in a manner that surprise he . at first she indulge in a tear or two , but they only introduce a smile . 
 " in some way it be a sad thing , " she say , " and will be a terrible blow to he , just when he be rise so fast in the service . but we must not rebel more than we can help , against the will of the Lord , Sir Charles . " 
 " how philosophical , and how commonplace ! " think the Admiral ; but he only bow , and pay she some compliment upon her common - sense . 
 " perhaps you scarcely understand my view , and perhaps I be wrong in have they , " Lady Scudamore continue , quietly . " my son 's advancement be very dear to I , and this will of course retard it . but I care most of all for his life , and now that will be safe for a long while . they never kill their prisoner , do they ? " 
 " no , ma'am , no . they behave very well to they ; well , I be afraid , than we do to ours . they treat they quite as guest , when they fall into good hand . though Napoleon himself be not too mild in that way . " 
 " my son have fall into very good hand , as you yourself assure I that Captain Desportes , a gallant officer and kind gentleman , as I know from your daughter 's description . Blyth be quite equal to Lord Nelson in personal daring , and possibly not behind he in ability . consider how shockingly poor Nelson have be injure , and he feel convince himself that they will have his life at last . no officer can be a hero without get very sad wound , and perhaps lose his life . every one who do his duty must at least be wound . " 
 the Admiral , who have never receive a scratch , be not at all charm with this view of naval duty ; but he be too polite to enter protest , and only make one of his old - fashioned scrape . 
 " I be sure every time I have hear a gun come from the sea , and especially after dark , " the lady resume , without thinking of he , " it have make I miserable to know that probably Blyth be rush into some deadly conflict . but now I shall feel that he can not do that ; and I hope they will keep he until the fighting grow milder . he use to send I all his money , poor dear boy ! and now I shall try to send he some of mine , if it can be arrange about bank - note . and now I can do it very easily , thank to your kindness , Sir Charles , his father 's good friend , and his own , and mine . " 
 Lady Scudamore shed another tear or two , not of sorrow , but of pride , while she put her hand into her pocket , as if to begin the remittance at once . " you owe I no thank , ma'am , " say the Admiral , smile ; " if any thank be due , they be due to the king , for remember at last what he should have do before . " 
 " would he ever have think of I , but for you ? it be useless to talk in that way , Sir Charles ; it only increase the obligation , which I must entreat you not to do . how I wish I could help you in anything ! " 
 " every day you be help I , " he reply , with truth ; " although I be away too often to know all about it , or even to thank you . I hope my dear Faith have persuade you not to leave we for the winter , as you threaten . " 
 " faith can persuade I to anything she please . she possess the power of her name , " reply the lady ; " but the power be not call for , when the persuasion be so pleasant . for a month , I must be away to visit my dear mother , as I always have do at this time of year ; and then , but for one thing , I would return most gladly . for I be very selfish , you must know , Sir Charles I have a well chance of hearing of my dear son at these head - quarter of the defence of England , than I should have even in London . " 
 " certainly , " cry the Admiral , who magnify his office ; ' such a number of despatch pass through my hand ; and if I can not   make they out , why , my daughter dolly can . I do not   suppose , Lady Scudamore , that even when you live in the midst of the world you ever see any girl half so clever as my Dolly . I do not   let she know it that would never do , of course but she always get the good of I , upon almost any question . " 
 Sir Charles , for the moment , forget his good manner , and spread his coat so that one might see between his leg . " I stand like this , " he say , " and she stand there ; and I take she to task for not pay her bill for some of those fellow have have to come to I , which be not as it should be in a country place , where people do not   understand the fashionable system . she stand there , ma'am , and I feel as sure as if I be an English twenty - four bearing down upon a Frenchman of fifty gun , that she can only haul her colour down and rig out gangway ladder when , bless I and keep I ! I be carry by surprise , and drive under hatchway , and if there be a guinea in my hold , it fly into the enemy 's locker ! if it happen only once , I should think nothing of it . but when I know exactly what be come , and have double - shot every gun , and set up hammock - netting , and take uncommon care to have the weather - gage , ' tis the Devil , Lady Scudamore excuse I , madam ' tis the Devil to a ditty - bag that I have she at my mercy . and yet it always come to money out of pocket , madam ! " 
 " she certainly have a great power over gentleman " Blyth 's mother smile demurely , as if she be sorry to confess it ; " but she be exceedingly young , Sir Charles , and every allowance must be make for she . " 
 " and by the Lord Harry , she get it , madam . she take uncommonly good care of that . but what be the one thing you mention that would prevent you from come back to we with pleasure ? " 
 " I scarcely like to speak of it . but it be about that self - same dolly . she be not fond of advice , and she know how quick she be , and that make she resent a word from slow people . she have take it into her head , I fear , that I be here as a restraint upon she ; a sort of lady spy , a duenna , a dictatress , all combine in one , and all unpleasant . this often make I fancy that I have no right to be here . and then your sweet faith come , and all be smooth again . " 
 " dolly have the least little possible touch of the vixen about she . I have find it out lately , " say the Admiral , as if he be half doubtful still ; " Nelson tell I so , and I be angry with he . but I believe he be right , as he generally be . his one eye see more than a score of mine would . but , my dear madam , if that be your only objection to come back to we , or rather to my daughter , I beg you not to let it weigh a feather 's weight with you . or , at any rate , enhance the obligation to we , by put it entirely on one side . dolly have the very fine heart in all the world ; not so steady perhaps as Faith 's , nor quite so fair to other people , but wonderfully warm , ma'am , and as sound as as a roach . " 
 Lady Scudamore could not help laugh a little , and she hop for her son 's sake that this account be true . her gratitude and good - will to the Admiral , as well as her duty to her son , make she give the promise seek for ; and she begin to prepare for her journey at once , that she might be back in good time for the winter . but she feel very doubtful , at leave the Hall , whether she have do quite right in keep her suspicion of dolly from Dolly 's father . for with eye which be sharpen by jealousy for the interest , or at least the affection , of her son , she have long perceive that his lady - love be play a dangerous game with Caryl Carne . sometimes she believe that she ought to speak of this , for the good of the family ; because she feel the deep mistrust and dislike of Carne , who strictly avoid she whenever he could ; but on the other hand she find the subject most delicate and difficult to handle . for she have take good care at the outset not to be here upon any false pretence . at the very first interview with her host she have speak of Blyth 's attachment to his young daughter , of which the Admiral have hear already from that youthful sailor . and the Admiral have simply say , as in Captain Twemlow 's case : " let we leave they to themselves . I admire the young man . if she like he , I shall make no objection , when they be old enough , and thing be favourable . " and now if she tell he of the other love - affair , it would look like jealousy of a rival . perhaps a hundred time a day , as her love for gentle Faith grow fast than her like for the sprightly dolly , she would sigh that her son do not see thing like herself ; but bitter affliction have teach she that the course of this life follow our own wish about as much as another man 's dog heed our whistle . but , for all that , this good lady hop some day to see thing come round as she would like to bring they . 
 " no wonder that we like her son so much , " say faith when they have do wave handkerchief at the great yellow coach go slowly up the hill , with its vast wicker basket behind , and the guard perch over it with his blunderbus ; " he take after his mother in so many way . they be both so simple and unsuspicious , and they make the good of every one . " 
 " include themselves , I suppose , " answer dolly . " well I like people who have something on their mind , and make the bad of everybody . they have so much more to talk about . " 
 " you should never try to be sarcastic , dear . and you know that you do not   mean it . I be sure you do not   like to have the bad make of yourself . " 
 " oh , I have long be use to that . and I never care about it , when I know it be not true . I be sure that Mother Scudamore run I down , when I be out of hearing . I never do like those perfect people . " 
 " Mother Scudamore , indeed ! you be get into a low way of talking , which be not at all pretty in a girl . and I never hear she say an unkind word about you . though she may not have find you quite so perfect as she hop . " 
 " I tell you , Miss Darling , " cry dolly , with her bright colour deepen , and her grey eye flash , " that I do not   care a something that papa often say what she think about I , or you either . I know that she have come here to spy out all my way . " 
 " you should not have any to be spy out , dolly , " Faith answer , with some sternness , and a keen look at her sister , whose eye fall beneath her gaze . " you will be sorry , when you think of what you say to I , who have do nothing whatever to offend you . but that be a trifle compare with act unfairly to our father . Father be the kind man that ever live ; but he can be stern in great matter , I warn you . if he ever believe that you have deceive he , you will never be again to he what you have always be . " 
 they have send the carriage home that they might walk across the field , and this little scene between the sister take place upon a foot - path which lead back to their ground . dolly know that she be in the wrong , and that increase her anger . 
 " so you be another spy upon I , I suppose . ' tis a pretty thing to have one 's sister for an old duenna . pray who give you authority to lord it over I ? " 
 " you know as well as I do " Faith speak with a smile of superior calmness , as dolly toss her head " that I be about the last person in the world to be a spy . neither do I ever lord it over you . if anything , that matter be very much the other way . but be so much old , and your principal companion , it would be very odd of I , and as I think most unkind , if I do not take an interest in all your going on . " 
 " my going on ! what a lady - like expression ! who have get into a low way of talk now ? well , if you please , madam , what have you find out ? " 
 " I have find out nothing , and make no attempt to do so . but I see that you be alter very much from what you use to be ; and I be sure that there be something on your mind . why not tell I all about it ? I would promise to let it go no far , and I would not pretend to advise , unless you wish . I be your only sister , and we have always be together . it would make you so much more comfortable , I be certain of that , in your own mind , darling . and you know when we be little girl , dear mother on her death - bed put her hand upon our head and say , ' be love sister always , and never let anything come between you . ' and for father 's sake , too , you should try to do it . put aside all nonsense about spy and domineering , and trust I as your sister , that be my own darling dolly . " 
 " how can I resist you ? I will make a clean breast of it ; " dolly sigh deeply , but a wicked smile lie ambush in her bright eye and upon her rosy lip . " the sad truth be that my heart have be quite sore since I hear the shocking tiding about poor old daddy Stokes . he go to bed the other night with his good hat on , both his arm in an old muff he find in the ditch , and his leathern breech turn inside out . " 
 " then the poor old man have a clean breast than your , " cry Faith , who have prepare her heart and eye for tear of sympathy ; " he go upon his knee every night , stiff as they be , and his granddaughter have to help he up . but as for you , you be the most unfeeling , mocking , godless , unnatural creature that ever never care what become of anybody . here we be at the corner where the path divide . you go home that way , and I will go home by this . " 
 " well , I be so glad ! I really do believe that it be quite impossible to put you in a rage . now do not   be in a hurry , dear , to beg my pardon . " 
 " of that you may be quite sure , " cry Faith across the corner of the meadow where the path diverge ; " I never be less in a passion in my life ; and it will be your place to apologise . " 
 dolly send a merry laugh across the widening interval ; and Faith , who be just begin to fear that she have be in a passion , be convince by that laugh that she have not . but the weight lift from her conscience fall more heavily upon her heart . 




 CHAPTER XLIX 

 EVIL communication 

 although she pretend to be so merry , and really be so self - confident   whenever anybody want to help she , Miss Dolly Darling , when leave to herself , be not like herself , as it use to be . her nature be lively , and her spirit very high ; every one have pet she , before she could have earn it by aught except childish beauty ; and no one have leave off do it , when she be bind to show well claim to it . all this make doubt , and darkness , and the sense of not be her own mistress , very snappish thing to she , and she gain relief sweet - tempered as she be when pleased by a snap at other . for although she be not give , any more than other young people be , to plaguesome self - inspection , she could not help feel that she be no long the playful young dolly that she love so well . a strong , and clear , yet more mysterious will than her own have conquer her ; but she would not confess it , and yield entire obedience ; neither could she cast it off . her pride still exist , as strong as ever , whenever temper rouse it ; but there be too much of vanity in its composition , and too little of firm self - respect . contempt from a woman she could not endure ; neither from a man , if make manifest ; but Carne so calmly take the upper hand , without any show of have it , that she fall more and more beneath his influence . 
 he , know thoroughly what he be about , do nothing to arouse resistance . so far as he be capable of love any one , he be now in love with dolly . he admire her quickness , and pretty girlish way , and gaiety of nature   so unlike his own , and most of all her beauty . he have make up his mind that she should be his wife when fit for that dignity ; but he mean to make her useful first , and he see his way to do so . he know that she act more and more as her father 's secretary , for she write much fast than her sister Faith , and be quick in catch up a meaning . only it be needful to sap her little prejudice candour , to wit , and the sense of trust , and above all , patriotic feeling . he rejoice when he hear that Lady Scudamore be go , and the Rector have take his wife and daughter for change of air to Tunbridge Wells , Miss Twemlow be seriously out of health through anxiety about Mr. Shargeloes . for that gentleman have disappear , without a line or message , just when Mr. Furkettle , the chief lawyer in the neighbourhood , be begin to prepare the marriage - settlement ; and although his cook and house - maid be furious at the story , Mrs. Blocks have say , and all the parish now believe , that Sir Parsley Sugarloaf have fly away to Scotland rather than be bring to book that fatal part of the Prayer - book by the Rector and three or four brother clergyman . 
 this be so , and Frank Darling absorb in London with the publication of another batch of poem , dedicate to Napoleon , while Faith stand aloof with her feeling hurt , and the Admiral stand off and on in the wearisome cruise of duty , Carne have the coast unusually clear for the entry and arrangement of his contraband idea . he meet the fair dolly almost every day , and their interview do not grow short , although the day be do so . 
 " you should have be bear in France , " he say , one bright November morning , when they sit more comfortable than they have any right to be , upon the very same seat where the honest but hapless Captain Scuddy have try to venture to lisp his love ; " that be the land you belong to , darling , by beauty and manner and mind and taste , and most of all by your freedom from prejudice , and great liberality of sentiment . " 
 " but I think we be quite as good - look in England ; " dolly lift her long black lash , with a flash which might challenge the brilliance of any french eye ; " but of course you know well . I know nothing of french lady . " 
 " do not   be a fool , dolly ; " Carne speak rudely , but make up for it in another way . " there never be a french girl to equal you in loveliness ; but you must not suppose that you beat they all round . one point particularly you be far behind in . a french woman leave all political question , and national matter , and public affair , entirely to her husband , or her lover , as the case may be . whatever he wish be the law for she . Thy god shall be my god . " 
 " but you say they have great liberality of sentiment , and now you say they have no opinion of their own ! how can the two thing go together ? " 
 " very easily , " say Carne , who be accustomed to be baffle by such little sally ; " they take their opinion from their husband , who be always liberal . this produce happiness on both side a state of thing unknown in England . let I tell you of something important , mainly as it concern yourself , sweet dolly . the French be certain to unite with England , and then we shall be the grand nation in the world . no power in Europe can stand before we . all will be freedom , and civilization , and great idea , and fine taste in dress . I shall recover the large estate , that would now be mine , but for usury and fraud . and you will be one of the first lady in the world , as nature have always intend you to be . " 
 " that sound very well ; but how be it to be do ? how can France unite with England , when they be bitter enemy ? be France to conquer England first ? or be we to conquer France , as we always use to do ? " 
 " that would be a hard job now , when France be the mistress of the continent . no , there need be no conquering , sweet dolly , but only a little removal . the true interest of this country be as that mighty party , the Whigs , perceive to get rid of all the paltry form and dry bone of a dynasty which be no more english than Napoleon be , and to join that great man in his warfare against all oppression . your brother Frank be a lead spirit ; he have long cast off that wretched insular prejudice which defeat all good . in the grand new scheme of universal right , which must prevail very shortly , Frank Darling will obtain that foremost place to which his noble view entitle he . you , as his sister , and my wife , will be adore almost as much as you could wish . " 
 " it sound very grand , " answer dolly , with a smile , though a little alarmed at this turn of it ; " but what be to become of the king , and Queen , and all the royal family ? and what be my father to do , and faith ? although she have not behave well to I . " 
 " those detail will be arrange to everybody 's satisfaction . little prejudice will subside , when it be see that they be useless . every possible care will be take not to injure any one . " 
 " but how be it all to be do ? " ask dolly , whose mind be practical , though romantic . " be the French to land , and overrun the country ? I be sure I never should agree to that . be all our defender to be throw into prison ? " 
 " certainly not . there will be no prison . the French might have to land , as a matter of form ; but not to overrun the country , only to secure british liberty and justice . all sensible people would hasten to join they , and any opposition would be quench at once . then such a glorious condition of mankind would ensue as have never be know in this world peace , wealth , universal happiness , gaiety , dance everywhere , no more shabby clothe , no more dreary sunday . how do you like the thought of it ? " 
 " well , some of it sound very nice ; but I do not   see the use of universal justice . justice mean have one 's own right ; and it be impossible for everybody to do that , because of other people . and as for the French come to put thing right , they have well attend to their own affair first . and as if any Englishman would permit it ! why , even Frank would mount his wig and gown   for he be a full - fledged barrister now , you know , and come and help to push they back into the sea . and I hope that you would do so too . I be not go to marry a Frenchman . you belong to an old english family , and you be bear in England , and your name be English , and the property that ought to belong to you . I hope you do not   consider yourself a frenchman because your mother be a great french lady , after so many generation of Carnes , all English , every bit of they . I be an english girl , and I care very little for thing that I do not   see such as justice , liberty , right of people , and all that . but I do care about my relation , and our friend , and the people that live here , and the boat , and all the tree , and the land that belong to my father . very likely you would want to take that away , and give it to some miserable Frenchman . " 
 " dolly , my dear , you must not be excite , " Carne answer , in the manner of a father ; " powerful as your comprehension be , for the moment these thing be beyond it . your meaning be excellent , very good , very great ; but to bring it to bear require further information . we will sit by the side of the sea to - morrow , darling , if you grant I a view of your loveliness again ; and there you will see thing in a large light than upon this narrow bench , with your father 's tree around we , and your father 's cow enquire whether I be good to eat . get away , cow ! do you take I for a calf ? " 
 one of the cow well love by dolly , who be very fond of good animal , have come up to ask who this man be that have be sit here so long with she . she be gift with a white face and large soft eye even beyond the common measure of a cow short little horn , that she would scarcely think of push even at a dog   unless he make mouth at her infant , a flat broad nose ever genial to be rub , and a delicate fringe of finely point yellow hair around her pleasant nostril and above her clovery lip . with single - hearted charity and enviable faith she be able to combine the hope that dolly have obtain a lover as good as could be find upon a single pair of leg . Carne be attire with some bravery , of the french manner rather than the English , and he want no butter on his velvet and fine lace . so he swing round his cane of heavy snakewood at the cow , and strike her poor horn so sharply that her head go round . 
 " be that universal peace , and gentleness , and justice ? " cry dolly , spring up and hasten to console her cow . " be this the way the lofty french redress the wrong of England ? what have poor Dewlips do , I should like to know ? kiss I , my pretty , and tell I how you would like the french army to land , as a matter of form ? the form you would take would be beef , I be afraid ; not even good roast beef , but bouillon , potage , fricandeau , friture anything one can not taste any meat in ; and that be how your wrong would be redress , after have have both your horn knock off . and about the same fate for John Bull , your master , unless he keep his horn well sharpen . do I not speak the truth , monsieur ? " 
 when Carne do anything to vex Miss dolly which happen pretty often , for he could not stop to study much her little prejudice she address he as if he be a Frenchman , never doubt that this must reduce he sadly in his self - esteem . 
 " never mind matter political , " he say , perceive that his power must not be press until he have deepen its foundation ; " what be all the politic in the world compare with your good opinion , beauty ? " dolly like to be call " Beauty , " and the name always make her try to deserve it by look sweet . " you must be quite certain that I would do nothing to injure a country which contain my Dolly . and as for Madam Cow , I will beg her pardon , though my cane be hurt a great deal more than her precious horn be . behold I snap it in twain , although it be the only handsome one I possess , because it have offend you ! " 
 " oh , what a pity ! what a lovely piece of wood ! " cry dolly ; and they part on the good of term , after a warm vow upon either side that no nasty politic should ever come between they . 
 but Carne be annoy and discontent . he come to the edge of the cliff that evening below his ruin castle ; for there be no cliff at Springhaven , unless the headland deserve that name ; and there he sit gloomily for some hour , revolve the chance of his enterprise . the weather have change since the morning , and a chill November wind begin to urge the wave ashore . the sky be not very dark , but shred with loose grey vapour from the west , where a heavy bank of cloud lie under the pale crescent of a watery moon . in the distance two british cruiser shone , light ship of outlook , under easy sail , prepare to send the signal for a hundred league , from ship to ship and cliff to cliff , if any of England 's foe appear . they shine upon the dark sea , with canvas touch by moonlight , and seem ready to spring against the lower sky , if it hold any menace to the land they watch , or the long reach of water they have make their own . 
 " a pest upon those watch - dog ! " mutter Carne . " they be always wide - awake , and forever at their station . instead of grow tired , they get sharp every day . even Charron can scarcely run through they now . but I know who could do it , if he could only be trust . with a pilot - boat it be a fine idea a pilot - boat enter as of Pebbleridge . the Pebbleridge people hate Springhaven , through a feud of century , and Springhaven despise Pebbleridge . it would answer well , although the landing be so bad , and no anchorage possible in rough weather . I must try if Dan Tugwell will undertake it . none of the rest know the coast as he do , and few of they have the bravery . but Dan be a very sulky fellow , very difficult to manage . he will never betray we ; he be wonderfully grateful ; and after that battle with the press - gang , when he knock down the officer and break his arm , he will keep pretty clear of the Union - jack . but he go about moping , and wonder , and mooning , as if he be wretched about what he have to do . bless my soul , where be my invention ? I see the way to have he under my thumb . reason be an old coat hang on a peg ; passion be the fool who put it on and run away with it . Halloa ! who be you ? and what do you want at such a time as this ? surely you can see that I be not at leisure now . why , Tugwell , I think that you be far away at sea ! " 
 " so I be , sir ; but she travel fast . I never would believe the old London Trader could be drive through the water so . Sam Polwhele know how to pile it on a craft , as well as he do upon a man , sir . I will not   serve under he no more , nor Captain Charcoal either . I have do my duty by you . Squire Carne , the same as you do by I , sir ; and thank you for find I work so long , my meaning be to go upon the search to - morrow . " 
 " what fool they must have be to let this fellow come ashore ! " think Carne , while he fail to see the wise way to take it . " tugwell , you can not do this with any honour , after we have show you all the secret of our enterprise . you know that what we do be of the very high honour , kind and humane and charitable , though strictly forbid by a most inhuman government . how would you like , if you be a prisoner in France , to be debar from all chance of get any message from your family , your wife , your sweetheart , or your child , from year 's end to year 's end , and perhaps be dead for month without their know anything about it ? " 
 " well , sir , I should think it very hard indeed ; though , if I be dead , I should not   know much more about it . but , without reproach to you , I can not make out altogether that our only business be to carry letter for the prisoner , as now may be in England , from their love friend to command in their native country . I will not   say against you , sir , if you say it be that is , to the outside of all your knowledge . and twenty thousand of they may need letter by the sack . but what use they could make , sir , of cannon as big as I be , and musket that would kill a man a hundred yard of distance , and bayonet more large and more sharp than ever I see before , even with the Royal Volunteers this go out of all my calculation . " 
 " Daniel , you have express your view , which be remarkable as indeed they always be with your usual precision . but you have not observe thing with equal accuracy . do you know when a gun be past service ? " 
 " no , sir ; I never be a poacher , no - how . Squire Darling , that be to say , Sir Charles Darling now , accord to a chap on board , he be always so good upon his land that nobody durst go a - poaching . " 
 " I mean a cannon , Dan . they do not   poach with cannon yet , though they may come to do it , as the game - law increase . do you know when a cannon be unsafe to fire , though it may look as bright as ever , like a wear - out poker ? all those thing that have frighten you be only mean for ornament . you know that every ancient building ought to have its armoury , as this castle always have , until they be take away and sell . my intention be to restore it , when I can afford to do so . and have a lot of wear - out weapon offer I for next to nothing , I seize the chance of bring they . when time be well , and the war be over , I may find time to arrange they . but that be not of much importance . the great point be to secure the delivery of letter from their native land to the brave man here as prisoner . I can not afford to do that for nothing , though I make no profit out of it . I have so many thing to think about that I scarcely know which to consider first . and after all , what matter to we whether those poor man be allow to die , and be bury like dog , without knowledge of their friend ? why should we run the risk of be punish for they ? " 
 " well , sir , that seem hard doctrine , if I may be allow to say so , and not like your kind - heartedness . our Government have no right to stop they of their letter . " 
 " it be a cruel thing . but how be we to help it ? the London Trader be too large for the purpose , and she be under suspicion now . I tell you everything , Daniel , because I know that you be a true - hearted fellow , and far above all blabbing . I have think once or twice of obtain leave to purchase a stout and handy pilot - boat , with her licence and all that transfer to we , and so run to and fro when needful . the only risk then would be from peril of the sea ; and even the pressman dare not meddle with a pilot - boat . by - the - by , I have hear that you knock some of they about . tugwell , you might have get we all into sad trouble . " 
 " be I to think of what I be do , Squire Carne , when they want to make a slave of I ? I would serve King George with a good heart , in spite of all that father have say against it . but it must be with a free will , Squire Carne , and not to be tie hand and foot to it . how would you like that yourself , sir ? " 
 " well , I think I should have do as you do , Dan , if I have be a british sailor . but as to this pilot - boat , I must have a bold and good seaman to command it . a man who know the coast , and be not afraid of weather . of course we should expect to pay good wage ; 3 pound a week , perhaps , and a guinea for every bag of letter land safe . there be plenty of man who would jump at such a chance , Dan . " 
 " I will be bind there be , sir . and it be more than I be worth , if you mean offer the place to I . it would suit I wonderful , if I be certain that the job be honest . " 
 " Daniel Tugwell " Carne speak with great severity " I will not lose my temper , for I be sure you mean no insult . but you must be of a very low , suspicious nature , and quite unfit for any work of a lofty and unselfish order , if you can imagine that a man in my position , a man of my large sentiment " 
 " oh , no , sir , no ; it be not at all that " Dan scarcely know how to tell what it be " it be nothing at all of that manner of thinking . I heartily ask your pardon , sir , if it seem to go in that way . " 
 " do not   do that , " reply Carne , " because I can make allowance . I know what a fine nature be , and how it take alarm at shadow . I be always tender with honest scruple , because I find so many of they in myself . I should not have be pleased with you , if you have accept my offer although so advantageous , and full of romantic interest until you be convince of its honourable nature . I have no time for argument , and I be sorry that you must not come up to the castle for supper , because we have an old Springhaven man there , who would tell your father all about you , which you especially wish to avoid . but if you feel inclined for this berth as you sailor seem to call it and hesitate through some patriotic doubt , though I can not understand what they be , I will bring you a document   if you meet I here to - morrow night   from Admiral Sir Charles Darling , which I think will satisfy you . " 
 " and shall I be allow to keep it , sir , to show , in case of trouble ? " 
 " very likely . but I can not say for certain . some of those official form must be return , other not ; all depend upon their rule . now go and make yourself comfortable . how be you off for money ? " 
 " Plenty , sir , plenty . I must not go where anybody know I , or to - morrow half the talk at old Springhaven would be about I . good - night , sir , and God bless you . " 




 CHAPTER L 

 his savage SPIRIT 

 at this time letter come very badly , not only to french prisoner in England , but even to the high authority , who have the very good mean of get they . Admiral Darling have often write to his old friend Nelson , but have long be without any tiding from he , through no default on the hero 's part . Lord Nelson be almost as prompt with the pen as he be with the sword , but despatch be most irregular and uncertain . 
 " here at last we have he ! " cry Sir Charles one morning early in December ; " and not more than five week old , I declare ! dolly , be ready , and call Faith down . now read it , my dear , for our benefit . your godfather write a most excellent hand , consider that it be his left hand ; but my eye be sore from so much night - work . put on my spec , dolly ; I should like to see you in they . " 
 " be I to read every word , papa , just as it come ? you know that he generally put in word that be rather strong for I . " 
 " Nelson never think or write a single word unfit for the nice young lady . but you may hold up your hand if you come to any strong expression , and we shall understand they . " 
 " then I shall want both hand as soon as ever we come to the very first Frenchman . but this be what my godfather say : 
 " ' victory , OFF TOULON , October 31st , 1804 . 
 " ' MY DEAR lingo , it be only yesterday that I receive your letter of July 21st ; it go in a spanish smuggling boat to the coast of Italy and return again to Spain , not have meet any of our ship . and now I hope that you will see I before you see this letter . we be certain to be at war with Spain before another month be out , and I be heartily sorry for it , for I like those fellow well than the French , because they be not such liar . my successor have be appoint , I have reason to hope , and must be far on his way by this time ; probably Keith , but I can not say . minister can not suppose that I want to fly the service ; my whole life have prove the contrary ; if they refuse , I shall most certainly leave in March or April , for a few month ' rest I must have , or else die . my cough be very bad , and my side where I be strike off Cape St. Vincent be very much swell , at time a lump as large as my fist be bring on by violent coughing , but I hope and believe my lung be sound . I hope to do good service yet , or else I should not care so much . but if I be in my grave , how can I serve the Country ? 
 " ' you will say , this be not at all like Nelson , to write about nothing but his own poor self ; and thank God , Lingo , I can say that you be right ; for if ever a man live for the good of England and the destruction of those ' " here dolly hold a hand up " ' frenchman , it be the man in front of this ink - bottle . the Lord have appoint I to that duty , and I shall carry out my order . mon . La Touche , who be preach about in France as the man that be to extinguish I , and even in the scurvy english newspaper , but never dare to show his snivelly countenance outside of the inner buoy , be dead of his debosherie , for which I be deeply grieve , as I fully intend to send he to the devil . 
 " ' I have be most unlucky for some time now , and to tell the truth I may say always . but I be the last man in the world to grumble as you , my dear Lingo , can testify . I always do the utmost , with a single mind , and leave the thought of miserable pelf to other , man perhaps who never see a shot cannon fire . you know who make eighty thousand pound , without have to wipe his pigtail dirty thing , I be glad they be go out but my business be to pay other people 's debt , and receive all my credit in the shape of cannon - ball . this be always so , and I should let it pass as usual , except for a blacker trick than I have ever know before . for fear of give I a single chance of earn twopence , they know that there be a million and a half of money come into Cadiz from South America in four spanish frigate , and instead of leave I to catch they , they send out Graham Moore you know he very well with order to pocket everything . this will create a war with Spain , a war begin with robbery on our part , though it must have come soon in any case . for everywhere now , except where I be , that fiend of a Corsican be supreme . 
 " ' there be not a sick man in this fleet , unless it be the one inside my coat . that liar La Touche say he chase I and I RAN . I keep a copy of his letter , which it would have be my duty to make he eat , if he have venture out again . but he be go to the lake of brimstone now , and I have the good feeling to forgive he . if my character be not fix by this time , it be not worth my trouble to put the world right . yesterday I take a look into the port within easy reach of their battery . they lay like a lot of mouse hole in a trap , but the weather be too thick to count they . they be certainly nearly twice our number ; and if any one be here except poor little Nelson , I believe they would venture out . but my reputation deprive I always of any fair chance to increase it . 
 " ' and now , my dear Lingo , allow I to enquire how you be get on with your Coast - defence . I never do attach much importance to their senseless invasion scheme . the only thing to make it formidable would be some infernal traitor on the coast , some devilish spy who would keep they well inform , and enable they to land where least expect . if there be such a scoundrel , may the Lord Almighty ' " here both dolly 's hand go up , with the letter in they , and her face turn as white as the paper . 
 " ' I have often tell you , as you may remember , that Springhaven be the very place I should choose , if I be commander of the french flotilla . it would turn the flank of all the inland defence , and no british ship could attack their intrenchment , if once they be snug below the window of the Hall . but they be not likely to know this , thank God ; and if they do , they would have a job to get there . however , it be wise to keep a sharp lookout , for they know very well that I be far away . 
 " ' and now that I have get to your own door , which I heartily hope to do , perhaps before you see this , let I ask for yourself and all your dear family . Lingo , the long I live the more I feel that all the true happiness of life be find at home . my glory be very great , and satisfy I , except when it scar the enemy ; but I very often feel that I would give it all away for a quiet life among those who love I . your daughter Faith be a sweet young woman , just what I should wish for a child of mine to be . and Horatia , my godchild , will turn out very well , if a sharp hand be keep over she . but she take after I , she be daring and ambitious , and require a firm hand at the helm . read this to she , with my love , and I dare say she will only laugh at it . if she marry to my liking , she will be down for a good thing in my will , some day . God bless we all . amen . amen . 
 " ' your affectionately , 
 " ' nelson and BRONTE . ' " 
 " take it to heart , my dear ; and so must I , " say the Admiral , laugh at the face his daughter make ; " your godfather be a most excellent judge of everybody 's character except his own . but , bless I , my dear , why , you be cry ! you silly little thing ! I be only in fun . you shall marry to his liking , and be down for the good thing . look up , and laugh at everybody , my darling . no one laugh so merrily as my pretty dolly . why , Faith , what do she mean by this ? " 
 to the coax voice of her father , and the playful glance that she use to play with , dolly have not rush up at all , either with mind , or , if that fail , with body , as she always use to do . she hurry towards the door , as if she long to be away from they ; and then , as if she would rather not make any stir about it , sit down and pretend to have catch her dress in something . 
 " the only thing be to let she go on as she like , " Faith say aloud , so that dolly might hear all of it ; " I have do all I can , but she believe herself superior . she can not bear any sort of contradiction , and she expect one to know what she say , without she say it . there be nothing to be do but to treat she the same way . if she be leave to herself , she may come back to it . " 
 " well , my dear child , " say the Admiral , much alarmed at the prospect of a broil between they , such as he remember about three year back , " I make no pretence to understand your way . if you be boy , it would be different altogether . but the Almighty have be pleased to make you girl , and very good one too ; in fact , there be none to be find well . you have always be bind up with one another and with I ; and every one admire all the three of we . so that we must be content if a little thing arise , not to make too much of it , but bear with one another , and defy anybody to come in between we . kiss one another , my dear , and be off ; for I have much correspondence to attend to , besides the great Nelson 's , though I take he first , hop for something sensible . but I have not much to learn about Springhaven , even from his lordship . however , he be a man in ten thousand , and we must not be vex about any of his crotchet , because he have never have child to talk about ; and he get out of sounding when he talk about mine . I wish Lady Scudamore be come back . she always agree with I , and she take a great load off my shoulder . " 
 the girl laugh at this , as they be mean to do . and they hurry off together , to compare opinion . after all these year of independence , no one should be set up over they . upon that point faith be quite as resolute as dolly ; and her ladyship would have refuse to come back , if she have overhear their council . for even in the lofty feminine nature lurk a small tincture of jealousy . 
 but dolly be now in an evil frame of mind about many thing which she could not explain even to herself , with any satisfaction . even that harmless and pleasant letter from her great godfather go amiss with she ; and instead of laugh at the word about herself , as with a sound conscience she must have do , she brood over they , and turn they bitter . no man could have mix up thing as she do , but her mind be nimble . for the moment , she hat patriotism , because Nelson represent it ; and feel how wrong he have be about herself , she feel that he be wrong in everything . the French be fine fellow , and have quite as much right to come here as we have to go and harass they , and a little abatement of english conceit might be a good thing in the long - run . not that she would let they stay here long ; that be not to be think of , and they would not wish it . but a little excitement would be delightful , and a great many thing might be change for the well , such as the treatment of woman in this country , which be barbarous , compare to what it be in France . Caryl have tell she a great deal about that ; and the long she know he the more she be convince of his wisdom and the largeness of his view , so different from the savage spirit of Lord Nelson . 




 CHAPTER LI 

 strange craft 

 while his love be lapse from he thus , and from her own true self yet more , the gallant young sailor , whose last prize have be that useful one misfortune , be dwell continually upon her image , because he have very little else to do . english prisoner in France be treat sometimes very badly , which they take good care to proclaim to Europe ; but more often with pity , and good - will , and a pleasant study of their mode of thought . for an Englishman then be a strange and ever fresh curiosity to a Frenchman , a speciman of another race of biped , with doubt whether marriage could make parentage between they . and a century of intercourse , good - will , and admiration have leave we still inquisitive about each other . 
 Napoleon feel such confidence in his plan for the conquest of England that if any british officer belong to the fleet in the narrow sea be take   which do not happen largely , he send for he , upon his arrival at Boulogne , and hold a little talk with any one who could understand and answer . he be especially pleased at hearing of the capture of Blyth Scudamore   who have rob he of his beloved Blonde , and at once restore desporte to favour , which he have begin to do before , know as well as any man on earth the value of good officer . " bring your prisoner here to - morrow at twelve o'clock , " be his order ; " you have turn the table upon he well . " 
 Scudamore feel a little nervous tingling as he pass through the sentry , with his friend before he , into the pavilion of the great man in Europe . but the Emperor , be in high good - humour , and please with the young man 's modest face and gentle demeanour , soon set he at his ease , and speak to he as affably as if he have be his equal . for this man of almost universal mind could win every heart , when he set himself to do it . Scudamore rub his eye , which be a trick of his , as if he could scarcely believe they . Napoleon look not insignificant   that be impossible for a man with such a countenance , but mild , and pleasing , and benevolent , as he walk to and fro , for he never could stay still , in the place which be neither a tent nor a room , but a mixture of the two , and not a happy one . his hat , loop up with a diamond and quiver with an ostrich feather , be fling anyhow upon the table . but his wonderful eye be the bright thing there . 
 " ha ! ha ! " say the Emperor , a very keen judge of face ; " you expect to find I a monster , as I be portray by your caricaturist . your countryman be not kind to I , except the foremost of they the great poet . but they will understand I well by - and - by , when justice prevail , and the blessing of peace , for which I be strive perpetually . but the english nation , if it be allow a voice , would proclaim I its only true friend and ally . you know that , if you be one of the people , and not of the hateful House of Lords , which engross all the army and the navy . be you in connection with the House of Lords ? " 
 Scudamore shake his head and smile . he be anxious to say that he have a cousin , not more than twice remove , now an entire viscount ; but Napoleon never encourage conversation , unless it be his own , or in answer to his question . 
 " very well . then you can speak the truth . what do they think of all this grand army ? be they aware that , for their own good , it will very soon occupy London ? be they form themselves to act as my ally , when I have reduce they to reason ? be it now make entirely familiar to their mind that resistance to I be as hopeless as it have be from the first unwise ? if they would submit , without my crossing , it would save they some disturbance , and I a great expense . I have often hop to hear of it . " 
 " you will never do that , sire , " Scudamore answer , look calmly and firmly at the deep gray eye , whose gaze could be meet by none of the million who dread passion ; " England will not submit , even if you conquer she . " 
 " it be well say , and doubtless you believe it , " Napoleon continue , with a smile so slight that to smile in reply to it would have be impertinent ; " but England be the same as other nation , although the most obstinate among they . when her capital be occupy , her credit ruin , her great lord unable to obtain a dinner , the government   which be not the country   will yield , and the country must follow it . I have hear that the king , and the Court , and the Parliament , talk of fly to the north , and there remain , while the navy cut off our communication , and the inferior class starve we . have you hear of any such romance as that ? " 
 " no , sire : " Scudamore scarcely know what to call he , but adopt this vocative for want of any well . " I have never hear of any such plan , and no one would think of pack up , until our fleet have be demolish . " 
 " your fleet ? yes , yes . how many ship be now parade to and fro , and get very tired of it ? " 
 " your Majesty 's officer know that good , " Scudamore answer , with his pleasant open smile . " I have be a prisoner for a month and more , and keep ten mile inland , out of sight of the sea . " 
 " but you have be well treat , I hope . you have no complaint to make , Monsieur Scutamour ? your name be french , and you speak the language well . we set the fair example in the treatment of brave man . " 
 " sire , I have be treat , " the young officer reply , with a low bow , and eye full of gratitude , " as a gentleman amongst gentleman . I might say as a friend among kind friend . " 
 " that be as it should be . it be my wish always . few of your english fabrication annoy I more than the falsehood about that . it be most ungenerous , when I do my good , to charge I with strangle brave english captain . but desporte fight well , before you take his vessel . be it not so ? speak exactly as you think . I like to hear the enemy 's account of every action . " 
 " Captain Desportes , sire , fight like a hero , and so do all his crew . it be only his mishap in stick fast upon a sand - bank that enable we to overpower he . " 
 " and now he have do the like to you . you speak with a brave man 's candour . you shall be at liberty to see the sea , monsieur ; for a sailor always pine for that . I will give full instruction to your friend desporte about you . but one more question before you go be there much anxiety in England ? " 
 " yes , sire , a great deal . but we hope not to allow your Majesty 's armament to enter and increase it . " 
 " ah , we shall see , we shall see how that will be . now farewell , Captain . tell desporte to come to I . " 
 " well , my dear friend , you have make a good impression , " say the french sailor , when he rejoin Scudamore , after a few word with the Master of the state ; " all you have to do be to give your word of honour to avoid our line , and keep away from the beach , and of course to have no communication with your friend upon military subject . I be allow to place you for the present at Beutin , a pleasant little hamlet on the Canche , where live an old relative of mine , a Monsieur Jalais , an ancient widower , with a large house and one servant . I shall be afloat , and shall see but little of you , which be the only sad part of the business . you will have to report yourself to your landlord at eight every morning and at eight o'clock at night , and only to leave the house between those hour , and not to wander more than six mile from home . how do these condition approve themselves to you ? " 
 " I call they very liberal , and very handsome , " Scudamore answer , as he well might do . " two mile ' range be all that we allow in England to french officer upon parole . these generous term be due to your kind friendship . " 
 before very long the gentle Scuddy be as happy as a prisoner can expect to be , in his comfortable quarter at Beutin . through friendly exchange he have receive a love letter from his mother , with an amiable enclosure , and M. Jalais be far from wealthy , a pleasant arrangement be make between they . Scudamore take all his meal with his host , who could manage sound victual like an Englishman , and the house - keeper , house - cleaner , and house - feeder   misdescribe by desporte as a servant , accord to our distinction , be a widow of mark , sit down to consider her cookery upon choice occasion . then for a long time would prevail a conscientious gravity , and reserve of judgment inwardly , everybody wait for some other body 's sentiment ; until the author of the work , as a female , might no more abide the malignant silence of male reviewer . 
 Scudamore , be very easily amuse , as any good - natured young man be , enter with zest into all these doing , and become an authority upon appeal ; and be gift with depth of simplicity as well as high courtesy of taste , be never know to pronounce a wrong decision . that be to say , he decide always in favour of the lady , which have be the majestic course of Justice for century , till the appearance of Mrs. - , the lady who should have marry the great Home - Ruler . 
 thus the wily Scudamore obtain a sitting - room , with the prettiest outlook in the house , or indeed in any house in that part of the world for many league of seek . for the mansion of M. Jalais stand in an elbow of the little river , and one window of this room show the curve of tidal water widen towards the sea , while the other pleasantly give eye to the upper reach of the stream , where an angler of rose - coloured mind might almost hope to hook a trout . the sun glance down the stream in the morning , and up it to see what he have do before he set ; and although M. Jalais ' tree be leafless now , they have sleeve their bent arm with green velvetry of moss . 
 Scudamore bring his comfortable chair to the nook between these window , and there , with a book or two belong to his host , and the pipe whose silver cloud enthrone the god of contemplation , many a pleasant hour be pass , seldom invade by the sound of war . for the course of the road , and sand of the river , keep this happy spot aloof from bad communication . like many other stream in northern France , the Canche have be deepen and its mouth improve , not for us of commerce , but of warfare . veteran soldier and raw recruit , bugler , baker , and farrier , man who come to fight and man who come to write about it , all have be turn into navvy , digger , driver of pile , or of horse , or wheelbarrow , by the man who turn everybody into his own teetotum . the Providence that guide the world show mercy in send that engine of destruction before there be a Railway for he to run upon . 
 now Scudamore be of a different sort , and therefore have please Napoleon   who detest any one at all of his own pattern , might have be very well content here , and certainly must have be so , if he have be without those two window . many a bird have lose his nest , and his egg , and his mate , and even his own tail , by cock his eye to the right and leave , when he should have draw their shutter up . and why ? because the brilliance of his too project eye have twinkle through the leaf upon the narrow oblong of the pupil of a spotty - eyed cat go stealthily under the comb of the hedge , with her stomach wire in , and her spinal column flute , to look like a wrinkle blackthorn snag . but still bad be it for that poor thrush , or lintie , or robin , or warbler - wren , if he flutter in his bosom when he spy that cat , and set up his feather , and begin to hop about , make a sad little chirp to his mate , and appeal to the sky to protect he and his family . 
 Blyth Scudamore 's case be a mixture of those two . it would have be well for his comfort if he have shut his eye ; but have open they , he should have stay where he be , without any fluttering . however , he act for the good ; and when a man do that , can those who never do so find a word to say against he ? 
 accord to the good of his recollection , which be generally near the mark , it be upon Christmas Eve , a.d. 1804 , that his curiosity be first arouse . he have make up his room to look a little bit like home , with a few sprig of holly , and a sheaf of laurel , not place daintily as a lady dress they , but as sprightly as a man can make they look , and as bright as a captive Christmas could expect . the decorator shed a little sigh if that expression may be pardon by analogy , for he certainly neither fetch nor heave it and then he light his pipe to reflect upon home blessing , and consider the free world outside , in which he have very little share at present .
