 mild blue eye , such as this young man possess , be often short - sighted at a moderate range , and would be fit up with glass in these artificial time , and yet at long distance they be most efficient , and can make out object that would puzzle keener organ . and so it be that Scudamore , with the sink sun to help he , descry at a long distance down the tidal reach a peaceful - looking boat , which make his heart beat fast . for a sailor 's glance assure he that she be English English in her rig and the stiff cut of her canvas , and in all those point of character to a seaman so distinctive , which apprise he of his kindre through the length of air and water , as clearly as we landsman know a man from a woman at the measure of a furlong , or a quarter of a mile . he perceive that it be an English pilot - boat , and that she be stand towards he . at first his heart flutter with a warm idea , that there must be good news for he on board that boat . perhaps , without his knowledge , an exchange of prisoner might have be agree upon ; and what a grand Christmas - box for he , if the order for his release be there ! but another thought show he the absurdity of this hope , for order of release do not come so . nevertheless , he watch that boat with interest and wonder . 
 presently , just as the sun be set , and shadow cross the water , the sail   which have be gleam like a candle - flame against the haze and upon the glaze   flicker and fall , and the bow swing round , and her figure be draw upon the tideway . she be now within half a mile of M. Jalais ' house , and Scudamore , though long for a spy - glass , be able to make out a good deal without one . he see that she be an English pilot - boat , undecked , but fit with a cuddy forward , rig luggerwise , and build for speed , yet fit to encounter almost any Channel surge . she be light in the water , and bear little except ballast . he could not be sure at that distance , but he think that the sailor must be Englishmen , especially the man at the helm , who be beyond reasonable doubt the captain . 
 then two long sweep be man amidship , with two sturdy fellow to tug at each ; and the quiet evening air lead through the soft rehearsal of the water to its bank the creak of tough ash thole - pin , and the groan of gunwale , and the splash of oar , and even a sound of human staple , such as be accept by the civilized world as our national diapason . 
 the captive Scuddy , who observe all this , be thoroughly puzzle at that last turn . though the craft be visibly English , the crew might still have be doubtful , if they have hold their tongue , or keep they in submission . but that word stamp they , or at any rate the one who have be strike in the breast by the heavy timber , as of genuine british birth . yet there be no sign that these man be prisoner , or act by compulsion . no french boat be near they , no battery there command their course , and the pilot - boat carry no prize - crew to direct reluctant labour . at the mouth of the river be a float bridge , for the use of the force on either side , and no boat could have pass it without permission . therefore these could be no venturesome Britons , spy out the quarter of the enemy ; either they must have be allow to pass for some special purpose , under flag of truce , or else they be traitor , in league with the French , and despatch upon some dark errand . 
 in a few minute , as the evening dusk begin to deepen round she , the mysterious little craft disappear in a hollow of the upland on the other side of the water , where a narrow creek or inlet such as be call a " pill " in some part of England form a shelter landing - place , overhang with clustering tree . then Scudamore rise , and fill another pipe , to meditate upon this strange affair . " I be justly forbid , " he think , as it grow dark , " to visit the camp , or endeavour to learn anything do by the army of invasion . and I have pledge myself to that effect . but this be a different case altogether . when englishman come here as traitor to their country , and in a place well within my range , my duty be to learn the meaning of it ; and if I find treachery of importance work , then I must consider about my parole , and probably withdraw it . that would be a terrible blow to I , because I should certainly be send far inland , and keep in a french prison perhaps for year , with little chance of hear from my friend again . and then she would give I up as lose , that faithful darling , who have put aside all her bright prospect for my sake . how I wish I have never see that boat ! and I think it be come to bring I such good news ! I be bind to give they one day 's grace , for they might not know where to find I at once , and to - night I could not get near they , without overstay my time to be in - door . but if I hear nothing to - morrow , and see nothing , I must go round , so as not to be see , and learn something about she the very next morning . " 
 hear nothing and see no more , he spend an uncomfortable Christmas Day , disappoint his host and kind Madame Fropot , who have do all they know to enliven he with a genuine english plum - pudding . and the next day , with a light foot but rather heavy heart , he make the long round by the bridge up - stream , and examine the creek which the english boat have enter . he approach the place very cautiously , know that if his suspicion be correct , they might be confirm too decisively , and his countryman , if they have fire - arm , would give he a warm reception . however , there be no living creature to be see , except a poor terrify ox , who have escape from the slaughter - house of the distant camp , and hop for a little rest in this dark thicket . he be wear out with his long flight and sadly wound , for many man have shoot at he , when he desire to save his life ; and although his mouth be little more than the length of his tail from water , there he lie gasp with his lip stretch out , and his dry tongue quiver between his yellow tooth , and the only moisture he could get be run out instead of into his mouth . 
 Scudamore , see that the coast be clear , and no enemy in chase of this poor creature , immediately fill his hat with fresh water for the tide be out now , and the residue be sweet and speak very gently in the english language , for he see that he must have be hard - shout at in French , be allow without any more disturbance of the system to supply a little glad refreshment . the sorely afflict animal lick his lip , and look up for another hatful . 
 Captain Scuddy deserve a new hat for this though very few englishman would not have do the like and in the end he get it , though he must have catch a bad cold if he have go without a hat till then . 
 pursue his search , with grateful eye pursue he , he soon discover where the boat have ground , by the impress of her keel and forefoot on the stiff retentive mud . he could even see where a hawser have be make fast to a staunch old trunk , and where the soil have be prod with a pole in push she off at the turn of tide . also deep track of some very large hound , or wolf , or unknown quadruped , in various place , scar the bank . and these mark be so fresh and bright that they must have be make within the last few hour , probably when the last ebb begin . if so , the mysterious craft have spend the whole of Christmas Day in that snug berth ; and he blame himself for permit his host 's festivity to detain he . then he take a few bearing to mark the spot , and feed the poor crippled ox with all the herbage he could gather , resolve to come with a rope to - morrow , and lead he home , if possible , as a Christmas present to M. Jalais . 




 CHAPTER LII 

 KIND enquiry 

 that notable year , and signal mark in all the great annal of England , the year 1805 , begin with gloom and great depression . food be scarce , and so be money ; war , and rumour of bad than war ; discontent of man who owe it to their birth and country to stand fast , and trust in God , and vigorously defy the devil ; sinking even of strong heart , and quailing of spirit that have never quail before ; passionate outcry for peace without honour , and even without safety ; savage murmuring at wise measure and at the burden that must be bear none but those who live through all these trouble could count half of they . if such come now , would the body of the nation strive to stand against they , or fall in the dust , and be kick and trample , sputter namby - pamby ? Britannia now be always wrong , in the opinion of her wise son , if she dare to defend herself even against weak enemy ; what then would her crime be if she buckle her corselet against the world ! to prostitute their mother be the philanthropy of Communists . 
 but while the anxious people who have no belief in foreigner be watch by the dark wave , or at the twilight window trembling   if ever a shooting - star draw train , like a distant rocket - signal , or in their sleepy bed scar , and jump up if a bladder burst upon a jam - pot , no one attempt to ridicule they , and no public journal pronounce that the true british flag be the white feather . it have be leave for time when the power of England be tenfold what it be then , and her duty a hundredfold , to tell we that soon than use the one for the proper discharge of the other , we must break it up and let they go to pot upon it , for fear of hurt somebody that stick we in the back . 
 but who of a right mind know not this , and who with a wrong one will heed it ? the only point be that the commonest truism come upon utterance sometimes , and take didactic form too late ; even as we shout to our comrade prone , and begin to rub his poor nose , " look out ! " and this be what everybody do with one accord , when he be down upon his luck which be far more momentous than his nose to any man in the case of Rector Twemlow . 
 that gentleman now have good reason for be in less than his usual cheer and comfort . everything around he be uneasy , and everybody seem to look at he , instead of look up to he , as the manner use to be . this be enough to make he feel unlike himself ; for although he be resolute in his way , and could manage to have it with most people , he be not of that iron style which take the world as wax to write upon . Mr. Twemlow like to heave his text at the people of his parish on Sunday , and to have his joke with they on Monday ; as the fire that have burn a man make the kettle sing to comfort he . and all who meet he throughout the week be pleased with he doubly , when they remember his faithfulness in the pulpit . 
 but now he do his duty softly , as if some of it have be do to he ; and if anybody thank he for a fine discourse , he never endeavour to let he have it all again . so far be he go from his natural state that he would rather hear nothing about himself than be praise enough to demand reply ; and this show a world - wide depression to have arrive in the latitude of a british waistcoat . however , he go through his work , as a Briton always do , until he hang himself ; and he try to try some of the high consolation , which he know so well how to administer to other . 
 those who do not understand the difference of this might have be incline to blame he ; but all who have see a clever dentist with the toothache be aware that his knowledge add acuteness to the pain . Mr. Twemlow have bear great trouble well , and be cheerful even under long suspense ; but now a disappointment close at home , and the grief of behold his last hope fade , be embitter by mystery and dark suspicion . in despair at last of recover his son , he have fasten upon his only daughter the interest of his decline life ; and now he be vex with misgiving about she , which vary as frequently as she do . it be very unpleasant to lose the chance of have a grandchild capable of rock in a silver cradle ; but that be a trifle compare with the prospect of have no grandchild at all , and perhaps not even a child to close his eye . and even his wife , of long habit and fair harmony , from whom he have never keep any secret frightful as might be the cost to his honour even Mrs. Twemlow shake her head sometimes , when the arrangement of her hair permit it , and doubt whether any of the Carne Castle Carnes would have bear with such indignity . 
 " prosecute he , prosecute he , " this good lady always say . " you ought to have be a magistrate , Joshua the first magistrate in the Bible be that and then you would have know how to do thing . but because you would have to go to Sir Charles Darling whose Sir can never put he on the level of the Carnes you have some right feeling against take out a summon . in that I agree with you ; it would be very dreadful here . but in London he might be punish , I be sure ; and I know a great deal about the law , for I never have any one connect with I who be not a magistrate ; the Lord Mayor have a Court of his own for try the corporation under the chair ; and if this be put properly before he by a man like Mr. Furkettle , upon the understanding that he should not be pay unless he win his case , I be sure the result would be three year ' imprisonment . by that time he would have wear out his coat with jailer 's key upon it , which first attract our poor Eliza ; or if he be not allow to wear it , it would go out of fashion , and be harmless . no one need know a word about it here , for Captain Stubbard would oblige we gladly by cut it out of the London paper . my dear , you have nobody ill in the parish ; I will put up your thing , and see you off to - morrow . we will dine late on Friday , to suit the coach ; and you will be quite fit for Sunday work again , if you keep up your leg on a chair all Saturday . " 
 " if ever I see a straightforward man , " Mr. Twemlow use to answer , " it be poor Percival shargeloe . he be go to a well world , my dear . and if he continue to be amenable to law , this be not a criminal , but a civil case . " 
 " a nice case of civility , Joshua ! but you always stand up for your sex . do the coach take people to a well world ? a stout gentleman , like he , be see inside the coach , muffle up in a cravat of three colour , and eat at frequent interval . " 
 " the very thing poor Percival never do . that dispose to my mind of that foolish story . my dear , when all truth come to light , you will do justice to his memory . " 
 " yes , I dare say . but I should like to do it now . if you entertain any dark idea , it be your duty to investigate they . also to let I share they , Joshua , as I have every right to do . " 
 this be just what the Rector could not do ; otherwise he might have be far more happy . remember that last conversation with his prospective son - in - law , and the poor man 's declaration that the suspicious matter at the castle ought to be thoroughly search out at once , he nourish a dark suspicion , which he fear to impart to his well half , the aunt of the person suspect . but the long he conceal it , the more unbearable grow this misery to a candid nature , until he be compel , in self - defence , to allow it some sort of outlet . " I will speak to the fellow myself , " he say , heartily dislike the young man now , " and judge from his manner what next I ought to do . " 
 this resolution give he comfort , much as he hat any interview with Carne , who treat he generally with cold contempt . and , like most people who have form a decision for the easing of the conscience , he accept very patiently the obstacle encounter . in the first place , Carne be away upon business ; then he be lay up with a heavy cold ; then he be much too hard at work   after lose so much time   to be able to visit Springhaven ; and to seek he in his ruin be most unsafe , even if one like to do it . for now it be say that two gigantic dog , as big as a bull and as fierce as a tiger , rove among the ruin all day , and be always famish , would devour in two minute any tempting stranger with a bit of flesh or fat on he . the Rector , pat his gaiter , feel that instead of a pastor he might become a very sweet repast to they , and his delicacy be renew and deepen . he be bind to wait until his nephew appear at least inside his parish . 
 therefore the time of year be come almost to the middle of February when Mr. Twemlow at last obtain the chance he require and dread . he hear that his nephew have be see that day to put up his horse in the village , and would probably take the homeward road as soon as it grow too dark to read . so he get through his own work   consist chiefly of newspaper , dinner , and a cool clay pipe , to equalise mind with matter , and have thus escape the lady , off he set by the lobby door , carry a good thick stick . as the tide would be up , and only deep sand leave for the heavy track of the traveller , he choose the inland way across the low part of the Admiral 's ground , lead to the village by a narrow plank bridge across the little stream among some tree . here be bank of earth and thicket , shadowy dell where the primrose grow , and the cuckoo - pint , and wood - sorrel , and perhaps in summer the glowworm breathe her mossy gleam under the blackberry . 
 and here Parson Twemlow be astonish , though he have promise himself to be surprised no more , after all he have be through lately . as he turn a sharp corner by an ivied tree , a breathless young woman run into his arm . 
 " oh ! " cry the Rector , for he be walk briskly , with a well - nourish part of his system forward " oh , I hope you have not hurt yourself . no doubt it be my fault . why , dolly ! what a hurry you be in ! and all alone all alone , almost after dark ! " 
 " to be sure ; and that make I in such a hurry ; " Miss dolly be in sad confusion . " but I suppose I be safe in my father 's own ground . " 
 " from everybody , except yourself , my dear , " Mr. Twemlow reply , severely . " be your father aware , do your sister know , that you be at this distance from the house after dark , and wholly without a companion ? " 
 " it be not after dark , Mr. Twemlow ; although it be get dark than I mean it to be . I beg your pardon for terrify you . I hope you will meet with no other peril ! good - night ! or at least I mean , good - afternoon ! " 
 " the brazen creature ! " think Mr. Twemlow , as the girl without another word disappear . " not even to offer I any excuse ! but I suppose she have no fib handy . she will come to no good , I be very much afraid . Maria tell I that she be get very wilful ; but I have no idea that it be quite so bad as this . I be sorry for poor Scudamore , who think she such an angel . I wonder if Carne be at the bottom of this ? there be nothing too bad for that dark young man . I shall ascertain at any rate whether he be in the village . but unless I look sharp I shall be too late to meet he . oh , I can not   walk so fast as I do ten year ago . " 
 impel by duty to put good leg foremost , and take a short - cut above the village , he come out upon the lane lead towards the castle , some half - mile or so beyond the last house of Springhaven . here he wait to recover breath , and prepare for what he mean to say , and he be sorry to perceive that light would fail he for strict observation of his nephew 's face . but he choose the most open spot he could find , where the hedge be low , and nothing overhang the road . 
 presently he hear the sound of hoof approach leisurely up the hill , and could see from his resting - place that Carne be come , sit loosely and wearily on his high black horse . then the Rector , to cut short an unpleasant business , stand boldly forth and hail he . 
 " no time for anything now , " shout Carne ; " too late already . do you want my money ? you be come to the wrong man for that ; but the right one , I can tell you , for a bullet . " 
 " Caryl , it be I , your uncle Twemlow , or at any rate the husband of your aunt . put up your pistol , and speak to I a minute . I have something important to say to you . and I never can find you at the castle . " 
 " then be quick , sir , if you please ; " Carne have never condescend to call this gentleman his uncle . " I have little time to spare . out with it . " 
 " you be rid very slowly for a man in a hurry , " say the Rector , annoy at his roughness . " but I will not keep you long , young man . for some good reason of your own you have make a point of avoid we , your near relative in this country , and to whom you address yourself before you land in a manner far more become . have I ever press my attention upon you ? " 
 " no , I confess that you have not do that . you perceive as a gentleman how little there be in common between the son of a devoted Catholic and a heretic clergyman . " 
 " that be one way to put it , " Mr. Twemlow answer , smile in spite of his anger at be call a heretic ; " but I be not aware that you have strong religious view . however that may be , we should have many thing in common , as Englishmen , at a time like this . but what I come to speak of be not that . we can still continue to get on without you , although we would rather have meet with friendly feeling and candour , as become relative . but little as you know of we , you must be well aware that your cousin Eliza be engage to be married to a gentleman from London , Mr. Percival Shargeloes , and that he " 
 " I be sure I wish she all happiness , and congratulate you , my dear sir , as well as my aunt Maria . I shall call , as soon as possible , to offer my good wish . it be very kind of you to tell I . Goodnight , sir , good - night ! there be a shower come . " 
 " but , " exclaim the Rector , nonplus for the moment by this view of the subject , yet stand square before the horse , " shargeloe have disappear . what have you do with he ? " 
 Carne look at his excellent uncle as if he have much doubt about his sanity . " try to explain yourself , my dear sir . try to connect your idea , " he say , " and offer I the benefit another time . my horse be impatient ; he may strike you with his foot . " 
 " if he do , I shall strike he upon the head , " Mr. Twemlow reply , with his heavy stick ready . " it will be well for you to hear I out . otherwise I shall procure a search - warrant , and myself examine your ruin , of which I know every crick and cranny . and your aunt Maria shall come with I , who know every stone even well than you do . that would be a very different thing from an overhauling by Captain Stubbard . I think we should find a good many barrel and bale that have pay no duty . " 
 " my dear uncle , " cry Carne , with more affection than he ever yet have show , " that be no concern of your ; you have no connection with the revenue ; and I be sure that Aunt Maria would be loth to help in pull down the family once more . but do as you please . I be accustom to ill fortune . only I should like to know what this be about poor Cousin Eliza . if any man have wrong she , leave the case to I . you have no son now , and the honour of the family shall not suffer in my hand . I will throw up everything , busy as I be , to make such a rascal bite the dust . and Eliza so proud , and so upright herself ! " 
 " Caryl , " say his uncle , move more than he like to show by this fine feeling , " you know more , I see , than you like to show at first , doubtless through goodwill to we . your dear aunt wish to keep the matter quiet , for the sake of poor Eliza , and her future chance . but I say no . let we have it all out . if there be wrong , we have suffer , not do it . Concealment be odious to every honest mind . " 
 " deeply , deeply odious . upon that point there can be no two opinion " he forget his barrel , think the Rector " but surely this man , whatever his name be charleygoe must have be hide from you something in his own history . probably he have a wife already . city man often do that when young , and then put their wife somewhere when they get rich , and pay visit , and even give dinner , as if they be bachelor to be seek after . be charleygoe that sort of a man ? " 
 " his name be ' shargeloe , ' a name well know , as I be assure , in the high quarter . and he certainly be not seek after by we , but come to I with an important question bear on ichthyology . he may be a wanderer , as you suggest , and as all the lady seem to think . but my firm belief be to the contrary . and my reason for ask you about he be a very clear one . he have meet you twice , and feel interest in you as a future member of our family . you have never invite he to the castle ; and the last intention he express in my hearing be to call upon you without one . have he meet with an accident in your cellar ? or have your dog devour he ? he carry a good deal of flesh , in spite of all he could do to the contrary ; and any man naturally might endeavour to hush up such an incident . tell I the truth , Caryl . and we will try to meet it . " 
 " my two dog   who would never eat any one , though they might pull down a stranger , and perhaps pretend to bite he   arrive here the first week in January . when do charleygoe disappear ? I be not up in date , but it must have be week and week before that time . and I must have hear of it , if it have happen . I may give you my honour that Orso and Leo have not eat charleygoe . " 
 " you speak too lightly of a man in high position , who would have be Lord Mayor of London , if he have never come to Springhaven . but living or dead , he shall never be that now . can you answer I , in the same straightforward manner , as to an accident in your cellar ; which , as a gentleman upon a private tour , he have clearly no right to intrude upon ? " 
 " I can answer you quite as clearly . nothing accidental have happen in my cellar . you may come and see they , if you have any doubt about it . and you need not apply for a search - warrant . " 
 " God forbid , my dear fellow , " cry the uncle , " that I should intrude upon any little matter of delicacy , such as be apt to arise between artificial law and gentleman who happen to live near the sea , and to have large place that require restore ! I shall go home with a light heart . there be nothing in this world that bring the comfort of straightforwardness . " 




 CHAPTER LIII 

 TIME and PLACE 

 in a matter like that french invasion , which have be threaten for such a time , and keep so long impending , " the cry of wolf " grow stale at last , and then the real danger come . Napoleon have reckon upon this , as he always do upon everything , and for that good reason he have not grudge the time devote to his home affair . these be settle accord to his will , and mob turn into pomp as gaily as grub turn into butterfly , a strong desire for a little more glory arise in his mighty but ill - regulated mind . if he could only conquer England , or even without that fetch she down on her knee and make her lick her own dust off the foot of Frenchmen , from that day forth all the nation of the earth must bow down before he . Russia , Prussia , Austria , Spain , though they might have have the power , never would have pluck the spirit up , to resist he hand in hand , any more than skittle - pin can back one another up against the well - aim ball . 
 the balance of to - be or not - to - be , as concern our country   which many now despise , as the mother of such disloyal child , after all that long suspension , hang in the cloud of that great year ; and a very cloudy year it be , and thick with storm on land and sea . storm be what the Frenchmen long for , to disperse the british ship ; though storm make many an Englishman , pull up the counterpane as the window rattle , thank the Father of the weather for keep the enemy ashore and in a fright . but the great peril of all would be in the case of fog succeed storm , when the mighty flotilla might sweep across before our ship could resume blockade , or even a frigate intercept . 
 one of the strange point in all this period of wonder , to we who after the event be wise , be that even far - sight Nelson and his watchful colleague seem to have have no inkling of the enemy 's main project . Nelson believe Napoleon to be especially intent on Egypt ; Collingwood expect a sudden dash on Ireland ; other be sure that his object be Jamaica ; and many maintain that he would step ashore in India . and these last come near to the mark upon the whole , for a great historian   who declare , like Caryl Carne , that a french invasion be a blessing to any country   show that , for at least a month in the spring of 1805 , his hero be revolve a mighty scheme for rob poor England of blissful ravage , and transfer it to India . 
 however , the master of the world as he be call already , and mean soon to be suddenly return to his early design , and fix the vast power of his mind upon it . he push with new vigour his preparation , which have be slacken awhile , he add 30,000 well - train soldier to his force already so enormous , and he breathe the quick spirit of enterprise into the mighty mass he move . then , to clear off all obstacle , and ensure clear speed of passage , he send sharp order to his Admirals to elude and delude the british fleet , and resolve to enhance that delusion by his own brief absence from the scene . 
 meanwhile a man of no importance to the world , and of very moderate ambition , be pass a pleasant time in a quiet spot , content to be scarcely a spectator even of the drama in rehearsal around he . Scudamore still abode with M. Jalais , and have win his hearty friendship , as well as the warm good - will of that important personage Madame Fropot . neither of these could believe at first that any Englishman be kind and gentle , playful in manner , and light - hearted , easily pleased , and therefore truly pleasing . but as soon as they see the poor wound ox bring home by a ford , and settle happily in the orchard , and receive he as a free gift from their guest , national prejudice dwindle very fast , and domestic good feeling grow fast . M. Jalais , although a sound Frenchman , hat the Empire and all that lead up to it ; and as for Madame Fropot , her choice piece of cookery might turn into cinder , if anybody mention conscription in her presence . for she have lose her only son , the entire hope of her old day , as well as her only daughter 's lover , in that lottery of murder . 
 nine out of ten of the people in the village be of the same way of thinking . a great army can not be quarter anywhere , even for a week , without scatter brand of ill - will all around it . the swagger of the troop , their warlike air , and loud contempt of the undrilled swain , the dash of a coin on the counter when they deign to pay for anything , the insolent wink at every modest girl , and the coarse joke run along apish mouth even before dark crime begin , native antipathy be sow and thrive . and now for nearly four year this coast have never be free from the arrogant strut , the clanking spur , and the loud guffaw , which in every age and every clime have be consider the stamp of valour by plough - boy at the pap of Bellona . so weary be the neighbourhood of this race , new conscript always keep up the pest , that even the good M. Jalais long to hear that the armament lie at the bottom of the Channel . and Scudamore would have be follow by the good wish of every house in the village , if he have lift his hat and say , " good - bye , my dear friend ; I be break my parole . " 
 for this , though encourage by the popular voice , he be not sufficiently liberal , but stay within bound of space and time more carefully than if he have be watch . Captain Desportes , who have be in every way a true friend to he , come to see he now and then , be now in command of a division of the prame , and naturally anxious for the signal to unmoor . much discourse be hold , without brag on either side , but with equal certainty on both side of success . and in one of these talk the Englishman in the simple manner tell the Frenchman all that he have see on Christmas Eve , and his own suspicion about it . 
 " understand this well , " continue Scudamore ; " if I discover any treachery on the part of my own countryman , I shall not be able to stop here on the term that have be allow I . whatever the plan may be , I shall feel as if I be a party to it , if I accept my free range and swallow my suspicion . with your proceeding I do not meddle , accord to fair compact , and the liberal condition offer . but to see my own countryman play my country false be more than I could stand . you know more of such thing than I do . but if you be an Englishman , could you endure to stand by and hide treachery , for the sake of your own comfort ? " 
 " beyond a doubt , no , " Captain Desportes answer , spread his hand with decision : " in such a case I should throw up my parole . but a mere suspicion do not justify an act so ungracious to the commander , and personally so unkind to I . I hop that bright eye might persuade you to forego hard knock , and wear none but gentle chain among we . nature intend you for a Frenchman . you have the gay heart , and the easy manner , and the grand philosophy of our great nation . your name be Blyth , and I know what that intend . " 
 Scudamore blush , for he know that Madame Fropot be do she good to commit he with a lovely young lady not far off , who have feel a tender interest in the cheerful english captive . but after try to express once more the deep gratitude he feel towards those who have be so wonderfully kind and friendly , he ask with a smile , and a little sigh behind it , what he must do , if compel by duty to resign his present privilege . 
 " my faith ! I scarcely know , " reply desporte ; " I have never have such a case before . but I think you must give I a write notice , sign by yourself and by M. Jalais , and allow a week to pass , and then , unless you have hear from I , present yourself to the commandant of the near post , which must be , I suppose , at Etaples . rather a rough man he be ; and I fear you will have reason for regret . the duty will then remain with he . but I beg you , my dear friend , to continue as you be . tush , it be nothing but some smuggler 's work . " 
 Scudamore hop that he might be right , and for some little time be not disturb by any appearance to the contrary . but early in the afternoon one day , when the month of March be near its close , he leave his book for a little fresh air , and stroll into the orchard , where his friend the ox be dwell . this worthy animal , endow with a virtue deny to none except the human race , approach he lovingly , and beg to draw attention to the gratifying difference betwixt wound and scar . he offer his broad brow to the hand , and his charitable ear to be tickle , and breathe a quick issue of good feeling and fine feeding , from the sensitive tuck of his nostril , as a large - hearted smoker make the air go up with gratitude . 
 but as a burn child dread the fire , the seriously perforate animal keep one eye vigilant of the northern aspect , and the other studious of the south . and the gentle Scuddy   who be find all thing happy , which be the only way to make they so   be startle by a sharp jerk of his dear friend 's head . follow the clue of gaze , there he see , come up the river with a rollick self - trust , a craft uncommonly like that craft which have mount every sort of rig and flag , and carry every kind of crew , in his many dream about she . this make he run back to his room at once , not only in fear of be see upon the bank , but also that he might command a well view , with the help of his landlord 's old spy - glass . 
 use this , which he have clean from the dust of age , he could clearly see the face of the man on board . of these there be six , of whom five at least be Englishmen , or of english breed . as the pilot - boat draw nearer , and the sunlight fall upon she , to his great surprise he become convinced that the young man at the tiller be Dan Tugwell , the son of the captain of Springhaven . four of the other be unknown to he , though he fancy that he have see two of they before , but could not remember when or where . but he watch with special interest the tall man lounge against the little door of the cuddy in the bow , whose profile only be present to he . then the boat cant round towards the entrance of the creek , and have his glass upon the full face of the man , he recognise he as Caryl Carne , whom he have meet more than once at Springhaven . 
 his darkest suspicion be at once redouble , and a gush of latent jealousy be add to they . in happy day , when he be near his lady - love , some whisper have reach he about this fellow , whose countenance have always be repulsive to he , arrogant , moody , and mysterious . his good mother also , though most careful not to harass he , have mention that Carne in her late letter , and by no mean in a manner to remove his old misgiving . as a matter now of duty to his country and himself , the young sailor resolve to discover , at any risk , what traitorous scheme have bring this dark man over here . 
 to escape the long circuit by the upper bridge , he have obtain leave , through M. Jalais , to use an old boat which be keep in a bend of the river about a mile above the house . and now , after see that english boat make for the creek where she have be berth on Christmas Eve , he beg Madame Fropot to tell his host not to be uneasy about he , and take no weapon but a ground - ash stick , set forth to play spy upon traitor . as surely as one foot come after the other , he know that every step be towards his grave , if he make a mistake , or even meet bad luck ; but he twirl his light stick in his broad brown hand , and gently invade the french tree around with an old English song of the day when still an Englishman could compose a song . but this make he think of that old - fashioned place Springhaven ; and sadness fall upon he , that the son of its captain should be a traitor . 
 instead of pull across the river , to avoid the splash of oar he scull with a single oar astern , not stand up and wallow in the boat , but sit and cut the figure of 8 with less noise than a skater make . the tide be just at slack - water , this give he quite as much way as he want , and he steer into a little bight of the southern bank , and make fast to a stump , and look about ; for he durst not approach the creek until the light should fade and the man have stow tackle and begin to feed . the vale of the stream afford shelter to a very decent company of tree , which could not have put up with the tyranny of the west wind upon the bare brow of the coast . Most of these tree stand back a little from the margin of high tide , reluctant to see themselves in the water , for fear of the fate of Narcissus . but where that clandestine boat have glide into gloom and greyness , a fosse of Nature 's digging , deeply line with wood and thicket , offer snug harbourage to craft and fraud . 
 Scudamore have take care to learn the up and down of the riverside ere this , and know they now as well as a native , for he have pay many visit to the wound ox , whom he could not lead home quite as soon as he have hop , and he have find a firm place of the little river , easy to cross when the tide be out . with the help of this knowledge he make his way to the creek , without much risk of be observe , and then , as he come to the crest of the thicket , he lie down and watch the interloper . 
 there be the boat , now imbed in the mud , for the little creek be nearly dry by this time . her crew have all land , and kindle a fire , over which hang a kettle full of something good , which they seem to regard with tender interest ; while upon a grassy slope some few yard to the right a trooper 's horse be tether . Carne be not with they , but have cross the creek , as the mark of his boot in the mud declare ; and creep some little way along the thicket , Scudamore descry he walk to and fro impatiently in a little hollow place , where the sailor could not see he . this be on Scudamore 's side of the creek , and scarcely fifty yard below he . " he be wait for an interview with somebody , " think Scuddy : " if I could only get down to that little shanty , perhaps I should hear some fine treason . the wind be the right way to bring I every word he say . " 
 keep in shelter when the traitor walk towards he , and steal on silently when his back be turn , the young sailor manage to ensconce himself unseen in the rough little wattle shed make by his own hand for the shelter of his patient , when a snow - storm have visit the valley of the Canche last winter . nothing could be well fit for his present purpose , inasmuch as his lurking - place could scarcely be descry from below , be shelter by two large tree and a screen of droop ivy , betwixt and below which it look no more than a casual meeting of bush ; while on the other hand the open space beneath it be curve like a human ear , to catch the voice and forward it . 
 while Scudamore be wait here and keenly watch everything , the light begin to falter , and the late gleam of sunset tremble with the breath of spring among the bud and catkin . but the tall man continue his long , firm stride , as if the watch in his pocket be the only thing worth heed . until , as the shadow lose their line and flow into the general depth , Carne spring forward , and a horse and rider burst into the silence of the grass and moss and tree . 
 Carne make a low obeisance , retire a little , and stand hat in hand , until it should please the other man to speak . and Scudamore see , with a start of surprise , that the other man be Napoleon . 
 this great man appear , to the mild english eye that be watch he so intently , of a very different mood and visage from those of their last view of he . then the face , which combine the beauty of Athens with the strength of Rome , be calm , and gentle , and even sweet , with the rare indulgence of a kindly turn . but now , though not disturb with wrath , nor trouble by disappointment , that face   which have help to make his fortune , more than any woman 's have ever do for she   be cast , even if the mould could be the same , in a very different metal . stern force and triumphant vigour shone in every lineament , and the hard bright eye be intent with purpose that would have no denial . 
 Refusing Carne 's aid , he remain on his horse , and stroke his mane for a moment , for he love any creature that serve he well , and be tender of heart when he could afford it ; which add to his power with mankind . 
 " be all your man well out of earshot ? " he ask ; and receive assurance from Carne , go on . " now you will be satisfied at length . you have long be impatient . it be useless to deny it . all be arrange , and all come to a head within three month , and perhaps within two . only four man will know it besides yourself , and three of those four be commander of my fleet . a short time will be occupy in mislead those british ship that beleaguer we ; then we concentrate ours , and command the Channel ; if only for three day , that will be enough . I depart for Italy in three day or in four , to increase the security of the enemy . but I shall return , without a word to any one , and as fast as horse can lay belly to the ground , when I hear that our ship have break out . I shall command the invasion , and it will be for England to find a man to set against I . " 
 " England will have difficulty , sire , in do that , " Carne answer , with a grim smile , for he share the contempt of English Generals then prevalent . " if the continent can not do it , how can the poor England ? once let your Majesty land , and all be over . but what be your Majesty 's order for I ? and where do you propose to make the landing ? " 
 " never ask more than one question at a time , " Napoleon answer , with his usual curtness ; " my order to you be to return at once . prepare your supply for a moment 's notice . through private influence of some fair lady , you have command of the despatch of that officer at Springport , who have the control of the naval force there . ha ! what be that ? I hear a sound up yonder . hasten up , and see if there be any listener . it seem to be there , where the wood grow thick . " 
 Blyth Scudamore , forgetful of himself , have move , and a dry stick crack beneath his foot . Carne , at the Emperor 's glance and signal , spring up the bank , with the help of some bush , draw his sword and pass it between the wattle , then part they and rush through , but see no sign of any one . for Scuddy have slip away , as lightly as a shadow , and keep in a mossy trough , have gain another shelter . here he be oblige to slink in the small possible compass , kneeling upon both knee , and shrug in both shoulder . peer very sharply through an intertwist of sucker   for his shelter be a stool of hazel , throw up to repair the loss of stem , he perceive that the Emperor have move his horse a little when Carne rejoin and reassure he . and this prevent Scudamore from be half so certain as he would have like to be , about further particular of this fine arrangement . 
 " no , " be the next thing he hear Napoleon say whose power of say " no " have make his " yes " invincible ; " no , it be not to be do like that . you will await your instruction , and not move until you receive they from my own hand . make no attempt to surprise anybody or anything , until I have ten thousand man ashore . ten thousand will in six hour attain to fifty thousand , if the shore prove to be as you describe ; so great be the merit of flat - bottom boat . your duty will be to leave the right surprise to we , and create a false one among the enemy . this you must do in the distance of the West , as if my Brest fleet be ravage there , and perhaps destroy Plymouth . you be sure that you can command the signal for this ? " 
 " Sire , I know everything as if I sit among it . I can do as I please with the fair secretary ; and her father be an ancient fool . " 
 " then success be more easy than I wish to have it , because it will not make good esteem . if Nelson come at all , he will be too late , as he generally be too early . London will be in our hand by the middle of July at the late , probably much early , and then Captain Carne shall name his own reward . meanwhile forget not any word of what I say . make the passage no more . you will not be want here . your service be far more important where you be . you may risk the brave Charron , but not yourself . send over by the 20th of May a letter to I , under care of Decres , to be open by no hand but mine , upon my return from Italy , and let the messenger wait for my reply . among they must be the young man who know the coast , and we will detain he for pilot . my reply will fix the exact date of our landing , and then you will despatch , through the mean at your command , any english force that might oppose our landing , to the West , where we shall create a false alarm . be all this clear to you ? you be not stupid . the great point be to do all at the right time , have consideration of the weather . " 
 " all be clear , and shall be carry out clearly , to the good of your Majesty 's humble servant 's power . " 
 Napoleon offer his beautiful white hand , which Carne raise to his lip , and then the Emperor be go . Carne return slowly to the boat , with triumph write prematurely on his dark stern face ; while Scudamore 's brisk and ruddy feature be draw out to a wholly unwonted length , as he quietly make his way out of the covert . 




 CHAPTER LIV 

 in a SAD PLIGHT 

 " how shall I get out of this parole ? or shall I break it , instead of get out ? which shall I think of first , my honour , or my country ? the safety of million , or the pride of one ? an old Roman would have settle it very simply . but a Christian can not do thing so . thank God there be no hurry , for a few day yet ! but I must send a letter to desporte this very night . then I must consider about wait for a week . " 
 Scudamore , unable to think out his case as yet especially after run as if his wind could turn a vane be sit on the bank , to let the river - bed get dark , before he put his leg into the mud to get across . for the tide be out , and the old boat high and dry , and a very weak water remain to be cross   though , like nearly all thing that be weak , it be muddy , but the channel have a moist gleam in the dry spring air , and anybody move would be magnify afar . he feel that it would never do for he , with such a secret , to be catch , and bring to book , or even to awake suspicion of his have it . the ancient roman of whom he have think would have break parole for his country 's sake , and then fall on his sword for his own sake ; but although such behaviour should be much admire , it be nice to read of such thing than to do they . Captain Scuddy be of large and steady nature , and nothing come to he with a jerk or jump perhaps because he be such a jumper and he wear his hat well on the back of his head , because he have no fear of lose it . but for all that he find himself in a sad quandary now . 
 to begin with , his parole be not an ordinary leave , afford by his captor to save themselves trouble ; but a special grace , issue from friendship , and therefore require to be treat in a friendly vein . the liberality of these term have enable he to dwell as a friend among friend , and to overhear all that he have hear . in the balance of perplexity , this weigh heavily against his first impulse to cast away all except paramount duty to his country . in the next place , he know that private feeling urge he as hotly as public duty to cast away all thought of honour , and make off . for what he have hear about the " fair secretary " be rankle bitterly in his deep heart . he recall at this moment the admirable precept of an ancient sage , that in such a conflict of duty the doubter should incline to the course least agreeable to himself , inasmuch as the reason against it be sure to be urge the most feebly in self - council . upon the whole , the question be a nice one for a casuist ; and if there have not be a day to spare , duty to his country must have overridden private faith . 
 however , as there be time to spare , he resolve to reconcile private honour with the sense of public duty ; and return to his room , write a careful letter   of which he keep a copy   to his friend desporte , now on board , and command the flagship of one division of the flotilla . he simply say , without give his reason , that his parole must expire in eight day after date , allow one day for delivery of his letter . then he tell M. Jalais what he have do , and much sorrow be feel in the household . when the time have expire without any answer from Captain Desportes , who mean to come and see he but be unable to do so , Scudamore pack up a few thing needful , expect to be place in custody , and resolve to escape from it , at any risk of life . then he walk to Etaples , a few mile down the river , and surrender himself to the commandant there . this be a rough man as Desportes have say and with more work to do than he could manage . with very little ceremony he place the english prisoner in charge of a veteran corporal , with order to take he to the lock - up in the barrack , and there await further instruction . and then the commandant , in the hurry of his duty , forget all about he . 
 Captain Scuddy now find himself in quarter and under treatment very try to his philosophy . not that the man who have he in charge be purposely unkind to he , only they be careless about his comfort , and have more important work to see to , feed he at their leisure , which do not always coincide with his appetite . much of his food be watery and dirty , and seem to be grow its own vegetable , and sometimes to have overripene they . therefore he begin to lose substance , and his cheek become stranger to the buxom gloss which have be the delight of Madame Fropot . but although they do not feed he well , they take good care of he in other way , afford no chance of exit . 
 but sour fruit often contain good pip . Scudamore 's food be not worth say grace for , and yet a true blessing attend it : forasmuch as the Frenchmen diminish the width of their prisoner , but not of the window . fall away very rapidly , for his mind be fare as badly as his body   have nothing but regret to feed upon , which be no well diet than daisy soup , the gentle Scuddy , who must have become a good wrangler if he have stop at Cambridge , begin to frame a table of cubic measure , and consider the ratio of his body to that window , or rather the aperture thereof . one night , when his supper have be quite forget by everybody except himself , he lie awake thinking for hour and hour about his fair dolly and the wicked Carne , and all the lie he must have tell about she for not a single syllable would Scudamore believe and the next day he find himself become so soft and limp , as well as reduce to his low dimension , that he know , by that just measure which a man take of himself when he have but a shred of it leave , that now he be small enough to go between the bar . and now it be high time to feel that assurance , for the morning bring news that the order for his removal to a great prison far inland be come , and would be carry out the next day . " now or never " be the only chance before he . 
 have make up his mind , he feel refresh , and take his food with gratitude . then , as soon as the night be dark and quiet , and the mighty host for league and league launch into the realm of slumber , spring with both foot well together , as he spring from the tub at Stonnington , Scuddy lay hold of the iron bar which span the window vertically , open the lattice softly , and peep out in quest of sentinel . there be none on duty very near he , though he hear one pacing in the distance . then fling himself on his side , he manage , with some pain to his well - rounded chest , to squeeze it through the narrow slit , and hang from the bar , drop gently . the drop be deep , and in spite of all precaution he roll to the bottom of a grassy ditch . there he lie quiet to rest his bruise , and watch whether any alarm be raise . luckily for he , the moon be down , and no one have observe his venture . crawl on all four along a hollow place , he pass the outpost , and be free . 
 free in mind as well as body , acquit from all claim of honour , and able without a taint upon his name to bear most important news to England , if he could only get away from France . this would be difficult , as he be well aware ; but his plan have be thoroughly consider in his prison , and he set forth to make the good of it . before his escape have be discover , he be under M. Jalais ' roof once more , and find his good friend resolve never to betray he . " but I must not expose you to the risk , " say he , " of heavy fine and imprisonment . I shall have to say good - bye to all your goodness in an hour . and I shall not even allow you to know what road I take , lest you should be blame for send my pursuer on the wrong one . but search my room in three day ' time , and you will find a packet to pay for something which I must steal for the present . I pray you , ask nothing , for your own sake . " 
 they feed he well , and he take three loaf , and a little keg of cider , as well as the bag he have pack before he surrender himself at Etaples . Madame Fropot weep and kiss he , because he remind she of her lost son ; and M. Jalais embrace he , because he be not at all like any son of his . with hearty good wish , and sweet regret , and promise never to forget they , the Englishman quit this kind french house , and become at once a lawful and a likely mark for bullet . 
 the year be now fill with the flurry of Spring , the quick nick of time when a man be astonish at the power of Nature 's memory . a great many thing have be leave behind , mainly for their own good , no doubt some of the animal , some of the vegetable , some of the mineral kingdom even yet none of they start for anarchy . all be content to be pick up and bring on accord to the power of the world , make allowance for the pinch of hard time , and the blow of east wind that have blow themselves out . even the prime grumbler of the earth a bipe , who look up to heaven for that purpose mainly be as nearly content with the present state of thing as he can be with anything , until it be the past . Scudamore only meet one man , but that one declare it be a lovely night ; and perhaps he be easy to please because he have only one leg leave . 
 the star have appear , and the young leaf turn the freshness of their freedom towards they , whether from the crisp impulse of night , or the buoyant influence of kindness in the air . there be very little wind , and it be laden with no sound , except the distant voice of an indefatigable dog ; but Scudamore perceive that when the tide set downwards , a gentle breeze would follow down the funnel of the river . then he draw the ancient boat which he have use before to the mossy bank , and have place his good on board , fetch a pair of oar and the short mast and brown sail from the shed where they be keep , and at the top of a full tide launch forth alone upon his desperate enterprise . 
 there be faint light in the channel , but the bank look very dark ; and just as he cast loose he hear the big clock at Montreuil , a great way up the valley , slowly strike midnight . and he take it for good oman , as he swiftly pass the orchard , that his old friend the ox trot down to the corner , and show his white forehead under a sprawl apple - tree , and give he a salute , though he scarcely could have know he . by this time the breeze be freshen nicely , and Scudamore , cease to row , step the mast , and hoist the brown sail , glide along at a merry pace and with a hopeful heart . pass the mouth of the creek , he see no sign of the traitorous pilot - boat , neither do he meet any other craft in channel , although he see many moor at either bank . but nobody challenge he , as he keep in mid - stream , and brace up his courage for the two great peril still before he ere he gain the open sea . the first of these would be the outpost on either side at Etaples , not far from the barracks where he have be jail , and here no doubt the sentinel would call he to account . but a far great danger would be near the river 's mouth , where a bridge of boat , with a broad gangway for troop , span the tidal opening . 
 there be no bridge across the river yet near the town itself , but , upon challenge from a sentry , Scudamore stand up and wave his hat , and shout in fine nasal and provincial French , " the fisherman , Auguste Baudry , of Montreuil ! " and the man withdraw his musket , and wish he good success . then he pass a sandy island with some man asleep upon it , and begin to fear the daybreak as he near the bridge of boat . this cross the estuary at a narrow part , and have to bear much heavy traffic , be as solid as a float bridge can be . a double row of barge be lash and chain together , between pile drive deep into the river 's bed ; along they a road of heavy plank be lay , rise and fall as they rise and fall with tide , and a drawbridge near the middle of about eight yard ' span must suffice for the traffic of the little river . this fabric be protect from the heavy western surge by the shoal of the bar , and from any english dash by a strong shore battery at either end . at first sight it look like a black wall across the river . 
 the darkness of night be suppose to be deep just before dawn but that depend upon the weather and the sleep of weary man be often in its prime at that time . scudamore   although his life , and all that life hang on from heaven , be quiver at the puff of every breeze   be enable to derive some satisfaction from a yawn , such as go the round of a good company sometimes , like the smell of the supper of sleep that be to come . then he see the dark line of the military bridge , and lower his sail , and unstep his little mast . the strength of the tide be almost spend , so that he could deal with this barrier at his leisure , instead of be hurl against it . 
 unship the rudder and lay one oar astern , Scudamore fetch along the inner row of pile , for he durst not pass under the drawbridge , steer his boat to an inch while he sit with his face to the oar , work noiselessly . then he spy a narrow opening between two barge , and drive his boat under the chain that join they , and after some fending and grope with his hand in the darkness under the plank of the bridge , contrive to get out , when he almost despair of it , through the low tier of the supporter . he be quit of that formidable barrier now , but a faint flush of dawn and of reflection from the sea compel he to be very crafty . instead of push straightway for the bar and hoisting sail which might have bring a charge of grape - shot after he he keep in the gloom of the pile nearly into the left bank , and then hug the shadow it afford . nothing but the desolate sand survey he , and the pile of wrack cast up by gale from the west . then with a stout heart he step his little mast , and the breeze , which freshen towards the rise of the sun , carry he briskly through the tumble of the bar . 
 the young man kneel and say his morning prayer , with one hand still upon the tiller ; for , like most man who have fight well for England , he have staunch faith in the power that have make and guide the nation , until they rebel against it . so far his success have be more than his own unaided hand might work , or his brain with the utmost of its labour second . of himself he cast all thought away , for his love seem lost , and his delight be go ; the shore of his country , if he ever reach they , would contain no pleasure for he ; but the happiness of million might depend upon his life , and first of all that of his mother . 
 all by himself in this frail old tub , he could scarcely hope to cross the Channel , even in the good of weather , and if he should escape the enemy , while his scanty supply hold out . he have nothing to subsist on but three small loaf , and a little keg of cider , and an old tar tub which he have fill with brackish water , upon which the oily curdle of the tar be float . but , for all that , he trust that he might hold out , and retain his wit long enough to do good service . 
 the french coast , trend here for league and league nearly due north and south , be expose to the long accumulate power of a western gale , and the mountain roll of billow that have know no check . if even a smart breeze from the west spring up , his rickety little craft , intend only for inland navigation , would have small chance of live through the tumult . but his first care be to give a wide berth to the land and the many french vessel that be moor or move , whether belong to the great flotilla , or hasten to supply its want . many a time he would have stand forth boldly , as fast as the breeze and tide permit ; but no soon have he shape a course for the open sea than some hostile sail appear ahead and force he to bear away until she be far onward . thus , after a long day of vigilance and care , he be not more than five mile from land when the sun set , and probably far from the english coast than when he set forth in the morning ; because he have stand towards the south of west all day , to keep out of sight of the left wing of the enemy ; and as the straight outline of the coast begin to fade , he suppose himself to be about half - way between the mouth of the Canche and that of the little Authie . 
 watch with the eye of one accustom to the air the last communication of the sun , and his postscript   which , like a lady 's , be the gist of what he mean , Scudamore perceive that a change of weather might come shortly , and must come ere long . there be nothing very angry in the sky , nor even threaten ; only a general uncertainty and wavering ; " I wish you well all round , " instead of " here be a guinea apiece for you . " Scuddy understand it , and resolve to carry on . 
 have no compass , and small knowledge of the coast which lie out of range of the british investment he have make up his mind to lie by for the night , or at any rate to move no more than he could help , for fear of go altogether in the wrong direction . he could steer by the star as great mariner do , when the world be all discovery so long as the star hold their skirt up ; but , on the other hand , those star might lead he into the thick of the enemy . of this , however , he must now take his chance , rather than wait and let the wind turn against he . for his main hope be to get into the track where british frigate , and ship of light draught like his own dear Blonde , be upon patrol , inside of the course of the great war chariot , the ship of the line , that drave heavily . revolve much grist in the mill of his mind , as the sage Ulysses use to do , he find it essential to supply the motive power bodily . one of Madame Fropot 's loaf be very soon disposed of , and a good draught of sound cider help to renew his flagging energy . 
 throughout that night he keep wide - awake , and manage to make fair progress , steering , as well as he could judge , a little to the west of north . but before sunrise the arrear of sleep increase at compound interest , and he lower his sail , and discharge a part of the heavy sum score against he . but when he awake , and glance around he with eye that resent scanty measure , even a sleepy glance suffice to show much more than he wish to see . both sky and sea be overcast with doubt , and alarm , and evil foreboding . a dim streak lie where the land have be , and a white gleam quiver from the sunrise on the wave , as if he be spread water - lily instead of scatter rose . as the earth have its dew that foretell a bright day whenever the dew be of the proper sort , for three kind be establish now so the sea have a flit of bloom in the early morning   neither a colour , nor a sparkle , nor a vapour   which indicate peace and content for the day . but now there be no such fair token upon it , but a heavy and surly and treacherous look , with lump here and there ; as a man who intend to abuse we thrust his tongue to get sharp in his cheek . 
 Scudamore see that his poor old boat , scarcely sound enough for the man of Gotham , be already complain of the uncouth manner of the strange place to which she have be carry in the dark . that be to say , she be begin to groan , at a very quiet slap in the cheek , or even a thoroughly well - mean push in the rear . 
 " you be welcome to groan , if you do not   strain , " exclaim the heartless Captain Scuddy . 
 even as he speak he behold a trickle of water glisten down the forward bend , and then a little rill , and then a spurt , as if a serious leak be sprung . he find the source of this , and contrive to caulk it with a strand of tar rope for the present ; but the sinking of his knife into the forward timber show he that a great part of the bow be rotten . if a head - sea arise , the crazy old frame would be prone to break in bodily , whereas if he attempt to run before the sea , already begin to rise heavily from the west , there be nothing to save the frail craft from be poope . on every side it be a bad lookout , there be every sign of a gale impending , which he could not even hope to weather , and the only chance of rescue lie in the prompt appearance of some british ship . 
 even in this sad plight his courage and love of native land prevail against the acceptance of aid from Frenchmen , if any should approach to offer it . rather would he lie at the bottom of the Channel , or drift about among contend fish , than become again a prisoner with his secret in his mind , and no chance of send it to save his country . as a forlorn hope , he pull out a stump of pencil , and write on the back of a letter from his mother a brief memorandum of what he have hear , and of the urgency of the matter . then take a last draught of his tarry water , he empty the little tub , and fix the head in , after he have enclose his letter . then he fasten the tub to an oar , to improve the chance of its being observe , and lay the oar so that it would float off , in case of the frail boat founder . the other oar he keep at hand to steer with , as long as the boat should live , and to help he to float , when she should have disappear . 
 this be do , he feel easy in his mind , as a man who have prepare for the bad should do . he renew his vigour , which have begin to flag under constant labour and long solitude , by consume another of his loaf , and take almost the last draught of his cider , and after that he battle throughout the dreary day against the increase of bad weather . towards the afternoon he see several ship , one of which he take to be a british frigate ; but none of they espy his poor labour craft , or at any rate show sign of do so . then a pilot - boat run by he , stand probably for Boulogne , and at one time less than a league away . she appear to be English , and he be just about to make signal for aid , when a patch in her foresail almost convince he that she be the traitor of the Canche return . she be probably out of her proper course in order to avoid the investing fleet , and she would run inside it when the darkness fall . well to go to the bottom than invoke such aid ; and he drop the oar with his neckerchief upon it , and face the angry sea again and the lonely despair of impend night . 
 what follow be wipe from his memory for year , and the loss be not much to be regret . when he try to think about it , he find nothing but a roaring of wind and of wave in his ear , a numbness of arm as he labour with the oar thole abaft to keep her heavy head up , a prickly chill in his leg as the brine in the wallowing boat run up they , and then a great wallop and gollop of the element too abundant round he . 
 but at last , when long year should have bring more wisdom , he go poach for supper upon Welsh rabbit . that night all the ghastly time come back , and stand minute by minute before he . every swing of his body , and sway of his head , and swell of his heart , be repeat , the buffet of the billow when the plank be go , the numb grasp of the slippery oar , the sucking down of leg which seem turn into sea - weed , the dashing of dollop of surf into mouth and nose close ever so carefully , and then the last sense of have fight a good fight , but fall away from human arm , into " oh Lord , receive my spirit ! " 




 CHAPTER LV 

 in SAVAGE GUISE 

 " a man come out of the sea to - day , and make I believe we be all find out , " say the gay Charron to the gloomy Carne , a day or two after poor Scudamore 's wreck . " I never behold a more strange - look creature as the owner of our human face divine , as some of your poet have find to say . he have hair from his head all down to here " the little Captain point to a part of his system which would have be large in more tranquil time " and his clothe be so thin that one be able to see through they , and the tint of his face be of roasted sugar , such as it be not to obtain in England . a fine place for fat thing , but not for thin one . " 
 " my friend , you arouse my curiosity , " the master of the feast , which be not a very fat one , answer , as he lazily cross his long leg ; " you be always apprehensive about detection , of which I have cease to entertain all fear , during the short time that remain . this stranger of yours must have be very wet , if he have just appear out of the sea . be it that which make his clothe transparent , like those of the high class of lady ? " 
 " you have not the right understanding of word . he be appear out of the sea , but the wood of a boat be spread between they . he be as dry as I be ; and that be say much , with nothing but this squeeze of bad apple for to drink . " 
 " ah , we shall have well soon . what an impatient throat it be ! well , what become of this transparent man , make of burn sugar , and with hair below his belt ? " 
 " I tell you that you take it in a very different way . but he be a long man , as long almost as you be , and with much less of indolence in the moving of his leg . it be not sincerely wise for I to exhibit myself , in the land . I be watch for a signal from the sea , and a large ship , not of the navy but of merchant , be hang off about a league and delay for her boat . for this reason I prevent he from see I , and that create difficulty of my behold he . but he be go along the basin of the sea towards Springhaven ' Springport ' it be designate by the little Corporal ; ah ha , how the language of the English come leave to he ! " 
 " and how right it come to you , my friend , through your fine self - denial in speak it with I ! it be well for our cause that it be not sincerely wise for you to exhibit yourself in the land , or we should have you make sweet eye at english young lady , and settle down to roast beef and nut - brown ale . Fie , then , my friend ! where be your patriotism ? " 
 " these english young lady , " say the Frenchman , unabashed , " be very fine , in my opinion very fine indeed ; and they could be make to dress , which be sincerely an external thing . by occasion , I have see the very most belle , and charming and adorable of all the creature ever make by the good God . and if she be to say to I , ' Abandon France , my captain , and become my good husband ' and she have the money also the fair France would go to the bottom , and the good ship Charron hoist the Union - jack . " 
 " this become serious : " Carne have long learn to treat his french colleague with a large contempt : " I shall have to confine you in the Yellow Jar , my friend . but what young lady have bewitch you so , and lead your most powerful mind astray ? " 
 " I will tell you . I will make no secret of it . you have none of those lofty feeling , but you will be able in another to comprehend they . it be the daughter of the Coast - Defender Admiral Charles Sir Darling . " 
 " Admiral Darling have two daughter . which of they have the distinguished honour of win the regard of Captain Charron ? " 
 " if there be two , it be so much more well . if I succeed not with one , I will try with the other . but the one who have make I captive for the present be the lady with the dark hair do up like this . " 
 in a moment Charron have put up his hair , which be thick but short , into a double sheaf ; and Carne know at once that it be Faith whose charm have make havoc of the patriotism of his colleague . then he smile and say , " my friend , that be the eld daughter . " 
 " I have some knowledge of the law of England , " the Frenchman continue , complacently ; " the elder will have the most money , and I be not rich , though I be courageous . in the confusion that ensue I shall have the very good chance of commend myself ; and I confide in your honourable feeling to give I the push forward by occasion . Say , be it well conceive , my friend ? we never shall conquer these Englishmen , but we may be triumphant with their lady . " 
 " it be a most excellent scheme of invasion , " Carne answer , with his slow sarcastic smile , " and you may rely on I for what you call the push forward , if a Frenchman ever need it with a lady . but I wish to hear more about that brown man . " 
 " I can tell you no more . but the matter be strange . perhaps he be visit the fat Captain Stoobar . I feel no solicitude concern he with my angel . she would never look twice at such a savage . " 
 but the gallant French Captain miss the mark this time . the strange - look man with the long brown beard quit the shore before he reach the stepping - stone , and make a short - cut across the rabbit - warren , enter the cottage of Zebedee Tugwell , without even stop to knock at the door . the master be away , and so be all the child ; but stout Mrs. Tugwell , with she back to the door , be tend the pot that hang over the fire . at the sound of a footstep she turn round , and her red face grow white than the ash she be stir . 
 " oh , Mr. Erle , be it you , or your ghostie ? " she cry , as she fall against the door of the brick oven . " do ' e speak , for God 's sake , if he have give the power to ' e. " 
 " he have almost take it away again , so far as the english language go , " Erle Twemlow answer , with a smile which be visible only in his eye , through long want of a razor ; " but I be pick up a little . shake hand , Kezia , and then you will know I . though I have not quite recover that art as yet . " 
 " oh , Mr. Erle ! " exclaim Zebedee 's wife , with tear ready to start for his sake and her own , " how many a time I have   have you on my knee , afore I be bless with any of my own , and a bad sort of bless the good of them prove . not that I would listen to a word again ' he . I suppose you never do happen to run again ' my Dan'el , in any of they furrin part , from the way they make the hair grow . I do hear tell of he over to Pebbleridge ; but not likely , so nigh to his own mother , and never come no nigh . and if they furrin part put on the hair so heavily , who could ' a know he to Pebbleridge ? they never be like we be . they would as lief tell a lie as look at you , over there . " 
 in spite of his own long year of trouble , or perhaps by reason of they , Erle Twemlow , eager as he be to get on , listen to the sad tale that seek for his advice , and depart from wisdom as good - nature always do by offer useless counsel counsel that could not be take , and yet be far from be worthless , because it stir anew the fount of hope , towards which the parch affection creep . 
 " but Lor bless I , sir , I never think of you ! " Mrs. Tugwell exclaim , have think out her self . " what do Parson say , and your mother , and Miss Faith ? it must ' a be well than a play to see they . " 
 " not one of they know a word about it yet ; nor anybody in Springhaven , except you , Kezia . you be as good as my nurse , you know ; I have never have a chance of write to they , and I want you to help I to let they know it slowly . " 
 " oh , Mr. Erle , what a lovely young woman your Miss Faith be grow up by now ! some think more of Miss dolly , but , to my mind , you may as well put a mackerel before a salmon , for the sake of the stripe and the glittering . now what can I do to make you decent , sir , for they dud and that hair be barbarious ? my Tabby and Debby will be back in half an hour , and they grow up into young maiden now . " 
 Twemlow explain that after live so long among savage in a burn clime , he have find it impossible to wear thick clothe , and have be rig up in some indian stuff by the tailor of the ship which have rescue he . but now he suppose he must reconcile himself by degree to the old imprisonment . but as for his hair , that should never be touch , unless he be restore to the British Army , and oblige to do as the other do . with many little joke of a homely order , Mrs. Tugwell , regard he still as a child , supply he with her husband 's summer suit of thin duck , which be ample enough not to gall he ; and then she send her daughter with a note to the Rector , beg he to come at seven o'clock to meet a gentleman who wish to see he upon important business , near the plank bridge across the little river .
