Erle write that note , but do not sign it ; and after many year of happy freedom from the pen , his handwriting be so change that his own father would not know it . what he fear be the sudden shock to his good mother ; his father 's nerve be strong , and must be use as buffer . 
 " another trouble , probably ; there be nothing now but trouble , " Mr. Twemlow be think , as he walk unwillingly towards the place appoint . " I wish I could only guess what I can have do to deserve all these trial , as I become less fit to bear they . I would never have come to this lonely spot , except that it may be about shargeloe . everything now be turn upside down ; but the Lord know well , and I must bear it . Sir , who be you ? and what do you want I for ? " 
 at the corner where Miss dolly have rush into the Rector 's open arm so fast , a tall man , clothe in white , be stand , with a staff about eight foot long in his hand . have carry a spear for four year now , Captain Twemlow find no comfort in his native land until he have cut the tall growth in Admiral Darling 's osi bed , and peel it , and shave it to a seven - sided taper . he rest this point in a socket of moss , that it might not be blunt , and then reply : 
 " Father , you ought to know I , although you have grow much stouter in my absence ; and perhaps I be thin than I use to be . but the climate disagree with I , until I get to like it . " 
 " Erle ! do you mean to say you be my boy Erle ? " the Rector be particular about his clothe . " do not   think of touch I . you be hair all over , and I dare say never have a comb . I will not   believe a word of it until you prove it . " 
 " well , mother will know I , if you do not . " the young man answer calmly , have be toss upon so many horn of adventure that none could make a hole in he . " I think that you would have be glad to see I ; and I manage to bring a good many present ; only they be go on to London . they could not be get at , to land they with I ; but Captain Southcombe will be sure to send they . you must not suppose , because I be empty - handed now " 
 " my dear son , " cry the father , deeply hurt , " do you think that your welcome depend upon present ? you have indeed fall into savage way . come , and let I examine you through your hair ; though the light be scarcely strong enough now to go through it . to think that you should be my own Erle , alive after such a time , and with such a lot of hair ! only , if there be any palm - oil on it this be my last new coat but one . " 
 " no , father , nothing that you ever can have dream of . something that will make you a bishop , if you like , and I a member of the House of Lords . but I do not find it out myself which make success more certain . " 
 " they have teach you some great truth , my dear boy . the man who begin a thing never get on . but I be so astonished that I know not what I say . I ought to have thank the Lord long ago . have you get a place without any hair upon it large enough for I to kiss you ? " 
 Erle Twemlow , whose hand in spite of all adventure tremble a little upon his spear , lift his hat and find a smooth front , sure to be all the smoother for a father 's kiss . 
 " let we go home , " say the old man , try to exclude all excitement from his throat and heart ; " but you must stay outside until I come to fetch you . I feel a little anxious , my dear boy , as to how your dear mother will get over it . she have never be strong since the bad news come about you . and somebody else have to be consider . but that must stand over till to - morrow . " 




 CHAPTER LVI 

 the silver voice 

 many shrewd writer have observe that Britannia have a special luck which the more devout call Providence in hold her own , against not only her true and lawful enemy , but even those of her own bosom who labour most to ruin she . and truly she have need of all her fortune now , to save she from the skulk traitor , as well as the rag adversary . 
 " now I will have my revenge , " say Carne , " on all who have outrage and plunder I . crow carrion - crow I will turn they into owl without a nest . prowl owl , to come blink even now at the last of my poor relic ! Charron , what do that fellow say to old Jerry , the day I tie the dog up ? " 
 " he say , my dear friend , that he miss from the painting which he have take to his house the most precious of they all the picture of your dear grandmother , by a man whose name it be hard to pronounce , but a Captain in the British Army , very much fond of belove and paint all the most beautiful lady ; and since he have paint the mother of Vash Vash the man that conquer England in America all his work be go up to a wonderful price , and old Sheray should have one guinea if he would exhibit to he where to find it . Meedle or Beedle he have set his heart on get it . he declare by the good God that he would have it , and that you have get it under a tombstone . " 
 " a sample of their persecution ! you know that I have never see it , nor even hear of the Captain Middleton who go on his roving from Springhaven . and , again , about my own front - door , or rather the door of my family for some four century , because it be carve as they can not carve now , it be put into that vile indenture . I care very little for my ancestor benight Britons of the county type but these thing be personal insult to I . I seldom talk about they , and I will not do so now . " 
 " my captain , you should talk much about it . that would be the good relief to your extensive mind . revenge be not of the bright french nature ; but the sky of this island procreate it . my faith ! how I would rage at England , if it be not for the people , and their daughter ! we shall see ; in a few day more we shall astonish the fat John Bull ; and then his little kitten what do you call they ? calf of an ox , will come run to we . " 
 " enough of your foolish talk , " say Carne . " the woman be as resolute as the man . even when we have take London , not an english woman will come near we , until all the man have yield . go down to your station and watch for the boat . I expect an important despatch to - night . but I can not stay here for the chance of it . I have business in Springhaven . " 
 his business in Springhaven be to turn young love to the base use , to make a maiden   rash and flighty , but not as yet dishonourable   a traitor to her friend and father - land , and most of all to her own father . he have try to poison Dolly 's mind with dos of social nonsense in which he believe about as much as a quack believe in his own pill but his main reliance now be place in his hold upon her romantic heart , and in her vague ambition . pure and faithful love be not to be expect from his nature ; but he have invest in dolly all the affection he could spare from self . he have labour long , and suffer much , and the red crown of his work be nigh . 
 rid slowly down the hill about half a mile from the village , Carne see a tall man come towards he with a firm , deliberate walk . the stranger be dress very lightly , and wear a hat that look like a tobacco leaf , and carry a long wand in his hand , as if he be go to keep order in church . these thing take the eye afar , but at short range become as nothing , compare with the aspect of the man himself . this be grand , with its steadfast gaze no stare , but a calm and kind regard its large tranquillity and power of receive without believe the word of man ; and most of all in the depth of expression reserve by experience in the forest of its hair . 
 Carne be about to pass in silent wonder and uneasiness , but the other gently lay the rod across his breast and stop he , and then wait for he to ask the reason why . 
 " have you any business with I , good sir ? " Carne would have speak rudely , but see that rudeness would leave no mark upon a man like this . " if so , I must ask you to be quick . and perhaps you will tell I who you be . " 
 " I think that you be Caryl Carne , " say the strange , not unpleasantly , but as if it matter very little who be Caryl Carne , or whether there be any such existence . 
 Carne star fiercely , for he be of touchy temper ; but he might as well have star at a bucket of water in the hope of derange its tranquillity . " you know I . but I do not   know you , " he answer at last , with a jerk of his rein . 
 " be in no hurry , " say the other , mildly ; " the weather be fine , and time plentiful . I hope to have much pleasant knowledge of you . I have the honour to be your first cousin , Erle Twemlow . shake hand with your kinsman . " 
 Carne offer his hand , but without his usual grace and self - possession . Twemlow take it in his broad brown palm , in which it seem to melt away , firm though it be and muscular . 
 " I be go up to call on you , " say Twemlow , who have acquire a habit of speak as if he mean all the world to hear . " I feel a deep interest in your fortune , and hope to improve they enormously . you shall hear all about it when I come up . I have pass four year in the wild of Africa , where no white man ever trod before , and I have find out thing no white man know . we call those people savage , but they know a great deal more than we do . shall I call to - morrow , and have a long talk ? " 
 " I fear , " reply Carne , who be curse his luck for bring this fellow home just now , " that I shall have no time for a week or two . I be engage upon important business now , which will occupy my whole attention . let I see ! you be stay at the rectory , I suppose . the good plan will be for I to let you know when I can afford the pleasure of receive you . in a fortnight , or three week at the late " 
 " very well . I be never in a hurry . and I want to go to London to see about my thing . but I dare say you will not object to my roving about the old castle now and then . I love the old place as a boy , and I know every crick and cranny and snake - hole in it . " 
 " how glad they must have be to see you restore from the dead , and with such rich discovery ! but you must be more careful , my good cousin , and create no more anxiety . glad as I shall be to see you , when time allow that indulgence , I must not encourage you to further roving , which might end in your final disappearance . two boar - hound , exceedingly fierce and strong , and compel by my straiten circumstance to pick up their own living , be at large on my premise night and day , to remonstrate with my creditor . we fear that they eat a man last night , who have steal a valuable picture , and be eager for another by the same distinguished artist . his boot and hat be find unhurt ; but of his clothe not a shred remain , to afford any pattern for enquiry . what would my feeling be if Aunt Maria arrive hysterically in the pony - carriage , and at great personal risk enquire " 
 " I fear no dog , " say Erle Twemlow , without any flash of anger in his steadfast eye . " I can bring any dog to lick my foot . but I fear any man who sink low than a dog , by obtain a voice and speak lie with it . if you wish , for some reason of your own , to have nought to do with I , you should have say so ; and I might have respect you afterwards . but flimsy excuse and trumpery lie belong to the low race of savage , who live near the coast , and have be teach by Frenchmen . " 
 Erle Twemlow stand , as he leave off speak , just before the shoulder of Carne 's horse , ready to receive a blow , if offer , but without preparation for return it . but Carne , for many good reason which occur to his mind long afterwards control his fury , and console his self - respect by repay in kind the contempt he receive . 
 " well do , Mr. Savage ! " he say , with a violent effort to look amiable . " you and I be accustom to the opposite extreme of society , and the less we meet , the well . when a barbarian insult I , I take it as a foul word from a clodhopper , which do not hurt I , but may damage his own self - respect , if he cherish such an illusion . perhaps you will allow I to ride on , while you curb your very natural curiosity about a civilized gentleman . " 
 Twemlow make no answer , but look at he with a gentle pity , which infuriate Carne more than the keen insult . he lash his horse , and gallop down the hill , while his cousin stroke his beard , and look after he with sorrow . 
 " everything go against I now , " think Caryl Carne , while he put up his horse and set off for the Admiral 's roundhouse . " I want to be cool as a cucumber , and that insolent villain have make pepper of I . what devil send he here at such a time ? " 
 for the moment it do not cross his mind that this man of lofty rudeness be the long - expect lover of Faith Darling , and therefore in some sort entitle to a voice about the doing of the young sister . by many quiet sneer , and much expressive silence , he have set the brisk dolly up against the quiet Faith , as a man who understand fowl nature can set even two young pullet pull each other 's hackle out . 
 " so you be come at last ! " say dolly . " no one who know I keep I wait , because I be not accustomed to it . I expect to be call for at any moment , by matter of real importance not like this . " 
 " your mind be a little disturbed , " reply Carne , as he take her hand and kiss it , with less than the proper rapture ; " be it because of the brown and hairy man just return from Africa ? " 
 " not altogether . but that may be something . he be not a man to be laugh at . I wish you could have see my sister . " 
 " I would rather see you ; and I have no love of savage . he be my first cousin , and that afford I a domestic right to object to he . as a brother - in - law I will have none of he . " 
 " you forget , " answer dolly , with a flash of her old spirit , which he be subdue too heavily , " that a matter of that sort depend upon we , and our father , and not upon the gentleman . if the gentleman do not   like it , they can always go away . " 
 " how can they go , when they be chain up like a dog ? woman may wander from this one to that , because they have nothing to bind they ; but a man be of steadfast material . " 
 " Erle Twemlow be , at any rate though it be hard to see his material through his hair ; but that must come off , and I mean to do it . he be the well - natured man I have ever yet know , except one ; and that one have get nothing to shave . man never seem to understand about their hair , and the interest we feel concern it . but it do not matter very much , compare to their high principle . " 
 " that be where I carry every vote , of whatever sex you please " Carne see that this girl must be humour for the moment . " anybody can see what I be . straightforward , and ready to show my tooth . why should an honest man live in a bush ? " 
 " faith like it very much ; though she always use to say that it do seem so unchristian . could you manage to come and meet he , Caryl ? we shall have a little dinner on Saturday , I believe , that every one may see Erle Twemlow . his beloved parent will be there , who be go quite wild about he . Father will be at home for once ; and the Marquis of Southdown , and some officer , and Captain Stubbard and his wife will come , and perhaps my brother Frank , who admire you so much . you shall have an invitation in the morning . " 
 " such delight be not for I , " Carne answer , with a superior smile ; " unhappily my time be too important . but perhaps these festivity will favour I with the chance of a few word with my darling . how I long to see she , and how little chance I get ! " 
 " because , when you get it , you spend three - quarter of the time in argue , and the rest in find fault . I be sure I go as far as anybody can ; and I will not   take you into my father 's Roundhouse , because I do not   think it would be proper . " 
 " lady alone understand such subject ; and a gentleman be thankful that they do . I be quite content to be outside the Roundhouse so call because it be square , perhaps though the wind be go back to the east again , as it always do now in an english summer , accord to a man who have study the subject Zebedee Tugwell , the captain of the fleet . dolly , beloved , and most worthy to be more so , clear your bright mind from all false impression , whose only merit be that they be your , and allow it to look clearly at a matter of plain sense . " 
 she be pleased to have compliment pay to her mind , even more than to her body because there be no doubt about the merit of the latter and she say : " that be very nice . go on . " 
 " well , beauty , you know that I trust you in everything , because of your very keen discretion , and freedom from stupid little prejudice . I have be surprise at time , when I think of it in your absence , that any one so young , who have never be through any course of political economy , should be able to take such a clear view of subject which be far beyond the intellect of even the old lady . but it must be your brother ; no doubt he have help you to " 
 " not he ! " cry the innocent dolly , with fine pride ; " I rather look down upon his reasoning power ; though I never could make such a pretty tink of rhyme like the bell of the sheep when the ground be full of turnip . " 
 " he approve of your elevated view , " say Carne , look as grave as a crow at a church clock ; " they may not have come from he , because they be your own , quite as much as his poetry be his . but he perceive their truth , and he know that they must prevail . in a year or two we shall be wonder , sweet dolly , when you and I sit side by side , as the stupid old King and Queen do now , that it ever have be possible for narrow - minded nonsense to prevail as it do until we rise above it . we shall be admire as the benefactor , not of this country only , but of the whole world . " 
 Miss dolly be fairly endow with common - sense , but often fail to use it . she would fain have say now , " that sound wonderfully fine ; but what do it mean , and how be we to work it ? " but unluckily she could not bring herself to say it . and when million be fool by the glibness of one man even in these day of wisdom who can be surprised at a young maid 's weakness ? 
 " you wish I to help you in some way , " she say ; " your object be sure to be good ; and you trust I in everything , because of my discretion . then why not tell I everything ? " 
 " you know everything , " Carne reply , with a smile of affection and sweet reproach . " my object be the large that a man can have ; and until I see you , there be not the least taint of self - interest in my proceeding . but now it be not for the universe alone , for the grandeur of humanity , and the triumph of peace , that I have to strive , but also for another little somebody , who have come I be ashamed to say to outweigh all the rest in the balance of my too tender heart . " 
 this be so good , and so well deliver , that the lady of such love could do no less than vouchsafe a soft hand and a soft glance , instead of pursue hard reason . 
 " Beauty , it be plain enough to you , though it might not be so to stupid people , " Carne continue , as he press her hand , and vanquish the doubt of her enquire eye with the strength of his resolute gaze , " that bold measure be sometimes the only wise one . many english girl would stand aghast to hear that it be needful for the good of England that a certain number , a strictly limited number , of Frenchmen should land upon this coast . " 
 " I should rather think they would ! " cry dolly ; " and I would be one of they you may be quite sure of that . " 
 " for a moment you might , until you come to understand . " Carne 's voice always take a silver tone when his word be big with roguery ; as the man who be tout for his neighbour 's bee strike the frying - pan softly at first , to tone the pulsation of the murmur mob . " but every safeguard and every guarantee that can be demand by the wild prudence will be afford before a step be take . in plain truth , a large mind be almost shocked at such deference to antique prejudice . but the feeling of old woman must be consider ; and our measure be fence with such security that even the most timid must be satisfy . there must be a nominal landing , of course , of a strictly limited number , and they must be secure for a measurable period from any ill - judge interruption . but the great point of all be to have no blood - guiltiness , no outbreak of fanatic native against benefactor come in the garb of peace . a truly noble offer of the olive - branch must not be misinterpret . it be the fine idea that have ever be conceive ; and no one possess a liberal mind can help admire the perfection of this plan . for the sake of this country , and the world , and ourselves , we must contribute our little share , darling . " 
 Carne , with the grace of a lofty protector , as well as the face of an ardent lover , draw the bewildered maiden towards he , and tenderly kiss she pretty forehead , hold up his hand against all protest . 
 " it be useless to dream of draw back , " he continue ; " my beauty , and my poor outcast self , be in the same boat , and must sail on to success such success as there never have be before , because it will bless the whole world , as well as secure our own perfect happiness . you will be more than the Queen of England . statue of you will be set up everywhere ; and where could the sculptor find such another model ? I may count upon your steadfast heart , I know , and your wonderful quickness of perception . " 
 " yes , if I could only see that everything be right . but I feel that I ought to consult somebody of more experience in such thing . my father , for instance , or my brother Frank , or even Mr. Twemlow , or perhaps Captain Stubbard . " 
 " if you have think of it a little soon , and allow I time to reason with they , " Carne reply , with a candid smile , " that would have be the very thing I should have wish , as take a great responsibility from I . but alas , it would be fatal now . the main object now be to remove all chance of an ill - judge conflict , which would ruin all good feeling , and cost many valuable life , perhaps even that of your truly gallant father . no , my dolly , you must not open your beautiful lip to any one . the peace and happiness of the world depend entirely upon your discretion . all will be arrange to a nicety , and a happy result be certain . only I must see you , about some small point , as well as to satisfy my own craving . on Saturday you have that dinner party , when somebody will sit by your side instead of I . how miserably jealous I shall be ! when the gentleman be at their wine , you must console I by slip away from the lady , and come to the window of the little room where your father keep his paper . I shall quit everything and watch there for you among the shrub , when it grow dark enough . " 




 CHAPTER LVII 

 below the line 

 of the British Admirals then on duty , Collingwood alone , so far as now appear , have any suspicion of Napoleon 's real plan . 
 " I have always have an idea that Ireland alone be the object they have in view , " he write in July , 1805 , " and still believe that to be their ultimate destination that they   i. e. , the Toulon fleet   will now liberate the Ferrol squadron from Calder , make the round of the bay , and take the Rochefort people with they , appear off Ushant , perhaps with 34 sail , there to be join by 20 more . Cornwallis collect his out - squadron may have 30 and upwards . this appear to be a probable plan ; for unless it be to bring their great fleet and army to some point of service some rash attempt at conquest they have be only subject they to chance of loss ; which I do not believe the Corsican would do , without the hope of an adequate reward . this summer be big with event . " 
 this be write to Lord Nelson upon his return to Europe , after chase that Toulon fleet to the West Indies and back again . and a day or two later , the same Vice - Admiral write to his friend very clearly , as before : 
 " truly glad will I be to see you , and to give you my good opinion on the present state of affair , which be in the high degree intricate . but reasoning on the policy of the present french government , who never aim at little thing while great object be in view , I have consider the invasion of Ireland as the real mark and butt of all their operation . the flight to the West Indies be to take off the naval force , which be the great impediment to their undertaking . the Rochefort squadron 's return confirm I . I think they will now collect their force at Ferrol which Calder tell I be in motion pick up those at Rochefort , who , I be tell , be equally ready , and will make they above thirty sail ; and then , without go near Ushant or the Channel fleet , proceed to Ireland . detachment must go from the Channel fleet to succour Ireland , when the Brest fleet 21 I believe of they will sail , either to another part of Ireland , or up the Channel a sort of force that have not be see in those sea , perhaps ever . " 
 Lord Nelson just lately have suffer so much from the disadvantage of not " follow his own head , and so be much more correct in judgment than follow the opinion of other , " that his head be not at all in a receptive state ; and like all who have doubt about be right , and find the doubt wrong , he be harden into the merit of his own conclusion . " why have I go on a goose - chase ? " he ask ; " because I have twice as many ear as eye . " 
 this be so , he stick fast to the conviction which he have nourish all along , that the scheme of invasion be a sham , intend to keep the british fleet at home , while the enemy ravage our commerce and colony afar . and by this time the country , grow heartily tired of groundless alarm and suspend menace , be begin to view with contempt a camp that be wear out its own encampment . little be it dream in the sweet rose garden of England , or the fragrant hay - field , that the curl of blue smoke while the dinner be cook , the call of milkmaid , the haymaker 's laugh , or the whinny of Dobbin between his mouthful , might be turn   ere a man of good appetite be full   into foreign shout , and shriek of English maiden , crackle homestead , and blaze stack - yard , blare of trumpet , and roar of artillery , cold flash of steel , and the soft warm trickle of a father 's or a husband 's blood . 
 but the chance of this hang upon a hair just now . one hundred and sixty thousand soldier the fine son of Mars that demon have ever yet beget fifteen thousand warlike horse , ready to devour all the oats of England , cannon that never could be count   because it be not always safe to go near they , and ship that no reckoner could get to the end of , because he be always begin again . 
 who be there now to meet all these ? Admiral Darling , and Captain Stubbard , and Zebedee Tugwell   if he find they intrusive , and Erle Twemlow , as soon as he get his thing from London . there might be a few more to come forward , as soon as they see the necessity ; but Mr. John Prater could not be rely on because of the trade he might expect to drive ; Mr. Shargeloes have never turn up again ; and as for poor Cheeseman , he have lose himself so entirely now that he make up the weight of a pound of sausage , in the broad summer light , with a tallow candle . like other concern in this history , he have jump at the star , and crack his head against a beam , in manner to be record . 
 the country be destitute thus of defender for even Stubbard 's battery be not half man , because it have never be want the plan of invasion be thrive well , in all but one particular . the fleet under Villeneuve be at large , so be that under Lallemand , who have supersede Missiessy , so be the force of Gravina and another spanish admiral ; but Ganteaume have fail to elude the vigilance of that hero of storm , Cornwallis . Napoleon arrive at Boulogne on the 3rd of August , and review his troop , in a line on the beach some eight mile long . a fine sight he have never see , and he write in his pride : " the English know not what be hang over their ear . if we be master of the passage for twelve hour , England be conquer . " but all depend on Villeneuve , and happily he could not depend upon his nerve . 
 meanwhile the young man who be charge with a message which he would gladly have die to discharge be far away , eat out his heart in silence , or vainly relieve it with unknown word . at the last gasp , or after he cease to gasp for the time , and be drift insensible , but happily with his honest face still upward , a Dutchman , keep a sharp lookout for english cruiser , espy he . he be take on board of a fine bark bind from Rotterdam for Java , with order to choose the track least infest by that ravenous shark Britannia . Scudamore be treat with the warm kindness and the most gentle attention , for the captain 's wife be on board , and her tender heart be move with compassion . yet even so , three day pass by with no more knowledge of time on his part than the face of a clock have of its hand ; and more than a week be go before both body and mind be in tone and tune again . by that time the stout dutch bark , have give a wide berth to the wakes of war , be forty league west of Cape Finisterre , under order to touch no land short of the cape , except for fresh water at St. Jago . 
 Blyth Scudamore be bl with that natural feeling of preference for one 's own kin and country which the much large mind of the present period flout , and scout as barbarous . happily our periodical blight be expire , like cuckoo - spit , in its own bubble ; and the time be return when the bottle - blister will not be accept as the good ripe peach . Scudamore be of the time that have be   and perhaps may be come again , in the tooth and the jaw of universal suffrage , of resolute , vigorous , loyal people , hold fast all that God give they , and decline to be lead by the tail , by a gentleman who tack their tail on as his handle . 
 this certainty of belong still to a firm and substantial race of man   whose extinction would leave the world nothing to breed from   make the gallant Scudamore so anxious to do his duty , that he could not do it . why do we whistle to a horse overburden with a heavy load uphill ? that his mind may grow tranquil , and his ear train forward , his eye lose their nervous contraction , and a fine sense of leisure pervade he . but if he have a long hill to surmount , with none to restrain his ardour , the sense of duty grow strong than any consideration of his own good , and the good man have not the conscience needful to understand half his emotion . 
 thus the sense of duty keep Blyth Scudamore full of misery . every day carry he far from the all - important issue ; and the chance of return in time grow faint , and fainter at every sunset . the kindly Dutchman and his wife be aware of some burden on his mind , because of its many groan sally while astray from judgment . but as soon as his wit be clear again , and his body fit to second they , Blyth see that he could not crave their help , against the present interest of their own land . Holland be at enmity with England , not of its own accord , but under the pressure of the man who work so hard the great european mangle . Captain Van Oort have pick up some English , and his wife could use tongue and ear in French , while Scudamore afford himself and they some little diversion by attempt in Dutch . be of a wonderfully happy nature for happiness be the great wonder in this world he could not help many a wholesome laugh , in spite of all the project of Napoleon . 
 little thing seldom jump into bigness , till a man set his microscope at they . accord to the everlaste harmony , Blyth have not get a penny , because he have not get a pocket to put it in . a pocketful of money would have send he to the bottom of the sea , that breezy April night , when he drift for hour , with eye full of salt , twinkle feeble answer to the twinkle of the star . but he have make himself light of his little cash leave , in his preparation for a slow decease , and perhaps the fish have pay tribute with it to the Caesar of this Millennium . Captain Van Oort be a man of his inch in length , but in breadth about one - third more , be thicken and spread by the year that do this to a body contain a christian mind . " you will never get out of they , " say Mrs. Van Oort , when he get into her husband 's large smallclothe ; but he who have often jump out of a tub feel no despair about jump out of two . in every way Scudamore hop for the good which be the only right course for a man who have do his own good , and be helpless . 
 keep out of the usual track of commerce , because of the privateer and other pest of war waylay it , they meet no sail of either friend or foe until they cast anchor at St. Jago . here there be no ship bind for England , and little chance of find one , for week or perhaps for month to come . the good chance of get home lie clearly in go yet far away from home , and so he stick to the good ship still , and they weigh for the Cape on the 12th of May . everything set against poor Scuddy wind , and wave , and the power of man . it have be the 16th of April when he be rescue from the devouring sea ; some day have be spend by the leisurely Dutchman in provide fresh supply , and the stout bark 's favourite maxim seem to be , " the more haste the less speed . " baffle wind and a dead calm help to second this philosophy , and the first week of June be past before they swing to their mooring in Table Bay . 
 " what chance be there now of my do any good ? " the young Englishman ask himself , bitterly . " this place be again in the hand of the Dutch , and the english ship stand clear of it , or only receive supply by stealth . I be friendless here , I be penniless ; and bad of all , if I even get a passage home , there will be no home left . too late ! too late ! what use be there in strive ? " 
 tear stand in his blue eye , which be gentle as a lady 's ; and his forehead   usually calm and smooth and ready for the flicker of a very pleasant smile   be as grave and determine as the brow of Caryl Carne . Captain Van Oort would have lend he 500 guilde with the great pleasure , but Scudamore would not take more than fifty , to support he until he could obtain a ship . then with hearty good - will , and life - long faith in each other , the two man part , and Scudamore 's heart be uncommonly low for a substance that be not a " Jack - in - the - box " as he watch from the shore the slow fading into dream - land of the Katterina . 
 nothing except patriotic feeling may justify a man , who have do no harm , in long - continue misery . the sense of violent bodily pain , or of perpetual misfortune , or of the baseness of all in whom he trust , and other steady influx of many - fountaine sorrow , may wear he for a time , and even fetch his spirit low than the more vicarious woe can do . but the firm conviction that the family of man to which one belong , and be proud of belong , have fall into the hand of traitor , eloquent liar , and vile hypocrite , and can not escape without crawl in the dust this produce a large deep gloom , and a crush sense of doom beyond philosophy . Scudamore could have endure the loss and the disillusion of his love pure and strong as that power have be but the ruin of his native land would turn his lively heart into a lump of stone . 
 for two or three day he rove about among the people of the water - side boatman , pilot , shipping agent , store - keeper , stevedore , crimp , or any other likely to know anything to help he . some of these could speak a little English , and many have some knowledge of French ; but all shake their head at his eagerness to get to England . " you may wait week , or you may wait month , " say the one who know most of the subject ; " we be very jealous of the english ship . that country swallow up the sea so . it have be forbid to supply the english ship ; but for plenty money it be do sometimes ; but the finger must be place upon the nose , and upon the two eye what you call the guinea ; and in six hour where be they ? swallow up by the mist from the mountain . no , sir ! if you have the great money , it be very difficult . but if you have not that , it be impossible . " 
 " I have not the great money ; and the little money also have escape from a quicksand in the bottom of my pocket . " 
 " then you will never get to England , sir , " this gentleman answer , pleasantly ; " and unless I have be tell thing too severely , the good man that life have well not go there , without a rock of gold in his pocket grand enough to fill a thousand quicksand . " 
 Scudamore lift the relic of his hat , and go in search of some other Job 's comforter . instead of a passage to England , he see in a straight line before he the only journey which a mortal may take without pay his fare . 
 to save himself from this gratuitous tour , he earn a little money in a porter 's gang , till his quick step rouse the indignation of the rest . with the lofty perception of the right of man , they turn he out of that employment   for the one ' sacred principle of labour " be to play , and he , understand now the nature , of democracy , perceive that of all the many short - cut to starvation , the one with the few elbow to it be to work . 
 while he be meditate upon these point which person of big word love to call " question of political economy " his hat , now become a patent ventilator , sit accord to custom on the back of his head , expose his large calm forehead , and the kind honesty of his countenance . then he start a little , for his nerve be not quite as strong as when they have good feeding , at the sudden sense of be scrutinize by the most piercing gaze he have ever encounter . 
 the stranger be an old man of tall spare frame , wear a shovel - hat and long black gown draw in with a belt , and around his bare neck be a steel chain support an ebony cross . with a smile , which display the firm angle of his face , he address the young man in a language which Scudamore could not understand , but believe to be portuguese . 
 " Thy word I be not able to understand . but the Latin tongue , as it be pronounce in England , I be able to interpret , and to speak , not too abundantly . " Scudamore speak the good Latin he could muster at a moment 's notice , for he see that this gentleman be a catholic priest , and probably therefore of good education . 
 " art thou , then , an Englishman , my son ? " the stranger reply , in the same good tongue . " from thy countenance and walk , that opinion stand fast in my mind at first sight of thee . every Englishman be to I beloved , and every Frenchman unfriendly as many , at least , as now govern the state . Father Bartholomew be my name , and though most man here be heretical , among the faithful I avail sufficiently . what saith the great venusian ? ' in straiten fortune quit thyself as a man of spirit and of mettle . ' I find thee in straiten fortune , and would gladly enlarge thee , if that which thou art do be pleasing to the God omnipotent . " 
 after a few more word , he lead the hapless and hungry Englishman to a quiet little cot which overlook the noble bay , and itself be overlook by a tall flag - staff bear the colour of Portugal . here in the first place he regale his guest with the flank of a kid serve with cucumber , and fruit gather early , and some native wine , scarcely good enough for the venusian bard , but as rich as ambrosia to Scudamore . then he supply he with the fine tobacco that ever ascend in spiral incense to the cloud - compelling jove . at every soft puff , away fly the blue - devil , pagan , or Christian , or even scientific ; and the brightness of the sleep - forbid eye return , and the sweetness of the smile so long go hence in dread of trespass . Father Bartholomew , neither eating , drinking , nor smoking , till the sun should set for this be one of his fast - day be heartily pleased with his guest 's good cheer , and smile with the large benevolence which a lean face express with more decision than a plump and jolly one . " and now , my son , " he begin again , in Latin more fluent and classical than the sailor could compass after Cicero throw by , " thou hast return thank to Almighty God , for which I the more esteem thee . Oblige I , therefore , if it irk thee not , among smoke of the genial Nicotium , by tell thy tale , and explain what hard necessity hath drive thee to these distant shore . fear not , for thou seest a lover of England , and hater of France the infidel . " 
 then Scudamore , sometimes hesitate and laugh at his own bad Latin , tell as much of his story as be needful , strive especially to make clear the importance of his swift return , and his fear that even so it would be too late . 
 " Man may believe himself too late , but the Lord ariseth early , " the good priest answer , with a smile of courage refresh the heart of the Englishman . " behold how the hand of the Lord be steadfast over those who serve he ! to - morrow I might have be far away ; to - day I be in time to help thee . whilst thou wert feeding , I receive the signal of a swift ship for Lisbon , whose captain be my friend , and would neglect nothing to serve I . this night he will arrive , and with favourable breeze , which have set in this morning , he shall spread his sail again to - morrow , though he mean to linger perhaps for three day . be of good cheer , my son ; thou shalt sail to - morrow . I will supply thee with all that be needful , and thank God for a privilege so great . thou shalt have money as well for the passage from Lisbon to England , which be not long . remember in thy prayer for thou art devout that old man , Father Bartholomew . " 




 CHAPTER lviii 

 in early MORN 

 one Saturday morning in the month of August , an hour and a half before sunrise , Carne walk down to the big yew - tree , which stand far enough from the brink of the cliff to escape the salt , and yet near enough to command an extensive sea - view . this be the place where the young shoemaker , belong to the race of shank , have be scar so sadly that he lose his sweetheart , some two year and a half ago ; and this be the tree that have be love by painter , especially the conscientious Sharples , a pupil of Romney , who study the nick and the trick of the bole , and the many fantastic fret of time , with all the love care which ensure the truth of his simple and powerful portrait . but Sharples have long be away in the West ; and Carne , have taste for no art except his own , have despatch his dog Orso , the fiercer of the pair , at the only son of a brush who have lately make ready to encamp against that tree ; upon which he decamp , and go over the cliff , with a loss of much personal property . 
 the tree look ghostly in the shady light , and gaunt armstretch of depart darkness , go as if it have not sleep its sleep out . now be the time when the day be afraid of come , and the night unsure of go , and a large reluctance to acknowledge any change keep everything wait for another thing to move . what be the use of light and shadow , the fuss of the morning , and struggle for the sun ? fair darkness have fill all the gap between they , and why should they be sever'd into single life again ? for the gladness of daybreak be not come yet , nor the pleasure of see the way again , the lifting of the darkness leave heaviness beneath it , and if a rashly early bird flop down upon the grass , he can not count his distance , but quiver like a moth . 
 " pest on this abominable early work ! " mutter Carne with a yawn , as he grope his way through the deep gloom of black foliage , and enter the hollow of the ancient trunk ; " it be all very well for sailor , but too hard upon a quiet gentleman . very likely that fellow will not   come for two hour . what a cursed uncomfortable maggoty place ! but I will have put the sleep he have rob I of . " he stretch his long form on the rough bench inside , gather his cloak around he , and rouse the dull echo of the honey - comb hollow with long loud snore . 
 " awake , my vigilant commander , and behold I ! Happy be the landsman , to whom the star bring sleep . I have not sleep for three night , and the fruit be here for you . " 
 it be the lively voice of Renaud Charron ; and the rosy fan of the dawn , unfold over the sea and the gray rock , glance with a flutter of shade into the deep - rib tree . affect a lofty indifference , Carne , who have a large sense of his own dignity , rise slowly and come out into the well light . " sit down , my dear friend , " he say , take the sealed packet ; " there be bread and meat here , and a bottle of good Macon . you be nearly always hungry , and you must be starve now . " 
 Charron perceive that his mouth be offer employment at the expense of his eye ; but the kernel of the matter be his own already , and he smile to himself at the mystery of his chief . " in this matter , I should implore the tree to crush I , if my father be an Englishman , " he think ; " but every one to his taste ; it be no affair of mine . " just as he be get on good term with his refreshment , Carne come back , and watch he with a patronising smile . 
 " you be the brother of my toil , " he say , " and I will tell you as much as it be good for you to know . a few hour now will complete our enterprise . Napoleon be at Boulogne again , and even he can scarcely restrain the rush of the spirit he have provoke . the first Division be on board already , with a week 's supply , and a thousand horse , ready to sail when a hand be hold up . the hand will be hold up at my signal , and that I shall trust you to convey to - night , as soon as I have settle certain matter . where be that sullen young Tugwell ? what have you do with he ? " 
 " wonderfully clever be your new device , my friend , " Charron reply , after a long pull at the bottle . " to vanquish the mind by a mind superior be a glory of high reason ; but to let it remain in itself and compel it to perform what be desire by the other , be a stroke of genius . and under your pharmacy he must do it that have be prove already . the idea be grand , very noble , magnificent . it never would have show itself to my mind . " 
 " probably not . when that have be accomplish , we will hang he for a traitor . but , my dear friend , I have sad news for you , even in this hour of triumph . the lady of your adoration , the Admiral 's eld daughter , Faith , have recover the man for whom she have wait four year , and she mean to marry he . the father have give his consent , and her pride be beyond description . she have long love a mystery what woman can help it ? and now she have one for life , a husband eclipse in his own hair . my renaud , all rivalry be futile . your hair , alas , be quite short and scanty . but this man have discover in Africa a nut which turn a man into the husk of himself . no wonder that he come out of the sea all dry ! " 
 " Tush ! he be a pig . it be a pig that find the nut . I will be the butcher for that long pig , and the lady will rush into the arm of conquest . then will I possess all the Admiral 's land , and pursue the fine chase of the rabbit . and I will give dinner , such dinner , my faith ! ha ! that be excellent say embrace I my Faith will sit at the right side of the table , and explain to the english company that such dinner could proceed from nobody except a french gentleman commingle all the knowledge of the joint with the lofty conception of the hash , the mince the what you call ? ah , you have no name for it , because you do not know the proper thing . then , in the presence of admire Englishmen , I will lean back in my chair , the most comfortable chair that can be find " 
 " stop . you have get to get into it yet , " carne interrupt , rudely ; " and the way to do that be not to lean back in it . the fault of your system have always be that you want to enjoy everything before you get it . " 
 " and of your , " retort Charron , begin to imbibe the pugnacity of an english landlord , " that when you have get everything , you will enjoy what ? nothing ! " 
 " even a man of your levity hit the nail on the head sometimes , " say Carne , " though the blow can not be a very heavy one . nature have not fashion I for enjoyment , and therefore afford I very little . but some little I do expect in the great inversion come , in the upset of the scoundrel who have fatten on my flesh , and steal my land , to make country gentleman if it be possible of themselves . it will take a large chimney to burn their title - deed , for the robbery have last for a century . but I hold the great Emperor 's process sign for that ; and if you come to my cookery , you will say that I be capable of enjoyment . fight I enjoy not , as hot man do , nor guzzle , nor swigging , nor singing of song ; for all of which you have a talent , my friend . but the triumph of quiet skill I like ; and I love to turn the balance on my enemy . of these there be plenty , and among they all who live in that fishy little hole down there . " 
 Carne point contemptuously at Springhaven , that poor little village in the valley . but the sun have just lift his impartial face above the last highland that baulk his contemplation of the home of so many and great virtue ; and in the brisk moisture of his early salute the village in the vale look lovely . for a silvery mist be flush with rose , like a bridal veil warm by the blush of the bride , and the curve of the land , like a dewy palm leaf , shine and sink alternate . 
 " what a rare blaze they will make ! " continue Carne , as the sunlight glance along the russet thatch , and the blue smoke arise from the early chimney . " every cottage there shall be a bonfire , because it have cast off allegiance to I . the whole race of Darling will be at my mercy the pompous old Admiral , who refuse to call on I till his idiot of a son persuade he that wretched poetaster , who reduce I to the ignominy of read his own rubbish to he and the haughty young woman that worship a savage who have treat I with insult . I have they all now in the hollow of my hand , and a thorough good crumpling be prepared for they . the first house to burn shall be Zebedee Tugwell 's , that conceite old dolt of a fishing fellow , who give I a nod of suspicion , instead of pull off his dirty hat to I . then we blow up the church , and old Twemlow 's house , and the Admiral 's , when we have do with it . the fishing - fleet , as they call their wretched tub , will come home , with the usual fuss , to - night , and on Monday it shall be ash . how like you my programme ? be it complete ? " 
 " too much , too much complete ; too barbarous , " answer the kindly hearted Frenchman . " what harm have all the poor man do to you ? and what insanity to provoke enemy of the people all around who would bring we thing to eat ! and worse if the house be consume with fire , where will be the revenue that be design for I , as the fair son of the Admiral ? no , no ; I will allow none of that . when the landing be make , you will not be my master . Soult will have charge of the subject inferior , and he be not a man of rapine . to he will I address myself in favour of the village . thus shall I ascend in the favour of my charming , and secure my property . " 
 " Captain , I be your master yet , and I will have no interference . no more talk ; but obey I to the letter . there be no sign of any rough weather , I suppose ? you sailor see thing which we do not observe . " 
 " this summer have not be of fine weather , and the sky be always change here . but there be not any token of a temp now . though there be a little prospect of rain always . " 
 " if it rain , all the well , for it obscure the sea . you have feed enough now to last even you till the evening ; or if not , you can take some with you . remain to the westward , where the cliff be high , and look out especially for british ship of war that may be appear up Channel . take this second spy - glass ; it be quite strong enough . but first of all tell Perkins to stand off again with the pilot - boat , as if he be look out for a job , and if he see even a frigate come eastward , to run back and let you know by a signal arrange between you . Dan Tugwell , I see , be ship yesterday on board of Prame no . 801 , a very handy vessel , which will lead the van , and five hundred will follow in her track on Sunday evening . my excellent uncle will be at the height of his eloquence just when his favourite Sunday - school boy be bring an addition to his congregation . but the church shall not be blow up until Monday , for fear of premature excitement . by Monday night about two hundred thousand such soldier as Britain could never produce will be able to quell any childish excitement such as Great Britain be apt to give way to . " 
 " but what be for I , this same Saturday night ? I like very much to make polite the people , and to marry the most beautiful and the rich ; but not to kill more than there be to be help . " 
 " the breaking of the egg may cut the finger that have be suck till their skin be go . you have plague I all along with your english hankering , which in your post of trust be traitorous . " 
 Charron be accustom to submit to the infinitely strong will of Carne . moreover , his sense of discipline often check the speed of his temper . but he have never be able to get rid of a secret contempt for his superior , as a traitor to the race to which he really belong , at least in the Frenchman 's opinion . and that such a man should charge he with treachery be more than his honest soul could quite endure , and his quick face flush with indignation as he speak : 
 " your position , my commander , do not excuse such word . you shall answer for they , when I be discharge from your command ; which , I hope , will be the case next week . to be speak of as a traitor by you be very grand . " 
 " take it as you please , " Carne reply , with that cold contemptuous smile which the other detest . " for the present , however , you will not be grand , but carry out the order which I give you . as soon as it be dark , you will return , keep the pilot - boat in readiness for my last despatch , with which you will meet the frigate Torche about midnight , as arrange on Thursday . all that and the signal you already understand . wait for I by this tree , and I may go with you ; but that will depend upon circumstance . I will take good care that you shall not be keep starve ; for you may have to wait here three or four hour for I . but be sure that you do not go until I come . " 
 " but what be I to do if I have see some british ship , or perkin have give I token of they ? " 
 " observe their course , and learn where they be likely to be at nightfall . there will probably be none . all I fear be that they may intercept the Torche . Farewell , my friend , and let your sense of duty subdue the small suffering of temper . " 




 CHAPTER LIX 

 NEAR our SHORES 

 " this be how it be , " say Captain Tugwell , that same day , to Erle Twemlow : " the folk they go on with a thing , till a man as have any head leave twist it round on his neck , with his chin look down his starn - post . then the enemy cometh , with his spy - glass and his gun , and afore he can look round , he hath nothing leave to look for . " 
 " then you think , Tugwell , that the danger be not over ? that the french mean business even now , when every one be tired of hear of it ? I have be away so long that I know nothing . but the universal opinion be " 
 " opinion of the universe be dash ! " Master Zebedee answer , with a puff of smoke . " we call ourselves the universe , when we be the rope that drag astarn of it . Cappen , to my mind there be mischief in the wind , more than there hath be for these three year ; and that be why you see I here , instead of go with the smack . Holy Scripture saith a dream cometh from the Lord ; leastway , to a man of sense , as hardly ever dreameth . the wind be so bad again we , Monday afternoon , that we put off sailing till the Tuesday , and Monday night I lie on my own bed , without a thought of nothing but to sleep till five o'clock . I have not   take nothing but a quart of John Prater 's ale and you know what his measure be not a single sip of grog ; but the Hangel of the Lord he come and stand by I in the middle of the night . and he take I by the hand , or if he do not   it come to the same thing of my get there , and he set I up in a dark high place , the like of the yew - tree near Carne Castle . and then he saith , ' look back , Zeb ' ; and I look , and behold Springhaven be all afire , like the bottomless pit , or the thunder - storm of Egypt , or the city of Sodom and Gomorrah . and two figure be jump about in the flame , like the furnace in the plain of Dura , and one of they be young Squire Carne , and the other be my son Daniel , as behaveth below his name . and I call out , ' Daniel , thou son of Zebedee and Kezia Tugwell , come forth from the burn fiery furnace ' ; but he answer not , neither heed I . and then Squire Darling , Sir Charles be now the name of he , out he come from his Round - house , and by the white gate above high - water mark , to order out the fire , because they be all his own cottage . but while he be go about , as he doth for fear of be hard upon any one , out jump Squire Carne , from the thickest of the blaze , and take the poor squire by the forepart of his neck , which he liketh to keep open when he getteth off of duty , and away with he into the burn fiery furnace make of his own house ! that be more than I could put up with , even under the Hangel , and I give such a kick that Kezia , though she saith she be the most quiet of woman , feel herself a force to bounce I up . " 
 " a dream of that sort deserve notice , " answer Erle , who have pass many month among sailor ; " and over and above that , I see proof of a foolish security in England , and of sharp activity in France . last Monday I be only five mile from Boulogne , on board of our frigate the Melpomene , for I want the captain 's evidence to help I in my own affair ; and upon my word I be quite amazed at the massing of the french force there , and the evident readiness of their hundred of troop - ship . score of they even have horse on board , for I see they quite clearly with a spy - glass . but the officer only laugh at I , and say they be tired of see that . and another thing I do not   like at all be the landing of a french boat this side of Pebbleridge . I be come home after dark one night , and as soon as they see I they push off , and pretend to be english fisherman ; but if ever I see Frenchmen , these be french ; and I believe they have a ship not far away , for I see a light show and then turn off . I examine the place in the morning , and see the footprint of man on a path up the cliff , as if they have go inland towards Carne Castle . when the Admiral come home , I tell he of it ; but he seem to think it be only some smuggling . " 
 " ah , there be smooglin ' of a bad kind over there , to my belief . I would not   tell your honour not a quarter what I think , because of the young gentleman be near akin to you . but a thing or two have come to my ear , very much again a young squire over that way . a man as will do what he have do be a black one in some way ; and if some , why not in all ? " 
 " tell I what you mean , " say Twemlow , sternly . " after say so much , you be bind to say more . Caryl Carne be no friend of mine , although he be my cousin . I dislike the man , though I know but little of he . " 
 " for sartin then a kind gentleman like you will not   like he none the well for betraying of a nice young maid as put her trust in he , as lively and pretty a young maid as ever step , and might have have the pick of all the young man in the parish . " 
 " what ! " exclaim Erle , with a sudden chill of heart , for Faith have not conceal from he her anxiety about dolly . " tugwell , do you mean to say " 
 " yes , sir ; only you must keep it to yourself , for the sake of the poor young thing ; though too many know it already , I be afeared . and that be how poor Jem Cheeseman change from a dapper money - turn man , as pleasant as could be , to a down - hearte , stick - in - door , honest - weighted fellow . poor little Polly be as simple as a dove , and she mean to break none of the Lord 's commandment , unless it be a sin to look so much above she . he take she aboard her father 's trading - craft , and make pretence to marry she across the water , her know nothing of the lingo , to be sure ; and then when there come a thumping boy , and she demand for the sake of the young ' un that her marriage should be sartifie in the face of all the world , what do he do but turn round and ask she if she be fool enough to suppose that a Carne have marry a butter - man 's daughter ? with a few word more , she go off of her head , and have never be right again , they say ; and her father , who be mighty proud to have a grandson heir to an old ancient castle , he be so take aback with this disappointment that he puzzle all the village , include of I , as I be free to own , by jump into his own rope . ' twas only now just that I hear all this ; and as the captain of this here place , I shall ask leave of Cheeseman to have it out with Master Carne , as soon as may be do without hurt the poor thing . if she have be my child , the rope should have go round his neck first , if it come to mine there - arter ! " 
 " the - villain ! " Twemlow use a strong short word , without add heavily , it may be hop , to the score against he . " and to think that all this time he have be dare to address himself but never mind that now . it will be a bad time for he when I catch he by himself , though I must not speak of Polly . poor little polly ! what a pretty child she be ! I use to carry sugar - plum on purpose for she . good - bye , Tugwell ; I must think about all this . " 
 " and so must I , sir . what a strap chap ' a be ! " Captain Zebedee continue to himself , as Twemlow stride away with the light step of a mountain savage , carry a long staff from force of habit , and look even large than himself from the flow of chestnut hair and beard around he . " never do see such a hairy chap . never show no sign of it when ' a be a lad , and Miss ' Liza quite smooth in the front of her neck . must come of Hottentot climate , I reckon . they call it the bush , from the folk be so bushy . I use to think as my beard be a pretty good example ; but , Lord bless I and keep I , it would all go on his nose ! if ' a spreadeth that over the face of Squire Carne , ' a will ravish he , as the wicked doth ravish the poor . " 
 Twemlow have many sad thing to consider , and among they the impending loss of this grand mane . after diver delay , and infinitude of form , and much evidence of thing self - evident in the spirit which drive Sir Horatio Nelson to pin a certificate of amputation to the sleeve of his lost arm this Twemlow have establish that he be the Twemlow leave behind upon the coast of Africa , and have be capture in the service of his country , be entitle at least to restoration . in such a case small liberality be show in those day , even as now prevaileth , the object of all in authority be to be hard upon those who be out of it . at last , when he be become well weary , and nothing but an Englishman 's love of his country and desire to help in her danger prevent he from turn to private pursuit wherein he hold a key to fortune he find himself restore to his rank in the Army , and appoint to another regiment , which happen to be short of officer . then he fling to the wind , until peace should return , his prospect of wealth beyond reckon , and lock in a black leather trunk material worth their weight in diamond . but , as life be uncertain , he tell his beloved one the secret of his great discovery , which she , in sweet ignorance of mankind , regard as of no importance . 
 but as war appear and disappear , nation wax and wane , and the holy principle of one age become the scoff of the next , yet human nature be the same throughout , it would be wrong to cast no glance even with the French so near our shore at the remarkable discovery of this young man , and the circumstance lead up to it . for with keen insight into civilized thought , which yearn with the deep remorse for those blessing which itself have banish , he know that he hold a master - key to the treasury of Croesus , Mycerinus , Attalus , and every other King who have dazzle the world with his talent . the man who can minister to human need may , when he be lucky , earn a little towards his own ; the man who contribute to the pleasure of his fellow must find reward in his own ; but he who can gratify the vanity of his race be the master of their pocket . 
 Twemlow have be carry from the deadly coast   as before relate by Captain Southcombe   to the mountainous district far inland , by the great King Golo of the Quackwas nation , mighty warrior of lofty stature . here he be treat well , and soon learn enough of their simple language to understand and be understand ; while the King , who consider all white man as of canine origin , be pleased with he , and prepare to make he useful . then Twemlow be send , with an escort of chief , to the land of the Houlas , as a medicine - man , to win Queen Mabonga for the great King Golo . but she so strange be the perversity of woman behold this man of a pearly tint , as fair as the moon , and as soft as a river for he take many month to get properly tan with one long gaze of amazement yield to he what he seek for another . a dwarf and a whipster he might be among the great darky around she for he have only six foot and one inch of stature , and forty - two inch round the chest but , to her fine taste , tone and quality more than covered defect of quantity . the sight of male member of her race have never move she , because she have hear of their wickedness ; but the gaze of this white man , so tender and so innocent , set she on a long course of wonder about herself . then she draw back , and pass into the private hut behind , where no one be allow to disturb she . for she never have feel like this before , and she want nobody to notice it . 
 but the Houla maiden , with the deep interest in matter that come home to they outside their understanding , hold council with their mother , and these impart to the angelic stranger , as plainly as modesty permit , the distressing result of his whiteness , and implore he to depart , before further harm be do . Twemlow perceive that he have tumble into a difficult position , and the only way out of it be to make off . give pledge to return in two moon at the late , he make his salaam to the sensitive young Queen , whose dignity be only surpass by her grace , and expect to be shorten by the head , return with all speed to the great King Golo . Honesty be the good policy as we all know so well that we forbear to prove it and the Englishman see that the tale would be dark from the lip of his black attendant . the negro monarch be of much - endure mind , but these tiding outwent his philosophy . he order Twemlow 's head to come off by dinner - time , and , alas , that royal household keep very early hour ; and the poor captain , cord to a tree , sniff sadly the growth of good roast , which he never should taste , and could only succeed in succession of fare . for although that enlighten King have discard the taste of the nation around he , it be not half so certain as the prisoner could have wish that his prejudice would resist the relish of a candid rival in prime condition . 
 while Twemlow be dwell upon this nice question , and sympathise deeply with the animal on the spit , Tuloo , the head councillor of the realm , appear , an ancient negro full of wisdom and resource . discover that the white man set more value on his head than be usual with these philosopher , he propose condition which be eagerly accept , and release the captive , lead he into his own hut . here the man of wisdom spat three time into his very ample bosom , to exorcise evil spell , and take from a hole in the corner something which he handle very carefully , and with a touch as light as possible . follow everything with his good eye , Twemlow perceive in the hand of Tuloo a spongy - look substance of conical form , and in colour and size very like a morel , but possess a peculiar golden glow . " Kneel here , my son , and move not until I tell you , " the old man whisper , and be obey . then he strip off all cover from the white neck and shoulder , and begin immediately below the eye , brush all the cheek and the chin , throat and neck and upper part of the bosom , with the substance in his hand , from which a yellow powder pass , moist rather than dusty , into the open pore . " in one moon you will be a beast of the wood , and in two you shall return to the queen that love you , " say Councillor Tuloo , with a sly little grin . 
 but Twemlow be rob of no self - respect by the growth of a forest about he ; and when he be send again to Queen Mabonga , and the dewy glance of love die at the very first wink into a stony glare because of his face be cover with hair he say to himself that he know where he could inflict a very different impression upon lady . for these can not have too much hair in England , at the back of their own head , and front of their admirer ' . 
 Councillor Tuloo be gift with a deep understanding of a thing which look shallow to a man who have never yet hear of false bottom . he say to King Golo : " I know what woman be . as long as she never have think about man , you might crawl , and be only a hog to she . but her eye have be open to this white man , and there be room for a black one to go into they . and unless you be at hand , it will be do by some one else . " 
 in short , all be manage so beautifully that in six more moon the coy Mabonga split the Durra straw with King Golo , amid vast rejoicing and in din almost equal to that which a wedding in Wales arouse . but from time to time it be consider needful to keep up her Majesty 's repulsion by serve Erle Twemlow with another dose of that which would have create for the English fair capillary attraction . thus he become a great favourite with the King , who listen with deep interest to his description of the houseful of bead and button to be earn in England by a little proper management of Tuloo 's magic dust . before very long it be arrange that as soon as a good supply of Pong could be collect , Twemlow should be send back to the coast and place under the charge of Bandeliah , who be now a tributary of this great king . and here he might have wait year and year for the trading station be abandon now but for the benevolence of Captain Southcombe , who , be drive to the eastward of his course upon one of his return from India , stand in a little far to enquire about his friend , and with no small pleasure convey he home . 




 CHAPTER LX 

 no danger , GENTLEMEN 

 the little dinner at Springhaven Hall , appoint for that same Saturday , have now grow into a large one . Carne have refuse dolly 's offer to get he an invitation , and for many reason he be not invite . he ought to have be glad of this , because he do not want to be there ; but his nature , like a saw 's , be full of tooth , and however he be use , he grate . but without any aid of his tooth , a good dinner , well planned and well serve , bade fair in due course to be well digest also by forty at least of the forty - two people who sit down to consider it . for as yet the use of tongue be understand , and it be not allow to obstruct by perpetual motion the duty of the palate . and now every person in the parish of high culture which seem to be akin to the Latin for a knife , though a fork expel nature more forcibly as well as many other of locality less favour , join in this muster of good people and good thing . at the outset , the Admiral have intend nothing more than a quiet recognition of the goodness of the Lord in bring home a husband for the daughter of the house ; but what Englishman can forbear the pleasure of kill two bird with one stone ? 
 it be Stubbard who first suggest this , and Sir Charles at once see the force of it , especially with the Marquis of Southdown come . Captain Stubbard have never admire anybody , not even himself without which there be no happiness much less Mr. Pitt , or Lord Nelson , or the King , until justice be do to the race of Stubbard , and their hand be plunge into the revenue . but now , ever since the return of the war to its proper home in England , this Captain have be pay well for do the very good thing that a man can do , i. e. , nothing . he could not help desire to celebrate this , and as soon as he receive his invitation , he go to the host and put it clearly . the Admiral soon enter into his view , and as guest be not farm by the head as yet at table entertain self - respect , he perceive the advantage of a good dinner score to his credit with forty at the cost of twenty ; and Stubbard 's proposal seem thoroughly well time , so long be it now since the leader of Defence have celebrate their own vigilance . twenty - two , allow for the lady needful , be thus add to the score of chair intend , and the founder of the feast could scarcely tell whether the toast of the evening be to be the return of the traveller , or the discomfiture of Boney . that would mainly depend upon the wish of the Marquis , and these again be likely to be guide by the treatment he have meet with from the government lately and the commander of his Division . 
 this nobleman be of a character not uncommon eighty year ago , but now very rare among public man , because a more flexible fibre have choke it . Steadfast , honourable , simple , and straightforward , able to laugh without bitterness at the arrogant ignorance of mob , but never to smile at the rogue who lead they , scorn all shuffle of word , foul haze , and snaky maze of evasion , and refuse to believe at first sight that his country must be in the wrong and her enemy in the right , he add to all these exterminate foible a leisurely dignity now equally extinct . trimmer , time - server , and hypocrite fear he , as thief fear an honourable dog ; and none could quote his word against one another . this would have make he unpopular now , when perjury mean popularity . for the present , however , self - respect exist , and no one think any the bad of his lordship for not have find he a liar . especially with lady , who insist on truth in man as a pleasant proof of their sex , Lord Southdown have always be a prime favourite , and an authority largely misquote . and to add to his influence , he possess a quick turn of temper , which render it very agreeable to agree with he . 
 Lord Southdown be think , as he lead Miss Darling to her chair at the head of the table , that he never have see a more pleasing young woman , though he grieve at her taste in prefer the brown young man on she leave to his elegant friend Lord Dashville . also he marvel at hear so much , among the young officer of his acquaintance , concern the beauty of the young sister , and so little about this far sweet young person at least in his opinion . for verily dolly be not at she good ; her beautiful colour be go , her neck have lose its sprightly turn , and her gray eye move heavily instead of sparkle . " that girl have some burden upon her mind , " he think as he watch she with interest and pity ; ' she have put on her dress anyhow , and she do not even look to see who be look at she ! " 
 for the " Belle of all Sussex , " as the young spark entitle she , be ill at ease with herself , and ready to quarrel with every one except herself . she have conscience enough to confess , whenever she could not get away from it , that for week and month she have be slip far and far from the true and honest course . sometimes , with a pain like a stitch in the side , the truth would spring upon she ; and perhaps for a moment she would wonder at herself , and hate the man mislead she . but this happen chiefly when he be present , and say or do something to vex she ; and then he soon set it to right again , and make everything feel delightful . and this way of have her misgiving ease make they easy when they come again with no one to appease they . for she begin to think of what he have do , and how kind and considerate his mind must be , and how hard it must seem to mistrust he . 
 another thing that urge she to keep on now , without make any fuss about it , be the wonderful style her sister Faith have show since that hairy monster come back again . it be manifest that the world contain only one man of any high quality , and nobody must dare to think even twice about any conclusion he lay down . he have say to she , with a penetrate glance and it must have be that to get through such a thicket that dangerous people be about , and no girl possess any self - respect must think of wander on the shore alone . the more she be spy upon and admonish , the more she would do what she think right ; and a man who have live among savage for year must be a queer judge of propriety . but , in spite of all these defiant thought , her heart be very low , and her mind in a sad flutter , and she could not even smile as she meet her father 's gaze . suppose that she be frightened at the number of the guest , and the noise of many tongue , and the grandeur of the people , the gentle old man make a little signal to she to come and have a whisper with he , as a child might do , under courtesy of the good company . but dolly feign not to understand , at the penalty of many a heart - pang . 
 the dinner go on with a very merry sound , and a genuine strength of enjoyment , such as hearty folk have who know one another , and be meet together not to cut caper of wit , but refresh their goodwill and fine principle . and if any dinner party can be so arrange that only five per cent . have any trouble on its mind , the gentleman who whip away the plate , at a guinea a mouth , will have to go home with a face of willow pattern . 
 the other whose mind be away from her food , and reckless of its own nourishment , be Blyth Scudamore 's mother , as gentle a lady as ever try never to think of herself . in spite of all goodness , and faith in the like , she have enough to make she very miserable now , whenever she allow herself to think about it , and that be fifty - nine minute out of sixty . for a brief account of her son 's escape from Etaples have reach she , through the kindness of Captain Desportes , who find mean to get a letter deliver to the Admiral . that brave french officer speak most highly of the honourable conduct of his english friend , but have very small hope of his safety . for he add the result of his own inquiry to the statement of M. Jalais , and from these it be clear that poor Scuddy have set forth alone in a rickety boat , ill find and ill fit to meet even moderate weather in the open Channel . another young Englishman have do the like , after lurk in the forest of Hardelot , but he have be recapture by the French at the outset of his hopeless voyage .
