Scudamore have not be so retake ; and the Captain   who have not receive his letter until it be too late to interfere , by reason of his own despatch to Dieppe   have encounter a sharp summer gale just then , which must have prove fatal to the poor old boat . the only chance be that some english ship might have pick up the wanderer , and if so the highly respected Admiral would have hear of it before he receive this letter . as no such tiding have be receive , there could be little doubt about the issue in any reasonable mind . but the heart of a woman be not a mind , or the man that be bear of her might as well forego the honour . 
 however , as forty people be quite happy , the wise course be to rejoin they . the lady be resolve upon this occasion to storm the law of usage which require their withdrawal before the toast begin ; and so many gentle voice challenge the garrison of man behind their bottle that term of unusual scope be arrange . it be know that the Marquis would make a fine speech short , and therefore all the finer in propose the toast of the evening , to wit , " our king , and our Country . " under the vigorous lead of Mrs. Stubbard , the lady demand to hear every word ; after which they would go , and discuss their own affair , or possibly those of their neighbour . but the gentleman must endure their presence till his lordship have speak , and the Admiral reply . faith be against this arrangement , because she foresaw that it would make they very late ; but she yield to the wish of so many of her guest , console with the thought that she would be support by some one on her left hand , who would be her support for life . 
 when all have do well , except the two aforesaid , and good - will bear of good deed be crown comfort with jocund pleasure , and the long oak table , rich of grain and dark with the friction of a hundred year , shine in the waver flow of dusk with the gleam of purple and golden fruit , the glance of brilliant glass that puzzle the light with its claim to shadow , and the glow of amber and amethyst wine decant to settle that question then the bold Admiral , stand up , say , " bring in the light , that we may see his lordship . " 
 " I like to speak to some intelligence , " say the guest , who be shrewd at an answer . and dolly , be quick at occasion , seize it , and in the shifting of chair leave she own for some one else . 
 the curtain be draw across the western window , to close the conflict between God 's light and man 's , and then this well - know gentleman , have place his bottle handily for he never " put wine into two white , " to use his own expression arise with his solid frame as tranquil as a rock , and his full - fronted head like a piece of it . every gentleman bow to his bow , and wait with silent respect for his word , because they would be true and simple . 
 " my friend , I will take it for grant that we all love our country , and hate its enemy . we may like and respect they personally , for they be as good as we be ; but we be bind to hate they collectively , as man who would ruin all we love . for the stuff that be talk about freedom , democracy , march of intellect , and so forth , I have nothing to say , except to bid you look at the result among themselves . be there a man in France whose body be his own if he can carry arm , or his soul if it venture to seek its own good ? as for mind there be only the mind of one man ; a large one in many way ; in other a small one , because it consider its owner alone . 
 " but we of England have refuse to be strip of all that we hold dear , at the will of a foreign upstart . we have fight for year , and we still be fight , without any brag or dream of glory , for the right of ourselves and of all mankind . there have be among we weak - minded fellow , babbler of abstract nonsense , and even , I grieve to say traitor . but , on the whole , we have stand together , and therefore have not be tread on . how it may end be within the knowledge of the Almighty only ; but already there be sign that we shall be help , if we continue to help ourselves . 
 " and now for the occasion of our meeting here . we rejoice most heartily with our good host , the vigilant Defender of these shore , at the restoration to his arm or rather , to a still more delightful embrace of a british officer , who have prove a truth we know already , that nothing stop a british officer . I see a gentleman strike so keenly with the force of that remark , because he himself have prove it , that I must beg his next neighbour to fill up his glass , and allow nothing to stop he from toss it off . and as I be get astray from my text , I will clear my poor head with what you can see through . " 
 the Marquis of Southdown fill his glass from a bottle of grand old Chambertin six of which have be lay most softly in a cupboard of the wainscote for his use and then he have it fill again , and see his meaning brilliantly . 
 " our second point be the defeat of the French , and of this we may now assure ourselves . they have not be defeat , for the very good reason that they never would come out to fight ; but it come to the same thing , because they be give it over as a hopeless job . I have see too many up and down to say that we be out of danger yet ; but when our fleet have be chase theirs all over the world , be they likely to come and meet we in our own water ? Nelson have anchor at Spithead , and be rush up to London , as our host have hear to - day , with his usual impetuosity . every man must stick to his own business , even the mighty Nelson ; and he might not meddle with Billy Blue , or anybody else up Channel . still , Nelson be not the sort of man to jump into a chaise at Portsmouth if there be the very small chance of the French come over to devour we . 
 " well , my friend , we have do our good , and have some right to be proud of it ; but we should depart from our nature if we even exercise that right . the nature of an Englishman be this to be afraid of nothing but his own renown . feel this great truth , I will avoid offence by hide as a crime my admiration of the glorious soldier and sailor here , yet beg they for once to remember themselves , as have enable I to propose , and all present to pledge , the welfare of our King and Country . " 
 the Marquis wave his glass above his head , without spill a single drop , although it be a bumper , then drain it at a draught , invert it , and cleverly snap it in twain upon the table , with his other hand lay on his heart , and a long low reverence to the company . thereupon up stand squire and dame , and repeat the good toast , pledge it , with a deep bow to the proposer ; and as many of the gentleman as understand the art , without peril to fair neighbour , snap the glass . 
 his lordship be delighted , and in the spirit of the moment hold up his hand , which mean , " Silence , silence , till we all sing the National Anthem ! " in a clear loud voice he lead off the strain , Erle Twemlow from his hairy depth strike in , then every man , follow as he might , and with all his might , sustain it , and the lady , accord to their wo not , give proof of the height they can scale upon rapture . 
 the Admiral , standing , and beat time now and then with his heel though all the time deserve incessant beating enjoy the performance a great deal more than if it have be much well , and join in the main roar as loudly as he think his position as host permit . for although he be near the haven now of threescore year and ten , his throat and heart be so sea - worthy that he could very sweetly have outroar they all . but while he be prepare just to prove this , if encourage , and smile very pleasantly at a friend who say , " strike up , Admiral , " he be call from the room , and in the climax of the roar slip away for a moment , unheeded , and mean to make due apology to his guest as soon as he come back . 




 CHAPTER LXI 

 DISCHARGED from DUTY 

 while loyalty thus rejoice and throve in the warmth of its own geniality , a man who be loyal to himself alone , and have no geniality about he , be watch with contempt these british doing . Carne have tether his stout black horse , who deserve a well master , in a dusky dell of dark - wing tree at the back of the eastern shrubbery . here the good horse might rest unseen , and consider the mysterious way of man ; for the main approach be by the western road , and the shade of evening stretch their arm to the peaceful yawn of sunset . and here he find good stuff spread by nature , more worthy of his attention , and tuck back his foreleg , fare as well as the iron between his tooth permit . 
 then the master draw his green riding - coat of thin velvet close round he , and button the lappet in front , because he have heavy weight in the pocket . keep warily along the line of shadow , he gain a place of vantage in the shrubbery , a spot of thick shelter have loop of outlook . above and around he hang a curtain of many - point ilex , and before he a barberry bush , whose coral cluster catch the wan light . in this snug nook he rest calmly , lean against the ilex trunk , and finish his little preparation for anything adverse to his plan . in a belt which be hide by his velvet coat he wear a short dagger in a sheath of shagreen , and he fix it so that he could draw it in a moment , without unfasten the riding - coat . then from the pocket on either side he draw a pair of pistol , prim they well from a little flask , and replace they with the butt beneath the lappet . " death for at least three man , " he mutter , " if they be fool enough to meddle with I . my faith , these darling be grow very grand , on the strength of the land that belong to we ! " 
 for he hear the popping of champagne cork , and the clink of abundant silver , and tuning of instrument by the band , and he see the flash of light , and the dash of serve - man , and the rush of hot hospitality ; and although he have not enough true fibre in his stomach to yearn for a taste of the good thing go round , there can be little doubt , from what he do thereafter , that his gastric juice must have turn to gall . 
 with all these sound and sight and scent of thing that he have no right to despise , his patience be try for an hour and a half , or at any rate he believe so . the beautiful glow in the west die out , where the sun have be ripen his harvest - field of sheafy gold and awny cloud ; and the pulse of quivering dusk beat slowly , so that a man might seem to count it , or rather a child , who see such thing , which later man lose sight of . the form of the deepening distance against the departure of light grow faint , and prominent point become obscure , and line retire into mass , while Carne maintain his dreary watch , with his mood become dark . as the sound of joyful voice , and of good - will double by good fare , come to his unfed vigil from the open window of the dining - room , his heart be not enlarge at all , and the only solace for his lip be to swear at british revelry . for the dining - room be at the western end , some fifty yard away from he , and its principal window face the sunset , but his lurking - place afford a view of the southern casement obliquely . through these he have see that the lamp be bring , and hear the increase of merry noise , the clapping of hand , and the jovial cheer at the rise of the popular Marquis . 
 at last he see a white kerchief wave at the window near to he , the window of the Admiral 's little study , which open like a double door upon the eastern grass - plat . with an ill - condition mind , and body stiff and lacking nourishment , he cross the grass in a few long stride , and be admit without a word . 
 " what a time you have be ! I be give it up , " he whisper to the trembling dolly . " where be the candle ? I must strike a light . surely you might have bring one . Bolt the door , while I make a light , and close the curtain quietly , but leave the window open . do not   shake , like a child that be go to be whip . too late now for nonsense . what be you afraid of ? silly child ! " 
 as he speak he be strike a light in a little french box contain a cube of jade , and with very little noise he light two candle stand on the high oak desk . dolly draw a curtain across the window , and then go softly to the door , which open opposite the corner of a narrow passage , and make pretence to bolt it , but shoot the bolt outside the socket . 
 " come and let I look at you , " say Carne , for he know that he have be rough with she , and she be not of the kind that submit to that . " Beauty , how pale you look , and yet how perfectly lovely in this evening gown ! I should like to kill the two gentleman who sit next to you at dinner . darling , you know that whatever I do be only for your own sweet sake . " 
 " if you please not to touch I , it will be well , " say the lady , not in a whisper , but a firm and quiet voice , although her hand be tremble ; " you be come upon business , and you should do it . " 
 if Carne have but catch she in his arm , and hold she to his heart , and vow that all business might go to the devil while he hold his angel so , possibly the glow of nobler feeling might have be lose in the fire of passion . but he keep his selfish end alone in view , and neglect the womanly road to it . 
 " a despatch from London arrive today ; I must see it , " he say , shortly ; " as well as the copy of the answer send . and then my beauty must insert a not in the order to be issue in the morning , or otherwise invert its meaning , simply to save useless bloodshed . the key for a moment , the key , my darling , of this fine old piece of furniture ! " 
 " be it likely that I would give you the key ? my father always keep it . what right have you with his private desk ? I never promise anything so bad as that . " 
 " I be not to be trifle with , " he whisper , sternly . " do you think that I come here for kiss ? the key I must have , or break it open ; and how will you explain that away ? " 
 his rudeness settle her grow purpose . the misery of indecision vanish ; she would do what be right , if it cost her life . her face be as white as her satin dress , but her dark eye flash with menace . 
 " there be a key that open it , " she say , as she point to the bookcase ; " but I forbid you to touch it , sir . " 
 Carne 's only reply be to snatch the key from the upper glass door of the book - shelf , which fit the lock of the Admiral 's desk , though the owner be not aware of it . in a moment the intruder have unlock the high and massive standing - desk , throw back the cover , and place one candlestick among the document . many of they he brush aside , as useless for his purpose , and become bewildered among the rest , for the Commander of the Coast - defence be not a man of order . he never know where to put a thing , nor even where it might have put itself , but find a casual home for any paper that deserve it . this lack of method have one compensation , like other human defect , to wit , that it puzzle a clandestine searcher more deeply than cypher or cryptogram . Carne have the Admiral 's desk as wide as an oyster throw back on his valve , and just be undertucked with the knife , to make he go down easily . yet so great be the power of disorder that nothing could be make out of anything . " watch at the door , " he have say to dolly ; and this suit her intention . 
 for while he be thus absorb , with his back towards she , she open the door a little , and presently see the trusty Charles come hurry by , as if England hang upon his labour . " tell my father to come here this moment ; go softly , and say that I send you . " as she finish her whisper she close the door , without any sound , and stand patiently . 
 " show I where it be ; come and find it for I . everything here be in the vile mess , " cry Carne , grow reckless with wrath and hurry . " I want the despatch of this morning , and I find tailor ' bill , way to make water - proof blacking , a list of old woman , and a stump of old pipe ! come here , this instant , and show I where it be . " 
 " if you forget your good manner , " answer dolly , still keep in the dark near the door , " I shall have to leave you . surely you have practice enough in spying , to find what you want , with two candle . " 
 Carne turn for a moment , and star at she . her attitude surprise he , but he could not believe in her courage to rebel . she stand with she back to the door , and meet his gaze without a sign of fear . 
 " there be no official paper here , " he say , after another short ransack ; " there must have be some , if this desk be the one . have you dare to delude I by show the wrong desk ? " 
 dolly meet his gaze still , and then walk towards he . the band have strike up , and the company be sing with a fine patriotic roar , which ring very nobly in the distance " Britannia , rule the wave ! " dolly feel like a Briton as the word roll through she , and the melody lift her proud heart . 
 " you have delude yourself , " she say , stand proudly before the baffled spy ; " you have ransack my father 's private desk , which I allow you to do , because my father have no secret . he leave it open half the time , because he be a man of honour . he be not a man of plot , and wile , and trickery upon woman . and you have delude yourself , in dream that a daughter of his would betray her Country . " 
 " by the God that make I , I will have your life ! " cry Carne in French , as he dash his hand under his coat to draw his dagger ; but the pressure of the desk have displace that , so that he could not find it . she think that her time be come , and shriek for she be not at all heroic , and love life very dearly but she could not take her eye from his , nor turn to fly from the spell of they ; all she could do be to step back ; and she do so into her father 's arm . 
 " Ho ! " cry the Admiral , who have enter with the smile of good cheer and good company glow on his fine old countenance ; " my dolly and a stranger at my private desk ! Mr. Carne ! I have have a glass or two of wine , but my eye must be play I extraordinary trick . a gentleman search my desk , and apparently threaten my dear daughter ! have the kindness to explain , before you attempt to leave we . " 
 if the curtain have not be draw across the window , Carne would have make his escape , and leave the situation to explain itself . but the stuff be thick , and it get between his leg ; and before he could slip away , the stout old Admiral have he by the collar with a sturdy grasp , attest the substance of the pass generation . and a twinkle of good - humour be in the old eye still such a wonder be his Dolly that he might be do wrong in lay hand of force upon a visitor of her . thing as strange as this have be within his knowledge , and prove to be of little harm with forbearance . but his eye grow stern , as Carne try to dash his hand off . 
 " if you value your life , you will let I go , " say the young man to the old one . 
 " I will not let you go , sir , till you clear up this . a gentleman must see that he be bind to do so . if I prove to be wrong , I will apologise . what ! be you go to fire at I ? you would never be such a coward ! " 
 he drop upon the floor , with a bullet in his brain , and his course of duty end . Carne dash aside the curtain , and be nearly through the window , when two white arm be cast round his waist . he throw himself forward with all his might , and wrench at the little hand clasp around he , but they hold together like clench iron . " will you force I to kill you ? " " you may , if you like " be the dialogue of these lover . 
 the strength of a fit be in her despair . she set her bent knee against the window - frame , and a shower of glass fall between they ; but she flinch not from her convulsive grasp . " let I come back , that I may shoot myself , " Carne pant , for his breath be straiten ; " what be life to I after lose you ? " she make no answer , but take good care not to release so fond a lover . then he throw himself back with all his weight , and she fall on the floor beneath he . her clasp relax , and he be free ; for her eye have encounter her father 's blood , and she swoon away , and lie as dead . 
 Carne arise quickly , and bolt the door . his breath be short , and his body trembling , but the wit of the traitor be active still . " I must have something to show for all this , " he think as he glance at the body on the floor . " those reveller may not have hear this noise . I know where it be now , and I will get it . " 
 but the sound of the pistol , and shriek of the girl , have ring through the guest , when the wine be at their lip , and all be nod to one another . faith spring up , and then fall back trembling , and several man run towards the door . Charles , the footman , meet they there , with his face whiter than his napkin , and hold up his hand , but could not speak . Erle Twemlow dash past he and down the passage ; and Lord Southdown say : " gentleman , see to the lady . there have be some little mishap , I fear . Bob , and Arthur , come with I . " 
 Twemlow be first at the study door , and find it fasten , strike with all his force , and shout , at the very moment when Carne stand before the true desk of office . " good door , and good bolt , " muttered Carne ; " my rule be never to be hurry by noise . dolly will be quiet for a quarter of an hour , and the old gentleman forever . all I want be about two minute . " 
 Twemlow step back a few yard , and then with a good start deliver a rushing kick ; but the only result be a jar of his leg through the sole of his thin dress sandal . 
 " the window ! " cry the Marquis . " we will stop here ; you know the house ; take the short cut to the window . whoever be there , we shall have he so . I be too slow . boy Bob , go with he . " 
 " what a fool I be not to think of that ! " shout Twemlow , as he set off for the near house door , and unluckily Carne hear he . he have strike up the ledge of the desk with the butt of the pistol he have fire , and pocket a roll of fresh despatch , he stride across the body of the Admiral , and with a glance at dolly whose eye be wide open , but her face draw aside , like a peach with a split stone out he go . he smile as he hear the thundering of full - bodied gentleman against the study door , and their oath , as they damage their knuckle and knee - cap . then he set off hot - foot , but be stop by a figure advance from the corner of the house . 
 this be not a graceful figure , as of gentle maiden , nor venerable and slow of foot , as that of an ancient mariner , but a man in the prime of strength , and largely endow with that blessing the mate of truth . Carne perceive that he have meet his equal , and perhaps his well , in a bout of muscle , and he try to escape by superior mind . 
 " Twemlow , how glad I be that I have meet you ! you be the very man I want . there have be a sad accident in there with one of the Admiral 's pistol , and the dear old man be badly wounded . I be off for a doctor , for my horse be at hand . for God 's sake run in , and hold his head up , and try to staunch the bleeding . I shall be back in half an hour with the man that live at Pebbleridge . do not   lose a moment . Particulars hereafter . " 
 " particular now ! " reply Twemlow , sternly , as he plant himself before his cousin . " for year I have live among liar , and they call a lie Crom , and worship it . if this be not Crom , why do you bolt the door ? " 
 " you shall answer for this , when time allow . if the door be bolt , he must have do it . let I pass ; the last chance depend on my speed . " 
 Carne make a rush to pass , but Twemlow catch he by the breast , and hold he . " come back , " he say , fiercely , " and prove your word . without that , you go no far . " 
 Carne seize he by the throat , but his mighty beard , like a collar of hemp , protect he , and he bring his big brown fist like a hammer upon the traitor 's forehead . carne wrench at his dagger , but fail to draw it , and the two strong man roll on the grass , fight like two bull - dog . Reason , and think , and even sense of pain be lose in brutal fury , as they writhe , and clutched , and dig at one another , gash their knuckle , and gnash their tooth , froth with one another 's blood , for Carne bit like a tiger . at length tough condition and power of endurance get the mastery , and Twemlow plant his knee upon the gasp breast of Carne . 
 " Surrend , " he say , for his short breath could not fetch up the third syllable ; and Carne with a sign of surrender lie on his back , and put his chin up , and shut his eye as if he have faint . Twemlow with self - congratulation wait a little to recover breath , still keep his knee in the post of triumph , and pin the foe 's right arm to his side . but the foe 's left hand be free , and with the eye still shut , and a continuance of gasp , that left hand steal its way to the left pocket , quietly draw forth the second pistol , press back the hammer on the grass , and with a flash   both of eye and of flint   fire into the victor 's forehead . the triumphant knee roll off the chest , the body swing over , as a log be roll by the woodman 's crowbar , and Twemlow 's back be on the grass , and his eye be close to the moonlight . 
 Carne scramble up and shake himself , to be sure that all his limb be sound . " Ho , ho , ho ! " he chuckle ; " it be not so easy to beat I . why , who be you ? down with you , then ! " 
 Lord Robert Chancton , a lad of about sixteen , the eld son of the Marquis , have lose his way inside the house , in try to find a short - cut to the door , and come up after the pistol be fire , make a very gallant rush at the enemy . with a blow of the butt Carne send he sprawl ; then dash among the shrub and tree , in another minute be in the saddle , and gallop towards the ancestral ruin . 
 as he strike into the main road through the ground , Carne pass and just miss by a turn of the bridle another horseman ascend the hill , and urge a weary animal . the face of the man shoot past each other within a short yard , and gaze meet gaze ; but neither in the dark flash know the other , for a big tree bar the moonlight . but Carne , in another moment , think that the man who have pass must be Scudamore , probably fraught with hot tiding . and the thought be confirm , as he meet two trooper rid as hard as ride they might ; and then see the beacon on the headland flare . from point to point , and from height to height , like a sprinkle of blood , the red light run ; and the roar of gun from the moon - light sea make echo that they be ready . then the rub - a - dub - dub of the drum arise , and the thrilling blare of trumpet ; the great deep of the night be heave and break with the stir of human storm ; and the staunch and strong piece of earth our England be ready to defend herself . 




 CHAPTER LXII 

 the way out of it 

 " my father ! my father ! I must see my father . who be you , that dare to keep I out ? let I know the bad , and try to bear it . what be any of you to he ? " 
 " but , my dear child , " Lord Southdown answer , hold the door against poor faith , as she strove to enter the room of death , " wait just one minute , until we have lift he to the sofa , and let we bring your poor sister out . " 
 " I have no sister . she have kill my father , and the good thing she can do be to die . I feel that I could shoot she , if I have a pistol . let I see he , where he lie . " 
 " but , my poor dear , you must think of other . your dear father be beyond all help . your gallant lover lie on the grass . they hope to bring he round , God willing ! go where you can be of use . " 
 " how cruel you be ! you must want to drive I mad . let his father and mother see to he , while I see to my own father . if you have a daughter , you would understand . be I cry ? do I even tremble ? " 
 the Marquis offer his arm , and she take it in fear of fall , though she do not tremble ; so he lead she to her father 's last repose . the poor Admiral lie by the open window , with his head upon a stool which Faith have work . the ghastly wound be in his broad smooth forehead , and his fair round cheek be white with death . but the heart have not quite cease to beat , and some remnant of the mind still hover somewhere in the lacerated brain . Stubbard , sob like a child , be lift and clumsily chafe one numb hand ; while his wife , who have sponge the wound , be make the white curl wave with a fan she have shape from a long official paper find upon the floor . 
 dolly be recover from her swoon , and sit upon a stool by the bookcase , faintly wonder what have happen , but afraid to ask or think . the corner of the bookcase , and the burly form of Stubbard , conceal the window from she , and the torpid oppression which ensue upon a fit lay between she and her agony . faith , as she pass , dart one glance at she , not of pity , not of love , but of cold contempt and satisfaction at her misery . 
 then Faith , the quiet and gentle maid , the tranquil and the self - control   whom every one have charge with want of heart , because she have bear her own grief so well , stand with the body of her father at her foot , and utter an exceed bitter cry . the other have see enough of grief , as every human being must , but nothing half so sad as this . they fear to look at her face , and durst not open lip to comfort she . 
 " do not   speak . do not   look at he . you have no right here . when he come to himself , he will want none but I . I have always do everything for he since dear mother die ; and I shall get he to sit up . he will be so much well when he sit up . I can get he to do it , if you will only go . oh , father , father , it be your own Faith come to make you well , dear , if you will only look at I ! " 
 as she take his cold limp hand and kiss it , and wipe a red splash from his soft white hair , the die man feel , by nature 's feeling , that he be be touch by a child of his . a faint gleam flit through the dimness of his eye , which he have not the power to close , and the longing to say " farewell " contend with the drooping of the underlip . she be sure that he whisper , " bless you , darling ! " though nobody else could have make it out ; but a sudden rush of tear improve her hearing , as rain bring high voice down . 
 " dolly too ! " he seem to whisper next ; and Faith make a sign to Mrs. Stubbard . then dolly be bring , and fall upon her knee , at the other side of her father , and do not know how to lament as yet , and be scarcely sure of have anything to mourn . but she spread out her hand , as if for somebody to take they , and bow her pale face , and close her lip , that she might be rebuke without answer . 
 her father know she ; and his yearning be not to rebuke , but to bless and comfort she . he have forget everything , except that he be die , with a daughter at each side of he . this appear to make he very happy , about everything , except those two . he could not be expect to have much mind leave ; but the last of it be busy for his child 's good . once more he try to see they both , and whisper his last message to they " forgive and love each other . " 
 Faith bow her head , as his fall back , and silently offer to kiss her sister ; but dolly neither move nor look at she . " as you please , " say Faith ; " and perhaps you would like to see a little more of your handiwork . " 
 for even as she speak , her lover 's body be carry past the window , with his father and mother on either side , support his limp arm and sobbing . then dolly arise , and with one hand grasp the selvage of the curtain , fix one long gaze upon her father 's corpse . there be no tear in her eye , no sign of anguish in her face , no proof that she know or feel what she have do . and without a word she leave the room . 
 " hard to the last , even hard to you ! " cry Faith , as her tear fall upon the cold forehead . " oh , darling , how could you have love she so ? " 
 " it be not hardness ; it be madness . follow your sister , " Lord Southdown say . " we have have calamity enough . " 
 but Faith be fight with all her strength against an attack of hysteric , and fetch long gasp to control herself . " I will go , " reply Mrs. Stubbard ; " this poor child be quite unfit . what on earth be become of Lady Scudamore ? a doctor 's widow might have do some good . " 
 the doctor 's widow be do good elsewhere . in the first rush from the dining - room , Lady Scudamore have be push back by no less a person than Mrs. Stubbard ; when at last she reach the study door she find it close against she , and enter the next room , see the flash of the pistol fire at Twemlow . bravely hurry to the spot by the near outlet she could find , she become at once entirely occupy with this new disaster . for two man who run up with a carriage lamp declare that the gentleman be as dead as a door - nail , and hasten to make good their word by swing he up heel over head . but the lady make they set he down and support his head , while she bath the wound , and send to the house for his father and mother , and when he could be safely bring in - door , help with her soft hand beneath his hair , and then become so engross with he that the arrival of her long - lost son be for several hour unknown to she . 
 for so many thing come all at once be enough to upset any one . urgent despatch come hot for the hand that now be cold for ever ; not a moment to lose , when time have cease for the man who be to urge it . there be plenty of officer there , but no one clearly entitle to take command . moreover , the public service clash with the personal rage of the moment . some be for rush to the stable , mount every horse that could be find , and scour the country , sword in hand , for that infernal murderer . some , have just descry the flash of beacon from the headland , and hear the alarm - gun from shore and sea , be for hurrying to their regiment , or ship , or home and family   accord to the head - quarter of their life , while other put their coat on to ride for all the doctor in the county , who should fetch back the Admiral to this world , that he might tell everybody what to do . Scudamore stand with his urgent despatch in the large well - candle hall , and vainly desire to deliver they . " send for the Marquis , " suggest some one . 
 Lord Southdown come , without be send for . " I shall take this duty upon myself , " he say , " as Lord - Lieutenant of the county . Captain Stubbard , as commander of the near post , will come with I and read these order . gentleman , see that your horse be ready , and have all of the Admiral 's saddle . Captain Scudamore , you have discharge your trust , and doubtless ride far and hard . my order to you be a bottle of wine and a sirloin of roast beef at once . " 
 for the sailor be now in very low condition , weary , and worry , and in want of food . rid express , and change horse twice , not once have he recruit the inner man , who be therefore quite unfit to wrestle with the power of sudden grief . when he hear of the Admiral 's death , he stagger as if a horse have stumble under he , and his leg be stiff from hard sticking to saddle , have as much as they could do to hold he up . yet he feel that he could not do the right thing now , he could not go and deal with the expedient victual , neither might he dare intrude upon the lady now ; so he go out to comfort himself by attend to the trouble of his foundered horse , and by shed unseen among the tree the tear which have gather in his gentle eye . 
 accord to the sure law of nature , that break - down animal have be forget as soon as he be do with . he would have give his four leg if he could legally dispose of they for a single draught of sweet delicious rapturous ecstatic water ; but his bloodshot eye seek vainly , and his welt tongue find nothing wet , except the flake of his own salt foam . until , with the help of the moon , a sparkle   worth more to his mind than all the diamond he could draw   a sparkle of the pure water gleam into his dim eye from the distance . recall to his mind 's eye the grand date of his existence when he be a colt , and have a meadow to himself , with a sparkling river at the end of it , he set forth in good faith , and , although his leg be weary , " negotiate " as the sporting writer say the distance between he and the object of his desire . he have not the least idea that this have cost ten guineas as much as his own good self be worth ; for it happen to be the first dahlia see in that part of the country . that gaudy flower at its first appearance make such a stir among gardener that Mr. Swipes give the Admiral no peace until he allow he to order one . and so great be this gardener 's pride in his profession that he would not take an order for a rooted slip or cutting , from the rich man in the neighbourhood , for less than half a guinea . therefore Mr. Swipes be attend to the plant with the diligence of a wet - nurse , and the weather be dry , he have soak it overhead , even before he do that duty to himself . 
 a man of no tooth can take his nourishment in soup ; and nature , invert her manifold device which she would much rather do than be beat have provide that a horse can chew his solid into liquid , if there be a drop of juice in their composition , when his artificial life have fail to supply he with the bucket . this horse , be very dry , lay his tongue to the water - drop that sparkle on the foliage . he find they delicious , and he long for more , and very soon his ready mind suggest that the wet must have come out of the leaf , and there must be more there . proceed on this argument , he find it quite correct , and ten guineas ' worth of dahlia be go into his stomach by the time that Captain Scudamore come courteously to look after he . 
 Blyth , in equal ignorance of his sumptuous repast , give he a pat of approval , and be turn his head towards the stable yard , when he see a white figure glide swiftly through the tree beyond the belt of shrubbery . weary and melancholy as he be , and bewilder with the tumult of disaster , his heart bound hotly as he perceive that the figure be that of his Dolly dolly , the one love of his life , steal forth , probably to mourn alone the loss of her beloved father . as yet he know nothing of her share in that sad tale , and therefore feel no anxiety at first about her purpose . he would not intrude upon her grief ; he have no right to be her comforter ; but still she should have some one to look after she , at that time of night , and with so much excitement and danger in the air . so the poor horse be again abandon to his own resource , and be well use to such treatment , gaze as wistfully and delicately after the young man Scudamore as that young man gaze after his lady - love . 
 to follow a person stealthily be not conducive to one 's self - respect , but something in the lady 's walk and gesture impel the young sailor to follow she . she appear to be hasten , with some set purpose , and without any heed of circumstance , towards a part of the ground where no house be , no living creature for company , nor even a bench to rest upon . there be no foot - path in that direction , nor anything to go to , but the inland cliff that screen the Hall from northeastern wind , and at its foot a dark pool have no good name in the legend of the neighbourhood . even Parson Twemlow would not go near it later than the afternoon milking of the cow , and Captain Zeb would much rather face a whole gale of wind in a twelve - foot boat than give one glance at its dead calm face when the moon like a ghost stand over it . 
 " she be go towards corpse - walk pit , " think Scuddy " a cheerful place at this time of night ! she might even fall into it unawares , in her present state of distraction . I be absolutely bind to follow she . " 
 Duty fall in with his wish , as it have a knack of do . forget his weariness , he follow , and become more anxious at every step . for the maiden walk as in a dream , without regard of anything , herself more like a vision than a good substantial being . to escape Mrs. Stubbard she have go upstairs and lock herself in her bedroom , and then slip out without change dress , but throw a dark mantle over it . this have fall off , and she have not care to stop or think about it , but go on to her death exactly as she go in to dinner . her dress of white silk take the moonlight with a soft gleam like itself , and her clustering curl   release from fashion by the power of passion   fall , like the shadow , on her sweet white neck . but she never even ask herself how she look ; she never turn round to admire her shadow : tomorrow she would throw no shade , but be one ; and how she look , or what she be , would matter , to the world she use to think so much of , never more . 
 suddenly she pass from the moonlight into the blackness of a lonely thicket , and force her way through it , without heed of bruise or rent . at the bottom of the steep lie the long dark pit , and she stand upon the brink and gaze into it . to a sane mind nothing could look less inviting . all above be air and light , freedom of the wind and play of moon with summer foliage ; all below be gloom and horror , cold eternal stillness , and oblivion everlasting . even the new white frock awake no flutter upon that sullen breast . 
 dolly heave a sigh and shudder , but she do not hesitate . her mind be wander , but her heart be fix to make atonement , to give its life for the life destroy , and to lie too deep for shame or sorrow . suddenly a faint gleam catch her eye . the sob of self - pity from her fair young breast have bring into view her cherish treasure , bright keepsake of the girlish day when many a lover worship she . take from her neck the silken braid , she kiss they , and lay they on the bank . " they be all too good for I , " she think ; " they shall not perish with I . " 
 then , with one long sigh , she call up all her fleeting courage , and spring upon a fall trunk which overhang the water . " there will be no Dan to save I now , " she say as she reach the end of it . " Poor Dan ! he will be sorry for I . this be the way out of it . " 
 her white satin shoe for a moment shone upon the black bark of the tree , and , with one despairing prayer to Heaven , she leap into the liquid grave . 
 Dan be afar , but another be near , who love she even more than Dan . Blyth Scudamore hear the plunge , and rush to the brink of the pit , and tear his coat off . for a moment he see nothing but black water heaving silently ; then something white appear , and move , and a faint cry arose , and a hopeless struggle with engulf death begin . 
 " keep still , do not   struggle , only spread your arm , and throw your head back as far as you can , " he cry , as he swam with long stroke towards she . but if she hear , she could not heed , as the light of the deep sky come and go , and the choke water flash between , and gurgle into her ear and mouth , and smother her face with her own long hair . she dash her poor helpless form about , and fling out her foot for something solid , and grasp in dim agony at the wave herself have make . then her dress become heavily bagged with water , and the love of life be quench , and the night of death envelop she . without a murmur , down she go , and the bubble of her breath come up . 
 Scudamore utter a bitter cry , for his heart be almost break within an arm' - length of his love , and she be go for ever ! for the moment he do not perceive that the clasp of despair must have drown they both . point his hand and throw up his heel , he make one vain dive after she , then he know that the pit be too deep for the bottom to be reach in that way . he swam to the trunk from which dolly have leap , and judge the distance by the sullen ripple , dash in with a dive like a terrified frog . like a bullet he sink to the bottom , and grope with three fathom of water above he . just as his lung be give out , he feel something soft and limp and round . grasp this by the trail hair , he strike mightily up for the surface , and draw a long breath , and sustain above water the head that fall back upon his pant breast . 
 some three hour later , dolly Darling lie in her own little bed , as pale as death , but sleep the sleep of the world that see the sun ; while her only sister kneel by her side , weep the tear of a high world than that . " how could I be so brutal , and so hard ? " sob Faith . " if father have see it , will he ever forgive I ? his last word be ' forgive , and love . ' " 




 CHAPTER LXIII 

 the FATAL step 

 as Carne ride up the hill that night towards his ruin castle , the flush of fierce excitement and triumphant struggle die away , and self - reproach and miserable doubt strike into he like ague . for the death of Twemlow as he suppose he feel no remorse whatever . he he have shoot in furious combat , and as a last necessity ; the fellow have twice insult he , and then insolently collar he . and Faith , who have thwart he with dolly , and be from the first his enemy , now would have to weep and wail , and waste her youth in constancy . all that be good ; but he could not regard with equal satisfaction the death of the ancient Admiral . the old man have bring it upon himself by his stupid stubbornness ; and look fairly upon that matter , Carne scarcely see how to blame himself . still , it be a most unlucky thing , and must lead to a quantity of mischief . to - morrow , or at the late Monday , be to have crown with grand success his year of toil and danger . there still might be the landing , and he would sail that night to hasten it , instead of arrange all ashore ; but it could no long be a triumph of crafty management . the country be up , the Admiral 's death would spread the alarm and treble it ; and bad of all , in the hot pursuit of himself , which be sure to follow when people 's wit come back to they , all the store and ammunition , bring together by so much skill and patience and hardihood , must of necessity be discover and fall into the hand of the enemy . farewell to his long - cherish hope of specially neat retribution , to wit , that the ruin of his family should be the ruin of the land which have reject he ! then a fierce thought cross his mind , and become at once a stern resolve . if he could never restore Carne Castle , and dwell there in prosperity , neither should any of his oppressor . the only trace of his ancestral home should be a vast black hole in earth . 
 for even if the landing still succeed , and the country be subdue , he could never make his home there , after what he have do to - night . dolly be lose to he for ever ; and although he have love she with all the ardor he could spare from his high purpose , he must make up his mind to do without she , and perhaps it be all the well for he . if he have marry she , no doubt he could soon have teach she her proper place ; but no one could tell how she might fly out , through her self - will and long indulgence . he would marry a french woman ; that would be the good ; perhaps one connect with the Empress Josephine . as soon as he have make up his mind to this , his conscience cease to trouble he . 
 from the crest of the hill at the eastern gate many a bend of shore be clear , and many a league of summer sea lie waver in the moonlight . along the beach red torch flare , as man of the Coast - Defence push forth , and yellow flash of cannon inland signal for the Volunteers , while the light gleam   like window open from the depth   where sloop and gun - boat , frigate and ship of the line , be crowd sail to rescue England . for the semaphore , and when day be out the beacon - light , have glow along the backbone of the english hill , and England call every Englishman to show what he be make of . 
 " that will do . enough of that , John Bull ! " defy his native land , Carne shake his fist in the native manner . " stupid old savage , I shall live to make you howl . this country have become too hot to hold I , and I will make it hotter before I have do . here , Orso and Leo , good dog , good dog ! you can kill a hundred british bull - dog . Mount guard for an hour , till I call you down the hill . you can pull down a score of Volunteers apiece , if they dare to come after I . I have an hour to spare , and I know how to employ it . Jerry , old Jerry Bowles , stir your crooked shank . what be you rub your blear eye at ? " 
 the huge boar - hound , who obey no voice but his , take post upon the rugged road   which have never be repair since the Carnes be a power in the land , and sit side by side beneath the crumble arch , with their long fang glistening and red eye roll in the silver moonlight , while their deep chest pant for the chance of good fresh human victual . then Carne give his horse to ancient Jerry , say , " feed he , and take he with his saddle on to the old yew - tree in half an hour . wait there for Captain Charron , and for I . you be not to go away till I come to you . who be in the old place now ? think well before you answer I . " 
 " no one now in the place but she " the old man lift his elbow , as a coachman do in pass " and he down in the yellow jug . all the french sailor be at sea . only she will not   go away ; and she moaneth worse than all the owl and ghost . ah , your honour should never ' a do that respectable folk to Springhaven too ! " 
 " it be a slight error of judgment , Jerry . what a mealy lot these English be , to make such a fuss about a trifle ! but I be too soft - hearted to blow she up . tell she to meet I in half an hour by the break dial , and to bring the brat , and all her affair in a bundle such as she can carry , or kick down the hill before she . in half an hour , do you understand ? and if you care for your stiff old bone , get out of the way by that time . " 
 in that half - hour Carne gather in small compass , and strap up in a little " mail " as such light baggage then be call all his important document , despatch , letter , and paper of every kind , and the cash he be entrust with , which he use to think safe at Springhaven . then he take from a desk which be fix to the wall a locket bright with diamond , and kiss it , and fasten it beneath his neck - cloth . the wisp of hair inside it come not from any young or lovely head , but from the resolute brow of his mother , the woman who hat England . he should have put something well to his mouth ; for instance , a good beef sandwich . but one great token of his perversion be that he never do feed well a sure proof of the unrighteous man , as suggest by the holy Psalmist , and more distinctly put by Livy in the character he give Hannibal . 
 regard as a light thing his poor unfurnishe stomach , Carne mount the broken staircase , in a style which might else have be difficult . he have make up his mind to have one last look at the broad land of his ancestor , from the last that ever should be see of the wall they have rear and ruin . he stand upon the high vantage - point that he could attain with safety , where a shaggy gnarl of the all - pervade ivy serve as a friendly stay . to the right and left and far behind he all have once be their domain every tree , and meadow , and rock that face the moon , have belong to his ancestor . " be it a wonder that I be fierce ? " he cry , with unwonted self - inspection ; " who , that have be rob as I have , would not try to rob in turn ? the only thing amazing be my patience and my justice . but I will come back yet , and have my revenge . " 
 descend to his hyena den as Charron always call it he catch up his packet , and take a lantern , and a coil of tow which have be prepare , and stride forth for the last time into the slop court behind the wall . pass towards the eastern vault , he see the form of some one by the break dial , above the hedge of bramble , which have once be of rose and sweetbriar . " oh , that woman ! I have forget that affair ! " he mutter , with annoyance , as he push through the thorn to meet she . 
 Polly Cheeseman , the former belle of Springhaven , be lean against the wreck dial , with a child in her arm and a bundle at her foot . her pride and gaiety have leave she now , and she look very wan through frequent weeping , and very thin from nursing . her beauty   like her friend   have prove unfaithful under shame and sorrow , and little of it now remain except the long brown tress and the large blue eye . those eye she fix upon Carne with more of terror than of love in they ; although the fear be such as turn with a very little kindness to adore love . 
 Carne leave she to begin , for he really be not without shame in this matter ; and Polly be far well suited than dolly for a scornful and arrogant will like his . deeply despise all the female race as the greek tragedian call they save only the one who have give he to the world , he might have be a God to polly if he have but behave as a man to she . she look at he now with an implore gaze , from the gentleness of her ill - use heart . 
 their child , a fine boy about ten month old , break the silence by say " booh , booh , " very well , and hold out little hand to his father , who have often be scornfully kind to he . 
 " oh , Caryl , Caryl , you will never forsake he ! " cry the young mother , hold he up with rapture , and support his fat arm in that position ; " he be the very image of you , and he seem to know it . baby , say ' da - da . ' there , he have put his mouth up , and his memory be so wonderful ! oh , Caryl , what do you think of that and the first time of try it by moonlight ? " 
 " there be no time for this nonsense , polly . he be a wonderful baby , I dare say ; and so be every baby , till he get too old . you must obey order , and be off with he . " 
 " oh no ! you be come to take we with you . there , I have cover his face up , that he may not suppose you look cross at I . oh , Caryl , you would never leave he behind , even if you could do that to I . we be not grand people , and you can put we anywhere , and now I be nearly as well as ever . I have put up all his little thing ; it do not matter about my own . I be never bring up to be idle , and I can earn my own living anywhere ; and it might be a real comfort for you , with the great people go against you , to have somebody , not very grand , of course , but as true to you as yourself , and belong altogether to you . I know many people who would give their eye for such a baby . " 
 " there be no time for this , " Carne answer , sternly ; " my arrangement be make , and I can not take you . I have no fault to find with you , but argument be useless . " 
 " yes , I know that , Caryl ; and I be sure that I never would attempt to argue with you . you should have everything your own way , and I could attend to so many thing that no man ever do properly . I will be a slave to you , and this little darling love you , and then you will feel that you have two to love you , wherever you go , and whatever you do . and if I speak crossly when first I find out that that I go away for nothing with you , you must have forgive I by this time , and I never will remind you again of it ; if I do , send I back to the place I belong to . I belong to you now , Caryl , and so do he ; and when we be away from the people who know I , I shall be pleasant and cheerful again . I be only two - and - twenty the day the boat come home last week , and they use to say the young man jump into the water as soon as they catch sight of I . try to be kind to I , and I shall be so happy that I shall look almost as I use to do , when you say that the great lady might be grander , but none of they fit to look into my look - glass . Dear Caryl , I be ready ; I do not   care where it be , or what I may have to put up with , so long as you will make room for your polly , and your baby . " 
 " I be not at all a hard man , " say Carne , retreat as the impulsive Polly offer he the baby , " but once for all , no more of this . I have quite forgive any strong expression you may have make use of when your head be light ; and if all go well , I shall provide for you and the child , accord to your rank in life . but now you must run down the hill , if you wish to save your life and his . " 
 " I have run down the hill already . I care not a pin for my own life ; and hard as you be you would never have the heart to destroy your own little Caryl . he may be call Caryl you will not deny he that , although he have no right to be call Carne . oh , Caryl , Caryl , you can be so good , when you think there be something to gain by it . only be good to we now , and God will bless you for it , darling . I have give up all the world for you , and you can not have the heart to cast I off . " 
 " what a fool the woman be ! have you ever know I change my mind ? if you scorn your own life , through your own folly , you must care for the brat 's . if you stop here ten minute , you will both be blow to piece . " 
 " through my own folly ! oh , God in heaven , that you should speak so of my love for you ! Squire Carne , you be the bad man that ever live ; and it serve I right for trust you . but where be I to go ? who will take I and support I , and my poor abandon child ? " 
 " your parent , of course , be your natural supporter . you be hurt your child by this low abuse of I . now put aside excitement , and run home , like a sensible woman , before your good father go to bed . " 
 she have watch his face all the time , as if she could scarcely believe that he be in earnest , but he prove it by leave she with a wave of his hat , and hasten back to his lantern . then take up that , and the coil of tow , but leave his package against the wall , he disappear in the narrow passage lead to the powder vault . polly stand still by the break dial , with her eye upon the moon , and her arm around the baby , and a pang in her heart which prevent she from speak , or move , or even know where she be . 
 then Carne , stepping warily , unlock the heavy oak door at the entrance of the cellarage , hold down his lantern , and fix with a wedge the top step of the ladder , which have be make to revolve with a pin and collar at either end , as before describe . after try the step with his hand , to be sure that it be now wedge safely , he fling his coil into the vault and follow . some recollection make he smile as he be go down the step : it be that of a stout man lie at the bottom , shake in every bone , yet sound as a grape ensconce in jelly . as he touch the bottom he hear a little noise as of some small substance fall , but see a piece of old mortar dislodge , he do not turn round to examine the place . if he have do so he would have find behind the ladder the wedge he have just insert to secure the level of the " Inspector 's step . " 
 unwind his coil of tow , which have be steep in saltpetre to make a long fuse , with a toss of his long leg he cross the barricade of solid oak rail about six foot high securely fasten across the vault , for the enclosure of the dangerous storage . inside it be a passage , between chest of arm , dismount cannon , and case from every department of supply , to the explosive part of the magazine , the devourer of the human race , the pulp of the marrow of the Furies gunpowder . 
 of this there be now collect here , and store in tier that reach the roof , enough to blow up half the people of England , or lie they all low with a bullet before it ; yet not enough , not a millionth part enough , to move for the breadth of a hair the barrier betwixt right and wrong , which a very few barrel be enough to do with a man who have sap the foundation . treading softly for fear of a spark from his boot , and guard the lantern well , Carne approach one of the cask in the low tier , and lift the tarpaulin . then he slip the wooden slide in the groove , and allow some five or six pound to run out upon the floor , from which the cask be raise by timber baulk . leave the slide partly open , he spread one end of his coil like a broad lamp - wick in the pile of powder which have run out , and put a brick upon the tow to keep it from shift . then he pay out the rest of the coil on the floor like a snake some thirty foot long , with the tail about a yard inside the barricade . with a very steady hand he take the candle from inside the horn , and kindle that tail of the fuse ; and then replace his light , he recrosse the open timber - work , and swiftly remount the ladder of escape . " twenty minute ' or half an hour 's grace , " he think , " and long before that I shall be at the yew - tree . " 
 but , as he plant his right foot sharply upon the top step of the ladder , that step swing back , and cast he heavily backwards to the bottom . the wedge have drop out , and the step revolve like the treadle of a fox - trap . 
 for a minute or two he lie stunned and senseless , with the lantern before he on its side , and the candle burn a hole in the bubbly horn . slowly recover his wit , he strive to rise , as the deadly peril be bear in upon he . but instead of rise , he fall back again with a curse , and then a long - draw groan ; for pain   like the thrill of a man on the rack   have get hold of he and mean to keep he . his right arm be snap at the elbow , and his left leg just above the knee , and the jar of his spine make he feel as if his core have be split out of he . he have no fat , like shargeloe , to protect he , and no sheath of hair like Twemlow 's . 
 writhe with anguish , he hear a sound which do not improve his condition . it be the spluttering of the fuse , eat its merry way towards the five hundred cask of gunpowder . in the fury of peril he contrive to rise , and stand on his right foot with the other hang limp , while he stay himself with his left hand upon the ladder . even if he could crawl up this , it would benefit he nothing . before he could drag himself ten yard , the explosion would overtake he . his only chance be to quench the fuse , or draw it away from the priming . with a hobble of agony he reach the barricade , and strove to lift his crippled frame over it . it be hopeless ; the power of his back be go , and his limb be unable to obey his brain . then he try to crawl through at the bottom , but the opening of the rail would not admit his body , and the train of ductile fire have leave only ash for he to grasp at . 
 quiver with terror , and mad with pain , he return to the foot of the step , and clung till a gasp of breath come back . then he shout , with all his remain power , " polly , oh , Polly , my own polly ! " 
 Polly have be stand , like a statue of despair , beside the break dial . to she it matter little whether earth should open and swallow she , or fire cast she up to heaven . but his shout arouse she from this trance , and her heart leap up with the fond belief that he have relent , and be call she and the child to share his fortune . there she stand in the archway and look down , and the terror of the scene overwhelm she . through a break arch beyond the barricade pale moonbeam cross the darkness , like the bar of some soft melody ; in the middle the serpent coil be hiss with the deadly nitre ; at the foot of the step be her false lover husband he have call himself with his hat off , and his white face turn in the last supplication towards she , as hers have be turn towards he just now . should a woman be as pitiless as a man ? 
 " come down , for God 's sake , and climb that curse wood , and pull back the fuse , pull it back from the powder . oh , Polly ! and then we will go away together . " 
 " it be too late . I will not risk my baby . you have make I so weak that I could never climb that fence . you be blow up the castle which you promise to my baby ; but you shall not blow up he . you tell I to run away , and run I must . good - bye ; I be go to my natural supporter . " 
 Carne hear her step as she flee , and he fancy that he hear therewith a mocking laugh , but it be a sob , a hysterical sob . she would have help he , if she dare ; but her wit be go in panic . she know not of his shattered limb and horrible plight ; and it flash across she that this be another trick of his to destroy she and the baby , while he flee . she have prove that all his vow be lie . 
 then Carne make his mind up to die like a man , for he see that escape be impossible . limp back to the fatal barrier , he raise himself to his full height , and stand proudly to see , as he put it , the last of himself . not a quiver of his haughty feature show the bodily pain that rack he , nor a flinch of his deep eye confess the tumult move in his mind and soul . he pull out his watch and lay it on the top rail of the old oak fence : there be not enough light to read the time , but he could count the tick he have to live . suddenly hope flash through his heart , like the crack of a gun , like a lightning fork a big rat be bite an elbow of the yarn where some tallow have fall upon it . would he cut it , would he drag it away to his hole ? would he pull it a little from its fatal end ? he be strong enough to do it , if he only understand . the fizz of saltpetre disturb the rat , and he hoist his tail and skip back to his home . 
 the last thought of this unhappy man go back upon his early day ; and thing , which he have pass without think of , stand before he like his tombstone . none of his recent crime come now to his memory to disturb it there be time enough after the body for they but trifle which have first deprave the mind , and slip whose repetition have make slippery the soul , like the alphabet of death , grow plain to he . then he think of his mother , and cross himself , and say a little prayer to the Virgin . 
 Charron be wait by the old yew - tree , and Jerry sit tremble , with his eye upon the castle , while the black horse , rope to a branch , be mourn the scarcity of oats and the abundance of gnat . 
 " pest and the devil , but the coast be all alive ! " cry the Frenchman , soothe anxiety with solid and liquid comfort . " something have go wrong behind the tail of everything . and there go that big Stoobar , blaze with his sordid battery ! arouse thee , old Cheray ! the time too late be over . those light thrice accurse will display our little boat , and John Bull be rush with a thousand sail . the Commander be mad . they will have he , and we too . shall I dance by a rope ? it be the only dance probable for I in England . " 
 " I have never expect any good to come , " the old man answer , without move . " the curse of the house be upon the young Squire . I see it in his eye this morning , the same as I see in his father 's eye , when the sun be go down the very night he die . I shall never see he more , sir , nor you either , nor any other man that bid to the right side of his coffin . " 
 " Bah ! what a set you be of funeral , you englishman ! but if I think he be in risk , I would stay to see the end of it . " 
 " here come the end of it ! " the old man cry , leap up and catch at a rugged cord of trunk , with his other hand point up the hill . from the base of the castle a broad blaze rush , show window and battlement , arch and tower , as in a flicker of the northern light . then up go all the length of fabric , as a wanton child toss his Noah 's ark . keep and buttress , tower and arch , mullioned window and battlement , in a fiery furnace leap on high , like the outburst of a volcano . then , with a roar that rock the earth , they break into a storm of ruin , sweep the heaven with a flood of fire , and spread the sea with a mantle of blood . follow slowly in stately spire , and calmly swallow everything , a fountain of dun smoke arise , and solemn silence fill the night . 
 " all over now , thank the angel and the saint ! my faith , but I make up my mind to join they , " cry Charron , who have fall , or be fell by the concussion . " Cheray , art thou still alive ? the smoke be in my neck . I can not liberate my word , but the lump must be all come down by this time , without add to the weight of our poor brain . something fall in this old tree , a long way up , as high as where the crow build . it be like a long body , with one leg and one arm . I hope it be not the Commander ; but one thing be certain he be go to heaven . let we pray that he may stop there , if St. Peter admit a man who be sell the key of his country to the enemy . but we must do duty to ourselves , my Cheray . let we hasten to the sea , and give the signal for the boat . La Torche will be a weak light after this . " 
 " I will not go . I will abide my time . " the old man stagger to a broken column of the ancient gateway which have fall near they , and fling his arm around it . " I remember this since I first could toddle . the way of the Lord be wonderful . " 
 " come away , you old fool , " cry the Frenchman ; " I hear the tramp of soldier in the valley . if they catch you here , it will be drum - head work , and you will swing before morning in the ruin . " 
 " I be very old . my time be short . I would liefer hang from an english beam than deal any more with your outlandish lot . " 
 " farewell to thee , then ! thou art a faithful clod . here be five guineas for thee , of English stamp . I doubt if napoleon shall ever be coin in England . " 
 he be off while he might a gallant Frenchman , and an honest enemy ; such as our country have respect always , and often endeavour to turn into fast friend . but the old man stand and watch the long gap , where for century the castle of the Carnes have tower . and his sturdy faith be reward . 
 " I be starve " these word come feebly from a gaunt , ragged figure that approach he . " for three day my food have be forget ; and bad as it be , I miss it . there come a great rumble , and my wall fall down . Ancient Jerry , I can go no far . I be empty as a shank bone when the marrow - toast be serve . your duty be to feed I , with inferior stuff at any rate . " 
 " no , sir , no ; " the old servitor be rouse by the charge of neglect duty . " Sir Parsley , it be no fault of mine whatever . Squire undertake to see to all of it himself . do not   blame I , sir ; do not   blame I . " 
 " never mind the blame , but make it good , " Mr. Shargeloes answer , meagrely , for he feel as if he could never be fat again . " what do I see there ? it be like a crust of bread , but I be too weak to stoop for it . " 
 " come inside the tree , sir . " the old man lead he , as a grandsire lead a famished child . " what a shame to starve you , and you so hearty ! but the Squire clean forget it , I doubt , with his foreign trick come to this great blow - up . here , sir , here ; please to sit down a moment , while I light a candle . they french chap be so wasteful always , and always grumble at good english victual . here be enough to feed a family Captain Charron have throw by bread , and good mutton , and pretty near half a ham , and a bottle or so of thin nasty foreign wine . eat away , Sir Parsley ; why , it do I good to see you . you feed something like an Englishman . but you know , sir , it be all your own fault at bottom , for come among they foreigner a - meddling . " 
 " you be a fine fellow . you shall be my head butler , " Percival Shargeloes reply , while he make such a meal as he never make before , and never should make again , even when he come to be the right honourable the Lord Mayor of London . 




 CHAPTER LXIV 

 WRATH and SORROW 

 the two most conspicuous man of the age be sadden and cast down just now one by the natural kindly sorrow into which all man live for other , till other live into it for they ; and one by the petulant turn of fortune , twisting and break his well - weave web . Lord Nelson arrive at Springhaven on Monday , to show his affection for his dear old friend ; and the Emperor Napoleon , at the same time , be pace the opposite cliff in grief and dudgeon . 
 he have take his post on some high white land , about a league southward of Boulogne , and with strong field - glass , which he pettishly exchange in doubt of their power and truth , he be scan all the roadway of the shore and the trackless breadth of sea . his quick brain be burn for despatch overland whether from the coast road past Etaples , or far inland by the great route from Paris , or away to the southeast by special courier from the austrian frontier as well as for signal out at sea , and the movement of the british ship , to show that his own be come . he have treat with disdain the suggestion of his faithful Admiral Decres , who have fear to put the truth too plainly , that the fleet order up from the west have fail , and with it the Master 's mighty scheme . have yet to learn the lesson that his good plan might be foil , he be furious when doubt be cast upon this pet design . like a giant of a spider at the nucleus of his web , he watch the broad fan of radiant thread , and the hovering of filmy woof , but without the mild philosophy of that spider , who be verse in the very sad capriciousness of fly . 
 just within hearing   and fain to be far , in his present state of mind   be several young officer of the staff , make little mouth at one another , for want of well pastime , but look as grave , when the mighty man glance round , as schoolboy do under the master 's eye . " send Admiral Decres to I , " the Emperor shout , as he lay down his telescope and return to his petulant to - and - fro . 
 in a few minute Admiral Decres arrive , and after a salute which be not acknowledge , walk in silence at his master 's side . the great man , talk to himself aloud , and revile almost every one except himself , take no more notice of his comrade for some minute than if he have be a poodle keep pace with he . then he turn upon he fiercely , with one hand throw out , as if he would have like to strike he . 
 " what then be the meaning of all this ? " he speak too fast for the other to catch all his word . " you have lose I three day of it . how much long will you conceal your knowledge ? Carne 's scheme have fail , through treachery probably his own . I never like the man . he want to be the master of I of I ! I can do without he ; it be all the well , if my fleet will come . I have three fleet , besides these . any one of they would do . they would do , if even half their crew be dead , so long as they disturb the enemy . you know where Villeneuve be , but you will not tell I . " 
 " I tell your Majesty what I think , " M. Decres reply , with dignity , " but it do not please you to listen to I . shall I now tell your Majesty what I know ? " 
 " ha ! you have dare to have secret despatch ! you know more of the movement of my fleet than I do ! you have be screen he all along . which of you be the bad traitor ? " 
 " your Majesty will regret these word . Villeneuve and myself be devoted to you . I have not hear from he . I have receive no despatch . but in a private letter just receive , which be here at your Majesty 's service , I find these word , which your Majesty can see . ' from my brother on the spanish coast I have just hear . Admiral Villeneuve have sail for Cadiz , believe Nelson to be in chase of he . my brother see the whole fleet crowd sail southward . no doubt it be the good thing they could do . if they come across Nelson , they would be knock to piece . ' your Majesty , that be an opinion only ; but it seem to be share by M. Villeneuve . " 
 Napoleon 's wrath be never speechless except upon one great occasion and its outburst put every other in the wrong , even while he know that he be in the right . regard Decres with a glare of fury , such as no other eye could pour , or meet a glare as of burnish steel fire from a cannon he drive he out of every self - defence or shelter , and shatter he in the dust of his own principle . it be not the difference of rank between they , but the difference in the power of their mind , that chase like a straw before the wind the very stable sens of the man who understand thing . he know that he be right , but the right be rout , and away with it fly all capacity of reason in the pitiless torrent of passion , like a man in a barrel , and the barrel in Niagara . 
 M. Decres know not head from tail , in the rush of invective pour upon he ; but he take off his hat in soft search for his head , and to let in the compliment rain upon it . 
 " it be good , " reply the Emperor , reply to himself , as the foam of his fury begin to pass ; " you will understand , Decres , that I be not angry , but only lament that I have such a set of fool . you be not the bad . I have big fool than you . alas that I should confess it ! " 
 Admiral Decres put his hat upon his head , for the purpose of take it off , to acknowledge the kindness of this compliment . it be the first polite expression he have receive for half an hour . and it would have be the last , if he have dare to answer . 
 " villeneuve can not help it that he be a fool , " continue Napoleon , in a milder strain ; " but he owe it to his rank that he should not be a coward . Nelson be his black beast . Nelson have reduce he to a condition of wet pulp . I shall send a braver man to supersede he . be french fleet forever to turn tail to an inferior force of stupid English ? if I be on the sea , I would sweep Nelson from they . our man be far braver , when they learn to spread their leg . as soon as I have finish with those filthy german , I will take the command of the fleet myself . it will be a bad day for that brag Nelson . give I pen and paper , and send Daru to I . I must conquer the continent once more , I suppose ; and then I will return and deal with England . " 
 in a couple of hour he have shape and finish the plan of a campaign the most triumphant that even he ever plan and accomplish . then his mind become satisfied with good work , and he mount his horse , and for the last time ride through the grand encampment the sun have ever see , distribute his calm smile , as if his nature be too large for tempest . 
 on the sacred white coast , which the great of Frenchmen should only approach as a prisoner , stand a man of less imperious mould , and of sweet and gentle presence a man who be able to command himself in the keen disappointment , because he combine a quick sense of humour with the power of prompt action , and be able to appreciate his own great quality without conclude that there be no other . his face , at all time except those of hot battle , be fill with quiet sadness , as if he be send into the world for some great purpose beyond his knowledge , yet surely not above his aim . year of deep anxiety and ever urgent duty have make he look old before his time , but in no wise abate his natural force . he know that he have duty before he still , and he feel that the only discharge be death . 
 but now , in the tenderness of his heart , he have forget all about himself , and even for the moment about his country . Nelson have take the last fond look at the dear old friend of many changeful year , so true and so pleasant throughout every change . though one eye have fail for the work of the brain , it still be in sympathy with his heart ; and a tear shone upon either wrinkle cheek , as the us of sadness outlast the bright view . he hold Faith by the hand , or she hold by his , as they come forth , without know it , through nature 's demand for an open space , when the air be choke with sorrow .
