 " my dear , you must check it ; you must leave off , " say Nelson , although he be go on himself . " it be useless for I to say a word to you , because I be almost as bad myself . but still I be old , and I feel that I ought to be able to comfort you , if I only know the way . " 
 " you do comfort I , more than I can tell , although you do not   say anything . for any one to sit here , and be sorry with I , make it come a little light . and when it be a man like you , Lord Nelson , I feel a sort of love that make I feel less bitter . Mr. Twemlow drive I wild with a quantity of text , and a great amount of talk about a well land . how would he like to go to it himself , I wonder ? there be a great hole in my heart , and nothing that anybody say can fill it . " 
 " and nothing that any one can do , my dear , " her father 's friend answer , softly , " unless it be your own good self , with the kindness of the Lord to help you . one of the good thing to begin with be to help somebody else , if you can , and lead yourself away into another person 's trouble . be there any one here very miserable ? " 
 " none that I can think of half so miserable as I be . there be great excitement , but no misery . Miss Twemlow have recover her Lord Mayor the gentleman that wear that extraordinary coat oh , I forget , you be not here then . and although he have have a very sad time of it , every one say that the total want of diet will be much well for he than any mere change . I be ashamed to be talk of such trifle now ; but I respect that man , he be so straightforward . if my brother Frank have be at all like he , we should never have be as we be this day . " 
 " my dear , you must not blame poor Frank . he would not come down to the dinner because he hat warlike speech . but he have see the error of his way . no more treasonable stuff for he . he think it be large , and poetic , and all that , like give one 's shirt to an impostor . all of we make mistake sometimes . I have make a great many myself , and have always be the foremost to perceive they . but your own brave lover have you forget he ? he fight like a hero , I be tell ; and nothing could save his life except that he wear a new - fashioned periwig . " 
 " I would rather not talk of he now , Lord Nelson , although he have no periwig . I be deeply thankful that he escape ; and no doubt do his good , as he be bind to do . I try to be fair to everybody , but I can not help blame every one , when I come to remember how blind we have be . Captain Stubbard must have be so blind , and Mrs. Stubbard a great deal bad , and bad of all his own aunt , Mrs. Twemlow . oh , Lord Nelson , if you have only stop here , instead of hurry away for more glory ! you see the whole of it ; you predict everything ; you even warn we again in your last letter ! and yet you must go away , and leave we to ourselves ; and this be how the whole of it have end . " 
 " my dear child , I will not deny that the eye of Nelson have a special gift for pierce the wile of the scoundrelly foe . but I be under order , and must go . the nation believe that it could not do without I , although there be other man every bit as good , and in their own opinion superior . but the enemy have never be of that opinion ; and a great deal depend upon what they think . and the rule have be always to send I where there be many kick but few copper . I have never be know to repine . we all err ; but if we do our duty as your dear father do his , the Lord will forgive we , when our enemy escape . when my time come , as it must do soon , there will be plenty to carp at I ; but I shall not care , if I have do my good . your father do his good , and be happy . " 
 Faith Darling take his hand again , and her tear be for he quite as much as for herself . " give I one of the button of your coat , " she say ; " here be one that can not last till you get home . " 
 it be hang by a thread , and yet the hero be very loth to part with it , though if it have part with he , the chance be ten to one against his miss it . however , he conquer himself , but not so entirely as to let she cut it off . if it must go , it should be by his own hand . he pull out a knife and cut it off , and she kiss it when he give it to she . 
 " I should like to do more than that , " he say , though he would soon have part with many guineas . " be there nobody here that I can help , from my long good - will to Springhaven ? " 
 " oh , yes ! how stupid I be ! " cry Faith . " I forget everybody in my own trouble . there be a poor young man with a broken heart , who come to I this morning . he have do no harm that I know of , but he fall into the power of that wicked but I will use no harsh word , because he be go most dreadfully to his last account . this poor youth say that he only care to die , after all the thing that have happen here , for he have always be fond of my father . at first I refuse to see he , but they tell I such thing that I could not help it . he be the son of our chief man here , and you say what a fine british seaman he would make . " 
 " I remember two or three of that description , especially young Dan Tugwell . " Nelson have an amazing memory of all who have serve under he , or even have wish to do so . " I see by your eye that it be young Tugwell . if it will be any pleasure to you , I will see he , and do what I can for he . what have he do , my dear , and what can I do for he ? " 
 " he have fall into black disgrace , and his only desire be to redeem it by die for his country . his own father have refuse to see he , although he be mainly the cause of it ; and his mother , who be Erle Twemlow 's nurse , be almost out of her mind with grief . a braver young man never live , and he be once the pride of Springhaven . he save poor dolly from drown , when she be very young , and the boat upset . his father chastise he cruelly for fall under bad influence . then he run away from the village , and seem to have be in french employment . but he be keep in the dark , and have no idea that he be act against his own country . " 
 " he have be a traitor , " say Lord Nelson , sternly . " I can not help such a man , even for your sake . " 
 " he have not be a traitor , but betray , " cry Faith ; " he believe that his only employment be to convey private letter for the poor french prisoner , of whom we have so many hundred . I will not contend that he be right in that ; but still it be no very great offence . even you must have often long to send letter to those you love in England ; and you know how hard it be in war time . but what they really want he for be to serve as their pilot upon this coast . and the moment he discover that , though they offer he bag of gold to do it , he face his death like an Englishman . they attempt to keep he in a stupid state with drug , so that he might work like a mere machine . but he find out that , and would eat nothing but hard biscuit . they have he in one of their shallow boat , or prame , as they call they , which be to lead they in upon signal from the arch - traitor . this be on Saturday , Saturday night that dreadful time when we be all so gay . they hold a pair of pistol at poor Dan 's head , or at least a man be hold one to each of his ear , and they cord his arm , because he venture to remonstrate . that be before they have even start , so you may suppose what they would have do to we . Poor Daniel make up his mind to die , and it would have ease his mind , he say now , if he have do so . but while they be wait for the signal , which through dear father 's vigilance they never do receive , Dan manage to free both his hand in the dark , and as soon as he see the man get sleepy , he knock they both down , and jump overboard ; for he can swim like a fish , or even well . he have very little hope of escape , as he say , and the french fire fifty shot after he . with great presence of mind , he give a dreadful scream , as if he be shoot through the head at least , then he fling up his leg , as if he be go down ; but he swam under water for perhaps a hundred yard , and luckily the moon go behind a black cloud . then he come to a boat , which have break adrift , and although he do not dare to climb into she , he hold on by she , on the further side from they . she be drift away with the tide , and at last he venture to get on board of she , and find a pair of oar , and be pick up at daylight by a smuggling boat run for Newhaven . he be land last night , and he hear the dreadful news , and have plenty of money , he hire a post - chaise , and never stop until he reach Springhaven . he look wear out now ; but if his mind be easy , he would soon be as strong as ever . " 
 " it be a strange story , my dear , " say Nelson ; " but I see that it have do you good to tell it , and I have know many still strange . but how could he have money , after such a hard escape ? " 
 " that show as much as anything how brave he be . he have make up his mind that if he succeed in knock down both those sentinel , he would have the bag of gold which be put for his reward in case of his steer they successfully . and before he jump overboard he snatch it up , and it help he to dive and to swim under water . he put it in his flannel shirt by way of ballast , and he stick to it up to the present moment . " 
 " my dear , " reply Lord Nelson , much impressed , ' such a man deserve to be in my own crew . if he can show I that bag , and stand question , I will send he to Portsmouth at my own expense , with a letter to my dear friend Captain Hardy . " 




 CHAPTER LXV 

 TRAFALGAR 

 Lord Nelson sail from Portsmouth on the 15th of September , in his favourite ship the victory , to take his last command . he know that he never should come home , except as a corpse for burial , but he fasten his mind on the work before he , and neglect nothing . " a fair fight , and no favour , " be the only thing he long for . 
 and this he do obtain at last . the french commander - in - chief come forth , with all his mighty armament , not of his own desire , but goad by imperious sneer , and sting that make his manhood tingle . he spread the sea - power of two nation in a stately crescent , double - line   as the moon be double when beheld through fine plate - glass   a noble sight , a paramount temptation for the british tow - rope . 
 " what a lot for we to take to Spithead ! " be the british tar 's remark , as forty ship of the line and frigate show their glossy side , and canvas bosom with the gentle air and vein with gliding sunlight . a grander spectacle never be of laborious man 's creation ; and the work of the Lord combine to show it to the good advantage dark headland in the distance stand as a massive background , long pellucid billow lift bulk titanic , and lace - like maze , sweet air wander from heaven , early sun come fresh from dew , all the good - will of the world inspire man to merriness . 
 Nelson be not fierce of nature , but as gentle as a lamb . his great desire , as he always prove , be never to destroy his enemy by the number of one man spareable . he have always be lead by the force of education , confirm by that of experience , to know that the duty of an Englishman be to lessen the stock of Frenchmen ; yet he never be free from regret when compel to act up to his conscience , upon a large scale . 
 it be an old saying that nature have provide for every disease its remedy , and challenge man to find it out , which they be clever enough not to do . for that deadly disease Napoleon , the remedy be Nelson ; and as soon as he should be consume , another would appear in Wellington . such be the fortune of Britannia , because she never boast , but grumble always . the boaster soon exhaust his subject ; the grumbler have matter that last for ever . 
 Nelson have much of this national virtue . " half of they will get away , " he say to Captain Blackwood , of the Euryalus , who be come for his late order , " because of that rascally port to leeward . if the wind have hold as it be last night , we should have have every one of they . it do seem hard , after wait so long . and the sky look like a gale of wind . it will blow to - night , though I shall not hear it . a gale of wind with disabled ship mean terrible destruction . do all you can to save those poor fellow . when they be beat , we must consider their life even more than our own , you know , because we have be the cause of it . you know my wish as well as I do . remember this one especially . " 
 " good - bye , my lord , till the fight be over . " Captain Blackwood love his chief with even more than the warm affection feel by all the fleet for he . " when we have get they , I shall come back , and find you safe and glorious . " 
 " God bless you , Blackwood ! " Lord Nelson answer , look at he with a cheerful smile . " but you will never see I alive again . " 
 the hero of a hundred fight , who know that this would be his last , put on his favourite ancient coat , threadbare through many a conflict with hard time and hard enemy . its beauty , like his own , have suffer in the cause of duty ; the gold embroidery have take leave of absence in some place , and in other show more fray of silk than gleam of yellow glory ; and the four star fasten on the left breast want a little plate - powder sadly . but Nelson be quite content with they , and like a child for he always keep in his heart the childhood 's freshness he gaze at the star he be proud of , the Star of the Bath , and through a fond smile sigh . through the ray of that star his death be come , ere a quarter of a day should be add to his life . 
 with less pretension and air of greatness than the captain of a penny steamer now display , Nelson go from deck to deck , and visit every man at quarter , as if the battle hang on every one . there be scarcely a man whom he do not know , as well as a farmer know his winter hand ; and loud cheer rang from gun to gun when his order have be answer . his order be , " reserve your fire until you be sure of every shot . " then he take his stand upon the quarter - deck , assure of victory , and assure that his last bequest to the british nation would be honour sacredly about which the less we say the well . 
 in this great battle , which crush the naval power of France , and save our land from further threat of inroad , Blyth Scudamore be not engage , be still attach to the Channel fleet ; but young Dan Tugwell bear a share , and no small share by his own account and that of his native village , which receive he proudly when he come home . place at a gun on the upper deck , on the starboard side near the mizzen - mast , he fight like a Briton , though daze at first by the roar , and the smoke , and the crash of timber . Lord Nelson have notice he more than once , as one of the smart of his crew , and have say to he that very morning , " for the honour of Springhaven , Dan , behave well in your first action . " and the youth have never forget that , when the sulphurous fog envelop he , and the rush of death lift his curly hair , and his foot be sodden and his stocking hot with the blood of shattered messmate . 
 in the wildest of the wild pell - mell , as the victory lie like a pelt log , roll to the storm of shot , with three ship at close quarter hurl all their metal at she , and a fourth alongside clutched so close that muzzle be tompion for muzzle , while the cannon - ball so thickly fly that many sailor with good eye see they meet in the air and shatter one another , an order be issue for the starboard gun on the upper deck to cease firing . an eager - minded Frenchman , adapt his desire as a spring - board to his conclusion , be actually able to believe that Nelson 's own ship have surrender ! he must have be off his head ; and his inductive process be soon amend by the logic of fact , for his head be off he . the reason for silence those gun be good they be likely to do more damage to an english ship which lie beyond than to the foe at the porthole . the man who have serve those gun be order below , to take the place of man who never should fire a gun again . Dan Tugwell , as he turn to obey the order , cast a glance at the Admiral , who give he a little nod , meaning , " well do , Dan . " 
 Lord Nelson have just make a little joke , such as he often indulge in , not from any carelessness about the scene around he which be truly awful but simply to keep up his spirit , and those of his brave and beloved companion . Captain Hardy , a tall and portly man , clothe in bright uniform , and advance with a martial stride , cast into shade the mighty hero quietly walk at his left side . and Nelson be cover with dust from the quarter - gallery of a pound ship , which he have not stop to brush away . 
 " thank God , " think Dan , " if those fellow in the top , who be pick we off so , shoot at either of they , they will be sure to hit the big man first . " 
 in the very instant of his thought , he see Lord Nelson give a sudden start , and then reel , and fall upon both knee , strive for a moment to support himself with his one hand on the deck . then his hand give way , and he fall on his left side , while Hardy , who be just before he , turn at the cabin ladderway , and stoop with a loud cry over he . Dan run up , and place his bare arm under the wound shoulder , and help to raise and set he on his staggering leg . 
 " I hope you be not much hurt , my lord ? " say the Captain , do his good to smile . 
 " they have do for I at last , " the hero gasp . " hardy , my backbone be shoot through . " 
 through the roar of battle , sob of dear love sound along the blood - stain deck , as Dan and another seaman take the pride of our nation tenderly , and carry he down to the orlop - deck . yet even so , in the deadly pang and drain of the life - blood , the sense of duty never fail , and the love of country conquer death . with his feeble hand he contrive to reach the handkerchief in his pocket , and spread it over his face and breast , lest the crew should be dishearten . 
 " I know who fire that shot , " cry Dan , when he see that he could help no more . " he never shall live to boast of it , if I have to board the french ship to fetch he . " 
 he run back quickly to the quarterdeck , and there find three or four other eager to give their life for Nelson 's death . the mizzen - top of the redoutable , whence the fatal shot have come , be scarcely so much as fifty foot from the starboard rail of the victory . the man who be station in that top , although they have no brass cohorn there , such as those in the main and fore top ply , have take many english life , while the thick smoke surge around they . 
 for some time they have work unheeded in the loud roar of cannon , and when at last they be observe , it be hard to get a fair shot at they , not only from the rolling of the entangled ship , and cloud of blind vapour , but because they retire out of sight to load , and only come forward to catch their aim . however , by the exertion of our marine who should have be at they long ago these sharp - shooter from the coign of vantage be now reduce to three brave fellow . they have only do their duty , and perhaps have no idea how completely they have do it ; but naturally enough our man look at they as if they be " too bad for hang . " Smoky as the air be , the three man see that a very strong feeling be arouse against they , and that none of their own side be at hand to back they up . and the language of the English though they could not understand it be clearly that of bitter condemnation . 
 the least resolute of they become depressed by this , be doubtless a Radical who have be teach that Vox populi be Vox Dei . he endeavour , therefore , to slide down the rigging , but be shoot through the heart , and dead before he have time to know it . at the very same moment the most desperate villain of the three as we should call he or the most heroic of these patriot   as the french historian describe he   pop forward and shoot a worthy Englishman , who be shake his fist instead of point his gun . 
 then an old quartermaster , who be stand on the poop , with his leg spread out as comfortably as if he have his Sunday dinner on the spit before he , shout " that be he , boy that glaze hat beggar ! have at he all together , next time he come forrard . " as he speak , he fall dead , with his tooth in his throat , from the fire of the other Frenchman . but the carbine drop from the man who have fire , and his body fall dead as the one he have destroy , for a sharp little Middy , behind the quartermaster , send a bullet through the head , as the hand draw trigger . the slayer of Nelson remain alone , and he keep back warily , where none could see he . 
 " all of you fire , quick one after other , " cry Dan , who have pick up a loaded musket , and be kneel in the embrasure of a gun ; " fire so that he may tell the shot ; that will fetch he out again . sing out first , ' there he be ! ' as if you see he . " 
 the man on the quarter - deck and poop do so , and the Frenchman , who be watch through a hole , come forward for a safe shot while they be load . he point the long gun which have kill Nelson at the smart young officer on the poop , but the muzzle fly up ere he pull the trigger , and lean forward he fall dead , with his leg and arm spread , like a jack for oil axle . Dan have go through some small - arm drill in the fortnight he spend at Portsmouth , and his eye be too keen for the bull' - eye . with a rest for his muzzle he lay it truly for the spot where the Frenchman would reappear ; with extreme punctuality he shoot he in the throat ; and the gallant man who deprive the world of Nelson be thus despatch to a well one , three hour in front of his victim . 




 CHAPTER LXVI 

 the last bulletin 

 to Britannia this be but feeble comfort , even if she hear of it . she have lose her pet hero , the simple and dearest of all the thousand she have bear and nurse , and for every penny she have grudge he in the flesh , she would lay a thousand pound upon his bone . to put it more poetically , her smile be turn to tear which cost she something and the laurel droop in the cypress shade . the hostile fleet be destroy ; brave France would never more come out of harbour to contend with England ; the foggy fear of invasion be like a morning fog disperse ; and yet the fund   the pulse of England   fall at the loss of that one defender . 
 it be a gloomy evening , and come time for good people to be in - door , when the big news reach Springhaven . since the Admiral sleep in the green churchyard , with no despatch to receive or send , the importance of Springhaven have decline in all opinion except its own , and even Captain Stubbard could not keep it up . when the squire be shoot , and Master Erle as well , and Carne Castle go high than a lark could soar , and folk be fool enough to believe that Boney would dare put his foot down there , John Prater have do a most wonderful trade , and never a man who could lay his tongue justly with the pen that come splutter from London have any call for a fortnight together to go to bed sober at his own expense . but this bright season end quite as suddenly as it have begin ; and when these great " hunger " as those veteran be entitle who deal most freely with the marvellous have lay their head together to produce and confirm another guinea 's worth of fiction , the London press would have none of it . public interest have rush into another channel ; and the man who have thrive for a fortnight on their tongue be drive to employ they on their hand again . 
 but now , on the sixth of November , a new excitement be in store for they . the calm obscurity of night flow in , through the tree that belong to Sir Francis now , and along his misty meadow ; and the only sound in the village lane be the murmur of the brook beside it , or the gentle sigh of the retire sea . boy of age enough to make much noise , or at least to prolong it after nightfall , be away in the fishing - boat , receive whack almost as often as they need they ; for those time   unlike these   be equal to their fundamental duty . in the wind lane outside the ground of the Hall , and shape its convenience naturally by that of the more urgent brook , a man to show what the time be come to have lately set up a shoeing forge . he have do it on the strength of the trooper ' horse come down the hill so fast , and often with their cog wear out , yet go as hard as if they have no knee , or at least none belong to their rider . and although he be not a Springhaven man , he have be allow to marry a Springhaven woman , one of the Capers up the hill ; and John Prater   who be akin to he by marriage , and perhaps have an eye to the inevitable ailment of a man whose horse be ail   back up his daring scheme so strongly that the Admiral , anxious for the public good , have allow this smithy to be set up here . 
 John Keatch be the man who establish this , of the very same family   still thrive in West Middlesex   which for the service of the state supply an official whose mantle it be now find hard to fill ; and the blacksmith be know as " Jack Ketch " in the village , while his forge be become the centre of news . Captain Stubbard employ he for battery us , and find his swing - shutter large than those of Widow Shanks , and more cheaply light up by the glow of the forge , be now begin , in spite of her remonstrance , to post all his very big proclamation there . 
 " rouse up your fire , Ketch , " he say that evening , as he stand at the door of the smithy , with half a dozen of his child at his heel . " bring a dozen clout - nail ; here be a tremendous piece of news ! " 
 the blacksmith make a blaze with a few stroke of his bellow , and swing his shutter forward , so that all might read . 
 " GREAT and glorious victory . twenty line - of - battle ship destroy or capture . Lord Nelson shoot dead . God save the king ! " 
 " keep your fire up . I will pay a shilling for the coal , " cry the Captain , in the flush of excitement . " bring out your cow 's horn , and go and blow it at the corner . and that drum you have to mend , my boy and girl will beat it . Jack , run up to the battery , and tell they to blaze away for their very life . " 
 in less than five minute all the village be there , with the reader put foremost , all read together at the top of their voice , for the benefit of the rest . behind they stand Polly Cheeseman , peep , with the glare of the fire on her sad pale face and the ruddy cheek of her infant . " make way for Widow Carne , and the young Squire Carne , " the loud voice of Captain Zeb command ; " any man as stand afront of she will have I upon he . now , ma'am , stand forth , and let they look at you . " 
 this be a sudden thought of Captain Tugwell 's ; but it fix her rank among they , as the order of the King might . the strong sense of justice , always ready in Springhaven , back up her right to be what she have believe herself , and would have be , but for foul deceit and falsehood . and if the proud spirit of Carne ever wander around the ancestral property , it would have receive in the next generation a righteous shock at descry in large letter , well pick out with shade : " Caryl Carne , Grocer and Butterman , Cheese - monger , Dealer in Bacon and sausage . license to sell Tea , Coffee , Snuff , Pepper , and Tobacco . " 
 for Cheeseman raise his head again , with the spirit of a true british tradesman , as soon as the nightmare of traitorous plot and contraband import be over . Captain Tugwell on his behalf lead the fishing fleet against that renegade La Liberte , and cast the foreigner overboard , they restore her integrity as the London Trader . Mr. Cheeseman shed a tear , and put on a new apron , and entirely reform his political view , which have be loose and whiggish . uprightness of the most sensitive order that which have slip and strain its tendon stamp all his dealing , even in the butter line ; and fact have furnish a creditable motive for his rash reliance upon his own cord , he turn amid applause to the pleasant pastime of a smug church - warden . and when he be waft to a still sublimer sphere , his grandson carry on the business well . 
 have spread the great news in this striking manner , Captain Stubbard though grow very bulky now with good living , ever since his pay be double set off at a conscientious pace against the stomach of the hill , lest haply the Hall should feel aggrieved at hear all this noise and have to wonder what the reason be . he know , and be grateful at know , that Carne 's black crime and devilish plot have work an entire revulsion in the candid but naturally too soft mind of the author of the Harmodiad . Sir Francis be still of a liberal mind , and still admire his own work . but forget that nobody read they , he fear the extensive harm they might produce , although he be now resolve to write even well in the opposite direction . on the impulse of literary conscience , he hold a council with the gardener Swipes , as to the good composition of bonfire for the consumption of poetry . Mr. Swipes recommend dead pea - haulm , with the stick leave in it to ensure a draught . then the poet in the garden with a long bean - stick administer fire to the whole edition , not only of the Harmodiad , but also of the Theiodemos , his later and even grander work . person incapable of lofty thought attribute this the most sage and practical of all form of palinode to no high source than the pretty face and figure , and sweet patriotism , of Lady Alice , the young sister of Lord Dashville . and subsequent fact , to some extent , confirm this interpretation . 
 the old house look gloomy and dull of brow , with only three window show light , as stout Captain Stubbard , with his short sword swing from the bulky position where his waist have be , strode along the winding of the hill towards the door . at a sharp corner , under some tree , he come almost shoulder to shoulder with a tall man strike into the road from a foot - path . the Captain draw his sword , for his nerve have be flurry ever since the great explosion , which lay he on his back among his own cannon . 
 " a friend , " cry the other , " and a great admirer of your valour , Captain , but not a worthy object for its display . " 
 " my dear friend shargeloe ! " reply the Captain , a little ashamed of his own vigilance . " how be you , my dear sir ? and how be the system ? " 
 " the system will never recover from the trick that infernal Carne have play with it . but never mind that , if the intellect survive ; we all owe a debt to our country . I have meet you in the very nick of time . yesterday be Guy Fawkes ' day , and I want to be marry then ; but the people be not ready . I intend to have it now on New - Year 's day , because then I shall always remember the date . I be go up here to make a strange request , and I want you to say that it be right and proper . an opinion from a distinguished sailor will go a long way with the daughter of an Admiral . I want the young lady to be my bridesmaid and then for the little one , your Maggy and your Kitty . I be bind to go to London for a month to - morrow , and then I could order all the bracelet and the brooch , if I be only certain who the blessed four would be . " 
 " I never have any bridesmaid myself , and I do not   know anything about they . I think that the lady be the people to settle that . " 
 " the lady be glad to be relieve of the expense , and I wish to start well , " reply Shargeloes . " why be ninety - nine man out of a hundred henpecke ? " 
 " I be sure I do not   know , except that they can not   help it . but have you hear the great news of this evening ? " 
 " the reason be , " continue the member of the Corporation , " that they begin with be nobody . they leave the whole management of their wedding to the woman , and they never recover the rein . Miss Twemlow be one of the most charming of her sex ; but she have a decide character , which properly guide will be admirable . but to give it the lead at the outset would be fatal to future happiness . therefore I take this affair upon myself . I pay for it all , and I mean to do it all . " 
 " what thing you do learn in London ! " the Captain answer , with a sigh . " oh , if I have only have the money but it be too late to talk of that . once more , have you hear the news ? " 
 " about the great battle , and the death of Nelson ? yes , I hear of all that this morning . but I leave it to come in proper course from you . now here we be ; mind you back I up . the Lord Mayor be come to be my good man . " 
 the two sister , dress in the deep mourning , and pale with long sorrow and loneliness , look wholly unfit for festive scene ; and as soon as they hear of this new distress the loss of their father 's dear friend , and their own beloved hero they leave the room , to have a good cry together , while their brother entertain the visitor . " it can not   be do now , " Mr. Shargeloes confess ; " and after all , Eliza be the proper person . I must leave that to she , but nothing else that I can think of . there can not   be much harm in my letting she do that . " 
 it be do by a gentleman after all , for the worthy Rector do it . the bride would liefer have dispense with bridesmaid so much fair than herself , and although unable to advance that reason , find fifty other against ask they . but her father have set his mind upon it , and together with his wife so press the matter that Faith and dolly , much against their will , consent to come out of mourn for a day , but not into gay habiliment . 
 the bride be attire wonderfully , stunningly , carnageously as Johnny , just gifted with his commission , and thereby with much slang , describe she ; and in truth she carry her bunt well , as Captain Stubbard tell his wife , and Captain Tugwell confirm it . but the eye of everybody with half an eye follow the two form in silver - grey . that be the near approach to brightness those lover of their father allow themselves , within five month of his tragic death ; though if the old Admiral could have look down from the main - top , probably he would have shout , " no flag at half - mast for I , my pet ! " 
 two young man with melancholy glance follow these fair bridesmaid , be tantalize by these nuptial rite , because they know no well . one of they hop that his time would come , when he have push his great discovery ; and if the art of photography have be know , his face would have be his fortune . for he bear at the very top of it the seal and stamp of his patent the manifest impact of a bullet , diffract by the power of Pong . the root of his hair the terminus of blush , accord to all good novelist have serve an even more useful purpose , by enable he to blush again . strengthen by Pong , they have defy the lead , and deflect it into a shallow channel , already begin to be overgrow by the aid of that same potent drug . Erle Twemlow look little the bad for his wound ; to a lady perhaps , to a man of science certainly , more interesting than he have be before . as he gaze at the bride all bespangle with gold , he feel that he have in his trunk the mean of bespangle his bride with diamond . but the bad of it be that he must wait , and fight , and perhaps get kill , before he could settle in life and make his fortune . as an officer of a march regiment , order to rejoin immediately , he must flesh his sword in lather first for he have find no razor strong enough and postpone the day of rich till the golden date of peace . 
 the other young man have no solace of wealth , even in the blue distance , to whisper to his troubled heart . although he be a real " Captain Scuddy " now , be post to the Danae , 42 - gun frigate , the capacity of his cocked hat would be try by no shower of gold impending . for mighty dread of the Union - jack have fall upon the tricolor ; that gallant flag perceive at last that its proper flight be upon dry land , where as yet there be none to flout it . Trafalgar have reduce by 50 per cent . the british sailor 's chance of prize - money . 
 such computation be not , however , the chief distress of Scudamore . the happiness of his fair round face be less pronounced than usual , because he have vainly strive for an interview with his love one . with all her fault he love she still , and long to make they all his own . he could not help be sadly shock by her fatal coquetry with the traitor Carne , and slippery conduct to his own poor self . but love in his faithful heart maintain that she have already atone for that too bitterly and too deeply ; and the settle sorrow of her face , and listless submission of her movement , show that she be now a very different dolly . faith , who have always be grave enough , seem gaiety itself in comparison with her young sister , once so gay . in their simple dress grey jaconet muslin , sparely trim with lavender and wear no jewel or ornament , but a single snow - drop in the breast , the lovely bridesmaid look as if they defy all the world to make they bride . 
 but the rector would not let they off from come to the breakfast party , and with the well - breed sense of fitness they obey his bidding . Captain Stubbard   whose joke have miss fire too often to be satisfied with a small touch - hole now   be broadly facetious at their expense ; and Johnny , return thank for they , surprise the good company by his manly tone , and contempt of life before begin it . this invigorated Scudamore , by renew his faith in human nature as a thing beyond calculation . he whisper a word or so to his friend Johnny while Mr. and Mrs. Shargeloes be bow farewell from the window of a great family coach from London , which the Lord Mayor have lend they , to make up for not come . for come he could not though he long to do so , and all Springhaven expect he on account of the great preparation in hand for the funeral of Lord Nelson . 
 " Thy servant will see to it , " the boy reply , with a wink at his sister , whom he be to lead home ; for Sir Francis have make his way down to the beach , to meditate his new poem , Theriodemos . 
 " his behaviour , " think dolly , as she put on her cloak , " have be perfect . how thankful I feel for it ! he never cast one glance at I . he quite enter into my feeling towards he . but how much more credit to his mind than to his heart ! " 
 Scudamore , at a wary distance , keep his eye upon she , as if she have be a french frigate glide under strong land battery , from which he must try to cut she out . presently he see that his good friend Johnny have do he the service request . at a fork of the path lead to the Hall , Miss dolly depart towards the left upon some errand among the tree , while her brother and sister go on towards the house . forget the dignity of a Post - Captain , the gallant Scuddy make a cut across the grass , as if he be play prisoner 's base with the boy at Stonnington , and intercept the fair prize in a bend of the brook , where the winter sun be nurse the first primrose . 
 " you , Captain Scudamore ! " say the bridesmaid , turn as if she could never trust her eye again . " you must have lose your way . this path lead nowhere . " 
 " if it only lead to you , that be all that I could wish for . I be content to go to nothing , if I may only go with you . " 
 " my brother send I , " say dolly , look down , with more colour on her cheek than they have own for month , and the snow - drop quivering on her breast , " to search for a primrose or two for he to wear when he din at the rectory this evening . we shall not go , of course . we have do enough . but Frank and Johnny think they ought to go . " 
 " may I help you to look ? I be lucky in that way . I use to find so many thing with you , in the happy time that use to be . " Blyth see that her eyelid be quiver with tear . " I will go away , if you would rather have it so . but you use to be so good - natured to I . " 
 " so I be still . or at least I mean that people should now be good - natured to I . oh , Captain Scudamore , how foolish I have be ! " 
 " do not   say so , do not   think it , do not   believe it for a moment , " say Scudamore , scarcely know what he say , as she burst into a storm of sob . " oh , dolly , dolly , you know you mean no harm . you be break your darling heart , when you do not   deserve it . I could not bear to look at you , and think of it , this morning . everybody love you still , as much and more than ever . oh , dolly , I would rather die than see you cry so terribly . " 
 " nobody love I , and I hate myself . I could never have believe I should ever hate myself . go away , you be too good to be near I . go away , or I shall think you want to kill I . and I wish you would do it , Captain Scudamore . " 
 " then let I stop , " say the Captain , very softly . she smile at the turn of his logic , through her tear . then she weep with new anguish , that she have no right to smile . 
 " only tell I one thing may I hold you ? not of course from any right to do it , but because you be so overcome , my own , own dolly . " the Captain very cleverly put one arm round she , at first with a very light touch , and then with a firm clasp , as she do not draw away . her cloak be not very cumbrous , and her tumultuous heart be but a little way from his . 
 " you know that I never could help love you , " he whisper , as she seem to wonder what the meaning be . " may I ever hope that you will like I ? " 
 " I ! how can it matter now to anybody ? I use to think it do ; but I be very foolish then . I know my own value . it be less than this . this little flower have be a good creature . it have be true to its place , and hurt nobody . " 
 instead of seek for any more flower , she be take from her breast the one she have the snow - drop , and threaten to tear it in piece . 
 " if you give it to I , I shall have some hope . " as he speak , he look at she steadfastly , without any shyness or fear in his eye , but as one who know his own good heart , and have a right to be answer clearly . the maiden in one glance understand all the tale of his wonderful daring , which she never use to believe , because he seem afraid to look at she . 
 " you may have it , if you like , " she say ; " but , Blyth , I shall never deserve you . I have behave to you shamefully . and I feel as if I could never bear to be forgive for it . " 
 for the sake of peace and happiness , it must be hop that she conquer this feminine feeling , which spring from an equity of nature the desire that none should do to we more than we ever could do to they . certain it be that when the Rector hold his dinner party , two gallant bosom throb beneath the emblem of purity and content . the military Captain 's snow - drop hang where every one might observe it , and some gentle - witted joke be make about its whereabouts that morning . by - and - by it grow weary on its stalk and fall , and Erle Twemlow never miss it . but the other snow - drop be not see , except by the wearer with a steal glance , when people be make a loyal noise a little glance steal at his own heart . he have make a little cuddy there inside his inner sarcenet , and down his plait neck - cloth run a sly companionway to it , so that his eye might steal a visit to the joy that be over his heart and in it . thus be woman adore by man , especially those who deserve it least . 
 " Attention , my dear friend , attention , if you please , " cry the Rector , rise , with a keen glance at Scuddy . " I will crave your attention before the lady go , and theirs , for it concern they equally . we have pass through a period of dark peril , a long time of trouble and anxiety and doubt . by the mercy of the Lord , we have escape ; but with loss that have empty our poor heart . England have lose she two foremost defender , Lord Nelson , and Admiral Darling . to they we owe it that we be now begin the New Year happily , with the blessing of Heaven , and my dear daughter marry . next week we shall attend the grand funeral of the hero , and obtain good place by due influence . my son - in - law , Percival shargeloe , can do just as he please at St. Paul 's . therefore let we now , with deep thanksgiving , and one hand upon our heart , lift up our glass , and in silence pledge the memory of our great man . with the spirit of Britons we echo the last word that fall from the lip of our die hero ' thank God , I have do my duty ! ' his memory shall abide for ever , because he love his country . " 
 the company rise , lay hand on heart , and deeply bow , say " amen ! " 
 the END .
